ActivatePersistentLevelTemplate.description:
Makes the persistent level template instance _LevelTemplate in the current level/region active.
If previously another persistent instance of that level template was active (in this level/region or in another one), by default all interactive objects present in that previous instance (within the LevelTemplateBoundTrigger specified in the Level -> Edit Level Data... of the template level) will be teleported to the newly activated one. This not only holds for objects defined inside the level template, but also for characters that entered the level template instance or items that were dropped there.
Further InformationCharacters that get teleported as a result of this activation and that were in combat, will leave this combat. They may immediately enter it again in case the alignments of nearby characters cause combat to start though. Items will remain in the combats they were involved in, if any. If you wish combats to remain active, use call ActivatePersistentLevelTemplateWithCombat instead.

SetVarInteger.description:
Sets the value of the behaviourscript variable %<_Varname> to _VarValue, if the variable exists and is of the INTEGER type.
Further InformationThe variable has to be global. The _Varname does not include the prefix % that is used for global variables in behaviourscript. E.g. a variable that is called %Variable in behaviourscript would simply be Variable for the purposes of this call.
SetVarInteger.examples:
IF
DB_CurrentLevel(_Region)
AND
GetRegion(_HarperStash, _Region)
THEN
SetVarInteger(_HarperStash, "IsOneShot", 1);
SetVarInteger.seeAlso:
SetVarObject
ClearVarObject
SetVarFloat
SetVarString
SetVarFixedString
SetVarFloat3

SetVarObject.description:
Sets the value of the behaviourscript variable %<_Varname> to _Object, if the variable exists and is of the right type.
Further InformationThe variable has to be global. The _Varname does not include the prefix % that is used for global variables in behaviourscript. E.g. a variable that is called %Variable in behaviourscript would simply be Variable for the purposes of this call.
SetVarObject.examples:
IF
EntityEvent((ITEM)_Plaque, "StoryReveal")
AND
DB_CentralSquare_Plaques(_Plaque)
AND
DB_CentralSquare_Plaques(_OtherPlaque)
AND
_OtherPlaque != _Plaque
THEN
SetVarObject(_OtherPlaque, "RevealOnlyInTrigger", _CentralSquare_HackForPerception);
SetVarObject.seeAlso:
ClearVarObject
SetVarInteger
SetVarFloat
SetVarString
SetVarFixedString
SetVarFloat3

ActivatePersistentLevelTemplateWithComba.description: The same as call ActivatePersistentLevelTemplate, except that any combat character that gets teleported as a result of this activation will remain in that same combat.

AttachHitProxy.description:
Attach the _ProxyObject to _ParentObject, in the _Attachment slot. If not filled, _Attachment will be "" by default.
Further InformationTo understand how HitProxies work, read [REAC] HitProxies .
_Attachment can be one of the following or an empty string:
Torso
Head
Tail
Front
Back
Left
Right
LeftArm
RightArm
LeftLeg
RightLeg
LeftFrontLeg
RightFrontLeg
LeftBackleg
RightBackLeg
LeftWing
RightWing
AttachHitProxy.seeAlso:
HitProxy
GetHitProxyOwner
IsHitProxy
IsHitProxyOwner
DetachHitProxy
DetachAllHitProxies
IterateHitProxies

AddToParty.description:
Adds the characters controlled by the user with User ID _Source to the party to which the user with User ID _Target belongs.
Further InformationA party is a group of all player-controlled characters from one or more users. By default, all users are in the same party. In Dos2, players were allowed to split parties by right-clicking on a character controlled by another user and selecting "Kick from party". In BG3 this was no longer allowed for players, but was still technically possible from script, even if it was never used.

AddTadpole.description:
Adds _Amount of Tadpoles to the specified _Character.
These are used to acquire Tadpole powers.

AllowNewPlayers.description:
Not used any more. Use SetJoinBlock instead.
Blocks new players from joining the game in Multiplayer.
_IsAllowed - pass in 1 to allow players to join, 0 not

ActOver.description: Pauses the game state and shows the message when you reached the end of the Early Access content.

CharacterMoveToAndTalk.description:
Makes _Character move to _Target.
Once _Character has arrived at its destination, the CharacterMoveToAndTalkRequestDialog event will be thrown with the _Character, _Target, _DialogID, _IsAutomated and _MoveID parameters.
_MovementSpeed can be set to “Walk” or “Run”.
A CharacterMoveToAndTalkFailed event will be thrown if:
The _Character does not exist or is dead
_Target does not exist or is dead/destroyed or is in a different level
It takes longer than _TimeOut seconds seconds to reach _Target

AppearAt.description:
_Character, which can currently be off-stage, appears at the position of _Target, snapped to the nearest valid AI-grid position. _Target can be another character, an item or a trigger. If _PlaySpawn is 1, the _CustomSpawnAnimation is played. Once the character has appeared on stage (and finished playing its spawning animation if requested), _AppearedEvent is raised for _Character.
Further InformationThis does not work if _Character is dead. Use call SetOnStage instead for such characters.

CharacterDisableCrime.description:
This call disables a specific disturbance _Crime for a given _Character.
Further InformationThis call is useful when you want a character who possesses the tag necessary to react to a given disturbance to not react to said disturbance.

AddPassive.description: Gives the _Entity the specific passive based on the inputted _PassiveID.

AddEntryToCustomBook.description:
If a custom book with the identifier _Bookname does not yet exist, this call internally creates a new "custom book" with that identifier.
Next, it adds the text associated with the translated string key _Entryname after the existing text in _Bookname (if any).
Further Information_Bookname is an arbitrary identifier of your choice. It does not have to correspond to any in-game item or translated string.
If you try to add the same translated string key multiple times to the same book, nothing will happen after the first time.

CreateSurface.description:
Creates a circular surface of type _SurfaceType, with a radius of _Radius meters and a lifetime of _Lifetime seconds, around the _Target.
The _Source acts as the cause of the created surface. (Optional to use, refer to the full definitions)
Further InformationThe _Lifetime perimeter can be set to -1.0 to make the surface permanent.
Although similar, CreateSurface will create a surface symmetrically in a circle while CreatePuddle creates a surface in semi-randomized directions.

ClearGodOverride.description:
Used to clear an existing deity override on _Character.
It will not clear a character’s original deity, only an existing overrideParameters:
_Character: Character who’s deity override should be cleared
Technically accepts any GUIDSTRING but only CHARACTER type can have deities

AddFogVolume.description:
Attaches fog volume root template to _Object entity.
Further InformationMake sure to use fog volume root template uuid as _FogVolume.
AddFogVolume.examples:
IF
StatusApplied(_Object, "MOONSHIELD_AURA", _, _)
AND
NOT DB_MoonShieldFogVolume(_Object)
AND
Exists(_Object,1)
AND
NOT IsInInventory((ITEM)_Object, 1)
THEN
DB_MoonShieldFogVolume(_Object);
AddFogVolume(_Object, _MoonlanternFogVolume);

CharacterMoveTo.description:
Causes _Character to start moving to _Target.
Once _Character has arrived at its destination, the event _Event will be raised for _Character.
_MovementSpeed can be set to “Walk” or “Run”.

CharacterStopCrime.description: This call will end the disturbance with name _CrimeType involving the criminal _Character. _Evidence can be provided to further narrow which disturbance should be stopped.

BlockNewCrimeReactions.description:
This call blocks a given _Character from investigating/reacting to any disturbances if _Block is set to 1. Setting _Block to 0 will allow investigations and reactions to happen again.
Further InformationThis call is reference-counted. Reactions will only be re-enabled after they have been unblocked as many times as they have been blocked.
Keep this in mind as other scripts unrelated to yours may block new crime reactions. 
This call is useful when you want to temporarily block crimes on a character.

AppearOutOfSightToPosition.description:
The AppearOutOfSightToPosition call allows a character to appear near the specified coordinates at a position that is out of player sight and from an optionally defined direction.
Parameters:
_Character: The character that will be teleported and appear
_X, _Y, _Z: Coordinates for the desired pivot point from which to look for nearest valid position out of sight
_DirectionFrom: Direction in relation to the specified coordinates in which _Character will try to appear at
_PlaySpawn: Whether _Character should play a spawn animation (value of 1) or not (value of 0)
_CustomSpawnAnimation: Only matters if _PlaySpawn is set to 1. If Null, _Character will play their default spawn animation and if specified they will play the custom specified animation instead
_AppearEvent: Event sent when _Character successfully appears
_PreventTeleportOnFailure: Whether the character should appear at the provided coordinates if a valid out of sight position is not foundIt gets the angle from the specified _X, _Y and _Z coordinates to the _DirectionFrom target and starts a flood pathfinding algorithm to find a valid position that is out of sight from the players in that direction. If a valid position is found, the _Character is teleported to it, otherwise they may or may not be teleported to the specified _X, _Y, _Z coordinates depending on the _PreventTeleportOnFailure value. If the _Character teleports, the _AppearedEvent event is sent to Osiris, otherwise an AppearTeleportFailedEvent is sent. It also supports playing a spawn animation if _PlaySpawn value is not equal to 0, which can also be made custom via the _CustomSpawnAnimation parameter.
After teleporting to a valid position out of sight, _Character will not automatically move towards the specified _X, _Y and _Z coordinates and will remain at the valid out of sight position until they are moved by something else (Osiris, Anubis behaviour, crimes etc).
If _DirectionFrom is Null, code will choose an angle along the Z axis and try to find a valid position in that direction
The AppearTeleportFailedEvent is not currently exposed to Osiris and cannot be checked
AppearOutOfSightToPosition.examples:
IF
EntityEvent((CHARACTER)_Guard,"CRIME_Guards_ReturnHomeDisappeared")
AND
DB_CRIME_Guards_Leaving(_Guard,_HomeX,_HomeY,_HomeZ)
AND
NOT DB_CRIME_Guards_SpawnedGuard(_Guard,_,_)
THEN
AppearOutOfSightToPosition(_Guard,_HomeX,_HomeY,_HomeZ,NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000,0,NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000,"CRIME_Guards_ReturnHomeAppeared");

ActivateTrade.description:
Starts a trade interface for _Player to trade with _Trader.
_CanSell set to 1 means that they will sell, any other value means they won’t.

CharacterEnableAllCrimes.description:
This call enables all disturbances for a given _Character.
Further InformationCharacters react to all disturbances by default. The only reason to use this call is if you’ve previously disabled a disturbance with the use of CharacterDisableCrime or CharacterDisableAllCrimes.

ApplyDamage.description:
Applies _Damage points of _DamageType damage to _Object.
If _Source is not NULL, it will be considered as the source of the damage.
Further InformationSupported damage types:
Bludgeoning
Slashing
Piercing
Acid
Thunder
Fire
Lightning
Cold
Psychic
Poison
Radiant
Necrotic
Force
ApplyDamage.examples:
PROC
PROC_DamagedByBoulder((GUIDSTRING) _Object)
AND
DB_BoulderAttacker(_Attacker)
THEN
ApplyDamage(_Object, 50, "Physical", _Attacker);

AddGoldToMagicPockets.description: Equally distribute _Gold value between everyone sharing magic pockets with _Character. Passing negative _Gold value removes gold, and it tries to divide the removed quantity equally as well if it can.
AddGoldToMagicPockets.examples:
IF
FlagSet(_Event_GetAllCommentatorMoney, _Player, _)
AND
GetGold(_Commentator, _Amount)
AND
IntegerSubtract(0, _Amount, _RemoveGold)
THEN
PROC_AddGoldToMagicPockets((CHARACTER)_Player, _Amount);
AddGold(_Commentator, _RemoveGold);

ClearCustomMaterialResourceOverrides.description:
Deprecated.
Use AddCustomMaterialOverride, RemoveCustomMaterialOverride, and ClearCustomMaterialOverrides instead.

AppearOutOfSightTo.description:
_Character should be off-stage when calling this routine. This call will place _Character on-stage out of sight of _Target at _DirectionFrom degrees (0 = North).
If _PlaySpawn is 1, _Character's spawn animation is played. If _PlaySpawn is 0, _Character is simply placed on-stage. _Event is triggered on _Character once it is on stage.

CharacterDisableAllCrimes.description:
This call disables all disturbances for a given _Character.
Further InformationThis call is useful when you have characters that are disabled in unconventional ways. You don’t have to worry about characters that are sleeping or knocked out, but if you do some custom work where a character is say, restrained, it would make sense to disable all crimes on the character.

AddGold.description: Call that adds an _Amount of gold to _InventoryHolder’s inventory. If negative _Amount, it removes gold.
AddGold.examples:
PROC
PROC_TryStealFromPlayer((CHARACTER)_Thief,(INTEGER)_Id)
AND
DB_DialogPlayers(_Id, _Player, 1)
AND
GetGold((CHARACTER)_Player, _Gold)
AND
_Gold > 35
THEN
AddGold(_Player, -35);
AddGold(_Thief, 35);
PROC_ProcessStolenItem(_Thief);

CharacterMoveToAndTalkFail.description:
This call ensures that the _Character that is in the middle of performing this call CharacterMoveToAndTalk fails to  move and start a dialogue with their _Target. The _MoveId holds the value of the movement that is to be cancelled and the _Reason is the specific reason that the dialogue and movement should fail.
(Examples of _Reason include: “Attacked”, “CharacterDied" and “TargetDied”)
CharacterMoveToAndTalkFail.examples:
IF
AttackedBy(_ConflictedFlind_Boss,(CHARACTER)_Source,_,_,_DamageAmount,_,_)
AND
DB_ConflictedFlind_MovingToPlayer(_Source, _MoveID)
AND
DB_PartyMembers(_Source)
AND
_DamageAmount > 4
THEN
CharacterMoveToAndTalkFail (_ConflictedFlind_Boss,_Source,_MoveID,"Attacked");

IF
Died(_Target)
AND
DB_ConflictedFlind_MovingToPlayer(_Target, _MoveID)
THEN
CharacterMoveToAndTalkFail(_ConflictedFlind_Boss,_Target,_MoveID,"TargetDied");

IF
Died(_ConflictedFlind_Boss)
AND
DB_ConflictedFlind_MovingToPlayer(_Target, _MoveID)
THEN
CharacterMoveToAndTalkFail(_ConflictedFlind_Boss,_Target,_MoveID,"CharacterDied");

AppearAtPosition.description:
_Character, which can currently be off-stage, appears at the position (_X, _Y, _Z), snapped to the nearest valid AI-grid position. If _PlaySpawn is 1, the character-specific spawn animation is played. Once the character has appeared on stage (and finished playing its spawning animation if requested), _Event is raised for _Character.
Further InformationThis does not work if _Character is dead. Use call SetOnStage  instead for such characters.
AppearAtPosition.examples:
IF
DialogEnded((DIALOGRESOURCE)_Helm_DragonAppears, _)
AND
DB_TUT_Helm_Devils((CHARACTER)_Devil)
AND
GetPosition(_Devil, _X, _Y, _Z)
THEN
AppearAtPosition(_Devil, _X, _Y, _Z, 1, (ANIMATION)_CUST_ImpDrop_01, "");

ApplyStatus.description:
Applies the status with stats name _Status to _Object, which can either be a character or an item. The status will last for _Duration seconds. If _Duration is -1.0, it will last until explicitly removed by either script or another status/effect interaction (e.g. a permanent BURNING status can still be removed by making the object WET).
If _Force is 0, then
incapacitating statuses except for those of the DOWNED category and those called KNOCKED_OUT will not be applied if the target is a character and it has active deferred Osiris tasks started from story (like call CharacterMoveTo , call Use , call UseSpell , etc). Additionally, starting one of those tasks after the status has been applied, will remove it again.
invulnerability will block most statusesIf it is 1, the status will be applied even in those cases, and will prevent Osiris tasks from removing them.
_Source specifies the object, either a character or item, that the game should consider as the source of this status effect. This is mainly useful for scripted skill applications, where you want _Object to react as if _Source applied this status. You can pass NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 if there is no specific source.
Further InformationThe overloads in the Shared mod use the defaults _Force = 0, and _Source = NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000.
Even when using _Force == 1, _Object may not actually have _Status after calling this routine, because it may have had a status that nullifies this one. E.g., if a character has the BURNNIG status and you force-apply WET, then this application will simply remove the BURNING status (since that status and WET cancel each other out)
The duration of a single combat round is 6.0 seconds
Statuses are the preferred way to apply (de)buffs to items and characters.
ApplyStatus.seeAlso:
RemoveStatus
RemoveHarmfulStatuses
StatusApplied
StatusAttempt
StatusAttemptFailed
StatusRemoved
HasActiveStatus

AddTrackedSoundEntity.description:
Calling AddTrackedSoundEntity will begin to track _Object as a tracked sound entity with _RtpcName.
Further Information_RtpcName can be an empty string.
AddTrackedSoundEntity.examples:
IF
EnteredTrigger(_Player, _BardSingArea)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
QRY_OnlyOnce("RangerDen_AddToTrackedSoundEntity")
THEN
PROC_AddToTrackedSoundEntity(_Bard,(TRIGGER)_RangerDen_SUB);

PROC
PROC_AddToTrackedSoundEntity((CHARACTER)_Character,(TRIGGER)_Region)
THEN
DB_Audio_TrackedSoundEntity(_Region,_Character);
SetForceUpdate(_Character, 1);
AddTrackedSoundEntity(_Character,"");

ClearFlag.description:
Use this to clear _Flag from _Object.
For global flags, _Object can be set to NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 or simply call ClearFlag((FLAG)_Flag).
ClearFlag.fullDefinitions:
call ClearFlag((FLAG)_Flag) 
call ClearFlag((FLAG)_Flag, (GUIDSTRING)_Object) 
call ClearFlag((FLAG)_Flag, (GUIDSTRING)_Object, (INTEGER)_DialogInstance) 
call ClearFlag((FLAG)_Flag, (GUIDSTRING)_Object, (INTEGER)_DialogInstance, (INTEGER)_SendFlagClearEventIfChanged)
ClearFlag.examples:
IF
DestroyedBy(_DangerousBook_Tome, _, _, _)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
GetFlag((FLAG)_DangerousBook_State_BookOwner, _Player, 1)
THEN
ClearFlag((FLAG)_DangerousBook_State_BookOwner, _Player);
ClearFlag((FLAG)_DangerousBook_State_Bound, NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000);

AddExplorationExperience.description: Gives the player's party experience points based on the set amount.
AddExplorationExperience.fullDefinitions:
AddExplorationExperience((CHARACTER)_Character, (INTEGER)_Gain)
ExampleIF
Died(_Bottomless_SpiderQueen)
AND
QRY_OnlyOnce("FOR_Bottomless_GiveXP")
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
THEN
AddExplorationExperience(_Player, 250);

AddCustomMaterialOverride.description:
Override materials of an object or character. Used for visual changes, such as changing the character's eye from the Volo surgery. This call gets its data from entries in the ScriptMaterialOverridePresets and ScriptMaterialOverrideParameters UUID object editor tables.
Parameters:
_Object - The target Object you want to apply the material Override to.
_Preset - The UUID of the material to be applied (as a string).

AddPreferredAiTargetTag.description:
This call sets a _Tag as a priority for _Character’s AI, heavily boosting the AI target score. Meaning, this character will try, if possible, to target characters or items with this tag, if they are hostile towards them.
Further InformationYou can use it on items, but make sure to add the item with  AiAddInterestingItem as otherwise the item is not valid for targeting. And most likely that call would be enough, as it boosts the AI target score as well.
AddPreferredAiTargetTag.examples:
INIT:
AddPreferredAiTargetTag((CHARACTER)_Cazador, _ASTARION);

KB:
PROC
PROC_VampireAmbush_Spawns_Init()
AND
DB_VampireAmbush_Spawns(_Spawn, _, _)
THEN
AddPreferredAiTargetTag(_Spawn, (TAG)_REALLY_ASTARION);

ClearIndividualRelation.description: Clears individual relation between _Entity and _Faction set by SetIndividualRelation.

AttachSpring.description:
Connects a physics-based spring object to another object.
Attaches the _AttachedBone of _SpringObject, to the _AttachToBone of the _AttachToObject
Further InformationThis is mainly used for chains.

ClearCustomMaterialOverrides.description: Clears all custom material overrides from _Object. (like the one applied by AddCustomMaterialOverride).

AddCustomMaterialResourceOverride.description:
Deprecated.
Use AddCustomMaterialOverride, RemoveCustomMaterialOverride, and ClearCustomMaterialOverrides instead.

CharacterGiveEquipmentSet.description:
Gives a predefined equipment set to a character.
Further InformationThe equipment set is defined in the stats editor.

AddCustomVisualOverride.description:
Forces a character to replace a character visual element (hair, beard etc.) with another one. These elements can are found in UUID table CharacterCreationSharedVisuals.
Further InformationThe call expects plain UUID.

CharacterEnableCrime.description:
This call makes a given _Character able to react to a given disturbance _Crime.
Further InformationCharacters react to all disturbances by default. The only reason to use this call is if you’ve previously disabled a disturbance with the use of CharacterDisableCrime or CharacterDisableAllCrimes.

ClearVarUUID.description: Clears the variable _VarName of _Source if it is a UUID (GUID).

AddIllustrationToCustomBook.description: Calling AddIllustrationToCustomBook adds illustration _Entryname to book _Bookname

BlockFlee.description:
Blocks characters from fleeing.
Parameters:
_Character: the character affected
BlockFlee.examples:
IF
DB_CombatFlee_BlockedInLevel(_Level)
AND
DB_CurrentLevel(_Level)
AND
DB_PartyMembers(_Player)
THEN
BlockFlee(_Player);

AiRemoveInterestingItem.description: If _Item was previously marked as “interesting” for _Character via AiAddInterestingItem, then it can be unmarked via this call.
AiRemoveInterestingItem.examples:
IF
Unlocked(_PrisonMainDoor, _, _)
AND
IsDestroyed(_PrisonMainDoor, 0)
AND
DB_DruidAttack_Druids(_Druid, _, _)
THEN
AiRemoveInterestingItem(_Druid, _PrisonMainDoor);

CharacterStopCrimeWithID.description:
This call will end the disturbance of _CrimeID involving the criminal _Character.
Further InformationOnly used this call if you are absolutely sure you only want to stop the disturbance for a certain criminal.

ClearCanOpenDoorsOverride.description: Resets any overrides done to the _Character defined and makes them use their default state to check if they can open doors.

ClearTradeGeneratedItems.description:
Clears "existing, unsold treasure" from _Character's inventory.
"Existing, unsold treasure" consists of all items generated by the GenerateItems call, or generated by treasure generation on dying, which have not yet been removed from the character's inventory at the time this operation is performed, and which the character did not have equipped either. One exception is unique items: these are never removed by this call.

AssignToUser.description:
Assigns control over the player-controlled character _Character to the user with ID _UserID. The character will also be moved to the new user's party.
Further InformationCharacters can also be assigned to users by the game engine itself when they connect to a game or when someone else disconnects.

CharacterMoveToPosition.description:
Makes the _Character move to _X, _Y, _Z. Once the character arrives there, the _Event will be sent to _Character.
If the _Character is in combat, they will move out of turn and ignoring remaining AP. If the destination is blocked, the call will fallback to a teleport to the destination or the nearest available position.
_MovementSpeed can be set to “Walk” or “Run”.

AddAttitudeTowardsPlayer.description:
Adds _Delta attitude to _Character for _Player
Further InformationAttitude affect item prices when trading with an NPC.
Adding a negative value will lower the characters attitude towards the player

AppearOnTrailOutOfSightTo.description:
Same as AppearOutOfSightTo , but _Character will select a position occupied by _Target over the last few seconds (so that if you then make _Character walk to _Target afterwards, it seems as if _Character was following _Target).
It will select the furthest cached position of _Target at angle _DirectionFrom.

CreateProjectileStrikeAt.description: Creates a spell cast of _SpellID, which must be a ProjectileStrike type spell, at _Target location. Projectilestrike is a kind of spell that creates a projectile that falls from the sky, like a meteorite storm.

CreateExplosion.description:
Shoots the projectile _SkillID at _Target as if it was cast by a caster of level _CasterLevel. The Log will report it as being cast by _Caster.
Further InformationIf _Target is an item, the projectile impact is at half the height of the item. If the item is in a container or character's inventory, the impact will be at the location and half the height of that container/character instead.
If _Target is a trigger, the projectile impact is at the Position of the trigger.
If _SkillID is not a projectile skill, this call will not do anything.
If _CasterLevel is <= 0, it will be set to the level of the trigger/item. If those are also <= 0, then the level trigger they are in will be used, if any (in case of an item in a container/inventory, the level trigger around that container/character will be used). If there was no level trigger or the result is still <= 0, then the level defined in the stats for the skill will be used. If that too does not exist or is <= 0, then this call will not do anything.

CreateSurfaceAtPosition.description:
Creates a circular surface of type _SurfaceType, with a radius of _Radius meters and a lifetime of _Lifetime seconds, centered at a point with the coordinates of _X, _Y, and _Z.
The _Source acts as the cause of the created surface. (Optional to use, refer to the full definitions)
Further InformationThe _Lifetime perimeter can be set to -1.0 to make the surface permanent.
Although similar, CreateSurfaceAtPosition will create a surface symmetrically in a circle while CreatePuddle creates a surface in semi-randomized directions.
Similar to CreateSurface but takes in coordinates rather than an object to determine the point of creation of the surface.
The _Y parameter isn't actually used because the surface is created on the AI grid anyway.
CreateSurfaceAtPosition.examples:
IF
TimerFinished("BookDestroyed_SummonShades_Timer")
AND
DB_BookDestoyedPos(_BookX, _BookY, _BookZ)
THEN
CreateSurfaceAtPosition(_BookX, _BookY, _BookZ, "SurfaceDarknessCloud", 3.0, 3.0, NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000);
PROC_SummonShades(3, _BookX, _BookY, _BookZ);

DeclutterArea.description:
Removes the items and characters from the area entity referenced, but leave the terrain.
Parameters:
_Entity - The area itself to “declutter”, usually a trigger
_MoveAsideCharacters - Integer - 0 means to remove the characters, 1 means to leave them there
_SendItemsToCamp - Integer - 0 means to not move items to camp, 1 means to move themFurther InformationSpecifically for use to declutter an area defined by something such as a trigger. If you instead wish to declutter a level template, use DeclutterLevelTemplate.
Items sent to camp will go to your camp chest.

DeclutterLevelTemplate.description:
Removes the items and characters from the area entity referenced, but leave the terrain.
Parameters:
_LevelTemplate - The area itself to “declutter”
_MoveAsideCharacters - Integer - 0 means to remove the characters, 1 means to leave them there
_SendItemsToCamp - Integer - 0 means to not move items to camp, 1 means to move themFurther InformationSpecifically for a Level Template, such as a Camp corner or a platform. If you need to look for an area within a trigger, instead use DeclutterArea.
Items sent to camp will go to your camp chest.
DeclutterLevelTemplate.examples:
IF
LevelTemplateLoaded(_LevelTemplate)
AND
DB_Camp_LevelTemplateToDeclutter(_LevelTemplate)
THEN
DeclutterLevelTemplate(_LevelTemplate, 1, 1);
NOT DB_Camp_LevelTemplateToDeclutter(_LevelTemplate);

ConstellationTriggerInputSocket.description:
Triggers the input socket of an object.
Params:
_ConstellationObject: The object the input socket of which to trigger
_InputSocketName: Name of the socket to be triggered
_Sender: The socket event will be sent as if originating from this object.
_ObjectParam: The param to be sent with the event. Typically null, except for specific configs.

CreateProjectileStrikeAtPosition.description: Creates a spell cast of _SpellID, which must be a ProjectileStrike type spell, at provided coordinates. Projectilestrike is a kind of spell that creates a projetile that falls from the sky, like a meteorite storm.

DebugBreak.description:
Returns an assert with the _Message as the contents of it that will pause the game.
Parameters:
_Message: string to show in the body of the assert.Further InformationKeep in mind that this will only happen in the editor as this is only used for debugging. Other tools useful for debugging are DebugText and DebugLog.
DebugBreak.examples:
PROC
PROC_GetFlagInfo((FLAG)_Flag)
AND
GetFlagName(_Flag, _FlagName)
AND
GetFlagDescription(_Flag, _FlagDescription)
AND
Concatenate(_FlagName, ": ", _Substring)
AND
Concatenate(_Substring, _FlagDescription, _Message)
THEN
DebugBreak(_Message);

CrimeSuspend.description:
This call can be used to suspend a disturbance of _CrimeID. While suspended, anything related to the crime is put on hold.
Further InformationYou would only consider suspending a crime if you want to put the resolution on hold and delay it until later. A classic example of this is calling guards for help. A character reacts to a disturbance, calls a guard (which is a separate disturbance) and the initial disturbance is suspended while the guard moves to the caller.
When the guard arrives, the original disturbance is resumed. Since the guard has higher reaction priority than the caller, the guard can now confront the criminal for the disturbance.

ClearTimelineScreenFade.description:
A call used to clear an existing Timeline specific Screen Fade. Mostly used to catch cases where the game would still understand a Timeline fade to exists, but the dialog is over.
Further InformationThe _UserID is an integer here, which is looking for the player number rather than the character themselves. (E.g. Player assigned to the “1” slot, rather than a GUID).
The _Seconds here are in seconds rather than milliseconds, unlike most timers.

CrimeResumeWithPosition.description: This call is used to resume a suspended crime of _CrimeID. It needs to be provided with a new position _X, _Y, _Z and the investigation procedure can be restarted with a _ForceInvestigation parameter of value 1.
CrimeResumeWithPosition.examples:
PROC
PROC_CRIME_Guards_ResumeOriginalCrime((INTEGER)_CrimeID,(CHARACTER)_Caller,(CHARACTER)_Guard,(CHARACTER)_Criminal)
AND
IsInCombat(_Guard,0)
AND
GetPosition(_Guard,_GuardX,_GuardY,_GuardZ)
AND
DB_CRIME_ShapeshiftTracker_Known("CRIME_CallGuards",_Guard,_Criminal,_,_Known)
AND
DB_Negate(_Known,_ForceInvestigation)
THEN
CrimeIgnoreCrime(_CrimeID,_Caller);
CrimeResumeWithPosition(_CrimeID,_GuardX,_GuardY,_GuardZ,_ForceInvestigation);
DB_CRIME_Guards_ResumedCrime(_CrimeID,_Guard);

CrimeAreaSetTensionModifier.description:
Override the tension level of a crime area trigger. Valid values for _Modifier are:
0: None
1: Low
2: Medium
3: HighThe current tension level is used to select reactions for NPCs based on the Tension field of the reaction (Any, Low, or High). It is a combination of the crime region and crime area tension levels. Every time an NPC reacts to a disturbance that does not have the IgnoreForRegionTensionIncrease property, the tension in the current crime region rises (crime regions triggers are a separate kind of triggers that you can place).
Hence, crime area triggers can be used to force a permanent higher tension level for certain areas. In general, you'll want a "base" crime area trigger with Low tension covering your entire gameplay area, as with None it will take a long time before NPCs switch to their high tension reactions. You can then add individual crime regions to further fine-tune the NPC reactions.
Further InformationIn case of overlapping crime areas, the tension of the area with the highest tension is used.
In case the reacting NPC is a in a crime region, but not in any crime area, only the current tension level of the crime region will be used to select the reaction. I.e., the effect is the same as if they would be in a crime area with tension None.
CrimeAreaSetTensionModifier.examples:
IF
DB_CurrentLevel("CTY_Main_A")
AND
DB_GlobalFlag(_Knows_TargetList)
AND
DB_VictimsAndCrimeTriggers(_, _Trigger)
THEN
CrimeAreaSetTensionModifier((TRIGGER)_Trigger, 3);

DebugText.description:
On call the supplied text appears for a few seconds above the specified entity.
Further InformationUsed for debugging purposes.

CopyCharacterEquipment.description: Copies the equipment from one character and applies it to another.
CopyCharacterEquipment.examples:
PROC
PROC_MirrorPlayer((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
THEN
CopyCharacterEquipment(_MirrorCharacter, _Player);

DEV EnableAnubis.description:
Replaces the character’s Anubis config with the passed one and initializes the tree.
Parameters:
_Character - character whose config to replace
_Config - config to attachFurther InformationThe config is loaded with default parameters, so the parameter won’t work with configs that have required parameters.
DEV EnableAnubis.examples:
IF
DB_ORI_Gale_DisappearedToMystra(1)
AND
DB_ActiveLevel("CTY_Main_A")
THEN
NOT DB_ORI_Gale_DisappearedToMystra(1);
DEV_EnableAnubis(_Player_Gale, "ORI_Gale_Act3");

CrimeAreaResetTensionModifier.description:
Undoes any effects from calls to CrimeAreaSetTensionModifier.
Parameters:
_Trigger: the crime area trigger for which to reset the tension modifier.Further InformationSee CrimeAreaSetTensionModifier.
CrimeAreaResetTensionModifier.examples:
IF
DB_Dead(_Victim)
AND
DB_VictimsAndCrimeTriggers(_Victim, _Trigger)
THEN
NOT DB_VictimsAndCrimeTriggers(_Victim, _Trigger);
CrimeAreaResetTensionModifier((TRIGGER)_Trigger);

CreateExplosionAtPosition.description:
Shoots the projectile _SkillID at position (_X, _Y, _Z) as if it was cast by a caster of level _CasterLevel. The Log will report it as being cast by _Caster.
Further InformationIf _SkillID is not a projectile skill, this call will not do anything.
If _CasterLevel is <= 0, it will be set to the level of the trigger/item. If those are also <= 0, then the level trigger they are in will be used, if any (in case of an item in a container/inventory, the level trigger around that container/character will be used). If there was no level trigger or the result is still <= 0, then the level defined in the stats for the skill will be used. If that too does not exist or is <= 0, then this call will not do anything.
It is recommended to use CreateExplosion where possible, using a trigger or other entity as a target. CreateExplosionAtPosition is best used if you need to create an offset from a target location, or can’t target an entity for some reason.
CreateExplosionAtPosition.examples:
IF
TimerFinished(_Str)
AND
DB_Combat_ExplodeOnDeath_ExplodeAfterTimer(_Str, _Char, _ExplodeSpell, _ExplodeHeightOffset)
AND
GetPosition(_Char,_X,_Y,_Z)
AND
RealSum(_Y,_ExplodeHeightOffset,_NewY)
THEN
NOT DB_Combat_ExplodeOnDeath_ExplodeAfterTimer(_Str, _Char, _ExplodeSpell, _ExplodeHeightOffset);
CreateExplosionAtPosition(_X,_NewY,_Z,_ExplodeSpell,1,_Char);

CreatePuddle.description:
Creates a permanent surface of type _SurfaceType, which will grow, in a random fashion, to a random final size between _CellAmountMin and _CellAmountMax (in AI grid tiles), by adding a random amount of tiles between _GrowAmountMin and _GrowAmountMax every _GrowTime seconds around the _Target.
Further InformationAlthough similar, CreateSurface will create a surface symmetrically in a circle while CreatePuddle creates a surface in semi-randomized directions.

Debug BlockTag.description:
Blocks or unblocks with _Block the ability to set a specific _Tag on an _Target entity.
Parameters:
_Target: entity for which we want to block a specific tag.
_Tag: which tag we want to block.
_Block: enable or disable blocking the tag.
Debug BlockTag.examples:
IF
FlagSet(_Flag,_Entity,_)
AND
DB_Debug_BlockTagFlag(_Tag,_Flag)
THEN
Debug_BlockTag(_Entity,_Tag,1);

DebugDialogSkillCheck.description:
Sets all skill checks to succeed or fail.
1 — makes every skill check roll a success
-1 — makes every skill check roll a failure
0 — returns skill check rolls to normalFurther InformationAs evident from the name, it’s a debug call: you shouldn’t use it in the scripts
For testing purposes you can use shortcuts:
Shift + Q — guarantees a success
Shift + W — guarantees a failure
Shift + E — returns skill check rolls to normal

CrimeConfrontationDone.description:
This call is used to indicate that the _Character participating in the disturbance _CrimeID has completed its reaction.
Further InformationMost generic disturbance reactions provided in our existing system will call this without requiring you to do so.
If you create custom Osiris reactions, it is very important to include this call at the end of your character’s Osiris reaction, otherwise the reaction will never end.

DialogRequestStop.description: Terminates all dialogs, both automated and interactive ones, in which _Speaker is involved.
DialogRequestStop.examples:
IF
ScreenFadeDone(_Character, "HAG_UglyTransition")
AND
DB_DialogName((DIALOGRESOURCE)_HAG_ForestIllusion_Illusioncheck_Prep, _ID)
AND
DB_DialogSpeakers(_ID, _Player, _)
THEN
DialogRequestStop(_Player);

DetachAllHitProxies.seeAlso:
HitProxy
GetHitProxyOwner
IsHitProxy
IsHitProxyOwner
AttachHitProxy
DetachHitProxy
IterateHitProxies

CompleteTutorialForUser.description:
Marks this user’s profile as having finished the tutorial level.
Further InformationThis is a manual set and can be done at any moment depending on design intent.
This record is used on subsequent new games. If this user hasn’t finished the tutorial we reset all tutorial messages. If they have, they get asked if they want them reset.
CompleteTutorialForUser.examples:
PROC
PROC_TUT_Helm_EndTutorial()
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
GetReservedUserID(_Player,_User)
THEN
CompleteTutorialForUser(_User);

ClearVarObject.description:
Clears the value of the behaviourscript variable %<_Varname>, if the variable exists and is of the right type (i.e. TRIGGER, CHARACTER or ITEM).
Further InformationThis is not equivalent to setting _VarName to NULL with SetVarObject. If a variable is set to NULL rather than cleared, IsEqual(%Variable, null) will evaluate to false for it.
The variable has to be global. The _Varname does not include the prefix % that is used for global variables in behaviourscript. E.g. a variable that is called %Variable in behaviourscript would simply be Variable for the purposes of this call.
ClearVarObject.seeAlso:
SetVarObject
SetVarInteger
SetVarFloat
SetVarString
SetVarFixedString
SetVarFloat3

DialogSetTeleportPartyOnEnded.description:
Once the dialog instance _InstanceID ends, teleport all parties to _Trigger.
Further InformationThe advantage of using this call over waiting for DialogEnded and then calling TeleportPartiesWithMovie is that the cinematic will directly transition to the loading screen, instead of first briefly showing the game world again.

CrimeMergeEvidenceFrom.description:
Add all tracked evidence from one disturbance/crime to another one.
Parameters:
_TargetCrimeID: the disturbance to which to add the evidence
_SourceCrimeID: the disturbance from which to copy the evidence (the evidence does not get removed from this source)Further InformationSee CrimeTransferEvidenceTo for how evidence can be added to a disturbance.
CrimeMergeEvidenceFrom.examples:
PROC
PROC_CharacterRegisterCrime_Success((CHARACTER)_Char,(STRING)_CrimeType,(INTEGER)_StoryActionID,(GUIDSTRING)_Evidence,(CHARACTER)_Victim,(INTEGER)_CrimeID)
AND
_CrimeType == "MySpecialUniqueCrime"
AND
DB_MySpecialUniqueCrimeID(_OriginalCrimeID)
THEN
CrimeMergeEvidenceFrom(_CrimeID,_OldCrimeID);
CharacterStopCrimeWithID(_Criminal,_CrimeID);

PROC
PROC_CharacterRegisterCrime_Success((CHARACTER)_Char,(STRING)_CrimeType,(INTEGER)_StoryActionID,(GUIDSTRING)_Evidence,(CHARACTER)_Victim,(INTEGER)_CrimeID)
AND
_CrimeType == "MySpecialUniqueCrime"
AND
NOT DB_MySpecialUniqueCrimeID(_CrimeID)
THEN
DB_MySpecialUniqueCrimeID(_CrimeID);

DeleteLevelCache.description: Deletes all the cache for _LevelName level.
DeleteLevelCache.examples:
PROC
PROC_LevelLoadedOnce(_NewLevel)
AND
DB_Level_InaccessibleAfterLoading(_OldLevel,_NewLevel)
AND
NOT QRY_LevelCache_BlockDelete(_OldLevel)
THEN
DeleteLevelCache(_OldLevel);

DisappearOutOfSightTo.description:
The DisappearOutOfSightTo call allows a character to disappear from sight, moving toward a specified target object, sending an event upon disappearing.
Parameters:
_Character: The character to disappear
_TargetObject: An object in the level towards which the character should try to disappear towards. This parameter can be NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000.
_MovementSpeed: The speed at which characters disappear. Valid values are: "Stroll", "Walk", "Run", "Sprint"
_IncreaseSpeed: Used to make a character’s speed increase over time. 0 for no, 1 for yes
_Event: Story event sent on _Character upon disappearing
(optional) _MovementID: An integer that tracks a specific instance of using this call. Set to a positive integer number or skip the parameter if not needed. It must be larger than -1 or it will throw an assert. If not set, it will default to -1 in code.It calculates an angle between the selected _Character and _TargetObject when called, then tries to find a valid position that is out of sight for players in the given angle direction using flood pathfinding methods. If a valid position is found, _Character is moved towards and disappears when it reaches it, sending the _Event.
If the specified _TargetObject is not valid, it defaults to an angle along the Z axis instead
The destination position is taken from _TargetObject’s position at the time of using the call and as such will not update if _TargetObject’s position changes afterwards
If a valid position is not found by the flood algorithm, the _Character’s current position is given as a target position, resulting in the character disappearing instantly
If an invalid _MovementSpeed is entered, it defaults to "Walk"
If _MovementID is -1 and the disappear task gets cancelled, the DisappearOutOfSightCancelled event will not be thrownIf a Disappear task gets cancelled before throwing a completed event, and _MovementID is >= 0, it will throw a DisappearOutOfSightCancelled event.

Equip.description: Makes the _Character equip _Item, if the _Character meets the prerequisites for equipping it.
Equip.fullDefinitions:
call Equip((CHARACTER)_Character, (ITEM)_Item)
call Equip((CHARACTER)_Character, (ITEM)_Item, (INTEGER)_AddToMainInventoryOnFail)
call Equip((CHARACTER)_Character, (ITEM)_Item, (INTEGER)_AddToMainInventoryOnFail, (INTEGER)_ShowNotification)
call Equip((CHARACTER)_Character, (ITEM)_Item, (INTEGER)_AddToMainInventoryOnFail, (INTEGER)_ShowNotification, (INTEGER)_ClearOriginalOwner)
Equip.seeAlso:
Unequip
Equipped
IsEquipable
Unequipped
EquipFailed
UnequipFailed
TemplateEquipped
TemplateUnequipped

AddBackgroundGoal.description: Completes a given background goal for a certain player character, provided they have the correct background associated with the background goal, as defined in the BackgroundGoals and Backgrounds UUID tables. The Category ID is either “Act1“/”Act2”/”Act3” depending on in which level this goal was completed.
AddBackgroundGoal.examples:
PROC
PROC_GLO_Backgrounds_GivePoint((CHARACTER)_Player, (GUIDSTRING)_UuidID)
AND
DB_ActiveLevel(_ActiveLevel)
AND
DB_GLO_Backgrounds_Category(_ActiveLevel, _Category)
THEN
AddBackgroundGoal(_Player, _UuidID, _Category);

DebugLog.description:
When called the supplied string can be found in the editor’s Message Log.
Further InformationUsed for debugging purposes. Consider using DebugText if you’re debugging a specific entity.
Tip: You can filter log messages by type (this message is of the type Script) and also by string contained within the message.

CrimeResetInterrogationForCriminals.description:
Code records that a criminal has already been interrogated during the investigation of a particular disturbance/crime, to prevent them from getting accosted more than once. This call can be used to reset the “has been interrogated” state.
Parameters:
_CrimeID: the disturbance/crime’s ID
_Criminal1 .. _Criminal4: the criminals for which to reset the “has been interrogated” state.

DismissAvatar.description:
If _Bool = 1 the call dismisses the _AvatarEntity from the party and puts it away for later retrieval.
If _Bool = 0, un-dismisses the avatar and returns it back into the game.Further InformationAvatars are player characters that went through character creation. Their character portrait has a little crown symbol to indicate this. In single player you start the game with only one avatar but in multiplayer it is possible to have more than one avatar in the party.
When a player leaves the game their avatars are automatically re-assigned to the host of the game. However, this call allows to dismiss these avatars and put them away, freeing a spot in the party.
In BG3 the Wardrobe was used as a container for all dismissed avatars. Removing a character from such container automatically puts it back into the game by Code, without a need of calling DismissAvatar with _Bool = 0. Whenever a new player joins the game and there’s an avatar stored in the container, they automatically get one assigned from it.
Note that the SetBlockDismiss call doesn’t work on avatars and will not affect DismissAvatar.
DismissAvatar.examples:
PROC
PROC_GLO_PartyMembers_DismissAvatar((CHARACTER)_Member)
AND
DB_Dead(_Member)
AND
GetPosition(_Member,_X,_Y,_Z)
THEN
PlayEffectAtPosition((EFFECTRESOURCE)_VFX_Item_CharacterToInventory,_X,_Y,_Z);
DismissAvatar(_Member,1);

CombatKillFor.description: Kills all hostile entities that are in combat with _Object.

ClearTag.description:
Clear the tag _Tag from _Source. Only tags that were set from Osiris can be cleared this way.
Further InformationTags that were set via the editor sidebar on a root template or on an object cannot be cleared (except by making use of a polymorph status: in that case, the root/sidebar tags will be replaced with those of the target root template; regular (Character)Transform calls do not affect them).
Tags set from Anubis or dialogs can only be cleared from those same systems.

DestroyNarrativeCombat.description:
Destroys the _CombatGuid for a narrative combat.
Further InformationFor normal combat, the engine will typically handle the creation and destruction of a combat GUID. For narrative combat, it’s handled by script.
As narrative combat GUIDs can exist without any attached combat entities, it’s important to track the GUID and destroy it when it’s no longer needed.
When a narrative combat is destroyed, it will end the combat for all the participating entities.
Once destroyed, generics will resume for them, so if the entities are hostile to each other, a normal combat will start after the narrative combat ends.
DestroyNarrativeCombat.examples:
PROC
PROC_EXA_Example_EndNarrativeCombat()
AND
DB_EXA_Example_NarrativeCombatID(_ID)
THEN
DestroyNarrativeCombat(_ID);
NOT DB_EXA_Example_NarrativeCombatID(_ID);

DetachFromPartyGroup.description:
Unchain player _Character, so that they will stop following other player characters.
Further InformationWill fail if _Character is not in a party.
Will have no effect in _Character is already unchained.
DetachFromPartyGroup.examples:
PROC
PROC_DEN_HarpyMeal_VictimWalkToHarpy()
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
HasActiveStatus(_Player, "LURING_SONG", 1)
AND
GetFlag(_HarpyMeal_Event_PlayerSuccumbedCharm, _Player, 1)
THEN
DetachFromPartyGroup(_Player); //player will be moved automatically with the StatusOverride behavior

CrimeIgnoreCrime.description:
This call is used to make _Character ignore the disturbance with _CrimeID.
Further InformationSometimes, tag reactions and faction relations are not enough to filter who reacts to a disturbance. This call is often used to make a group of NPCs ignore crimes committed towards another group of NPCs regardless of the relation between their factions. While guards typically care if you assault an NPC they are neutral towards, they might not care if you’re both in jail.
CrimeIgnoreCrime.examples:
PROC
PROC_CharacterRegisterCrime_Success(_Char, _CrimeType, _StoryActionID, _Evidence, _Victim, _CrimeID)
AND
DB_PrisonerVictim(_Victim)
AND
DB_UncaringGuards(_Guard)
THEN
CrimeIgnoreCrime(_CrimeID, _Guard);

CrimeDropEvidenceFromCriminals.description:
Drop all evidence for a particular crime/disturbance that’s in the inventory of the listed criminals, and set its ownership back to the original owner (before the player picked it up).
Parameters:
_CrimeID: the disturbance/crime ID
_FallbackOwner: if we can’t determine the original owner of the evidence, or it didn’t have one, give it this owner instead after dropping it.
_Criminal1 .. _Criminal4: the criminals from which to drop the evidence. Can be NULLFurther InformationSee CrimeTransferEvidenceTo for how evidence can be added to a disturbance.
CrimeDropEvidenceFromCriminals.examples:
IF
FlagSet(PlayerAgreedToDropEvidence_UUID, _Player, _ID)
AND
GetHandlingCrimeID((CHARACTER)_Interrogator,_CrimeID)
DB_DialogNPCs(_ID, _ConfrontingNPC, 1)
THEN
CrimeDropEvidenceFromCriminals(_CrimeID, _FallbackOwner, _Criminal1, _Criminal2, _Criminal3, _Criminal4);

DetachSpring.description: Detaches the _AttachedBone of the _SpringObject from the object it is attached to.

CrimeInterrogationDone.description:
This call is used to end the interrogation step of the reaction that the _Interrogator was attempting to perform on the _Criminals of crime _CrimeID.
Further InformationThis call is only used in our pipeline to unblock characters that fail to interrogate so that their reaction can move on.

CrimeIgnoreAllCrimesForCriminal.description:
Prevent an NPC from reacting to a particular criminal for all currently ongoing disturbances/crimes.
Parameters:
_Criminal: the criminal to ignore
_NPC: the NPC that should ignore the criminal
_IgnoreDuration: the number of seconds during which the  NPC should ignore the crimes for criminal. If smaller than 0, they will ignore them forever.Further InformationThis call does not affect disturbances/crimes that get registered afterwards.
The main purpose is for bribed NPCs to temporarily ignore all other crimes a player may have committed before, to prevent them from immediately confronting the player again after getting bribed.

DialogSetTeleportPartyToLevelOnEnded.description:
Once the dialog instance _InstanceID ends, teleport all parties to _Level.
Further InformationCheck call TeleportPartiesToLevelWithMovie for more information.

CrimeTransferEvidenceTo.description:
Transfers all evidence of a disturbance/crime held by a particular criminal to another character or container.
Parameters:
_CrimeID: identifier of the disturbance/crime
_Criminal: only the evidence in this _Criminal’s inventory will be transferred
_IncludingNearbyOnGround: also transfer evidence within TransferredEvidenceOnGroundRadius meters from _Criminal. This value is defined in stats, and the default is 8 meters.
_Target: the character/container in which the evidence should be placed.Further InformationEvidence can be added to a disturbance when it gets registered, manually afterwards (variant specifically for stolen items), through manual evidence merging, or when entire disturbances got merged.
CrimeTransferEvidenceTo.examples:
IF
FlagSet((FLAG)_CRIME_ReturnGoodsToOwner, (CHARACTER)_Interrogator, _ID)
AND
GetHandlingCrimeID(_Interrogator,_CrimeID)
AND
DB_DialogPlayers(_ID,_Criminal,1)
AND
CrimeHasProperty(_CrimeID,(DISTURBANCEPROPERTY)_TransferEvidenceOnGround,_TransferEvidenceOnGround)
THEN
ClearFlag((FLAG)_CRIME_ReturnGoodsToOwner, _Interrogator, _ID);
CrimeTransferEvidenceTo(_CrimeID,(CHARACTER)_Criminal,_TransferEvidenceOnGround,_Interrogator);
CrimeTransferEvidenceTo.seeAlso:
CharacterRegisterCrime
CrimeAddEvidence
CrimeAddItemEvidence
CrimeGetEvidence
CrimeGetNumberOfEvidence
CrimeMergeEvidenceFrom

EnableCampWaypoint.description:
Use this call to toggle enable camp waypoint for all players. You toggle it on with _Enabled = 1 and toggle it off with _Enabled = 0.
Further InformationThere’s no need to register the the camp trigger in any DB, using this call is enough. The reason is that it is hardcoded to use the active camp trigger already.

Drop.description:
Makes the character who is the top inventory owner of the item start an Osiris task to drop that item at their current location.
Further InformationTo make the character drop the item at a specific location, see: DropTo.
Will fire event DroppedBy.
Drop.examples:
IF
AddedTo(_DropItem,_Owner,_)
AND
DB_AlwaysDrop((ITEM)_DropItem)
AND
IsCharacter(_Owner,1)
THEN
Drop(_DropItem);

EnableSendToCamp.description:
_Enables camp for the party.
Further Information_Enabled
1: Camp travel is enabled for party.
0: Camp travel is disabled for party.

EnableTutorialEvent.description:
Enables a tutorial event disabled by call DisableTutorialEvent.
Further InformationMore about tutorial events here event TutorialEvent.

EnterCombat.description:
Attempts to force two entities to enter combat with each other.
Further InformationIt is possible for this call to fail if certain generic combat rules are not met.
If there are no hostile entities to enter combat with.
If the entities are too far from each other.
If they or their allies do not have proper sight lines to the other entity.
Some of this can be circumvented with combat groups, as any entity that is part of a combat group will bring all other entities within the same group into combat with them.
You can include opposing factions into the same combat group.
This can also be avoided with Narrative Combat calls, which must be used with great caution as it removes ALL combat generics, such as removing characters on death or ending combat when there are no more hostile entities.It is good to keep certain gameplay systems in mind such as stealth if you want to maintain believability.
EnterCombat.examples:
IF
EnteredTrigger(_Player, _EXA_TresspassTrigger)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
DB_EXA_Example_Guards(_Guard)
THEN
EnterCombat(_Guard, _Player);

FlushOsirisQueue.description:
If you start an Osiris action for a character (not for items!) while another action is still busy, or start multiple actions at the same time, then these actions are all queued and will execute one after the other.
This call will complete the currently executing and all queued actions for _Character instantly while preserving their side-effects. This means that move operations are turned into teleports, while animations are skipped (but their completion events will still be triggered, in order).
Further InformationThis will only flush actions queued from Osiris. It has no effect on actions started from behaviourScript or Anubis.
If you wish to discard the queued actions instead of make their effects happen immediately, use PurgeOsirisQueue instead.
FlushOsirisQueue.examples:
IF
DialogEnded(_Drider_HarpersAndPlayersAmbush, _)
AND
DB_SCL_Drider_HarpersGroup(_Harper, _, _)
AND
GetFlag(_Drider_State_HarperArrivedToAmbush, _Harper, 1)
THEN
FlushOsirisQueue(_Harper);

GameEndWithMovie.description:
End the game, play a (Bink) movie, and then return to the main menu.
Further InformationSee GameEnd.

ExecuteDeal.description:
This API must only be called in response to HappyWithDeal, with the _Character parameter from that event as _Character parameter here. If _Deal is 1, the proposed trade goes through, otherwise it is rejected.
_AttitudeDiff is used for showing how the trade offer affected the NPC's attitude in the interface. However, it does not update the attitude of the NPC (use AddAttitudeTowardsPlayer for that).

DisableTutorialEvent.description:
Disables a tutorial event from being triggered again for a user who currently controls supplied CHARACTER.
Can be reverted by EnableTutorialEvent.
Especially crucial for event that fire often (camera events are the best example).
Further InformationMore info on tutorial events can be found here TutorialEvent.

LeaveParty.description: All characters controlled by the user with User ID _UserID leave their current party and are immediately all added to a newly created party.

CrimeStopIgnoringCrime.description:
This call is used to make _Character stop ignoring the disturbance with _CrimeID.
Further InformationThe only reason to use this call is if you have previously set an ignore using CrimeIgnoreCrime. Since the ignore is done on a _CrimeID basis, this means that this is useful only in continuous disturbances. Making a character stop ignoring a disturbance that has ended will serve no purpose.
CrimeStopIgnoringCrime.examples:
IF
EntityEvent(_Character, "StopIgnoringCrimes")
AND
DB_TemporarilyIgnoredCrime(_CrimeID)
THEN
CrimeStopIgnoringCrime(_CrimeID, _Character);

Freeze.description:
Makes the _Character Story-frozen. Story-frozen characters cannot move or perform in-game actions and skip turns in combat. They also do not play animations.
Further InformationMake sure to unfreeze using call Unfreeze  so as not to block the player for longer than intended.
This is unrelated to the gameplay FROZEN status.

EndTurn.examples:
IF
TurnStarted(_Char)
AND
DB_CharacterSkipTurn(_Char)
THEN
EndTurn(_Char);

IterateInventory.description:
Iterates over all  _InventoryHolder’s inventory. For each item in it, it will send an Entity Event with the string _Event as an identifier. When all of the items have been iterated over, it will send the _CompletionEvent as an Entity Event.
Further InformationYou can pass "" as the _CompletionEvent if you don’t need to react to the iteration completing.

LockWaypoint.description:
Locks the waypoint that was previously unlocked (UnlockWaypoint) using _WaypointName for a specified _Character, so that it no longer appears in their waypoint list.
Further InformationIf no waypoint has been registered or unlocked for _Character with the name _WaypointName yet, this call will not do anything.
Locking a waypoint for a character only locks it for that specific character. This means you have to lock it for each player individually.

GameEnd.description:
End the game and return to the main menu. Used after showing the credits when finishing the game.
Further InformationWhen playing in honour mode, this also finalizes the save file as if you reached game over (ShowGameOverMenu).

EscortAddCharacter.description:
Adds a character or a target to an escort group.
Parameters:
_Target — The character to add to the group
_EscortGroup — The name of the groupFurther InformationIf the character is currently a member of an escort group, they will be removed from their current group and added to the specified _EscortGroup.
EscortAddCharacter.examples:
PROC
PROC_Drider_InitEscortParty()
THEN
EscortAddCharacter(_Drider, "DriderEscort");
EscortSetLeader(_Drider, "DriderEscort");

IF
DB_Drider_PartOfEscort(_Char)
AND
NOT DB_Defeated(_Char)
THEN
EscortAddCharacter(_Char, "DriderEscort");

IterateCharacters.description:
Causes entity event _Event to be thrown for every character in the current level. Once the last such character has been iterated, an additional entity event _CompletionEvent will be thrown for object NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, so that you can perform any necessary clean-up or finalize processing.
Further InformationAs mentioned in the description, this will only iterate over characters.

Die.description:
Kills _Target (item or character) as if it died from _DeathType, coming from _Source. Depending on whether _GenerateTreasure is 1 or 0, treasure will be generated as if a player killed _Target or not. If _Immediate is 1, death animations will be skipped, otherwise they will be played as usual.
_ImpactForce is only used if _Target is an item, and is used for items that have a physically simulated destruction. In this case, if _Source is not NULL, the source is also used to determine the direction from which the impact force gets applied.
Depending on the death type, the corpse and its pose can be different. Possible death types are:
DEATHTYPE.None: no specific death type/default.
DEATHTYPE.Acid: Died from acid/poison damage.
DEATHTYPE.Chasm: Died from falling into a chasm.
DEATHTYPE.DoT: Damage-over-Time.
DEATHTYPE.Electrocution: Died from electricity damage.
DEATHTYPE.Explode: Died from an explosion.
DEATHTYPE.Falling: Died from falling.
DEATHTYPE.Incinerate: Burnt to death.
DEATHTYPE.KnockedDown: A special death state for characters who died while knocked-down (so they don't fall down all over again).
DEATHTYPE.LifeTime: Summon's lifetime ran out.
DEATHTYPE.Necrotic: Died from necrotic damage.
DEATHTYPE.Physical: Died from physical damage.
DEATHTYPE.Psychic: Died from psychic damage.
DEATHTYPE.Radiant: Died from radiant damage.
DEATHTYPE.CinematicDeath: (temporary) death pose by the goblin kids in their dialog.Further InformationIf _Target is an item
this call will destroy it in game world terms: the item will break, and if any "Destroy Actions" are defined for this item in the side bar (such as transforming to a different root template), these will be performed.
see Exists for information on whether the item will still exist as far as the game engine is concerned after it has been destroyed.

IteratePlayerCharacters.description:
Iterates over all characters in that are in any party, and throws the event _Event for every one of them.
Further InformationSince only player characters can be in parties, and since all player characters are guaranteed to be in a party, the practical upshot is that this call sends _Event to all current player characters.

LookAtEntity.description:
Makes the character _Character for _Duration seconds look at object _Target, which may be a character, item or trigger.
If _Duration is set to -1.0, the duration will be infinite.
Further InformationThis call does not ensure that the _Character actually sees _Target in the game play sense: they may merely turn their head if the angle is not too big, which will not adjust their visual cone. If you need to turn the visual cone, use SteerTo.
This call only works for humanoids due to the way it works by turning the head if possible. For other characters, you can only use SteerTo.
LookAtEntity.examples:
QRY
QRY_Lair_HasNearbyThreat()
AND
DB_InRegion(_Player, _HagLair_NearbyBox)
AND
HasLineOfSight(_Hag, _Player, 1)
AND
IsInvisible(_Player, 0)
THEN
LookAtEntity((CHARACTER)_Hag, _Player); 
PROC_SpotPlayers_Spotted((CHARACTER)_Hag, "Hag_Lair", _Player);

EnterChasmProcessed.description:
This call is required if it’s required to override/extend on a default logic of processing the falling into chasm.
If it’s called with _HandledInStory equaling 1, the default logic is not going to be processed.
Further InformationCall needs to happen with _HandledInStory equaling 0 to get processed, but usually it’s handled in a last goal to allow all the override logic to happen before that.

EnterSharedTurnBaseMode.description:
Adds a _PlayerCharacter to an ongoing Shared Turn based zone with this _ZoneID.
Further InformationYou can catch the Zone ID of a shared turn based zone using the EnteredSharedForceTurnBased event.
Does nothing if _PlayerCharacter is invalid, not player character, or it is already in any kind of FTB (shared or not)
EnterSharedTurnBaseMode.examples:
IF
CombatEnded(_CombatID)
AND
DB_CRE_PlayersInCombatAtFTBStart(_CombatID, _Players)
AND
DB_CRE_SharedForceTurnBasedZone(_Zone)
THEN
EnterSharedTurnBaseMode(_Players, _Zone);
NOT DB_CRE_PlayersInCombatAtFTBStart(_CombatID, _Players);

ItemRotateYToAngle.description:
Rotate an item to a specific rotation.
Further InformationThis call will take the input angle and convert it to a rotation. E.g., if you input 1000.0 into _AngleDeg, code will convert that to the rotation of 280.0 then rotate the item to that rotation instead of rotating the item 3 times.
This call will also cancel any active rotation calls on the item that is executing at the time.
ItemRotateYToAngle.examples:
PROC
PROC_RotateStatuesTo45Degrees()
AND
DB_Statues(_Statue)
THEN
ItemRotateYToAngle(_Statue, 45.0, 15.0);

ItemMoveToPosition.description:
Makes _Item move with initial speed _Speed m/s and with acceleration _Acceleration m/s2 to _Target. When the item stops moving, the _Event should be raised for the item, but see the notes for details about this.
Further InformationIf the item is in a different level than the current one, this call behaves like a teleport inside that other level: the coordinates of the item are adjusted and the event is thrown immediately.
Movements for items cannot be queued. Starting a new movement for an item that was already moving will replace the previous movement (and arrival event) with the new one.
Triggers left/entered during the move will result in ItemEnteredTrigger and ItemLeftTrigger events as appropriate.
ItemMoveToPosition.examples:
IF
UseStarted(_, _MindFlayerColony_Elevator_B__ElevatorSwitch)
AND
DB_SCL_Benchmark_ElevatorMoveTo(_MoveTo)
AND
DB_SCL_Benchmark_ElevatorPoints(_MoveTo, _MoveToPoint, _Sound)
AND
GetPosition(_MoveToPoint, _X, _Y, _Z)
THEN
ItemMoveToPosition(_MindFlayerColony_SoundHelper, _X, _Y, _Z, 3.0, 0.0, "", 0);

IterateTagsCategory.description: Call to iterate through a TAGCATEGORY and trigger an event for every tag with the defined string _Event. Will also trigger an event when it completes the iteration with the defined _FinishEvent.

FleeFromObject.description:
Makes a character flee from a specific object.
Parameters:
_FleeingCharacter: The one who flees
_FleeFrom: From whom they flee
_FleeRange: How far they free
FleeFromObject.examples:
IF
DB_Is_InCombat(_Player_ShadowHeart, _Combat)
AND
DB_Is_InCombat(_Wolf, _Combat)
AND
IsTagged(_Wolf, (TAG)_WOLF, 1)
THEN
FleeFromObject(_Player_ShadowHeart, _Wolf, 10.0);

GenerateItems.description:
Existing trade treasure in _Trader's inventory is removed (see ClearTradeGeneratedItems for details). Then new trade treasure gets generated using the level of _Player and potentially based on the game's current difficulty level. These generated items are immediately added to the trader's inventory and are identified.
If a custom trade treasure table is set for the trader, this one is used, otherwise the default one(s) set for the trader character in the sidebar is/are used. If the used trade treasure table(s) is invalid/does not exist, no new items get generated.

IterateCharactersAround.description: Causes entity event _Event to be thrown for every character within _Radius meters of _Center. Once the last such character has been iterated, an additional entity event _CompletionEvent will be thrown for object NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, so that you can perform any necessary clean-up or finalize processing.

LeaveSharedTurnBaseMode.description: Make certain player character leave the shared FTB instance without the instance ending (will be dragged in the FTB back near other entities in FTB).

IterateActiveObjectsInSameCombatGroup.description: Iterates over all active objects in the same combat group as _Object. Throws_Event for every object and throws _CompletionEvent when all characters have been iterated.
IterateActiveObjectsInSameCombatGroup.examples:
IF
EnteredCombat(_Object,_Combat)
AND
NOT DB_CombatGroups_CheckedDialog(_Object,_Combat)
AND
GetCombatGroupID(_Object,_GroupID)
AND
_GroupID != ""
AND
NOT DB_CombatGroups_Iterator(_GroupID,_Combat,_,_)
AND
Concatenate("CombatGroupDialogCheck_",_GroupID,_IteratorEvent)
AND
Concatenate("CombatGroupDialogCheckFinished_",_GroupID,_IteratorFinishEvent)
THEN
DB_CombatGroups_Iterator(_GroupID,_Combat,_IteratorEvent,_IteratorFinishEvent);
IterateActiveObjectsInSameCombatGroup(_Object,_IteratorEvent,_IteratorFinishEvent);

FactionSetLootOwned.description:
Changes the color of the outline & icon for every local character with faction _Faction in the current level + all global characters with faction _Faction. If _Bool is 1, then if _Character is dead or when it dies, its items keep a red outline, and its corpse also gets a red outline. Additionally, the cursor shows that picking up/looting them is forbidden. If _Bool is 0, they will have a white outline and no "forbidden" cursors.
If not called, the default for a corpse is that its items and corpse are not owned.
Further InformationThis call in itself does not affect whether or not picking up the items/looting the character is a crime. For that, use DB_OwnedCorpse (which also uses this API to communicate the state)
The result of GetOwner is not affected either by this call, and as a result neither are whether CharacterStoleItem and RequestCanLoot events are fired

InitializePlayerData.description:
Initializes all data relating to an Origin character.
Further InformationOnly used at the very start of Character Creation.
InitializePlayerData.examples:
PROC
PROC_Shared_CharacterCreation_Init()
AND
DB_Origins(_Player)
THEN
InitializePlayerData(_Player);

LookFromTrigger.description:
Makes the _Character face the same direction as the _Trigger.
If _SnapToTarget is 0, the character will gradually rotate to face that way, otherwise they will snap.
LookFromTrigger.examples:
PROC
PROC_END_BrainBattle_TeleportParty(1)
AND
DB_PartyMembers(_GLO_Emperor)
THEN
LookFromTrigger(_GLO_Emperor, _END_BrainBattlePartyPos, 1);

IgnoreCrimeInRadiusWithTags.description: Using this call makes characters in _Radius ignore the disturbance of _CrimeID committed by _Criminal. An _OptionalTagExpression can be added to limit this ignoring to characters that possess a certain tag.

IterateHitProxies.description: This call iterates over every HitProxy from _OwnerObject, throwing an EntityEvent for every HitProxy object. Once the procedure finishes to iterate, _CompletionEvent will be called.
IterateHitProxies.seeAlso:
HitProxy
GetHitProxyOwner
IsHitProxy
IsHitProxyOwner
AttachHitProxy
DetachHitProxy
DetachAllHitProxies

Lock.description: Locks an item (such as door or container) and assigs a _Key value as a string. This value must match the string assigned to a key item for that key to unlock the locked item.

FleeFrom.description:
Makes character run away from characters with specific relationships (via factions) to them.
Parameters:
_Character: The character fleeing
_FleeFromRelationshipType: The relationship that should make the character flee
_FleeRanger: How far the character should fleeFurther InformationThe relationship type includes
Enemy
Ally
Any
Party
Player
FleeFrom.examples:
IF
DB_Is_InCombat(_Character, _Combat)
AND
DB_SIT_MyCharacter(_Character)
AND
DB_Is_InCombat(_Player, _Combat)
AND
DB_PartyMembers(_Player)
THEN
FleeFrom(_Character, "Party", 10.0);

LoadLevelTemplate.description:
Dynamically load a level template.
Parameters:
_LevelTemplate: the level template instance to loadFurther InformationYou can load a level template either because
you previously unloaded it using call UnloadLevelTemplate
the level template’s Starting Loaded property in the editor side bar was deselected, so it does not get loaded by defaultYou cannot specify the position of a level template, it gets loaded where it was placed in the editor.

GenerateTreasure.description:
Generates treasure from the _TreasureID treasure table and puts the item(s) generated in the _InventoryHolder's inventory.
Further InformationIf the _Level is greater than or equal to 0, it will be used to generate treasure.
If the _Level is less than 0, if the item is set (in the sidebar) to Use Party Level For Treasure, the highest level in the _Character 's party is used.
If the _Level is less than 0, and Use Party Level For Treasure is false, the item's Treasure Level setting is used.
If the Treasure Level setting is also less than 0, the level of the nearest character is used.
This adds the contents of the treasure to whatever is already inside the item's inventory. If it has a treasure table defined in the sidebar, it will get both. The treasure defined in the sidebar is generated whenever the first of the following occurs:
The item is destroyed
The item is used (e.g. opened)
The item is picked up
The item is unlocked
The item is moved
Something is added to the item's inventory (this can happen if an item is added e.g. through Story).
GenerateTreasure.examples:
IF
AttackedByObject(ITEMGUID)_SnoozingAdventurerChest, _Player, _, _,_)
AND
CharacterGetLevel((CHARACTERGUID)_Player, _Level)
AND
NOT DB_OnlyOnce("_SnoozingAdventurerChest_Attacked")
THEN
GenerateTreasure(ITEMGUID)_SnoozingAdventurerChest, "RewardMedium", _Level, (CHARACTERGUID)_Player);

MagicPocketsMoveTo.description: If the _Item is in the inventory of _Source and party inventories accessible to them, it is moved to the inventory of _DestinationInventory. If _ShowNotification is 1, a popup will be shown that an item was taken via magic pockets. If _ClearOriginalOwner is 1, the original owner will be cleared.
MagicPocketsMoveTo.seeAlso:
SetMagicPocketsFlag
MagicPocketsDrop
MagicPocketsDropByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTag
MagicPocketsMoveToByTemplate
MagicPocketsMoveToByTag
ClearMagicPocketsFlag
IsInMagicPockets
TaggedItemsGetCountInMagicPockets
TemplateGetCountInMagicPockets
StatStringGetCountInMagicPockets

LieOnEntity.description:
Same as SitOnEntity but makes the character immediately lie on an entity instead of sit.
Further InformationThis call is not normally used to make characters sit on the ground as this is part of a character’s behavior and done in Anubis. In Osiris sometimes we use Use instead to force a character to interact with an item that can cause them to sit or lie on it.
LieOnEntity.seeAlso:
ReposeAdded
ReposeRemoved
EndRepose
SitOnGround
LieOnGround
SitOnEntity
StandOnEntity
IsReposed
IsReposedSitting
IsReposedLying
IsReposedStanding
IsReposedConstrained
TemplateCanSitOn

MagicPocketsDrop.description: If _Object is in the inventory of _Source or a party inventory accessible to it, it is dropped at its top inventory holder.
MagicPocketsDrop.examples:
IF
FlagSet(_TressymTrade_Event_GiveFishToTressym, (CHARACTER)_Player, _)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
GetItemByTagInInventory("_FISH", _Player, _Fish)
THEN
MagicPocketsDrop(_Player, _Fish);
PROC_SetOnStage(_Fish,0);
MagicPocketsDrop.seeAlso:
SetMagicPocketsFlag
MagicPocketsDropByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTag
MagicPocketsMoveTo
MagicPocketsMoveToByTemplate
MagicPocketsMoveToByTag
ClearMagicPocketsFlag
IsInMagicPockets
TaggedItemsGetCountInMagicPockets
TemplateGetCountInMagicPockets
StatStringGetCountInMagicPockets

LockUnequip.description: Locks the unequip option on the slot that the item passed is equipped to.
LockUnequip.examples:
PROC
PROC_LockCollar()
AND
IsEquipped(_SourceCollar, 1)
THEN
LockUnequip(_SourceCollar, 1);

LootRequestProcessed.description: _Character is the character that fired the request. If _RequestAccepted is 0, the action attempt is blocked. Otherwise, the action passes and continues.
LootRequestProcessed.examples:
PROC
PROC_ProcessLootCorpse((CHARACTER)_Looter, (CHARACTER)_Corpse)
AND
NOT DB_CustomLootCorpseResponse(_Looter, _Corpse, _)
AND
NOT DB_OwnedCorpse(_Corpse, _)
THEN
LootRequestProcessed(_Looter, 1);

ItemMoveTo.description: Performs the same operation as , by taking the position of _Target at the time this call is made, and moving the item to that position. Additionally, if _UseRotation is 1 then during its trajectory the item will gradually rotate to the rotation of _Target.

ItemRotateY.description:
Rotates an item to the given angle in degrees.
Further InformationThis call rotates the object in raw degrees. E.g., passing 720.0 for _AngleDeg will rotate the object in a full circle twice. Use this call if you want to rotate the object an X amount of degrees and are not concerned about the final angle of the item.
If the item is already rotating when this is called, the call will fail.
ItemRotateY.examples:
PROC
PROC_RotateStatues90Degrees()
AND
DB_Statues(_Statue)
THEN
ItemRotateY(_Statue, 90.0, 45.0);

LieOnGround.description:
Same as SitOnGround but it makes the character lie on the ground instead of sit.
Further InformationThis call is not normally used to make characters sit on the ground as this is part of a character’s behavior and done in Anubis.
LieOnGround.seeAlso:
ReposeAdded
ReposeRemoved
EndRepose
SitOnGround
SitOnEntity
LieOnEntity
StandOnEntity
IsReposed
IsReposedSitting
IsReposedLying
IsReposedStanding
IsReposedConstrained
TemplateCanSitOn

GiveInspirationPoints.description:
Gives character _Character a number of inspiration points (_Amount, a real number), with optional “reason“ and “description“ strings that correlate to translated string keys.
Further InformationShould not be used outside of debug or modding purposes. Inspiration points are normally awarded by code, through completing background goals. See AddBackgroundGoal.

IgnoreCrimesInRadiusWithTagsForDuration.description: Using this call makes characters in _Radius ignore all disturbances committed by _Criminal for a _Duration. An _OptionalTagExpressioncan be added to limit this ignoring to characters that possess a certain tag.

EscortRemoveAll.description:
Removes all the targets from a specific escort group.
Parameters:
_EscortGroup — The name of the group

MoveAllItemsTo.description:
A call to make all items on an object move to another object, with the option to include equipped items. (For example, this call could move all the items from Gale to Shadowheart, or a treasure chest, or said treasure chest to Gale, etc.)
Parameters:
_FromObject - The GUID of the object to move the items from
_ToObject - The GUID of the object to move the items to
_MoveEquippedArmor - Integer - 0 means to leave equipped armor where it is, 1 means to move it
_MoveEquippedWeapons - Integer - 0 means to leave equipped weapons where they are, 1 means to move them
_ClearOriginalOwner - Integer - 0 means to leave the original owner of the item, 1 means to clear it
_MoveVanityClothing - Integer - 0 means to leave vanity clothing where it is, 1 means to move itFurther InformationThis will move every single item, including items that normally wouldn’t drop (such as enemy-only items), but still leaves Story Items. If you only want normally lootable items, use MoveAllLootableItemsTo or for story items use MoveAllStoryItemsTo.
MoveAllItemsTo.examples:
PROC
PROC_Orin_RemoveAllItems()
AND
NOT DB_SavedOrinsInventory(1)
AND
NOT DB_GlobalFlag((FLAG)_State_OrinInTemple)
THEN
MoveAllItemsTo((CHARACTER)_Orin, (CHARACTER)_OrinDummy, 1, 1, 1, 1);

DialogSetVariableTranslatedString.description:
Sets a translated string type dialog variable with identifier _Variable to _Value for any instance of dialog _Dialog. This will work if this call is executed before that dialog starts.
