Further InformationThe given _Variable needs to correlate to a string version of a dialog variable GUID, e.g. “GEN_CheckMagicPocketGold_6057ad05-9492-4630-9f0a-be548b134c54“. Unlike that variable, it needs to be defined as a “TranslatedString“ type in the dialog editor’s dialog variables editor (under the “Tools” menu).

EscortSetLeader.description:
Sets the leader in an escort group.
Parameters:
_Target — The character to set as leader of the group
_EscortGroup — The name of the groupFurther InformationNote that in order for this to function correctly, the character must have already been added to the group via EscortAddCharacter. Otherwise, an assertion error will occur and the action will not be executed.
It is necessary to make this call at least once for every escort group, as movement will not be initiated without it.
For the cases if something happened with a designated leader, it’s possible to set up several of them, using EscortSetLeaderPriority.
EscortSetLeader.examples:
PROC
PROC_Drider_InitEscortParty()
THEN
EscortAddCharacter(_Drider, "DriderEscort");
EscortSetLeader(_Drider, "DriderEscort");

IF
DB_Drider_PartOfEscort(_Char)
AND
NOT DB_Defeated(_Char)
THEN
EscortAddCharacter(_Char, "DriderEscort");

LoadPartyPreset.description: Loads _Preset party preset and teleports to _TeleportToTarget.

EndRepose.description:
Makes a character end their current repose.
Reposing is an action where a character connects to a specific position, such as lying on a bed, sitting on a chair, or sitting on the ground and plays a default animation appropriate for the pose. The action aligns the character's position with an entity’s predefined socket if an entity is provided, or their current position if no object is specified.Parameters:
_Character: The character that should end repose
Only entities of type CHARACTER make sense for this call, even if it technically accepts all GUIDSTRING
EndRepose.examples:
IF
StatusRemoved(_Hostage, "IRONTHRONE_TIEDTOTORTUREBED", _, _)
AND
DB_PrisonerGroup(_, (CHARACTER)_Hostage)
THEN
EndRepose(_Hostage);
EndRepose.seeAlso:
ReposeAdded
ReposeRemoved
SitOnGround
LieOnGround
SitOnEntity
LieOnEntity
StandOnEntity
IsReposed
IsReposedSitting
IsReposedLying
IsReposedStanding
IsReposedConstrained
TemplateCanSitOn

IterateInventoryByTag.description:
Iterates over all  _InventoryHolder’s inventory. For each item in it with the _Tags, it will send an Entity Event with the string _Event as an identifier. When all of the items have been iterated over, it will send the _CompletionEvent as an Entity Event.
Note that while it is called _Tags, it is actually expecting a single Tag as a string.Further InformationYou can pass "" as the _CompletionEvent if you don’t need to react to the iteration completing.

MagicPocketsMoveToByTemplate.description: Finds _Amount of items of the root template _Template in the inventory of _Source and party inventories accessible to them and moves them to the inventory of _DestinationInventory. If _ShowNotification is 1, a popup will be shown that an item was taken via magic pockets. If _ClearOriginalOwner is 1, the original owner will be cleared.
MagicPocketsMoveToByTemplate.examples:
IF
FlagSet(_SocietyOfBrilliance_Event_GiveMushrooms, _Player, _)
AND
NOT DB_UND_SociectyOfBrilliance_GaveMushrooms("Tongue", _)
AND
TemplateGetCountInMagicPockets(_SocietyOfBrilliance_TongueOfMadness, (CHARACTER)_Player, _Count)
AND
_Count > 0
THEN
DB_UND_SociectyOfBrilliance_GaveMushrooms("Tongue", (ROOT)_SocietyOfBrilliance_TongueOfMadness);
MagicPocketsMoveToByTemplate(_Player, _SocietyOfBrilliance_TongueOfMadness, 1, _SocietyOfBrilliance_MindFlayer, 1, 1);
MagicPocketsMoveToByTemplate.seeAlso:
SetMagicPocketsFlag
MagicPocketsDrop
MagicPocketsDropByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTag
MagicPocketsMoveTo
MagicPocketsMoveToByTag
ClearMagicPocketsFlag
IsInMagicPockets
TaggedItemsGetCountInMagicPockets
TemplateGetCountInMagicPockets
StatStringGetCountInMagicPockets

ItemDragToPosition.description:
Move an item to a specific world position.
Further InformationThis simulates the action of the player dragging an item from one location to another.

MakeWar.description:
Throws a event UserMakeWar(_UserIDOfSource, _UserIDOfTarget, 1) event. The _IgnoreVote parameter is currently ignored.
Further InformationIf _Source or _Target is invalid or not currently a player character, nothing will happen.
If both characters are controlled by the same user, this will not have any effect.
This call does not do anything besides throwing the aforementioned UserMakeWar event. There is currently no support in story to do anything in reaction to that event, because the main BG3 campaign does not support multiple parties (the game engine still does).

MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTag.description:
Finds up to _Amount items that match the tag expression _Tags in the inventory of _Source and party inventories accessible to them and deletes these objects.
Further InformationThe call ignores objects that were originally Global (but not objects that are made global by entering a global inventory).
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTag.seeAlso:
SetMagicPocketsFlag
MagicPocketsDrop
MagicPocketsDropByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTemplate
MagicPocketsMoveTo
MagicPocketsMoveToByTemplate
MagicPocketsMoveToByTag
ClearMagicPocketsFlag
IsInMagicPockets
TaggedItemsGetCountInMagicPockets
TemplateGetCountInMagicPockets
StatStringGetCountInMagicPockets

IterateUsers.description: Throws an _Event for each user in the game.

LeaveCombat.description:
Makes _Target leave the combat it's currently involved it. Should not be called if _Target is currently not in combat.
Further InformationUnless you first teleported _Target away to a safe location, it's quite likely that it will rejoin the same combat during the next frame. If you wish to prevent it from rejoining any further combat, call SetCanJoinCombat instead (but be careful to restore it again later in case this is done for a player).
LeaveCombat.examples:
PROC
PROC_AmbushersDropCombat((STRING)_Ambush)
AND
DB_CultistAmbushCombatants(_Ambusher, _Ambush)
AND
IsInCombat(_Ambusher,1)
THEN
LeaveCombat(_Ambusher);

MagicPocketsDropByTemplate.description: Finds up to _Amount of objects with the root template _Root in the inventory of _Source and party inventories accessible to them, and drops each at the the location of its respective topmost inventory owner.
MagicPocketsDropByTemplate.seeAlso:
SetMagicPocketsFlag
MagicPocketsDrop
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTag
MagicPocketsMoveTo
MagicPocketsMoveToByTemplate
MagicPocketsMoveToByTag
ClearMagicPocketsFlag
IsInMagicPockets
TaggedItemsGetCountInMagicPockets
TemplateGetCountInMagicPockets
StatStringGetCountInMagicPockets

MakePlayerActive.description:
Makes _Target the currently controlled/focused character for its owning user (GetReservedUserID) /player.
Success/Failure: Fails if _Target does not exist or is NULL.
_User: The user ID of the player to which _Target is assigned. If it is not assigned to anyone, the special value -65536 is returned.Further Information"Users" are the game term for human players in the game. Every client that connects to the game, including the one that started the host game, gets assigned a User ID.
User IDs are volatile: they can change at any time, in particular when loading a saved game, or when someone disconnects and reconnects. Never store this value in a database. Only use it immediately after requesting it, within the same frame. If you need a persistent identifier for a user (or rather, user profile), pass the result of this query to GetUserProfileID.

MagicPocketsMoveToByTag.description: Finds _Amount of items that matches the tag expression _Tags in the inventory of _Source and party inventories accessible to them and moves them to the inventory of _DestinationInventory. If _ShowNotification is 1, a popup will be shown that an item was taken via magic pockets. If _ClearOriginalOwner is 1, the original owner will be cleared.
MagicPocketsMoveToByTag.examples:
IF
FlagSet(_Elfsong_Event_GivingPotion, _Player, _)
AND
DB_Players((CHARACTER)_Player)
THEN
MagicPocketsMoveToByTag(_Player, "_HEALING_POTION", 1, _Elfsong_Exterior_Patron, 0, 1);
MagicPocketsMoveToByTag.seeAlso:
SetMagicPocketsFlag
MagicPocketsDrop
MagicPocketsDropByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTag
MagicPocketsMoveTo
MagicPocketsMoveToByTemplate
ClearMagicPocketsFlag
IsInMagicPockets
TaggedItemsGetCountInMagicPockets
TemplateGetCountInMagicPockets
StatStringGetCountInMagicPockets

MakePlayer.description:
Turns NPC _TargetCharacter into a player-controlled character. The character will be assigned to the same user as the one controlling _OwnerCharacter, and will appear in the portrait list after _OwnerCharacter.
If _OwnerCharacter is NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, the character will be assigned to the host user.

MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTemplate.description:
Finds up to _Amount items with the root template _ItemTemplate in the inventory of _Source and party inventories accessible to them and deletes these objects.
Further InformationThe call ignores objects that were originally Global (but not objects that are made global by entering a global inventory).
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTemplate.seeAlso:
SetMagicPocketsFlag
MagicPocketsDrop
MagicPocketsDropByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTag
MagicPocketsMoveTo
MagicPocketsMoveToByTemplate
MagicPocketsMoveToByTag
ClearMagicPocketsFlag
IsInMagicPockets
TaggedItemsGetCountInMagicPockets
TemplateGetCountInMagicPockets
StatStringGetCountInMagicPockets

FireOsirisEvents.description:
All Osiris events are kept in a buffer that is usually flushed at the start of the frame.
This call allows to force flush the event queue and trigger these events.
This generally affects iterators like IterateCharactersAround, IterateInventory, etc.
Further InformationBe careful, since this will also fire e.g. any events already queued by SetEntityEvent in the same frame, and there may be scripting that did so expecting it to fire only in the next frame.
If you want to flush the task queue, FlushOsirisQueue should be used.

DropTo.description:
Makes the character who is the top inventory owner of the item start an Osiris task to drop that item at the specified location.
Further InformationTo make the character drop the item at their current location, see: Drop.
Will fire DroppedBy.
DropTo.examples:
IF
AddedTo(_DropItem,_Owner,_)
AND
DB_AlwaysDrop((ITEM)_DropItem)
AND
IsCharacter(_Owner,1)
AND
GetPosition(_Owner,_X,_Y,_Z)
THEN
DropTo(_DropItem,_X,_Y,_Z);

MakeNPC.description: Turn _Character into an NPC, which automatically removes them from their party.

MakePeace.description: Throws UserMakeWar (_UserIDOfSource, _UserIDOfTarget, 0) event. The _IgnoreVote parameter is currently ignored.

TeleportPartiesToLevelWithMovie.description:
Teleports all player characters to the different level _LevelName and starts playing the movie _Movie for all of them. Whenever a player character arrives at its destination, a story event _Event will be thrown for them.
Further Information_Movie can be an empty string.
Use call TeleportTo if you want to teleport individual characters (but not to a different level, unless they are NPCs).

StandOnEntity.description:
Same as SitOnEntity but makes the character immediately stand on an entity instead of sit.
Further InformationThis call is not normally used to make characters sit on the ground as this is part of a character’s behaviour and done in Anubis. In Osiris sometimes we use Use instead to force a character to interact with an item that can cause them to stand on it.
StandOnEntity.seeAlso:
ReposeAdded
ReposeRemoved
EndRepose
SitOnGround
LieOnGround
SitOnEntity
LieOnEntity
IsReposed
IsReposedSitting
IsReposedLying
IsReposedStanding
IsReposedConstrained
TemplateCanSitOn

ShowGameOverMenu.description:
Show the game over dialog box.
Parameters
_Reason: A translated string key of a message that indicates why the game is over.Further InformationWhen playing in honour mode, this also finalizes the save file.
If you call the overload without parameters, it uses a default reason.
ShowGameOverMenu.examples:
// Show the game over dialog box using the default message
ShowGameOverMenu();

ShowMapMarker.description:
If _Show is 1, then shows a map marker with ID _MarkerID to _Character.
If _Show is 0, then hides a map marker with ID _MarkerID to _Character.

MoveAllStoryItemsTo.description:
A call to make all story items on an object move to another object, with the option to include equipped items.
Parameters:
_FromObject - The GUID of the object to move the items from
_ToObject - The GUID of the object to move the items to
_MoveEquippedItems - Integer - 0 means to leave equipped armor where it is, 1 means to move it
_ClearOriginalOwner - Integer - 0 means to leave the original owner of the item, 1 means to clear itFurther InformationThis will move only story items, and leave any other items. If you want to move everything, instead use MoveAllItemsTo, or for only normally lootable items, use MoveAllLootableItemsTo.

SetVisible.description:
Makes _Object invisible if _Bool is 0, or visible if it is 1. _Object can be a character or an item.
Further InformationThis is not the same as applying the INVISIBLE status. The character isn't visible on-screen, but gets no specific status. The engine simply hides them on screen.
Characters who are made invisible in this way have their physics disabled (i.e. they will not block arrow shots nor sight). They still occupy room on the AI-grid though, can still be hit with area-of-effect skills and surfaces, and will still be visible as far as the engine is concerned (e.g. they will still trigger Saw events).

ShowError.description:
Shows an error message to the user controlling the provided _Character.
Further Information

Error (code passed into _Error)

Message (seen by player)


CannotReach

Can't reach


CannotUseLadders

Can't climb


CannotInteract

Can't use


FullyEncumbered

Can't move: Encumbered


Crippled

Can't move: Crippled


Enwebbed

Can't move: Enwebbed


Entangled

Can't move: Entangled


ZeroSpeed

Movement Speed is 0.


NotEnoughAP

Not enough AP


InvalidTarget

Target is invalid


TooFar

Target is too far


TooHeavy

Target is too heavy


OutOfSight

Target is out of sight


LadderBlocked

Ladder is blocked


Polymorphed

You cannot do this in your current form!


Disarmed

Character is disarmed


CantSee

Can't see!


NotEnoughSpace

Not enough space!


NotEnoughStrength

Not enough strength!


TargetBlocked

Target is blocked!
ShowError.examples:
PROC
PROC_BlockUseOfItem(_Char, _Book)
AND
DB_BooksBehindBars(_Book, _Door, _Trigger)
AND
IsInTrigger(_Book, _Trigger, 1)
AND
IsClosed((ITEM)_Door, 1)
THEN
DB_CustomUseItemResponse((CHARACTER)_Char,(ITEM)_Book,0);
ShowError((CHARACTER)_Char, "CannotReach");

SetVarString.description:
Sets the value of the behaviour script variable %<_Varname> to _VarValue, if the variable exists and is of the STRING type.
Further InformationThe variable has to be global. The _Varname does not include the prefix % that is used for global variables in behaviourscript. E.g. a variable that is called %Variable in behaviourscript would simply be Variable for the purposes of this call.
SetVarString.examples:
PROC
PROC_CRIME_Guards_CallFoundGuard((CHARACTER)_Caller,(INTEGER)_CrimeID,(STRING)_Action)
AND
NOT DB_CRIME_Guards_Called(_CrimeID,_,_Caller,_,_,_,_,_,_,_)
AND
NOT DB_CRIME_Guards_SpawnGuards_WaitForSpawn(_Caller,_CrimeID,_,_,_,_,_,_)
THEN
SetVarString(_Caller, "GuardSummonFailedReaction", _Action);
SetEntityEvent(_Caller, "CRIME_CallGuardsFailed",1);
SetVarString.seeAlso:
SetVarObject
ClearVarObject
SetVarInteger
SetVarFloat
SetVarFixedString
SetVarFloat3

SharedTurnBaseMode.description:
Enables (if _OnOff is 1) or disables (if _OnOff is 0) forced turn-based (FTB) mode for all player characters in the level, regardless of how far they are from each other. They are all part of the same initiative sequence.
Further InformationDoes nothing if _PlayerCharacter is invalid, not player character, or it is already in any kind of FTB (shared or not).
SharedTurnBaseMode.examples:
IF
CombatEnded(_CombatID)
AND
DB_CRE_CombatDuringCountdown(_CombatID)
AND
DB_GlobalFlag((FLAG)CRE_Lance_State_ExplosionCountdownActive)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
THEN
SharedTurnBaseMode(_Player, 1);

TeleportToPosition.description:
Teleports the object _SourceObject to the coordinates (_X, _Y, _Z). See TeleportTo for the explanation of the other parameters, as they work the exact same way.
Further InformationIf _SourceObject is a character, and the destination location is not valid on the AI Grid, the game will find a nearby valid location on the AI grid and place the character there.
TeleportToPosition.examples:
IF
ObjectTimerFinished(_Player, "ChasmRetrievalDelay")
AND
IsDead((CHARACTER)_Player, 1)
AND
DB_ChasmRetrievalDelay((CHARACTER)_Player, _ChasmX, _ChasmY, _ChasmZ)
THEN
ApplyStatus(_Player, "DEAD_WILL_O_WISP", -1.0, 1, NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000);
TeleportToPosition(_Player, _ChasmX, _ChasmY, _ChasmZ, "ChasmCorpseTeleported", 0, 0, 1);

ShowNotification.description:
Show a notification dialog with _Text as the message, for player who controls _Character.
This should not be used for anything other than debug text.
Use ShowError where applicable, or OpenMessageBox.

TeleportPartiesWithMovie.description:
Teleports all player characters to _Target and starts playing the movie _Movie for all of them. Whenever a player character arrives at its destination, a story event _Event will be thrown for them.
Further Information_Target must be global if it is located in a different level.
This call can be used to move all players to a new level.
_Movie can be an empty string.
Use call TeleportTo if you want to teleport individual characters (but not to a different level, unless they are NPCs).

ShowTutorial.description: Shows a tutorial defined in UUID table(s) UnifiedTutorials to a user who is currently controlling the CHARACTER mentioned in the call.
ShowTutorial.examples:
//example A; shows tutorial about summons after a new entry in player summons DB
IF
DB_PlayerSummons(_Summon)
AND
CharacterGetOwner(_Summon, _Player)
AND
NOT DB_Is_InCombat(_Player, _)
THEN
PROC_ShowUnifiedTutorialForPlayer((CHARACTER)_Player, (UNIFIEDTUTORIAL)_Summons);

StopDrawSurfaceOnPath.description: Stops a Surface Draw Action started with DrawSurfaceOnPath, using the identifier _SurfaceActionHandle returned by that query.

TriggerSetLighting.description:
Sets _Trigger LightingTrigger (specific trigger type) to lighting with resource uuid _LightingUUID.
Further InformationThe lighting to which you try to set the trigger needs to be added to the list under its Lightings property, otherwise the call won’t work.
After adding the lighting resource to the list build all light probes.

StartPickpocket.description:
Call in response to RequestPickpocket to tell code whether the pickpocket attempt should succeed or fail.
Further InformationIf not called at all, then after one second code will assume the corresponding pickpocket request failed.
See RequestPickpocket.

TeleportTo.description:
Teleports the object _SourceObject to the location of _TargetObject. Once the object arrives there, _Event will be sent to _SourceObject.
If _SourceObject is a player character, then
if _TeleportLinkedCharacters is 1, then all characters currently chained to _SourceObject will also be teleported, unless they are unavailable (see below).
if _TeleportPartyFollowers is 1, then all party followers of _SourceObject will also be teleported, unless they are unavailable (see below).
if _TeleportSummons is 1, then all summons of _SourceObject that are not turrets will also be teleported, unless they are unavailable (see below)."Unavailable" characters for teleporting as mentioned above are those for which one of the following conditions holds:
They are in combat and _SourceObject is not in the same combat.
They are dead or incapacitated.
They are in an interactive dialogue and _SourceObject is not in the same dialogue.If _LeaveCombat is 1, then _SourceObject will leave combat after being teleported, even if they are teleported inside the combat range of the original combat.
If _SnapToGround is 1, then _SourceObject will be placed on the highest available AI grid position at _TargetObject's location. Otherwise it will be teleported to _TargetObject's exact position, and if that's in the air it will immediately start falling afterwards.
Further InformationThe default values for unspecified parameters are as follows:
_Event: empty string (no event will be sent)
_TeleportLinkedCharacters: 1
_TeleportPartyFollowers: 0
_TeleportSummons: 1
_LeaveCombat: 0
_SnapToGround: 1
_SourceObject can be a character or an item. There is also limited, experimental support for triggers, as long as you do not teleport them out of their original level.
You can teleport objects from other levels (except for triggers) if they are global.
You can teleport objects that are off-stage.
You cannot use this command to teleport players to another level; use call DialogSetTeleportPartyToLevelOnEnded or call TeleportPartiesToLevelWithMovie  instead.
The snapping to the ground will apply only to a character or an item.
TeleportTo.examples:
PROC
PROC_WaypointTeleported((CHARACTER)_Char, (TRIGGER)_Waypoint)
AND
DB_GLO_Spells_DominatedCharacter(_Target, _, _Char)
AND
NOT DB_Camp(_, _, _, _Waypoint) // Avoid teleporting to CAMP
THEN
TeleportTo(_Target, _Char);

TriggerRegisterForCharacter.description:
Ensures that whenever _Character enters or leaves _Trigger, a EnteredTrigger or LeftTrigger event will be thrown for this trigger/character combination.
Further InformationIf you register a trigger for a character while the character is currently inside the trigger, a EnteredTrigger event will also be thrown. This even holds for characters placed in the editor and triggers registered in the INIT section of a story goal that gets initialized once the RegionStarted event for its containing level has executed.
Event triggers are automatically registered for all characters by the engine and should not be manually registered/unregistered.

TriggerUnregisterForItems.description:
Stops the generation of item(template) entered/left events for _Trigger.
Stops ItemEnteredTrigger, ItemLeftTrigger, TemplateEnteredTrigger or TemplateLeftTrigger events from throwing whenever an item enters/leaves _Trigger.
Further InformationUnlike with character triggers, unregistering a trigger for items will not throw ItemLeftTrigger/ItemTemplateLeftTrigger events for all items already in that trigger.

StopLoopEffect.description: Stops playing the VFX associated with the _FxHandle specified.

ShutdownCrimeSystem.description:
Stops the crime system from further processing disturbances/crimes.
Further InformationInternally, we only use this for test mods so that e.g. animators aren’t constantly sent to prison when they test their fancy new attack animations in game.
Once shut down, there is no way to restart the crime system.

StopCameraSpline.description:
Aborts the camera spline _Spline for _Character if it was previously started using StartCameraSpline.
Further InformationIf you specify NULL as _Character, the camera spline will be stopped for all player characters.
This call is only necessary if you wish to abort a camera spline prematurely, or if you want to stop a looping camera spline.
The fade out would be the same as it was defined in StartCameraSpline.

TriggerDespawnCrowd.description: Immediately despawns all crowds characters spawned by the crowd trigger.

UnlockRecipe.description: Use this to unlock a recipe for a character and choose whether to show a notification or not.

UnloadLevelTemplate.description:
Dynamically unload a level template.
Parameters:
_LevelTemplate: the level template instance to unloadFurther InformationSee LoadLevelTemplate.

ObjectTimerCancel.description:
A call to make an existing _Timer stop and end that was set on a particular _Object. Will only work if a timer has already been launched.
Further InformationFor Global timers, instead use call TimerCancel.
For Real Time timers, instead use call RealtimeObjectTimerCancel.
ObjectTimerCancel.examples:
IF
Died(_Player)
AND
ObjectTimerExists(_Player, _Timer, 1)
THEN
ObjectTimerCancel((GUIDSTRING)_Player, (STRING)_Timer)

SitOnGround.description:
Makes _Character sit on the ground.
Parameters:
_Character: The character prompted to sit
Only entities of type CHARACTER make sense for this parameter, even if it technically accepts all GUIDSTRINGFurther InformationThis call is not normally used to make characters sit on the ground as this is part of a character’s behavior and done in Anubis.
SitOnGround.seeAlso:
ReposeAdded
ReposeRemoved
EndRepose
LieOnGround
SitOnEntity
LieOnEntity
StandOnEntity
IsReposed
IsReposedSitting
IsReposedLying
IsReposedStanding
IsReposedConstrained
TemplateCanSitOn

ObjectQuestTimerLaunch.description:
In ObjectQuestTimerLaunch, a GUI element will appear to count the remaining number of rounds. This timer is unique in that it works both in Turn-Based Mode, Combat or even Real Time modes. It will still display in rounds the time remaining (Each round is 6 seconds) but can continue to count down in Real Time if desired.
This timer can be cancelled using ObjectTimerCancel, using an _Entity in the combat (whatever entity is in combat) and the name of the timer.
Further Information_Entity is any entity, as this works inside and outside of combat.
_Timer: the string identifier of the timer. This works exactly the same as other timers, like ObjectTimerLaunch.
_LocalizedTextKey: this is the string for a Text Key stored in the TranslatedStringKeys editor.
_Time: In milliseconds, the timer should be on multiples of six (6000 for one turn).
_ShouldTickOnTurnStart: 0 is that it should not, and 1 is that it should.

StopAnimation.description:
Stops any animation playing on _Target.
Further Information_Abort controls whether the animation finishes playing before stopping or if it’s aborted mid animation.

TriggerUnregisterForPlayers.description:
Stops EnteredTrigger events from being thrown whenever a player character enters _Trigger.
Further InformationIf you unregister a trigger for a character while the character was inside the trigger and the trigger was previously registered for that character, a LeftTrigger event will be thrown.
Event triggers are automatically registered for all characters by the engine and should not be manually registered/unregistered.

OpenMessageBoxYesNo.description: Opens a message box _Message requiring a yes/no response, for the specified _Character.
OpenMessageBoxYesNo.examples:
QRY
QRY_REALLY_BlockFastTravel(_TravellingPlayer)
AND
CharacterGetOwner(_PLA_StuckHalfElf, _Owner)
AND
IsPartyFollower(_PLA_StuckHalfElf, 1)
AND
QRY_PLA_StuckHalfElf_ShouldBlockFastTravel(_TravellingPlayer, _Owner)
THEN
OpenMessageBox(YesNo(_Owner, "PLA_Message_RemoveStuckElfFromParty");

ObjectTimerLaunch.description:
A call to make when you want a _Timer to activate related to a particular _Object, such as if you wish for an object to do something after the player tries to use it. The time for this in Osiris always set in milliseconds.
Further InformationThe advantage to ObjectTimerLaunch is that both Osiris and Anubis can utilise it, and it is capable of working in both real time and turn based modes.
For Global timers, instead use call TimerLaunch.
There is also a variant of RealtimeObjectTimerLaunch which works similarly, but will only work in real time and ignores turn based mode.

TemplateRemoveFrom.description: Destroys up to _Count instances of the root template _ItemTemplate that are in _Object's inventory. _Object can either be a character or a container object. If _Object has fewer than the specified number of _ItemTemplate items, the ones it has are still destroyed.

StopWorldTimeline.description:
Stops a world timeline dialog instance with an ID _DialogID.
Further InformationIn order to find dialog’s ID by dialog’s name, a database DB_DialogName can be used. Note, that multiple instances of the same dialog can be played at the same time.

MoviePlay.description: Start a movie _Event, playing for the character _Character. If you want a fade in on the end, you can set the parameter to 1.

MusicPlayForPeer.description:
Plays music for a specific _Character. The sound is not 3D.
Further InformationFor 3D sound use call MusicPlayOnCharacter.

UnlockRecipes.description: Use this to unlock a collection of recipes for a character.

StartHenchmen.description:
This is for internal use only.
Part of the hireling pipeline in creating hireling.

TimerLaunch.description:
A call to make when you want a _Timer to activate, such as if you wish for countdown to begin before something explodes. The time for this in Osiris always set in milliseconds.
Further InformationThis is a Global timer, and will only run in real time - It does not follow turn based rules.
For Object timers, instead use call ObjectTimerLaunch.
There is also a variant of RealtimeObjectTimerLaunch which works similarly to an Object timer, but will only work in real time and ignores turn based mode, similarly to Global timers.

TriggerSetCrowdSpawningEnable.description:
Used to enable or disable crowd triggers spawning crowd NPCs.
Further InformationUsing this call to disable crowd spawning will not despawn crowd characters produced by this crowd trigger on its own: for that either TriggerDespawnCrowd or TriggerSetCrowdBehaviour need to be used.

AddSpell.description:
Adds a specific spell or skill to a character.
Parameters:
_Character: character that will get the spell.
_Skill: name of the spell or skill.
_ShowNotification (optional): shows a UI notification of the spell being added to the character if they are part of the party. Set by default to 1.
_AddContainerSpell (optional): if _Skill is a container spell, all the spells part of this container will also be added to the character. Set by default to 1.Further InformationSpells can be found in the stats editor under "ModName"/SpellData/"SpellType" and new ones can be created. Do not forget to add the type of spell at the start of the name, if you have the spell Scream in the Shout category you have to call it with Shout_Scream, with _ in between.
Note: Even if the call has the spell word in it, spells are not only magical actions, but other things like shove, jump, and attacking with a weapon.
AddSpell.seeAlso:
UseSpell
RemoveSpell
UseSpellAtPosition
CastedSpell
CastSpell
CastSpellFailed
LearnedSpell
StartedPreviewingSpell
UsingSpell
UsingSpellAtPosition
UsingSpellInTrigger
UsingSpellOnTarget
CanLearnSpell
CanShowSpellForCharacter
GetSpellFromSet
HasSpell
IsSpellActive
SpellHasSpellFlag

TriggerSetSoundRTPC.description:
Changes the behavior of a sound trigger.
Calling TriggerSetSoundRTPC will set the Wwise real-time parameter control _Name to _Value on _Trigger.
If _Recursive is set to 1, this will also be done for other sound triggers that are contained within _Trigger.

TransferItemsToParty.description:
Transfers all story items in _Character's inventory to another character of their party.
Further InformationThis is mainly used when dismissing companions. bullet points if needed.

TimerUnpause.description:
A call to make an existing _Timer end the current pause applied to it. Will only work if a timer has already been launched, then paused.
Further InformationThis applies to any existing timer, whether Object, Realtime or Global.
TimerUnpause.examples:
IF
LeftTrigger(_Player, _Trigger)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
DB_SafeZone(_Trigger)
THEN
TimerUnpause((STRING)_Timer)

TriggerResetAtmosphere.description:
Resets the atmosphere trigger to its default atmosphere, listed as first one in its Atmospheres sidebar property.
Further InformationFor call to take an effect, the default atmosphere of atmosphere trigger can be overridden with TriggerSetAtmosphere.

MoveWeaponsToContainer.description:
Moves the weapons currently equipped by a character to a container
Parameters:
_Character: The character whose weapons are moved.
_Container: The container to which weapons are moved.

TransformSurfaceAtPosition.description:
Subject any surfaces in the surface layer _TransformLayer at the _X, _Y, _Z coordinates to _TransformType, out to a distance of _Radius meters where applicable. The lifetime and owner of the resulting surface are given by _Lifetime and _Owner.
Further Information_TransformType can be one of the following strings:
"Ignite"
"Douse"
"Electrify"
"Deelectrify"
"Freeze"
"Melt"
"Vaporize"
"Condense"
"DestroyWater"
"Clear"
"Daylight"
"TurnHellfire"
"UnturnHellfire"
"DestroyBlood"
"SuperHeat"
"Bloodify"_TransformLayer should either be “Ground“ or “Cloud“ or “All“ for whether this is a surface on the ground or a cloud surface floating over the ground layer.

UnblockFlee.description:
Unblocks a character from fleeing.
Parameters:
_Character: Character no longer blocked from fleeing.
UnblockFlee.examples:
IF
DB_CombatFlee_BlockedFor(_Player)
AND
NOT DB_InCamp(_Player)
AND
NOT DB_InDangerZone(_Player,_)
AND
DB_CurrentLevel(_Level)
AND
NOT DB_CombatFlee_BlockedInLevel(_Level)
THEN
UnblockFlee(_Player);
NOT DB_CombatFlee_BlockedFor(_Player);

StartRespec.description:
A call that brings up the character customization screen for a respec (To change your class/skills/abilities).
Parameters:
_Target the target to be respecced.

TransferItemsToCharacter.description:
Transfers all story items in _Character's inventory to _ToCharacter.
Further InformationThis is mainly used when dismissing companions.

AddTadpolePower.description:
Acquire the specified Tadpole Power _Power for the specified _Character.
Further InformationIgnorePrequisites - whether or not to ignore the specified requirements for each Tadpole Power (e.g. to get Tadpole Power 2 you must have Tadpole Power 1.)

MoveItemTo.description:
Makes the _Character move a set _Amount of the declared _Item to the _Target specified.
The specified _Event being returned once it is called.

StopFollow.description: Stop _Follower from following another character, if it currently was following anyone.

Unequip.description: Makes the _Character unequip _Item, if the _Character has it equipped.
Unequip.examples:
PROC
PROC_RemoveWeapon()
AND
GetEquippedWeapon((CHARACTER)_Character, _Weapon)
THEN
Unequip((CHARACTER)_Character, _Weapon);
Unequip.seeAlso:
Equip
Equipped
IsEquipable
Unequipped
EquipFailed
UnequipFailed
TemplateEquipped
TemplateUnequipped

UseSpell.description:
Makes a character use a spell or skill on a target.
Parameters:
_Character: caster of the spell.
_SpellID: name of the spell including its spell category (Category_SpellName).
_Target: target to which the spell aims. Can also be the caster.
_Target2 (optional): any additional targets. If not specified it will be NULL by default.
_WithoutMove (optional): set to 1 for the caster to not move if needed to be in range. Set to 0 by default.Further InformationCasting a spell from Osiris will always ignore any pre-conditions, like if the character does not have access to it by default or if they don’t have any resources to cast it.
UseSpell.seeAlso:
AddSpell
RemoveSpell
UseSpellAtPosition
CastedSpell
CastSpell
CastSpellFailed
LearnedSpell
StartedPreviewingSpell
UsingSpell
UsingSpellAtPosition
UsingSpellInTrigger
UsingSpellOnTarget
CanLearnSpell
CanShowSpellForCharacter
GetSpellFromSet
HasSpell
IsSpellActive
SpellHasSpellFlag

TogglePassive.description: Toggles the _Entity's specific passive to turn on and off based on the inputted _PassiveID.
TogglePassive.examples:
IF
EnteredCombat(_FlamingSphere, _)
AND
DB_GLO_Spells_FlamingSphere_AuraToggled((CHARACTER)_FlamingSphere)
AND
DB_GLO_Spells_FlamingSpheres(_FlamingSphere, _StatusAura)
AND
HasActiveStatus(_FlamingSphere, _StatusAura, 0)
AND
DB_GLO_Spells_FlamingSphere_Auras(_StatusAura, _Passive)
THEN
TogglePassive(_FlamingSphere, _Passive);
NOT DB_GLO_Spells_FlamingSphere_AuraToggled(_FlamingSphere);

TriggerSetAtmosphere.description:
Changes the atmosphere in atmosphere trigger _Trigger to the atmosphere resource with UUID _AtmosphereUUID.
Further InformationAs mentioned above, the UUID must be the UUID of a resource, not of a root template. Additionally, the _Trigger must be an atmosphere trigger and not any other kind of trigger.
The atmosphere you specify must have been added as one of the possible atmospheres that can be used in that atmosphere trigger via the "Atmospheres" property of the sidebar. Afterwards, you must also render all light probes again.
If the atmosphere changes abruptly, make the fading time of the atmosphere trigger longer. This means that the exposure of the HDR can't keep up. It's kind of like pointing a video camera from a dark room out into a sunlit street - it'll take a bit of time to adjust.

TriggerSetItemsOwner.description: Sets _Owner as current owner for all items inside of _AreaTrigger.

NotifyCharacterCreationFinished.description:
Deprecated. This call does nothing now.
Further InformationThis is used in BG3 and is part of the old code base. It used to serve the purpose of putting players into a formation. In code, this call just immediately return true with no effect.

Use.description:
This makes the defined _Character use the _Item specified, with the specified _Event being returned once it is called.
Further Information_IsInteraction is for item interaction animations while _UseItem is for whether or not the item should actually be used. 
I.e., you can have an item interaction animation that just plays an animation (_IsInteraction == 1, UseItem == 0), or one that plays the animation but also gives the same effect as a normal use of the item (_IsInteraction == 1, UseItem == 1). The default if you don't specify either is IsInteraction == 0, _UseItem == 1.
It is advised to use the shorter definition provided below if you are not planning on using the functionalities mentioned above.
Use.examples:
IF
AddedTo(_Item,_ScoutCaptive, _)
AND
Exists(_Item,1)
AND
IsTagged((ITEM)_Item,_HEALING_POTION,1)
THEN
SetFlag(_GoblinScouts_Event_SavedCaptive,NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000);
Use(_ScoutCaptive,_Item,1,1,"");

AttachToPartyGroup.description:
Chain player character _Attaching to _ToCharacter, so that they will follow each other when the player moves one of them.
Further InformationWill fail if either _Attaching or _ToCharacter are not in a party, or if the characters belong to different users.
AttachToPartyGroup.examples:
PROC
PROC_UND_EbonLake_Raft_ChainParty()
AND
DB_UND_EbonLake_Raft_Captain(_Captain)
AND
IsInTrigger(_Captain, _LakeEncounterTrigger, 0)
AND
DB_UND_EbonLake_Raft_Crew(_Player)
AND
_Player != _Captain
THEN
AttachToPartyGroup(_Player, _Captain);

TriggerUnregisterForCharacter.description:
Stops EnteredTrigger and LeftTrigger events from being thrown whenever _Character enters/leaves _Trigger.
Further InformationIf you unregister a trigger for a character while the character was inside the trigger and the trigger was previously registered for that character, a LeftTrigger event will be thrown.
Event triggers are automatically registered for all characters (and items) by the engine and should not be manually registered/unregistered.

PlayAnimation.description:
Plays the animation with _Animation short name on _SourceObject. Once the animation has finished playing, _Event will be thrown as an Entity Event.
Further InformationTo access a specific animation, it needs to have a Short Name. Find all animations with a Short Name in the Short Names List Viewer.

Unlock.description:
Unlock an item (typically a door or container).
Further InformationYou can optionally add a character to the call as the source of the unlock. This can be useful if you intend to react to the unlock with an event like Unlocked.
Unlock.examples:
PROC
PROC_UnlockDoor()
THEN
Unlock((ITEM)_Door, (CHARACTER)_Guard);

PROC
PROC_UnlockOtherDoor()
THEN
Unlock((ITEM)_OtherDoor);

Pickup.description:
Makes character _Character pick up item _Item by starting an Osiris task. Sends an entity event _Event when the pickup is successful, unless _Event is an empty string ““. If _ForcePickUpOnFailure is true, the item will be forced into the character’s inventory should the request for the character to manually go pick it up fail.
Further InformationA pickup Osiris task on a character can be cancelled with PurgeOsirisQueue.

OpenReferencedBookUI.description:
Calling OpenReferencedBookUI will show the book interface with TranslatedStringKey _Bookname to player _Character, as if they read it using book item _BookGuid
Further Information_Bookname is the Book ID set in the Use action - Book on an item. The string is defined in the TranslatedStringKey editor.
OpenReferencedBookUI.examples:
IF
UseFinished(_Player, _Item, _)
AND
DB_AvailableDynamicLetters(_ID, _Item)
THEN
OpenReferencedBookUI(_Player, _ID, _Item);

ToInventory.description:
Transfers _Amount instances of _Object to _TargetObject. _TargetObject can either be a character or a container object. This item, or stack of items, must already exist in the game world. You cannot specify a root template as _Object (use call TemplateAddTo instead in that case).
If _ClearOriginalOwner is 1, then the original owner of the item will be reset and hence the item will never be considered to be stolen. If it is 0, then the original owner (if any) will remain set.
ToInventory.fullDefinitions:
call ToInventory((GUIDSTRING)_Object, (GUIDSTRING)_TargetObject) 
call ToInventory((GUIDSTRING)_Object, (GUIDSTRING)_TargetObject, (INTEGER)_Amount) 
call ToInventory((GUIDSTRING)_Object, (GUIDSTRING)_TargetObject, (INTEGER)_Amount, (INTEGER)_ShowNotification) 
call ToInventory((GUIDSTRING)_Object, (GUIDSTRING)_TargetObject, (INTEGER)_Amount, (INTEGER)_ShowNotification, (INTEGER)_ClearOriginalOwner)
ToInventory.examples:
IF
EntityEvent(_Item, "OmeluumInventory")
AND
IsItem(_Item, 1)
THEN
ToInventory(_Item, (ITEM)_ConfiscatedItemsBag, 1, 0, 1);

MusicPlayOnCharacter.description:
Plays music for a specific _Character. The sound is 3D and is affected by position.
Further InformationFor non 3D sound use call MusicPlayForPeer.

PartyAddGold.description:
Divides the gold amount equally among the party members of _Character.
Further InformationIf gold amount is negative it removes gold, and it tries to divide the removed quantity equally as well if it can.
Please note, this call doesn’t take into consideration whether player’s inventories are locked or not.
It is recommended you use AddGoldToMagicPockets instead, which takes locked inventories into account.
PartyAddGold.examples:
IF
TextEvent("ShareGold")
AND
GetHostCharacter(_Player)
THEN
PartyAddGold(_Player, 1000);

PlayEffectAtPosition.description: Plays a VFX _FxName scaled to _Scale, at the position _X, _Y, _Z

PlatformSetOnStage.description:
Sets the _Platform to be on stage with the _Bool parameter.
_Bool:
0: _Platform is not on stage (off stage).
1: _Platform is on stage.

NotifyCharacterCreationSkipped.description:
Deprecated. This call does nothing now.
Further InformationThis is used in BG3 and is part of the old code base. It used to serve the purpose of putting players into a formation. In code, this call just immediately return true with no effect.

UseSpellAtPosition.description:
Makes a character use a spell or skill aimed at a specific position.
Parameters:
_Caster: character casting of the spell.
_SpellID: name of the spell including its spell category (Category_SpellName).
_X, _Y, _Z: coordinates in which the spell will land.
_WithoutMove (optional): set to 1 for the caster to not move if needed to be in range. Set to 0 by default.Further InformationAs in UseSpell, casting a spell from Osiris will always ignore any pre-conditions.
UseSpellAtPosition.examples:
IF
StatusApplied(_Bow, "DIPPED_FIRE", _, _)
AND
DB_OnlyOnce("FuseLight")
AND
GetEquippedWeapon(_ZhentDungeon_Zhent02, (ITEM)_Bow)
AND
GetPosition(_ZhentDungeon_Fuse_013, _X, _Y, _Z)
THEN
PROC_PLA_ZhentDungeon_ExplosivesTriggered();
UseSpellAtPosition(_ZhentDungeon_Zhent02, "Projectile_MainHandAttack", _X, _Y, _Z);
NOT DB_OnlyOnce("FuseLight");
UseSpellAtPosition.seeAlso:
UseSpell
AddSpell
RemoveSpell
CastedSpell
CastSpell
CastSpellFailed
LearnedSpell
StartedPreviewingSpell
UsingSpell
UsingSpellAtPosition
UsingSpellInTrigger
UsingSpellOnTarget
CanLearnSpell
CanShowSpellForCharacter
GetSpellFromSet
HasSpell
IsSpellActive
SpellHasSpellFlag

TriggerRegisterForPlayers.description:
Registers the _Trigger for all current player characters, so that that whenever one of them enters or leaves the _Trigger, a respective EnteredTrigger or LeftTrigger event will be thrown for this trigger/character combination.
Further InformationEvent triggers are automatically registered for all characters by the engine and can not be unregistered.

ObjectSetTitle.description: Set the title of _Object to _LocalizedTextKey that is defined in the Translated Strings Editor.

PlatformMoveOnSpline.description:
Moves the _Platform along a specified spline set by the _Spline parameter. The start and end of the spline path to follow is can be set by inputting the desired starting and ending indexes of the spline into _StartNodeId and _EndNodeId.
The speed of the movement can be set in the _Speed parameter.
The movement of the platform can also be set to ignore turn based rules with the _IgnoreTurnbased parameter with the values of 0 for false and 1 for true.
The event ID for this movement can also be captured through _EventId.
Further InformationWorks like PlatformMoveTo except moves along a spline rather than just moving straight to a set position.
PlatformMoveOnSpline.examples:
IF
EntityEvent(_Student, "CRE_YouthTraining_StudentReachBoy")
AND
GetPosition(_Student, _X, _Y, _Z)
AND
RealSum(_Y, 1.3, _NewY)
AND
RealSum(_X, 0.5, _NewX)
AND
RealSum(_Z, 0.2, _NewZ)
THEN
PlatformMoveOnSpline(_CRE_FloatingBed, _CRE_FloatingBed_PathToBoy, 0, 5, 3.0, "FloatingBed_ArrivedNearBoy", 0);

PlayLoopingAnimation.description:
Plays a looping animation on _SourceObject.
Parameters:
_StartAnimation plays once, then the _LoopAnimation which loops indefinitely. Once StopAnimation is called, the _EndAnimation is played.
_LoopVariations  can be added if you want a different animation variations to be played each time the call is made.
_StartAnimation, _EndAnimation , and  _LoopVariations  are optional and can be NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000

PurgeOsirisQueue.description:
This call will remove the currently executing Osiris task and all queued Osiris tasks for _Character instantly as if they had never been queued in the first place. If _SendPurgedEvent is 1, then a QueuePurged event will be sent after the queue has been purged (regardless of whether there were any active or queued tasks).
Further InformationIf _SendPurgedEvent is not specified, it defaults to 1.
This will only purge tasks queued from Osiris. It has no effect on tasks started from behaviourScript nor Anubis.
If you wish to immediately complete the queued actions instead of removing them, use FlushOsirisQueue instead
In general, do pass 1 to _SendPurgeEvent. If you don't, generic scripting systems that keep track of queued tasks will become out of sync.

OpenMessageBox.description: Opens a message box _Message for the specified _Character.

RealtimeObjectTimerCancel.description:
A call to make an existing _Timer stop and end that was set on a particular _Object, that specifically runs in Real Time only and ignores Turn Based Mode. Will only work if a timer has already been launched.
Further InformationFor Global timers, instead use TimerCancel.
For timers that need to factor in Turn Based Mode, instead use ObjectTimerCancel.

PushUnsheathedState.description:
Sets a character to a specific unsheathed state.
Parameters:
_Character: The character whose state is changed
_EState: The unsheathe state, from the UNSHEATHSTATE enum.Further InformationThis has higher priority than repose.
PushUnsheathedState.examples:
IF
CharacterReservedUserIDChanged(_Character, _OldUser, _NewUser)
AND
DB_Camp_ForceSheathedWeaponsCharacter_Controlled(_Character, _OldUser)
AND
IsControlled(_Character, 0)
THEN
NOT DB_Camp_ForceSheathedWeaponsCharacter_Controlled(_Character, _OldUser);
PushUnsheathedState(_Character, UNSHEATHSTATE.Sheathed);

QuestMessageShow.description:
Shows the Quest Message.
Parameters:
_MessageId - string ID that should be used later to interact with the quest message.
_LocalizedTextKey - string key for a localized text

PlatformMoveTo.description:
Moves the _Platform to the location specified in the _TargetX, _TargetY and _TargetZ parameters.
The speed of the movement can be set in the _Speed parameter.
The movement of the platform can also be set to ignore turn based rules with the _IgnoreTurnbased parameter with the values of 0 for false and 1 for true.
The event ID for this movement can also be captured through _EventId.
Further Informationcall PlatformMoveOnSpline works much like this call, but instead allows the platform to move along a set path made with a spline rather than directly moving toward a position automatically.
PlatformMoveTo.examples:
IF
UseStarted(_Player, _ElevatorLever)
AND
DB_DEN_General_ElevatorLevers(_ElevatorLever)
AND
DB_DEN_ElevatorPosition(_IsDown)
AND
DB_DEN_ElevatorPoints(_IsDown, _Point)
AND
GetPosition(_Point,_X,_Y,_Z)
THEN
DB_DEN_General_ElevatorIsMoving(1);
PlatformMoveTo(_DEN_ElevatorPLT, _X, _Y, _Z, 3.0, "DEN_Elevation_Moved", 0);

RealtimeObjectTimerLaunch.description:
A call to make when you want a _Timer to activate related to a particular _Object, such as if you wish for an object to do something after the player tries to use it. This particular one ignores Turn Based Mode and instead only runs in Real Time. The time for this in Osiris always set in milliseconds.
Further InformationFor Global timers, instead use call TimerLaunch.
There is also a variant in ObjectTimerLaunch  which works similarly, but will also make the timer listen for Turn Based Mode and calculate the remaining time based on that.

RemoveSummons.description:
Removes all summons of the character.
_Character: The character whose summons need to disappear.
_Die: If set to 1, the summons will die rather than just stop existing.

RemoveSpell.description:
Removes a spell or skill from a character.
Parameters:
_Character: character that will get the spell.
_Skill: name of the spell.
_RemoveContainerSpells (optional): if _Spell is a container spell, all the spells part of this container will also be removed from the character. Set by default to 1.Further InformationSpells can be found in the stats editor under "ModName"/SpellData/"SpellType" and new ones can be created. Do not forget to add the type of spell at the start of the name, if you have the spell Scream in the Shout category you have to call it with Shout_Scream, with _ in between.
Note: Even if the call has the spell word in it, spells are not only magical actions, but other things like shove, jump, and attacking with a weapon.
RemoveSpell.examples:
IF
FlagCleared((FLAG)_State_GivenSlayerForm, _, _)
AND
DB_ORI_DarkUrge((CHARACTER)_DarkUrge)
AND
HasSpell(_DarkUrge, "Shout_DarkUrge_Slayer", 1)
THEN
RemoveSpell(_DarkUrge, "Shout_DarkUrge_Slayer", 1);
RemoveSpell.seeAlso:
UseSpell
AddSpell
UseSpellAtPosition
CastedSpell
CastSpell
CastSpellFailed
LearnedSpell
StartedPreviewingSpell
UsingSpell
UsingSpellAtPosition
UsingSpellInTrigger
UsingSpellOnTarget
CanLearnSpell
CanShowSpellForCharacter
GetSpellFromSet
HasSpell
IsSpellActive
SpellHasSpellFlag

ReadyCheckGlobal.description:
Allows you to request a ready check for all connected users.
_EventId - A custom event id that will be used in the callback for when it succeeds of fails.
_TranslationId - Which translated string should be used for the ready check. You can find these in the TranslatedStringKey Editor under ReadyChecks_Descriptions.
_Force - If the ready check should ignore any conditional checks or not.
Ignores: osiris call SetReadyCheckBlocked and characters in dialog
_Initiator - Who initiated the ready check.

PlayEffect.description:
Plays a VFX _FxName scaled to _Scale, at the specified _Bone of an _Object
Further InformationIf no bone is specified, the default bone is used.

RemoveGoldFromMagicPockets.description:
Remove _Gold from magic pockets _Source they have access to, and it tries to divide the removed quantity equally if it can. If negative amount is passed in, it adds that much gold.
Further InformationThis includes Camp Chest.

RemoveHarmfulStatuses.description: Removes all "harmful" statuses from _Target. Harmful statuses are those that have the "initiate combat" status flag.
RemoveHarmfulStatuses.seeAlso:
ApplyStatus
RemoveStatus
StatusApplied
StatusAttempt
StatusAttemptFailed
StatusRemoved
HasActiveStatus

QuestMessageHide.examples:
IF
FlagSet(_State_PartyMemberIsMindFlayer, _, _)
AND
DB_TadpoleReminderActive(1)
AND
DB_CrownTurn(_TurnCount)
AND
_TurnCount >= 4
THEN
NOT DB_TadpoleReminderActive(1);
QuestMessageHide("UseSupremeTadpoleID");

RequestAdvanceDay.description:
This is for internal use only.
Part of a complex handshake between code and story for the camp and long rest pipeline.

RemovePreferredAiTargetTag.description: This call removes the AI target score for this tag that was set with AddPreferredAiTargetTag.

RequestActiveRollVersusSkill.description:
This call triggers an active skill check on a chosen character against another entity’s skill roll: meaning, that entity’s skill check result becomes the difficulty of a roll.
Parameters are similar to RequestActiveRoll, but here _RollSubject is going to make a roll of their own, using skill _SubjectSkillOrAbility and advantage _SubjectAdvantage.

RemoveSplatters.description:
Remove all splatters from target Entity’s visuals.
Parameters:
_Entity: The entity you are removing splatters from.Further InformationSplatters are visual additions to entities that happen whenever they get into contact with mud, blood, sweat, etc.
They are usually added trough statuses and boosts.
This call will not remove bruises, since those are directly link to HP.
RemoveSplatters.examples:
PROC
PROC_TakeBath(_Player)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
THEN
RemoveSplatters(_Player);

RemoveAllPartyFollowers.description:
Remove all party followers from player-controlled character _Character.
Further InformationThe non player characters will resume their behaviors, and regain their relationships as before they were a party follower.
Removing party followers with call RemoveAllPartyFollowers will result in CharacterLeftParty being fired for every character that has been removed from the party.
For more information on party followers see call AddPartyFollower.
RemoveAllPartyFollowers.examples:
At the end of the game, all party followers are removed from all players.
PROC
PROC_DominationCompleted((CHARACTER)_Character)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
THEN
RemoveAllPartyFollowers(_Player);

RequestEndTheDay.description:
This is for internal use only.
Part of a complex handshake between code and story for the camp and long rest pipeline.

RemovePartyFollower.description:
Remove _Follower as a party follower of player-controlled character _Leader.
Further InformationThe non player character will resume its behaviors, and regain its relationships as before it was a party follower.
Removing a party follower with call RemovePartyFollower will result in event CharacterLeftParty.
For more information on party followers see call AddPartyFollower.

RegisterAsCompanion.description:
Tell code that the player has encountered the companion _Character and recruited them by either including them in the party of _Recruiter, or by sending them to camp.
Further Informationif _Character is an NPC, this makes them get long rest effects when a long rest is performed.
if _Recruiter is a player character and _Character is a party character that cannot be manually reassigned to a different user (summon, party follower), then whenever _Recruiter is assigned to a different user, _Character will also be reassigned to that same user.

RequestGatherAtCamp.description:
This is for internal use only.
Part of a complex handshake between code and story for the camp and long rest pipeline.

RemoveTrackedSoundEntity.description: Calling RemoveTrackedSoundEntity will stop tracking _Object as a tracked sound entity.

RequestLongRestConfirmed.description: Confirms to code to proceed with the long rest after RequestLongRest has been called and returns LongRestStarted().

RequestLongRestWaiting.description:
This is for internal use only.
Part of a complex handshake between code and story for the camp and long rest pipeline.

RequestInitialLevel.description: Sets the starting level for a character, allowing to manually level up towards the party level.

RemoveIllustrationFromCustomBook.description: Calling RemoveIllustrationFromCustomBook removes illustration _Entryname from book _Bookname
RemoveIllustrationFromCustomBook.examples:
PROC
PROC_Gazette_RemoveHeadline((STRING)_IssueToBuild)
AND
DB_Gazette_RemoveHeadline((STRING)_OldHeadline)
AND
DB_Gazette_Illustrations(_OldHeadline, _Image)
THEN
RemoveEntryFromCustomBook(_IssueToBuild, _OldHeadline);
RemoveIllustrationFromCustomBook (_IssueToBuild, _Image);

ReadyCheckSpecific.description:
Allows you to request a ready check for specific characters (which will use their users).
_EventId - A custom event id that will be used in the callback for when it succeeds of fails.
_TranslationId - Which translated string should be used for the ready check. You can find these in the TranslatedStringKey Editor under ReadyChecks_Descriptions.
_Force - If the ready check should ignore any conditional checks or not.
Ignores: SetReadyCheckBlocked and characters in dialog
_Initiator - Who initiated the ready check.
_Character1 - Optional additional character.
_Character2 - See above.
_Character3 - See above.

RequestPassiveRoll.description:
This call triggers a background skill check roll on a chosen character, that will give a fail or success result shortly after in RollResult event.
Parameters:_Roller — The character whose skills are going to be used
_RollSubject— The object about which the roll is
_RollType— The type of a skill check
In BG3 it’s "RawAbility", "SkillCheck" or "SavingThrow"
_SkillOrAbility — The skill or ability that is going to be used in the roll
Examples for BG3: "Constitution", "Perception", "Performance"
_DifficultyClassID — The difficulty of a roll, using a DifficultyClass object from UUID table
_RollerAdvantage — Does the character have an advantage for this roll or not.
1 - Has Advantage; 0 - Doesn’t have Advantage
_Event — A string for an event that is called when the roll is done.
You should catch a RollResult event, using this string.Further InformationThere’s an older call that doesn’t have _RollType or _RollerAdvantage
Those are "RawAbility" and 0 by default.
If you want to roll a passive skill check against someone else’s skill (e.g. D&D-style “Stealth” against “Perception”), use RequestPassiveRollVersusSkill.
RequestPassiveRoll.examples:
IF
EnteredTrigger(_Player, (TRIGGER)_PlagueShip_PlagueTrigger)
AND
NOT DB_PlagueShip_HadSavingThrow(_Player)
THEN
DB_PlagueShip_HadSavingThrow(_Player);
RequestPassiveRoll(_Player, _Player, "SavingThrow", "Constitution", (DIFFICULTYCLASS)_Act3_Hard, 0, "PlagueShip_PlagueRoll");

RemoveSurfaceLayer.description:
Removes ground or cloud surface specified by _SurfaceLayer at a set radius of _Radius at a target location of _Target.
Further Information_SurfaceLayer: "Ground" or "Cloud"
_Radius: Radius size in meters for cleanup

RequestRespec.description:
This call will Respec the character, allowing players to build the character from scratch.
Further InformationAfter using this call on a character, a “Level up” button will appear under the character’s portrait to start levelling up from scratch.
Use only on companions; it is not meant to be used on NPCs or Followers.
Note: this call can break the character’s portrait if the level you’re using doesn’t have the proper setup to create the character’s portraits.
RequestRespec.examples:
IF
TextEvent("Respec")
AND
GetHostCharacter(_MainCharacter)
THEN
RequestRespec(_MainCharacter);

RequestProcessed.description:
Response to RequestCanCombine, RequestCanDisarmTrap, RequestCanLockpick, RequestCanMove, RequestCanPickup or RequestCanUse events that are sent from code. _Character is the character and _RequestID is the identifier of the request, supplied by the relevant event. If _Result is 0, the action attempt is blocked. Otherwise, the action passes and continues.
The above requests time out and continue after 1 second if Osiris does not reply.
For RequestCanLoot, use LootRequestProcessed
RequestProcessed.seeAlso:
RequestCanCombine
RequestCanDisarmTrap
RequestCanLockpick
RequestCanMove
RequestCanPickup
RequestCanLoot
LootRequestProcessed

Resurrect.description:
Resurrects the _Character with a small amount of health.
A _ResurrectAnimationOverride can be applied, and XP can be reset or not.

RemoveSurfaceLayerAtPosition.description:
Removes ground or cloud surface specified by _SurfaceLayer at a set radius of _Radius at a target position of the inputted _X, _Y, _Z coordinates.
Further Information_SurfaceLayer: "Ground" or "Cloud"
_Radius: Radius size in meters for cleanup.
Works pretty much like RemoveSurfaceLayer except it takes specific coordinates for the position rather than an entity.

RequestActiveRoll.description:
This call triggers an active skill check roll on a chosen character, opening a roll window. After finishing the roll, it will send a RollResult event.
Parameters:
_Roller — The character whose skills are going to be used
_RollSubject— The object about which the roll is
_RollType— The type of a skill check
In BG3 it’s "RawAbility" or "SkillCheck"
_SkillOrAbility — The skill or ability that is going to be used in the roll
Examples for BG3: "Strength", "SleightOfHand", "Arcana"
_DifficultyClassID — The difficulty of a roll, using a DifficultyClass object from UUID table
_RollerAdvantage — Does the character have an advantage for this roll or not.
1 - Has Advantage; 0 - Doesn’t have Advantage
_Event — A string for an event that is called when the roll is done.
You should catch a RollResult event, using this string.Further InformationThere’s an older call that doesn’t have _RollType or _RollerAdvantage
Those are "RawAbility" and 0 by default.
If you want to roll a passive skill check against someone else’s skill (e.g. D&D-style “Athletics” against other character’s “Athletics”), use RequestActiveRollVersusSkill.

RequestAutoLevel.description:
Automatically level ups the character to the party’s level.
Chooses the default options when level up.
Further InformationThis call also can be used for Level 1 characters and makes sure the spells are prepared.

ScreenFadeTo.description: A call used to play a Fade to Black on the selected character, over a set amount of time.

SetCanExamine.description:
Used to enable or disable the examine interaction with _Item.
Parameters
_Item: The item which should be examinable or not
_Bool: If set to 0, examine will be disabled, otherwise it will be enabled
SetCanExamine.examples:
IF
DB_CurrentLevel(_Level)
AND
DB_MagicMirrorInstance(_Level,_MagicMirror)
THEN
SetCanExamine(_MagicMirror,0);
NOT DB_MagicMirrorInstance(_Level,_MagicMirror);

RequestModalTutorial.description:
Deprecated. Do not use.
Superseded by ShowTutorial. This call existed in DOS2 and Early Access version of BG3. It prompted a tutorial window which requires player confirmation to disappear (aka OK button). This type of tutorial still exists but is defined in data differently and is triggered using the superseding call.

SetCanFight.description:
Sets whether _Entity can fight in combats (get a turn in combat) depending on whether _Enabled is 0 (no) or 1 (yes).
Further InformationFighting in a combat means performing actions to heal/hurt other participants in the combat. Controlling whether or not a character/item will enter the turn-based mode of combats happens using SetCanJoinCombat.
Disabling this for NPCs means that they will not perform any actions when it is their turn in combat. Note that this can easily wreak havoc due to interactions with other systems, such as the crime system.
If CanFight is disabled for all enemies in combat, the combat will end immediately. Attacking a single, non-allied enemy whose CanFight is disabled may therefore start and immediately end a combat.
If you disable this for a player during a scripted event, ensure it is re-enabled in all possible exits!
If you have an entity that is marked as Inspector in the editor side bar, this normally means that it will join nearby combats (mainly so that its statuses switch to turn-based mode), but it will never initiate combat nor keep a combat ongoing by itself. The CanFight property in the editor sidebar, and hence also this call, can override that: if an entity can fight, then by definition it is no longer an inspector and hence it will keep a combat ongoing (as long as it has enemies in the combat, and can join combat, call SetCanJoinCombat).

RemoveStatus.description:
Remove the status with the name _Status to _Target, caused by _Cause.
_Target can be a CHARACTER or ITEM.
_Status is a string from any StatusData table in the Stats editor.
_Cause specifies the object, either a CHARACTER or ITEM, that the game should consider as the cause of this status removal. This is mainly useful for scripted skill applications where you want _Target to react as if _Cause removed this status.
You can pass NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 if there is no specific cause.Further InformationThe overload without the _Cause will default that parameter to NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000.
After using this call in a character with the _Status, StatusRemoved will be thrown once the status is properly removed from _Target.
If you want to remove generic statuses like Invisibility, you should use RemoveStatusesWithGroup.
RemoveStatus.seeAlso:
ApplyStatus
RemoveHarmfulStatuses
StatusApplied
StatusAttempt
StatusAttemptFailed
StatusRemoved
HasActiveStatus

SetCanOpenDoorsOverride.description:
Sets if the _Character can open doors.
If the _Integer is set to 1, the _Character can open doors.
If the _Integer is set to 0, the _Character cannot open doors.

ResetRelation.description:
Removes all dynamic custom relations defined from _Faction1 towards _Faction2:
Temporary hostile relations
Permanent relations defined from scriptThis means the relation from _Faction1 to _Faction2 will fall back to the relation defined in the faction editor, and in the absence of those, for their parent faction (and neutral otherwise).
Further InformationThis call resets the relations only in one way.
You can define new relations via SetRelation and SetRelationTemporaryHostile afterwards.
ResetRelation.examples:
PROC
PROC_Hagmasks_ClearNeutral((CHARACTER)_Character)
AND
DB_Hagmasks_Neutral(_Character, _Faction)
THEN
ResetRelation((FACTION)_MaskedVictims, _Faction);
ResetRelation(_Faction, (FACTION)_MaskedVictims);

SetCanBePickpocketed.description:
Enable or disable whether a particular entity can be pickpocketed.
Further InformationThe default state is what is set using the Can Be Pickpocketed property in the editor side bar
Disabling it means that the entity will never show up in the pickpocket UI.
SetCanBePickpocketed.examples:
// Disable pickpocketing of the gauntlet
SetCanBePickpocketed(_Gauntlet, 0);

ResetIsTradable.description:
Sets the IsTradableType of an object or _Entity back to its default state.
Further InformationMore information on the different tradable states an object or _Entity can be set to can be found here SetIsTradable.
ResetIsTradable.fullDefinitions:
ResetIsTradable((GUIDSTRING)_Entity)
ExamplePROC
PROC_Prelude_PlayerPickedUpStone((ITEM)_Stone, (CHARACTER)_Player, _)
AND
DB_GlobalFlag(_State_StonesAtBeach)
AND
DB_Prelude_LostStones(_Stone)
THEN
NOT DB_Prelude_LostStones(_Stone);
ResetIsTradable(_Stone);
PROC_Prelude_CheckAllStonesReceived((CHARACTER)_Player);

RemovePassive.description: Removes the specific passive based on the inputted _PassiveID from the the _Entity.

SetCharacterLootOwned.description:
If _Bool is 1, then if _Character is dead or when it dies, its items keep a red outline, and its corpse also gets a red outline. Additionally, the cursor shows that picking up/looting them is forbidden. If _Bool is 0, they will have a white outline and no "forbidden" cursors.
Further InformationIf not called, the default for a corpse is that its items and corpse are not owned.
This call in itself does not affect whether or not picking up the items/looting the character is a crime. For that, use DB_OwnedCorpse (which also uses this API to communicate the state).
The result of GetOwner is not affected either by this call, and as a result neither are whether CharacterStoleItem and RequestCanLoot events are fired.
SetCharacterLootOwned.examples:
IF
AddedTo(_DeadPerson, (CHARACTER)_InventoryHolder, _)
AND
DB_Players(_InventoryHolder)
THEN
PROC_SetCorpseOwner((CHARACTER)_DeadPerson, _InventoryHolder);
SetCharacterLootOwned(_DeadPerson, 0);

ResetCanBePickpocketed.description: Reset whether a particular entity can be pickpocketed to its setting in the editor’s sidebar, undoing any effects of previous calls to SetCanBePickpocketed.

SetAnimationEvent.description:
Sends a script event _EventName (OnScriptAnimation) to the animation system (Genome) blueprint for _Object.
Further InformationUsed to switch the state of the animation blueprint.

SetCharacterOnPortraitPainting.description: Sets the Player Portrait onto the custom material of an object.
SetCharacterOnPortraitPainting.examples:
PROC
PROC_SetPlayerPortraitToPainting((CHARACTER)_Player)
THEN
AddCustomMaterialOverride(_Painting, "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"); //The UUID of the material resource
SetCharacterOnPortraitPainting(_Player, _Painting);

SetCombatGroupAndEnterCombat.description:
Sets the combat group of the _GroupIDTarget with _GroupID before attempting to start combat between _GroupIDTarget  and _CombatTarget.
Further InformationSimilar to SetHostileAndEnterCombat, it streamlines the start of combat so deferred calls are less likely to cause issues before it attempts to start combat.
Take note of the combat group ID behaviour mentioned in SetCombatGroupID and GetCombatGroupID.
SetCombatGroupAndEnterCombat.examples:
IF
DialogEnded(EXA_Example_PreCombatDialog_XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX, _ID)
AND
DB_DialogPlayers(_ID, _Player, 1)
AND
DB_EXA_Example_Enemy(_Enemy)
THEN
SetCombatGroupAndEnterCombat(_Enemy, "EXA_Example_CombatCrew", _Player);

RequestPing.description:
Request a ping VFX to be placed in the world and on the mini map, similar as if a player had done that.
Parameters:
_X, _Y, _Z: the coordinates at which to ping
_Target: if not NULL, a ping highlight VFX will play on the target rather than only at the absolute coordinates. This target must be a character or item.
_Source: the player character that’s the source of the ping. Can be NULL. If it is NULL, the ping request will not show up in the combat log.Further InformationCalling RequestPing with a NULL _Source can be used to drawing the player’s attention to a particular item/character without spamming the combat log

SetBlockDismiss.description:
Blocks/Unblocks dismiss of a companion/hireling _Character by the Code to make room for a new avatar when a player joins the game.
Further InformationOn BG3 to be dismissible, by design a companion/hireling must be: able to talk, not in a danger or blocked zone.
SetBlockDismiss doesn’t work on avatars and as such doesn’t affect DismissAvatar.
SetBlockDismiss.examples:
IF
DB_Players(_Companion)
AND
NOT DB_Avatars(_Companion)
AND
NOT DB_BlockDismissable(_Companion)
AND
DB_CantTalk(_Companion)
THEN
DB_BlockDismissable(_Companion);
SetBlockDismiss(_Companion,1);

SetDualEntityEventReal.description:
Like SetEntityEventReal but with 2 entity parameters _Object1 and _Object2 .
Further InformationSee SetEntityEventReal for more information.
SetDualEntityEventReal.seeAlso:
EntityEvent
DualEntityEvent
SetEntityEvent
SetDualEntityEvent
SetEntityEventReal
SetEntityEventDialog
SetDualEntityEventDialog
SetEntityEventGUID

SetIsDroppedOnDeath.description: Sets a specific item to either be dropped on death of the holder or to not be dropped on death.
SetIsDroppedOnDeath.examples:
IF
AddedTo(_Entity, _NPC, _)
AND
IsDroppedOnDeath(_Entity, _CurrentBool)
THEN
SetIsDroppedOnDeath(_Entity, _NewBool);

SetCampQuality.description:
This is for internal use only.
Part of the camp internal pipeline.

SetEditionForCustomBook.description:
Calling SetEditionForCustomBook sets _Edition of _Bookname.
This edition will get displayed in the name of the book.

SetDualEntityEvent.description:
Sends the Dual Entity Event _Event with _Object1, _Object2, and _Event parameters.
Further InformationSee SetEntityEvent for more information.
SetDualEntityEvent.seeAlso:
EntityEvent
DualEntityEvent
SetEntityEvent
SetEntityEventReal
SetDualEntityEventReal
SetEntityEventDialog
SetDualEntityEventDialog
SetEntityEventGUID

SetCanPickUp.description:
Enables or disables an item from getting picked up by characters.
Further InformationIf this is set to false, the item may still be moveable, but unable to get added to an inventory. See SetMovable.
If this is called on an item that is currently in an inventory, it will be dropped at the top inventory holder’s position. See Drop and DroppedBy.
This can be queried with GetCanPickUp. Note that this does not get reset after a story reload! You will have to manually revert it to its desired state in the init.

SetIsTradable.description:
Sets if an object or _Entity can or cannot be traded with an NPC.
There are three types of tradable states an object or _Entity can be set to.
TRADABLETYPE.NonTradable (Does not allow the object to be traded)
TRADABLETYPE.Tradable (Allows the item to be traded)
TRADABLETYPE.Default (Defaults the item to its base setting to check if it can be traded or not)An object or _Entity ’s tradable state can also be set in the sidebar in the IsTradableType option.
SetIsTradable.fullDefinitions:
SetIsTradable((GUIDSTRING)_Entity, (TRADABLETYPE)_EIsTradableType)
ExampleIF
AddedTo(_Stone, _BeachSahuagin_Fishmonger, _)
AND
DB_Prelude_LostStones((ITEM)_Stone)
THEN
SetIsTradable(_Stone, TRADABLETYPE.Tradable);

RequestLongRestFinish.description:
This is for internal use only.
Part of a complex handshake between code and story for the camp and long rest pipeline.

SetImmortal.description: Makes character _Character mortal if _Bool is 0 and immortal if _Bool is 1.
SetImmortal.examples:
PROC
PROC_DEN_AttackOnDen_KillKids()
AND
DB_DEN_NPC(_Kid, "Hideout", _)
THEN
SetImmortal(_Kid, 0);

SetOnStage.description:
Depending on whether _Bool is 0 or 1, sets _Object either off-stage or on-stage. _Object can be a character or an item.
By default, all objects are on stage. When an object is set off-stage,
it becomes inactive
it will still receive events
it becomes invisible and cannot be interacted with by anything or anyone using game mechanics (combat, surfaces)
it won't take up room on the AI-grid: other items/characters can occupy the space where the object was located

SetForceUpdate.description:
Reactions from behaviour scripts (for items) and Anubis (for characters and items) are normally only active if at least one player character is "nearby" (currently: within 30 metres). This routine can be used to force _Character's reactions to remain active even if no player characters are nearby (and at the same time also activate all characters/items within a 30 metres radius of themselves), by passing 1 as _Bool. Once the object's behaviours no longer need to remain permanently active, you can make its activity status again depend on a player being nearby by calling it with 0 as _Bool.
This call increases an internal counter tied to the object every time you enable force-updating and decreases it again when you disable it. Force-updating only gets disabled when the counter is at zero (which is its initial value).
Combat participants are automatically force-updated for the duration of the combat.
This call also makes the object active on client, i.e., it will also be visible when moving the camera to its location even if no player is nearby.
Further InformationUse this functionality very sparingly, as it can easily lead to a lot of unnecessary overhead.
SetForceUpdate.examples:
PROC
PROC_Jailbreak_TryForceUpdateGuard((CHARACTER)_Guard)
AND
IsForceUpdate(_Guard, 0)
THEN
DB_Jailbreak_ForceUpdatedGuard(_Guard);
SetForceUpdate(_Guard, 1);

ResumeCombat.examples:
IF
FlagSet(_EXA_Example_Event_ResumeCombat, _, _)
AND
DB_EXA_Example_PausedCombatID(_CombatGUID)
THEN
ResumeCombat(_CombatGUID);
NOT DB_EXA_Example_PausedCombatID(_CombatGUID);

RequestPassiveRollVersusSkill.description:
This call triggers a background skill check roll on a chosen character against another entity’s skill roll.
Parameters are similar to RequestPassiveRoll, but here _RollSubject is going to make a roll of their own, using skill _SubjectSkillOrAbility and advantage _SubjectAdvantage.

SetAmbushing.description: If _IsAmbushing is 1, then hides the outline of _Entity and hides it on the mini-map. Pass 0 as _IsAmbushing to revert these changes.

SetArmourSet.description: Changes the current armor set of character _Character to _EArmorSet armor.
SetArmourSet.examples:
PROC
PROC_SetArmourSet((CHARACTER)_Player, (ARMOURSET)_ArmourSet)
AND
QRY_GetArmourSet(_Player)
AND
DB_QRYRTN_GetArmourSet(_CurrentArmourSet)
AND
_ArmourSet != _CurrentArmourSet
THEN
SetArmourSet(_Player, _ArmourSet);
DB_CachedArmourSet(_Player, _ArmourSet);

RemoveCustomMaterialOverride.description:
Remove a custom material override (like the one applied by AddCustomMaterialOverride). This call gets its data from entries in the ScriptMaterialOverridePresets and ScriptMaterialOverrideParameters UUID object editor tables.
Parameters:
_Object - The target Object you want to remove the material Override from.
_Preset - The UUID of the material to be removed (as a string).
RemoveCustomMaterialOverride.examples:
PROC
PROC_RevelryScene()
AND
DB_PartOfTheTeam(_Player)
AND
HasAppliedStatus(_Player, "PARTIAL_CEREMORPH", 1)
THEN
RemoveStatus(_Player, "PARTIAL_CEREMORPH", _Player);
RemoveCustomMaterialOverride(_Player, "398ca8ae-c3c0-47f5-8e45-d9402e198389");

SetAiHint.description: Sets the _AiHint tag that will be used by the _Target if they are enabled to use Ai Hints.
SetAiHint.examples:
IF
StatusRemoved((CHARACTER)_GateMaster, "OPENGATE_CHANNEL", _, _)
AND
DB_GithyankiGishAIHints(_GateMaster, _Gish)
THEN
SetAiHint(_Gish, NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000);
SetStayInAiHints(_Gish, 0);

SetEndTheDayState.description:
This is for internal use only.
Part of a complex handshake between code and story for the camp and long rest pipeline.

SetGodOverride.description:
Used to override _Character’s deity with a new _God deity.
_Character has to have a deity in the first place otherwise the call will failParameters:
_Character: Character who’s deity should be overridden
Technically accepts any GUIDSTRING but only CHARACTER type can have deities
_God: The new deityFurther InformationThe list of deities can be found in the UUID editor: <Mod>/Gods.

SetJoinBlock.description:
Prevent multiplayer users from joining the game during critical moments.
This block will always be removed after a long rest has completed.
Further Information(JOINBLOCKTYPE)_JoinBlockType: Decides the block behavior:
