JOINBLOCKTYPE.BlockAll will block any multiplayer users from joining the game
JOINBLOCKTYPE.BlockNew will block multiplayer users from creating a new character, users that already have a character are still allowed to join the game.
JOINBLOCKTYPE.None will remove the block
SetJoinBlock.examples:
PROC
PROC_GlobalFlagReactionAfterDialog((FLAG)_PrisonWasEntered)
AND
NOT QRY_Debug_RestoringState()
THEN
SetJoinBlock(JOINBLOCKTYPE.BlockNew);
IF
ReadyCheckPassed("ReturnToCrecheReadyCheck")
THEN
SetJoinBlock(JOINBLOCKTYPE.None);

SetEntityEventGUID.description:
Like SetEntityEvent but with an additional parameter _GuidParam.
Further InformationSee SetEntityEvent for more information.
Event is not sent to Osiris, and must be caught in Anubis/Constellations.
SetEntityEventGUID.seeAlso:
EntityEvent
DualEntityEvent
SetEntityEvent
SetDualEntityEvent
SetEntityEventReal
SetDualEntityEventReal
SetEntityEventDialog
SetDualEntityEventDialog

SetHotbarItem.description:
Adds _Item to _Player’s hotbar.
Parameters:
_Player: The player whose hotbar should receive the item
_Item: The item that should be added to the hotbar

SetOnlyOwnerCanUse.description: If _Bool is 1, the item can only be used by its owner after this call. Otherwise, it can be used by anyone. The owner of an item is the last character to have it in their inventory.

SetStayInAiHints.description:
Sets if the desired _Target should try to stay within AIHintArea Triggers which are tagged with the defined tag in SetAiHint.
Setting _Enabledto 1 will enable this behavior while setting it to 0 will disable it.

SetStackAmount.examples:
IF
DestroyedBy((ITEM)_TadpoleTransport_Nursery, _,_,_)
AND
DB_TadpoleTransport_TadpoleItem(_Item, _Amount)
AND
CreateAtObject((ITEMROOT)_TadpolePowerJar_Broken, _Item, 0, 0, "", 1, _NewItem)
THEN
SetStackAmount((ITEM)_NewItem, _Amount);
Die(_Item, DEATHTYPE.Explode, NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, 0, 0);

SetTrapArmed.description: Set the _Item to Armed if _Bool is 1 or not Armed if _Bool is 0.
SetTrapArmed.examples:
IF
ObjectTimerFinished(_Item,"CRIME_TrapSummoned")
AND
NOT DB_CRIME_SummonedTrap((ITEM)_Item,1)
AND
Exists(_Item,1)
AND
IsDestroyed((ITEM)_Item,0)
AND
GetPosition(_Item,_X,_Y,_Z)
AND
GetOwner(_Item,_Caster)
AND
DB_PartyMembers(_Caster)
AND
CrimeGetNewID(_CrimeID)
THEN
SetCanInteract((ITEM)_Item,0);
SetTrapArmed(_Item,1);
SetTrapDiscovered(_Item,0);

SetPlayersAreDreaming.description:
This is for internal use only.
Part of a complex handshake between code and story for the camp and long rest pipeline.

SetHostileAndEnterCombat.description:
Sets the faction relation between the two factions to hostile then attempts to make the _Source and _Target enter combat with each other.
Further InformationIt is common to have scripted events that result in faction relation changes before entering combat, but faction relation changes are deferred, meaning that it can be common for an EnterCombat call to fail if it’s called in the same frame as the _Source and _Target relation has not changed yet. This is a clean way to request both actions without this conflict.
SetHostileAndEnterCombat.examples:
IF
DialogEnded(EXA_Example_PreCombatDialog_XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX, _ID)
AND
DB_DialogPlayers(_ID, _Player, 1)
AND
GetFaction(S_EXA_Example_TargetBadGuy_XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX, _TargetFaction)
AND
GetFaction(_Player, _PlayerFaction)
THEN
SetHostileAndEnterCombat(_PlayerFaction, _TargetFaction, _Player, S_EXA_Example_TargetBadGuy_XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX);

SetCharacterLootable.description:
Dynamically changes the value of the lootable flag for _Character set using the side bar in the editor. A _Value of “1” sets it to true while a _Value of “0” sets it to false.
Further InformationBeing lootable also depends on being in a valid state, see OpenCharacterLootUI for the details.

SetRelation.description:
Changes the relation between two factions. Valid values for _Relation are:
0: Enemy
50: Neutral
51: Persistent Neutral. This is the same as neutral, but additionally means that if an entity with _SourceFaction joins a combat in which they are hostile to a faction that is allied to _TargetFaction, they will not automatically become hostile to _TargetFaction as well in that combat. Be careful with this setup, as it can result in being in combat without enemies.
100: AlliedFurther InformationA hostile relation does not force the participants to enter combat, unless they can see each other.

SetHitpoints.description:
Sets hit points of _Entity to the _HP absolute value, but not more than entity’s hit points maximum, and not less than zero.
Further InformationIf it increases the hit points of the entity, it will be considered as healing. In that case The _HealingTypes can be specified as following:
“Guaranteed” (default value) - heals target of any type,
“Living” - will not heal undead or constructs,
“Construct” - will not heal undead or living,
“Undead” - will not heal constructs or living.
Setting _HP to zero may kill/destroy the target entity (if it can be killed/destroyed).

SetSubRegionName.description:
Sets the subregion in which the character now is, for purposes of savegames, minimap header, etc.
Parameters:
_Character: Character whose subregion to update
_SubregionID: Translated key string name of the subregion’s name.
_ShowNotification: If 1, this will cause a banner with the name to appear.Further InformationDoes nothing for NPC’s because it doesn’t matter in what subregion they are.
SetSubRegionName.examples:
PROC
PROC_Subregion_Entered(_Character, _Trigger)
AND
DB_SubregionMarker(_Trigger, _Message, _Marker, _)
THEN
SetSubRegionName(_Character, _Message, 1);

SetOwner.description:
Changes the owner of _Item. If the item is added to a character's inventory, its owner is set to that character.
Further InformationIf a character dies and they are the owner of an item, the owner of that item is cleared.
The owner is not to be confused with the original owner, who is considered to be the 'rightful' owner of that item. If the owner does not match the original owner, the item is considered stolen.

SetDoNotFaceFlag.description:
Makes _Character automatically to face or not the entities they are addressing to during dialogues specified by _Value (0 to face others, 1 to avoid facing others).
Parameters:
_Character: character for which we want to disable facing capabilities.
_Value: boolean to enable or disable them.

ClearDialogTag.description:
Clear a tag that was set in a dialog
Parameters:
_Target: the entity on which to clear the tag
_Tag: the tag to clearFurther InformationIn BG3, tags set from different subsystems are stored separately, so the regular call ClearTag call does not work for tags set in dialogues (you can normally only clear them via a dialogue). This call was added mainly for savegame patching, to fix cases where a tag had been wrongly set in a dialogue.

SetVarFloat.description:
Sets the value of the behaviourscript variable %<_Varname> to _VarValue, if the variable exists and is of the FLOAT type.
Further InformationThe variable has to be global. The _Varname does not include the prefix % that is used for global variables in behaviourscript. E.g. a variable that is called %Variable in behaviourscript would simply be Variable for the purposes of this call.
SetVarFloat.seeAlso:
SetVarObject
ClearVarObject
SetVarInteger
SetVarString
SetVarFixedString
SetVarFloat3

SetShortRestAvailable.description:
Sets the availability of short rests on a global level through _IsEnabled.
Further Information_IsEnabled
1: Short rests are available.
0: Short rests are unavailable.

SetTutorialShown.description:
Sets a particular tutorial as shown to the user who owns this character.
Further InformationShown tutorials do not popup if they are called to show again.
Tutorials being shown is saved in user’s profile data.

SetVarFixedString.description:
Sets the value of the behaviourscript variable %<_Varname> to _VarValue, if the variable exists and is of the FIXEDSTRING type.
Further InformationThe variable has to be global. The _Varname does not include the prefix % that is used for global variables in behaviourscript. E.g. a variable that is called %Variable in behaviourscript would simply be Variable for the purposes of this call.
SetVarFixedString.seeAlso:
SetVarObject
ClearVarObject
SetVarInteger
SetVarFloat
SetVarString
SetVarFloat3

SetMovable.description:
Sets the _Item to become Movable.
When _Boolis set to 1 the _Item is Movable.
When _Boolis set to 0 the _Item is not Movable.Further InformationThe Movable trait of an _Item can also be set in the sidebar under the Item tab.

SetIsBoss.description: Marks _Target as a boss character if _Enabled is 1, unmarks _Target as a boss character if _Enabled is 0.

SetVarFloat3.description:
Sets the value of the variable %<_Varname> to {_X;_Y;_Z}, if the variable exists and is of the FLOAT3 type.
Further InformationThe variable has to be global. The _Varname does not include the prefix % that is used for global variables in behaviourscript. E.g. a variable that is called %Variable in behaviourscript would simply be Variable for the purposes of this call.
SetVarFloat3.seeAlso:
SetVarObject
ClearVarObject
SetVarInteger
SetVarFloat
SetVarString
SetVarFixedString

SetRelationTemporaryHostile.description:
Set a temporary hostile relation between the factions of _Character and _OtherCharacter. This temporary hostile relation will be removed again once the characters have entered combat with each other, and that combat subsequently ended.
The call will not do anything (no failure event either) if
_Character and _OtherCharacter are both party members
_Character and _OtherCharacter are the same character
_Character and _OtherCharacter are in an interactive dialogFurther InformationUnlike SetRelation, setting a temporary hostile relation does force the participants to enter combat with each other immediately (except if they're out of combat range).
Temporary hostile relations are a layer on top of regular relations: once a temporary hostile relation ends, the relation reverts to the regular relation (which may have changed since the start of combat).
There are no temporary neutral/allied relations.

SetReadyCheckBlocked.description: If _Value is 0 then ReadyChecks for character _Character are allowed. If _Value is 1, then ReadyChecks for character _Character are blocked.

SetCustomTradeTreasure.description:
Overrides the current default or previously set custom trade treasure table(s) for _Character with the table called _Treasure.
This call will not regenerate the trade treasure for _Character. Use GenerateItems to do so.

SetStoryItem.description:
Sets or clears the story item flag on an item. When an item is a story item, the following special behaviors apply to it:
it cannot be marked as wares, nor can any container that contains it be marked as wares, nor can it be added to the wares when picking it up
it is marked as a quest item in the UI
it cannot be traded to an NPC, and NPCs won't sell it either
NPCs will never consume the item (e.g. in combat)
When a trader NPC dies, its inventory will normally be culled except for the top five most expensive items. Story items, however, will never be deleted in this process.Further InformationAn item can also be marked as a story item in the properties of the item via the editor's sidebar.
SetStoryItem.examples:
IF
AddedTo(_Treasures_Gauntlets, _)
AND
NOT DB_PermaDefeated(_Helsik)
THEN
SetStoryItem(_Treasures_Gauntlets, 1);

SetLongRestAvailable.description:
Manually blocks or allows long rests from Osiris script. Define bool in _IsEnabled.
Further Information_IsEnabled
1: Long rests are enabled.
0: Long rests are disabled.
SetLongRestAvailable.examples:
IF
ReadyCheckPassed("ReturnReadyCheck")
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
THEN
ClearFlag((FLAG)_State_InPrison, _Player);
SetLongRestAvailable(1);

SetStoryDisplayName.description: Set the display name of _Target to _LocalizedTextKey where _LocalizedTextKey is defined in the Translated Strings Editor.

ClearMagicPocketsFlag.description: Clears the _Flag on the _Source and any character who has access to its inventory via magic pockets.
ClearMagicPocketsFlag.examples:
IF
RemovedFrom(_Item,_Owner)
AND
DB_HasItemEvent((ITEM)_Item,_Event)
THEN
ClearMagicPocketsFlag(_Owner, _Flag, 1);
ClearMagicPocketsFlag.seeAlso:
SetMagicPocketsFlag
MagicPocketsDrop
MagicPocketsDropByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTag
MagicPocketsMoveTo
MagicPocketsMoveToByTemplate
MagicPocketsMoveToByTag
IsInMagicPockets
TaggedItemsGetCountInMagicPockets
TemplateGetCountInMagicPockets
StatStringGetCountInMagicPockets

SetTadpoleTreeState.description:
Set the Tadpole Tree of a specified _Character to be a specific state _TadpoleTreeState.
Further Information_TadpoleTreeState is 0, 1, or 2.
0 - Hidden - Tadpole Tree is not available in the UI
1 - Locked - Tadpole Tree can be selected, but player cannot unlock any Tadpole Powers
2 - Available - Tadpole Tree can be selected and powers can be unlocked when Tadpoles are available.
SetTadpoleTreeState.examples:
IF
DB_Tadpole_AllowUIEvents(1)
AND
DB_Tadpole_Activated(_Player)
AND
DB_GLO_TadpoleTreeEnabled(1)
AND
NOT DB_GLO_DaisyAccepted(_Player)
AND
IsTagged(_Player, (TAG)_ILLITHID, 1)
THEN
SetTadpoleTreeState(_Player,1);

DetachHitProxy.seeAlso:
HitProxy
GetHitProxyOwner
IsHitProxy
IsHitProxyOwner
AttachHitProxy
DetachAllHitProxies
IterateHitProxies

ShroudRender.description:
The maps are usually covered by Shroud that hides the map and uncovers with exploration.
This call allows to remove it completely or return back, depending on the parameter:
0 removes the Shroud
1 enables the ShroudFurther InformationThis call doesn’t remove the Room Shroud that is created with Room Portals.

StartRespecToSubclass.description:
A call that brings up the character customization screen for a respec (To change your class/skills/abilities)
Parameters:
_Target the target to be respecced.
_Subclass the subclass to respec into.
A list of Subclasses can be found in the UUID object editor, under Shared > Class Descriptions

TriggerMovieEndFadeIn.description: Finishes the movie with a fade in for _Character.

SetMagicPocketsFlag.description:
Sets the _Flag on the _Source and any character who has access to its inventory via magic pockets.
Further InformationThis call expects an object flag.
SetMagicPocketsFlag.examples:
IF
AddedTo(_Item,_Owner, _)
AND
DB_HasItemEvent((ITEM)_Item,_Event)
AND
Exists(_Item,1)
THEN
SetOnStage(_Item,1);
SetMagicPocketsFlag(_Owner,_Flag,1);
SetMagicPocketsFlag.seeAlso:
MagicPocketsDrop
MagicPocketsDropByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTag
MagicPocketsMoveTo
MagicPocketsMoveToByTemplate
MagicPocketsMoveToByTag
ClearMagicPocketsFlag
IsInMagicPockets
TaggedItemsGetCountInMagicPockets
TemplateGetCountInMagicPockets
StatStringGetCountInMagicPockets

StartSightEvents.description:
Use to enable sight events for _Character. Sight events are Saw and LostSightOf that arrive when a character sees and stops seeing another entity.
Sight events are generated by default for all characters except simple and crowd characters.Parameters:
_Character: The character whose sight events should be started.

SteerTo.description:
Makes the _Character rotate themselves toward the _Target.
_SnapToTarget: 0 = Slow Snap, 1 = Instant Snap
SteerTo.examples:
IF
TimerFinished("Escape_DevourersLookAtPlayer")
AND
DB_DiningDevourersLookAt(_Player)
AND
DB_DiningDevourer(_Devourer, _)
AND
NOT DB_Is_InCombat(_Devourer, _)
AND
NOT DB_Defeated(_Devourer)
THEN
SteerTo(_Devourer, _Player,0);

ToTransform.description:
Moves and rotates _Item. The position and rotation are updated instantly and without animation.
Further InformationThis call requires _Pitch, _Yaw and _Roll to be given in radians while all other calls and queries use degrees to measure angles. Degrees can be converted to radians by multiplying them by 0.0174533.
This is the only call that can change the amount of an existing item in the world (e.g. a gold pile).
ToTransform.examples:
IF
DestroyedBy(_Chest, _, _, _)
AND
DB_FOR_ThayanCellar_AmbushStopped(0)
AND
DB_FOR_ThayanCellar_UndeadContainer(_Chest, _Undead)
AND
GetWorldTransformFromLocal(_Chest, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0 ,-90.0, 0.0, _DestX, _Y, _DestZ, _RotX, _RotY, _RotZ)
AND
RealSum(_Y, 2.0, _DestY)
THEN
ToTransform(_SpawnHelper, _DestX, _DestY, _DestZ, 0.0, _RotY, 0.0);

TriggerLaunchIterator.description:
Causes story event _Event to be thrown for each character currently inside _Trigger. This does not require _Trigger to be first registered for these or any other characters.
Once all characters inside the trigger have been iterated through, _CompletionEvent story event is sent for NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
Further InformationIt is not possible to iterate all items in a trigger in the same way. You have to use call TriggerRegisterForItems and keep track of the items entering/exiting the trigger in that case.
TriggerLaunchIterator.examples:
PROC
PROC_StartDragonScene((CHARACTER)_)
THEN
TriggerLaunchIterator(_GithBridgeOnBox, "OnBridge", "");

IF
EntityEvent((CHARACTER)_NPC, "OnBridge")
AND
NOT DB_Fodder(_NPC, _, _)
AND
NOT DB_Gith(_NPC,_)
THEN
TeleportTo(_NPC, _GithBridgeOutPoint, "");

TurnBasedTimerCancel.description:
TurnBasedTimerCancel will cancel an active Turn based timer.
_Entity is a special parameter here because is not important for the timer and is only used to get the parent Turn entity that handles the combat. There is no need that the _Entity used here to be the same like the use on TurnBasedTimerLaunch call.
After this timer ends, it throws an ObjectTimerFinished event, it doesn’t have its own event. But this ObjectTimerFinished event has one peculiarity: the _Entity will be always NULL. The reason behind this is, again, that _Entity is not tied to the one used on the call but the entity is the turn entity in charge of managing the time. Since ObjectTimerFinished expects to return a CHARACTER or ITEM, and the turn entity is neither, it will return null instead.
Further Information_Entity needs to be an entity that is active in combat. This entity will be used only to get the turn entity and there is no need to be the same entity used for TurnBasedTimerLaunch.
_Timer: the string identifier of the timer. This works exactly the same as other timers, like call ObjectTimerLaunch needs to be an entity that is active in combat. This entity will be used only to get the turn entity. Later, you can kill, destroy or Set Off Stage the entity without problems, because the timer itself will be only tied to a different turn entity we scripters cannot access.

TriggerRemoveItemTemplates.description:
Removes all items of _ItemTemplate root template inside of _Trigger.
Further InformationItems removed this way don’t send DestroyedBy events as they don’t get destroyed. Instead, they are permanently removed from the game.

StopAllEffectsWithName.description: Stops playing all VFX with the name _FxName on _Character.

UnregisterAsCompanion.description:
Permanently remove _Character as a companion.
Further InformationUnregisterAsCompanion will prevent characters from benefiting from long rests.

TurnBasedTimerLaunch.description:
In TurnBasedTimerLaunch, after using the call properly, a GUI element will appear to count the remaining number of rounds. This timer only can happen on Turn-Based Mode or Combat, exclusively, since the timer itself is tied to the Turn entity.
_Entity is a special parameter here because it is not important for the timer and is only used to get the parent Turn entity that handles the combat. So, after this call succeeds, the entity can die, be removed or whatever.
This timer can be cancelled using TurnBasedTimerCancel, using an _Entity in the combat (whatever entity is in combat) and the name of the timer.
After this timer ends, it throws an ObjectTimerFinished event, it doesn’t have its own event. But this ObjectTimerFinished event has one peculiarity: the _Entity will be always NULL. The reason behind this is, again, that _Entity is not tied to the one used on the call but the entity is the turn entity in charge of managing the time. Since ObjectTimerFinished expects to return a CHARACTER or ITEM, and the turn entity is neither, it will return null instead.
Further Information_Entity needs to be an entity that is active in combat. This entity will be used only to get the turn entity. Later, you can kill, destroy or Set Off Stage the entity without problems, because the timer itself will be only tied to a different turn entity we scripters cannot access.
_Timer: the string identifier of the timer. This works exactly the same as other timers, like ObjectTimerLaunch.
_LocalizedTextKey: this is the string for a Text Key stored in the TranslatedStringKeys editor.
_Time: In milliseconds, the timer should be on multiples of six (6000 for one turn).

SitOnEntity.description:
Makes _Character immediately sit on the specified entity.
Parameters:
_Character: The character prompted to sit
Only entities of type CHARACTER make sense for this parameter, even if it technically accepts all GUIDSTRING
_Entity: The entity being sat onFurther InformationThis call is not normally used to make characters sit on the ground as this is part of a character’s behavior and done in Anubis. In Osiris sometimes we use Use instead to force a character to interact with an item that can cause them to sit or lie on it.
SitOnEntity.seeAlso:
ReposeAdded
ReposeRemoved
EndRepose
SitOnGround
LieOnGround
LieOnEntity
StandOnEntity
IsReposed
IsReposedSitting
IsReposedLying
IsReposedStanding
IsReposedConstrained
TemplateCanSitOn

TriggerResetLighting.description:
Resets the lighting trigger (specific trigger type) to its default lighting, listed as first one in its Lightings sidebar property.
Further InformationFor call to take an effect, the default lighting of the lighting trigger can be overridden with TriggerSetLighting.

Transform.description:
Transforms _Object (character or item) into the template _TargetTemplate using _ShapeshiftRules. _TargetTemplate can be a root template, a global entity, or a local entity in the current level.
When you shapeshift, usually you don't need to apply all parameters of _TargetTemplate. For example if you are using disguise, you want to change only the appearance, but not the attributes or known spells. _ShapeshiftRules describe what exact properties of target template will be applied to a shapeshifting object.
You can find the list of available shapeshift rules and what they affect in UUID Object Editor -> Shapeshift -> Rulebook.Transforming an object more than once is supported. If RemoveTransforms is called it removes all transforms from the object.

TriggerSetCrowdBehaviour.description: Sets behavior of all crowd characters spawned by the crowd trigger to either: Cower, Disperse or Flee.
TriggerSetCrowdBehaviour.examples:
IF
FlagSet((FLAG)_State_CiviliansPanicked,_,_)
AND
DB_CrowdTriggers(_CrowdTrigger)
THEN
TriggerSetCrowdBehaviour(_CrowdTrigger, CROWDBEHAVIOUR.Flee);
TriggerSetCrowdSpawningEnable((TRIGGER)_CrowdTrigger,0);

UnlockWaypoint.description:
Unlocks the waypoint that was registered using _WaypointName for _Character, so that it appears in their waypoint list. When they choose to go to the waypoint, they will be teleported to _Trigger.
Further InformationIf no waypoint has been registered with the name _WaypointName yet, this call will do nothing.
_Trigger must be global, otherwise this call will do nothing. The reason is the engine supports going to a waypoint in another level, and only global objects can be referenced while in another level.
Unlocking a waypoint for a character only unlocks it for that specific character. This means you have to unlock it for each player individually.
You can use the shared mod helper DB_WaypointInfo to register waypoints and to support easily locking/unlocking them per party.
Unlocking a waypoint this way does not automatically enable the anchor/waypoint travel button near the minimap, unless your mod depends on the main campaign.
UnlockWaypoint.examples:
PROC
PROC_UnlockWaypointForAll((STRING)_WaypointID)
AND
DB_WaypointInfo(_, _WaypointID, _, _)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
THEN
PROC_UnlockWaypoint(_WaypointID, _Player);

ForceTurnBasedMode.description:
Creates a new or removes an existing force-turn-based (FTB) zone around _PlayerCharacter. This places that character and all characters and items within a radius defined in stats in FTB mode, or removes them again from it.
Further InformationDoes nothing if _PlayerCharacter is invalid, not a player character, or it is already in any kind of FTB (shared or not).
ForceTurnBasedMode.examples:
IF
EntityEvent(_, "UND_Bulette_TriggerSpawn")
AND
DB_UND_Bulette_IsActive(_ActiveEncounter)
AND
DB_UND_Bulette_CombatArenas(_ActiveEncounter, _ArenaTrigger)
AND
DB_InRegion(_Player, _ArenaTrigger)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
GetDistanceTo(_Player, _Bulette_BurrowHelper, _Dist)
AND
_Dist < 15.0
AND
IsInCombat(_Player, 0)
AND
IsInForceTurnBasedMode(_Player, 0)
THEN
ForceTurnBasedMode(_Player, 1);

EndCombat.description:
Every time a combat starts between two entities, a GUID is generated and assigned to the combat. To force end the combat, calling EndCombat and passing it the GUIDthat was generated will end the combat.
Further InformationA common practice is to track the GUID is to fetch it from the CombatStarted event and store it in a DB. Or you may get the GUID for a combat by querying an entity that is in the combat with CombatGetGuidFor.
When two active combats merge (two different combats combining into one), only one GUID will be used and the other discarded. It’s good practice to track the combat in a DB and updated the GUID with SwitchedCombat.
Ending combat with this call will not prevent generic hostile reactions to resume and start a new combat, so if two characters who are hostile are in view of each other when you end combat, they will react and enter combat with each other.
One way to prevent this is to change the faction relation between the entities before ending the combat SetRelation or changing their faction SetFaction.
EndCombat.examples:
IF
EnteredCombat(_EXA_ExampleLeader, _CombatGUID)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
IsInCombat(_Player, 1)
AND
CombatGetGuidFor(_Player, _CombatGUID) //Compares the Combat ID of the player with the ExampleLeader
THEN
DB_Example_CombatID(_CombatGUID);
IF
Died(_EXA_ExampleLeader)
AND
DB_Example_CombatID(_CombatGUID)
THEN
EndCombat(_CombatGUID);
NOT DB_Example_CombatID(_CombatGUID);

ItemDragTo.description:
Drags an item to the root position of a trigger.
Further InformationThis simulates the player action of dragging an item to a location to move it.

IterateInventoryByTemplate.description:
Iterates over all  _InventoryHolder’s inventory. For each item in it with the _Template, it will send an Entity Event with the string _Event as an identifier. When all of the items have been iterated over, it will send the _CompletionEvent as an Entity Event.
Further InformationYou can pass "" as the _CompletionEvent if you don’t need to react to the iteration completing.

HideTutorial.description: Hides that particular tutorial for the user who owns the _Character, if it’s showing, and sets it as shown.
HideTutorial.examples:
// Summons Tutorial
IF
DB_PlayerSummons(_Summon)
AND
CharacterGetOwner(_Summon, _Player)
AND
NOT DB_Is_InCombat(_Player, _)
THEN
PROC_ShowUnifiedTutorialForPlayer((CHARACTER)_Player, (UNIFIEDTUTORIAL)_SummonsTutorial);
IF
GainedControl(_Summon)
AND
CharacterGetOwner(_Summon, _Player)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
THEN
HideTutorial(_Player, (UNIFIEDTUTORIAL)_SummonsTutorial);

DialogRequestStopForDialog.description: Terminates all instances of _Dialog in which the _Speaker is currently involved in.
DialogRequestStopForDialog.examples:
IF
EnteredTrigger(_DEN_Griefling, _DEN_MidAvengePoint)
AND
DB_State_Current(_DEN_Griefling, "DEN_Griefling_CapturedGoblin", "DEN_Griefling_State_Avenge")
THEN
DialogRequestStopForDialog(_DEN_CapturedGoblin_AD_Guards, S_DEN_Griefling);
PROC_TryStartAD(_DEN_CapturedGoblin_AD_Guards, _DEN_Griefling);

EscortSetLeaderPriority.description:
Sets the priority for a character or a target for becoming a leader of an escort group.
If the current leader of an escort group dies or getting staged off, the character within that group with the highest leader priority value will assume the position of leader. In the case of multiple characters having the same priority value, the leader is determined by their entity ID (not GUID).
Parameters:
_Target — The character to set priority for
_EscortGroup — The name of the group
_Priority — The priority of the character to set. Can be a positive or negative integer.Further InformationWhen the character or target is added to an escort group, their default priority value is set to 0.
Setting a priority doesn’t change the current leader, it matters only when the leader dies or getting staged off.

SetVarUUID.description: Sets the variable _VarName of _Source to be equal to the UUID _Object.

MapMarkerChangeLevel.description:
Changes the desired _MarkerID Map Marker’s level that is set based on the input _NewRegionID.
Further InformationThis call only works on map markers that use a global object as a target that also switches levels.
MapMarkerChangeLevel.examples:
IF
WentOnStage(_Elminster, 1)
AND
DB_CurrentLevel(_Level)
THEN
MapMarkerChangeLevel("GLO_Elminster", _Level);

MoveAllLootableItemsTo.description:
A call to make all normally droppable items on an object move to another object, with the option to include equipped items. (For example, this call could move all the items from Wyll to Karlach, or a treasure chest, or said treasure chest to Wyll, etc.)
Parameters:
_FromObject - The GUID of the object to move the items from
_ToObject - The GUID of the object to move the items to
_MoveEquippedArmor - Integer - 0 means to leave equipped armor where it is, 1 means to move it
_MoveEquippedWeapons - Integer - 0 means to leave equipped weapons where they are, 1 means to move them
_ClearOriginalOwner - Integer - 0 means to leave the original owner of the item, 1 means to clear it
_MoveVanityClothing - Integer - 0 means to leave vanity clothing where it is, 1 means to move itFurther InformationThis will move only lootable items, and leave blocked items (such as Story items). If you want to move everything, instead use MoveAllItemsTo, or for story items only use MoveAllStoryItemsTo.

EscortRemoveCharacter.description:
Removes the character or target from an escort group, if assigned to any.
Parameters:
_Target — The character to set as leader of the groupFurther InformationIf the target is not part of a group, has no effect.

StartCameraSpline.description:
Makes the game camera for the user controlling _Character move along _Spline. The screen will start black and fade in during _FadeTime seconds when the spline starts, and fade out again over the same period before the end of the spline. If _HideUI is 1, the hotbar and other UI elements will be hidden while the camera is active. If _Freeze is 1, the character gets story-frozen while the camera spline is active. _StartIndex specifies the index of the spline at which the camera should start.
The camera will move along the spline until it reaches the end of the spline, unless it is a looping spline.
Further InformationIf you specify NULL as _Character, the camera spline will be activated for all player characters.

StartChangeAppearance.description: A call that starts the limited version of Character Creation intended for Magic Mirror.

OpenCraftUI.description:
This call no longer does anything.
Further InformationAdd a Combine or Insert use action to the item in the editor sidebar instead.

TimerPause.description:
A call to make an existing _Timer pause, but not end. It will remain in that state unless resumed or cancelled. Will only work if a timer has already been launched.
Further InformationThis applies to any existing timer, whether Object, Realtime or Global.
TimerPause.examples:
IF
EnteredTrigger(_Player, _Trigger)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
DB_SafeZone(_Trigger)
THEN
TimerPause((STRING)_Timer)

ObjectSetTitleHidden.description: Hides the title of an _Object. 1 for hiding, 0 for showing it again.
ObjectSetTitleHidden.examples:
PROC
PROC_Orin_RestoreTitle()
AND
DB_Orin_SavedTitle(_TranslatedStringKey, _TranslatedString)
THEN
ObjectSetTitleHidden(_Orin, 0);
PROC
PROC_Orin_ClearTitle()
THEN
ObjectSetTitleHidden(_Orin, 1);

StartRespecRestore.description:
A call that restores respec options.
Parameters:
_Target the target to be respecced.

PauseCombat.description:
Pauses an existing combat so combat turns will not progress.
Further InformationIf combat is paused through script, script is also responsible for resuming the combat.
It’s important to keep track of, contain, and closely manage paused combat, as this can easily lead to progression blockers or performance issues.
PauseCombat.examples:
IF
EntityEvent(_Char, "EXA_Example_ScriptedEvent")
AND
DB_Is_InCombat(_Char, _CombatGUID)
THEN
PauseCombat(_CombatGUID);

PopUnsheathedState.description:
Clears the unsheathed state set from Osiris.
Parameters:
_Character: The character whose state is set.
PopUnsheathedState.examples:
IF
TeleportedFromCamp(_Character)
AND
DB_Camp_ForceSheathedWeaponsCharacter(_Character)
THEN
PopUnsheathedState(_Character);
NOT DB_Camp_ForceSheathedWeaponsCharacter(_Character);

AiAddInterestingItem.description: This calls marks an item as “interesting” to a specific NPC. This means that in combat AI will try to prioritize hitting this item with attacks & spells.
AiAddInterestingItem.examples:
PROC
PROC_DruidAttack_Init()
AND
DB_DruidAttack_Druids(_Druid, _, _)
THEN
AiAddInterestingItem(_Druid, _PrisonMainDoor);

PlayBeamEffect.description: Plays a beam effect _FxName from the _SourceBone of _Object, to the _TargetBone of _Target.

QuestUpdateMessageCounter.description: Not currently supported. Do not use.

TriggerSetSoundState.description:
Changes the behavior of a sound trigger.
Calling TriggerSetSoundState will set _StateGroup to _State on _Trigger.
If _Recursive is set to 1, this will also be done for other sound triggers that are contained within _Trigger.
TriggerSetSoundState.examples:
PROC
PROC_Camp_SetModeToDay()
AND
DB_Camp(_Camp, _, _CampArea, _)
AND
DB_Camp_Ambiance(_Camp,_AtmosphereTrigger,_DayAtmo,_NightAtmo,_LightingTrigger,_DayLight,_NightLight,_SoundTrigger)
AND
Exists(_CampArea,1)
AND
GetRegion(_CampArea, _Region)
AND
DB_CurrentLevel(_Region)
THEN
TriggerSetAtmosphere(_AtmosphereTrigger,_DayAtmo);
TriggerSetLighting(_LightingTrigger,_DayLight);
TriggerSetSoundState(_SoundTrigger,"AMB_TimeofDay","Day",0);

OpenCustomBookUI.description:
Opens a book UI for the player controlling _Character that shows the entries added to the custom book using AddEntryToCustomBook with name _Bookname.
Further InformationIf _Character is invalid or no entries have been added to _Bookname yet, nothing will happen.

TransformSurface.description:
Subject the surface in the _TransformLayer at the location of the _Source to _TransformType, out to a distance of _Radius meters where applicable. The lifetime and owner of the resulting surface are given by _Lifetime and _Owner.
Further Information_TransformType are defined in the Surface Change sheet under Enumerations that can be accessed in the Stats Editor. Examples are:
“Ignite” - Ignites oil, poison.
“Douse” - Douses fire.
“Electrify” - Electrifies water and blood
“Deelectrify” - Removes electricity from water and blood
“Freeze” - Freezes water and blood.
“Melt” - Melts frozen water and blood.
“Vaporize” - Turns a ground-layer surface into a cloud.
“Condense” - Turns a cloud into a ground-layer surface.
_TransformLayer must be one of the following:
“Ground” - the transform affects surfaces on the ground.
“Cloud” - the transform affects clouds.
TransformSurface.seeAlso:
GetSurfaceSize
CreatePuddle
CreateSurface
CreateSurfaceAtPosition
DrawSurfaceOnPath
GetSurfaceCloudAt
GetSurfaceCloudOwnerAt
GetSurfaceGroundAt
GetSurfaceGroundOwnerAt
GetSurfaceNameByTypeIndex
GetSurfaceTurns
GetSurfaceTypeIndex
StopDrawSurfaceOnPath
TransformSurfaceAtPosition

RequestDeleteTemporary.description:
Deletes _Character if it was marked as temporary at creation.
Further Information_Character is permanently removed and does not die (will not trigger death related events).
RequestDeleteTemporary.examples:
IF
Died(_Zombie)
AND
DB_GLO_Spells_AnimateDead(_Zombie, _Owner)
THEN
NOT DB_GLO_Spells_AnimateDead(_Zombie, _Owner);
RequestDeleteTemporary((CHARACTER)_Zombie);

RemoveFogVolume.description: Detaches fog volumes attached to _Object using AddFogVolume.
RemoveFogVolume.examples:
IF
RemovedFrom(_Item, _Character)
AND
DB_MoonShieldFogVolume(_Character)
AND
Exists(_Character,1)
AND
NOT QRY_GotMoonShieldObject((CHARACTER)_Character)
THEN
NOT DB_MoonShieldFogVolume(_Character);
RemoveFogVolume(_Character);

RequestDelete.description:
Deletes _Item from the game.
Further Information_Item is permanently removed.
If the _Item is a stack of items, the whole stack is removed.
There is no destroy animation or leftover debris like the Die.

RequestSetSwarmGroup.description:
This query is part of the Swarm system. Assigns the _Target to a swarm group by its _SwarmGroup string id.
Further InformationIf you want to clear a swarm group, there’s no separate call for that. Set an empty string as a value for _SwarmGroup instead. A swarm group can also be set from an editor in a sidebar for a character.
RequestSetSwarmGroup.examples:
PROC
PROC_DruidAttack_Init()
AND
DB_DruidAttack_DruidAnimals(_Animal)
THEN
RequestSetSwarmGroup(_Animal, "DruidAttack_Animals");

ScatterStoryItems.description:
Drops all items tagged as Story Items in the selected _InventoryHolder’s inventory in nearby valid positions within a 5m radius of the _InventoryHolder.
The _InventoryHolder may be an Item or a Character.
ScatterStoryItems.examples:
IF
Died(_InventoryHolder)
AND
DB_ScatterItemsOnDeath(_InventoryHolder)
THEN
ScatterStoryItems(_InventoryHolder);

SetEntityEventReal.description:
Like SetEntityEvent but with an additional parameter _RealParam.
Further InformationSee SetEntityEvent for more information.
Event is not sent to Osiris, and must be caught in Anubis/Constellations.
SetEntityEventReal.examples:
IF
TurnStarted(_Harpy)
AND
DB_Harpies((CHARACTER)_Harpy,_)
AND
HasActiveStatus(_Harpy,"LURING_SONG_AURA",1)
THEN
SetEntityEventReal(_Harpy,"GLO_CombatWait",2.0);
SetEntityEventReal.seeAlso:
EntityEvent
DualEntityEvent
SetEntityEvent
SetDualEntityEvent
SetDualEntityEventReal
SetEntityEventDialog
SetDualEntityEventDialog
SetEntityEventGUID

RestoreParty.description:
Calls a restore on _Character.
Further InformationRestores all of the resources and breaks concentration on the character.
RestoreParty.examples:
PROC
PROC_ShadowheartRecruitmentInit()
AND
DB_State_Current(_Player_ShadowHeart,"Recruitment","BEACH")
AND
NOT DB_Dead(_Player_ShadowHeart)
THEN
RestoreParty(_Player_ShadowHeart);

RequestLongRestSyncedFinish.description: This is for internal use only.

SaveAnubisState.description:
Saves the state of the Anubis tree.
Parameters:
_Character: the character for whom the state is savedFurther InformationThe state can be restored with RestoreAnubisState.

RequestSetBaseArchetype.description:
Sets Archetype on character.
Further InformationArchetypes are used to influence AI scoring for which actions characters choose in combat
You can see examples of Archetypes under \Gustav\Data\Public\Gustav\AI\Archetypes
RequestSetBaseArchetype.examples:
IF
DB_AttackOnDen_WaveParticipants(_Raider,1)
AND
NOT DB_AttackOnDen_Spiders(_Raider)
AND
GetBaseArchetype(_Raider,_Archetype)
AND
_Archetype == "goblin_melee"
THEN
RequestSetBaseArchetype(_Raider,"melee_ignoresurfacedamage");
SetAiHint(_Raider,NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000);

ResetTutorialsRequest.description:
Sets all tutorial messages as not shown on this player’s profile. Depending on the second parameter it either does it automatically (= silently) or it pops a dialog window where it asks the player whether they want all their tutorials reset or not.
Further Information_AskPlayer is effectively a bool. 0 = don’t ask, 1 = ask.
ResetTutorialsRequest.examples:
IF
DB_CurrentLevel(_Level)
AND
IsCharacterCreationLevel(_Level,1)
AND
DB_ResetTutorialsRequest(_UserID, _TutCompleted)
THEN
NOT DB_ResetTutorialsRequest(_UserID, _TutCompleted);
ResetTutorialsRequest(_UserID, _TutCompleted);

RequestLongRestDeny.description:
This is for internal use only.
Part of a complex handshake between code and story for the camp and long rest pipeline.

SetCanInteract.description: Makes the item interactable if _Bool is 1 and non-interactable if 0.
SetCanInteract.examples:
IF
StatusApplied(_Object, "MASK_ILLUSION", _, _)
AND
IsItem(_Object, 1)
THEN
SetCanInteract((ITEM)_Object, 0);

RequestLongRest.description:
Makes a request to code for initiating a long rest to end a camp night on _Initiator, can be forced by _IsForced if the bool is set as 1 or remain unforced if bool is set as 0.
Further InformationCan react to the LongRestStarted(), LongRestFailed()or LongRestCancelled() events.
LongRestStarted()- Successfully started the long rest.
LongRestFailed() - Failed to start the long rest.
LongRestCancelled() - Long rest was cancelled.
RequestLongRest.examples:
PROC
PROC_RequestLongRest((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
DB_Camp_ForceNextLongRest(1)
THEN
RequestLongRest(_Player, 1);

SetCanSpotSneakers.description: Disables (_CanSpotSneakers is set to 0) or enables (_CanSpotSneakers is set to 1) the ability to spot sneaking character for character _Character.

ResetCooldowns.description: Resets all spell cooldowns on the given character _Character. This means that spells that can only be cast once per long rest, short rest, per combat or once per turn will be usable again despite its cooldown conditions.

SetCanTrade.description:
Enables player-initiated trades during dialogues with _Trader if _Bool is 1, otherwise disables them.
Further InformationChanging this setting for a character while it is already in a dialogue is supported and will adapt its trade setting on-the-fly.

SetCanJoinCombat.description:
Sets whether or not _Target can enter combats depending on whether _Enabled is 0 (no) or 1 (yes).
Further InformationJoining a combat means "participating in the turn order of a combat", i.e., not moving around in real time while nearby characters/items are in a combat, and having statuses applied to them switch to turn-based mode. It does not mean that the character/item will also actively participate in the combat (perform actions during its turns in a combat). Controlling whether or not a character/item in a combat will do so happens using SetCanFight.
If _Target is currently in a combat and 0 is passed to _Enabled, _Target will leave this combat immediately.
Disabling this can easily wreak havoc due to interactions with other systems, such as the crime system.
If you disable this for a player during a scripted event, ensure it is re-enabled in all possible exits.

SetLevel.description:
Set the experience level of a character.
Parameters:
_Object: the entity for which to set the level. In practice, this call only works for character
_Level: the new experience level for the character
SetLevel.examples:
PROC
PROC_DEN_WyllRecruitment_Init()
AND
NOT DB_Players((CHARACTER)_Player_Wyll)
THEN
SetLevel((CHARACTER)_Player_Wyll, 2);

RegisterWaypoint.description:
Registers a new waypoint with the game engine. The name of the waypoint in the waypoint list is given by the translated string whose key is _WaypointName, and the object that represents the shrine in the game world is _Item. The location of _Item is where the whirlwind-style icon appears on the (mini-)map.
Further Information_Item must be global, otherwise this call will not do anything. The reason is the engine supports going to a waypoint in another level, and only global objects can be referenced while in another level.
Registering a waypoint by itself does not unlock it for anyone. (UnlockWaypoint)

SetAnimationEventInt.description:
The same as SetAnimationEvent but with an Integer parameter _UserParam
The _UserParamis used to specify a state while keeping a standardized _EventName
Further InformationSee SetAnimationEvent for more information.

RestoreAnubisState.description:
Restores the stores state of the Anubis tree.
Parameters:
_CharacterFurther InformationThe Anubis state needs to first be saved using SaveAnubisState.
RestoreAnubisState.examples:
PROC
PROC_AnubisRestoreBaseConfig((CHARACTER)_Char)
AND
DB_AnubisConfigs(_Char, _DefaultConfig)
AND
DB_AnubisConfigs_CharacterSavedStates(_Char, _DefaultConfig)
THEN
RestoreAnubisState(_Char);
NOT DB_AnubisConfigs_CharacterSavedStates(_Char, _DefaultConfig);

ScatterAt.description:
Makes _Item move from the position (_X, _Y, _Z) in an arc to a random location around the source position. This location is picked by first selecting a position within a 10 metre square around the position, and then looking for a valid (green) AI-grid position at most 5 metres from this spot.
Further InformationIf no valid (green) AI-grid position can be found with the above constraints, this call will not do anything nor result in any events.
If (_X, _Y, _Z) is different from the item's current position, it will first be (internally) teleported there and resulting ItemLeftTrigger and ItemEnteredTrigger events will be thrown as appropriate (no Moved event though).
The scatter operation itself is a regular move task, similar to ItemMoveToPosition  with _DoHits = 1, except that the item will move in an arc rather than in a straight line. The remarks for that call therefore also apply to this one. In particular, if the item's movement is blocked in the randomly chosen direction, it may not move at all. You will still get an event Moved event in this case, as an item move operation was started and completed.
Due to implementation issues, scattering an item right after creating it will never change its position (it will still throw an Moved event). A workaround is to scatter it again after receiving the Moved event from the first scatter operation.
ScatterAt.examples:
IF
FlagSet((FLAG)_Knows_ThrewCopper, _, _)
AND
GetPosition(_ThrowCoinHelper, _X, _Y, _Z)
AND
CreateAt(_LOOT_Gold, _X, _Y, _Z, 0, 0, "", _ThrownCoin)
THEN
ScatterAt((ITEM)_ThrownCoin, _X, _Y, _Z);

SetGlobalPriceModifier.description:
Adjusts all prices in the game by some modifier.
Parameters:
_Modifier: The global factor
SetGlobalPriceModifier.examples:
IF
LevelGameplayStarted(_Level, _)
AND
DB_Level_PriceAdjustment(_Level, _Modifier)
THEN
SetGlobalPriceModifier(_Modifier);

RequestLongRestSetTimeline.description:
This is for internal use only.
Part of a complex handshake between code and story for the camp and long rest pipeline.

SetCombatGroupID.description:
Assigns a combat group ID to an entity. Entities who are assigned a combat group will enter combat when another entity of the same group enters combat.
Further InformationIf assigning a combat group that was generated within the combat group editor, use the GUID as a string.
Otherwise, use a readable name that is defined in script.
Setting the group as an empty string "" will remove the combat group from the entity.
It is typically safer and more human readable to store editor defined groups within a DB.
Example: DB_EXA_Example_CombatGroupCharacters(_Char, _CombatGroupA)
SetCombatGroupID.examples:
IF
DB_EXA_Example_Guard(_Guard)
THEN
SetCombatGroupID(_Guard, "EXA_Example_GuardGroup");
IF
Died(_Guard)
AND
DB_EXA_Example_Guard(_Guard)
THEN
SetCombatGroupID(_Guard, "");

IF
DB_EXA_Example_Minion(_Minion)
AND
GetCombatGroupID(S_EXA_MinionBoss_XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX, _EditorDefinedGroup)
THEN
SetCombatGroupID(_Minion, _EditorDefinedGroup);

SetOriginalOwner.description:
Changes the original owner of _Item. This is the 'legitimate' owner of an object, and if the owner does not match this value, it is considered stolen.
Further InformationAn item is considered stolen if its owner does not match its original owner.
If an item is not yet stolen (i.e. its owner and original owner are identical) and that original owner dies, the owner and original Owner are both cleared.
SetOriginalOwner.examples:
IF
AddedTo(_Item, _BackPack, _)
AND
DB_CompanionLeavingBackPack((CHARACTER)_Companion,(CHARACTER)_Player, _BackPack)
AND
// Only items have an (original) owner
QRY_IsExistingItem(_Item, 1)
AND
GetOriginalOwner((ITEM)_Item, _Companion)
THEN
SetOriginalOwner(_Item, _Player);

SetFaction.description:
Set the base faction of _Target to _Faction.
The base faction is the faction of an entity set by this Osiris SetFaction call or by Anubis SetFaction call, or if that never happened, the faction assigned to the entity directly in the editor in the “Faction” property.

SetDualEntityEventDialog.description:
Like SetEntityEventDialog but with 2 entity parameters _Object1 and _Object2.
Further InformationSee SetEntityEventDialog for more information.
SetDualEntityEventDialog.seeAlso:
EntityEvent
DualEntityEvent
SetEntityEvent
SetDualEntityEvent
SetEntityEventReal
SetDualEntityEventReal
SetEntityEventDialog
SetEntityEventGUID

SetPortalIsOpen.description:
Opens or closes the room portal associated with _Item.
Further InformationAll doors bordering a room automatically function as room portals, and when the door opens so does the associated room portal. So this call essentially is used when there’s a room portal that can’t be opened by a door.
SetPortalIsOpen.examples:
PROC
PROC_RemoveBoudoirField()
AND
QRY_OnlyOnce("OpenBoudoir")
THEN
SetPortalIsOpen(_BoudoirMagicField, 1);

SetTeleportTargetOverride.description:
Sets the target of the first teleport use action of _Source to _TeleportTarget. If _TeleportTarget is NULL', it clears the override again (if any).
Further InformationIf the target is an item or character that's in an inventory, on teleporting the the _Source will be teleported to the outermost container/character holding said character/item.
If _Source does not have a teleport use action (more info about that in GetTeleportTarget), this call will not do anything.
SetTeleportTargetOverride.examples:
IF
DB_GLO_Spells_SpawningArcaneGate(_ArcaneGateOne,_Character)
AND
DB_GLO_Spells_SpawningArcaneGate(_ArcaneGateTwo,_Character)
AND
_ArcaneGateOne != _ArcaneGateTwo
THEN
SetTeleportTargetOverride(_ArcaneGateOne, _ArcaneGateTwo);
SetTeleportTargetOverride(_ArcaneGateTwo, _ArcaneGateOne);

SetIndividualRelation.description: Sets an individual relation between _Entity and _Faction to _Relation. This call allows overriding the default relation between the faction of the _Entity and _Faction.

SetUseRemotely.description:
Sets or clears UseRemotely flag on _Item.
Further InformationThis flag can be set through the sidebar in the Editor.
It allows an item to be used from afar, without the need for the character to physically be close to it.

SetGravity.description: Sets gravity type of item _Item to _GravityType (Enabled, Disabled, DisabledUntilMove). _Instigator points to a character whose actions led to the gravity changing.
SetGravity.examples:
PROC
PROC_CHA_DestroyPillar((CHARACTER)_Attacker)
AND
QRY_OnlyOnce("CHA_PillarFall")
THEN
SetGravity(_Fissure_Boulder, GRAVITYTYPE.Enabled);
PlaySound(_Fissure_Boulder, "Fissure_Boulder_Fall");

SetFlag.description:
Use this to set _Flag on _Object.
For global flags, _Object can be set to NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 or simply call SetFlag((FLAG)_Flag).
SetFlag.fullDefinitions:
call SetFlag((FLAG)_Flag) 
call SetFlag((FLAG)_Flag, (GUIDSTRING)_Object) 
call SetFlag((FLAG)_Flag, (GUIDSTRING)_Object, (INTEGER)_DialogInstance) 
call SetFlag((FLAG)_Flag, (GUIDSTRING)_Object, (INTEGER)_DialogInstance, (INTEGER)_SendFlagSetEventIfChanged)
SetFlag.examples:
IF
StatusApplied(_Player, "HAV_SELUNEOINTMENT", _, _)
AND
DB_PartyMembers((CHARACTER)_Player)
THEN
SetFlag(_State_HasSeluneOintment, _Player);

SetStoryShortRestEnabled.description:
Enables short rest for a specific character defined in the _Character parameter.
Further InformationFactors that can block short rest;
SetStoryShortRestDisabled
Certain statuses
Being in combat
Being in FTB
Dialog state

SetDisableUse.description:
Disables any use actions of _Item. The item remains interactable apart from that, so it can still be picked up, moved, thrown, attacked, etc.
Further InformationThis call is reference-counted, so you need to re-enable the usability as many times as you disabled it.
If you want to make an item completely uninteractable, use SetCanInteract
SetDisableUse.examples:
IF
UseFinished(_Player, _BaseStem, 1)
AND
NOT DB_BrainquakeActive(1)
THEN
SetDisableUse(_BaseStem, 1);

SetTagPriceModifier.description: Set the price multiplication factor _Modifier on _NPCtrader when trading with characters that have_Tag. A higher _Modifier increases the selling price and reduces the buying price of the trader.

SetIsInDangerZone.description:
Sets the _Character to be _IsInDangerZone.
Further Information_IsInDangerZone
1:  True (Is danger zone)
0: False (Is not in danger zone)Be cautious in using this as it prevents the player from using a lot of functionality like long rest.
SetIsInDangerZone.examples:
PROC
PROC_DangerZone_Dirty((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
NOT DB_InDangerZone(_Player,_)
THEN
SetIsInDangerZone(_Player,0);

SetStoryShortRestDisabled.description:
Disables short rest for a specific character defined in the _Character parameter.
Parameters:
_Character - The character for who the short rest will be blocked.
_Reason - The notification that will be displayed for why it was disabled.Further InformationFactors that can block short rest;
SetStoryShortRestDisabled
Certain statuses
Being in combat
Being in FTB
Dialog state

StartRespecToOathbreaker.description:
A call that brings up the character customization screen for a respec (To change your class/skills/abilities) but in this case, specifically from being  a traditional Paladin to an Oathbreaker.
Parameters:
_Target the target to be respecced.

OpenWaypointUI.description: Opens the UI menu for _Character. If accessed trough using a Waypoint, it will be _CurrentWaypoint linked to _Item. If _Character uses the waypoint while fleeing, then _IsFleeing will be “1” (and the teleport will trigger the TeleportToFleeWaypoint instead of TeleportToWaypoint).

StopSightEvents.description:
Use to disable sight events for a _Character. Sight events are Saw and LostSightOf that arrive when a character sees and stops seeing another entity.
Sight events are generated by default for all characters except simple and crowd charactersParameters:
_Character: The character whose sight events should be stopped.

SetEntityEvent.description:
Sends the Entity Event _Event with _Object and _Event parameters.
Further InformationBy default _Unicast is set to 0 and a broadcast will be sent.
Unicast - The event will only be sent to Osiris, and the EntityEvent handlers of Anubis states that are attached to _Object.
Broadcast - The event is sent to Osiris and all EntityEvent handlers of all entities in the current level.
SetEntityEvent.seeAlso:
EntityEvent
DualEntityEvent
SetDualEntityEvent
SetEntityEventReal
SetDualEntityEventReal
SetEntityEventDialog
SetDualEntityEventDialog
SetEntityEventGUID

RemoveEntryFromCustomBook.description:
Removes the text associated with the translated string key _Entryname from the existing text of _Bookname.
Further InformationIf the custom book with identifier _Bookname does not exist, or the translated string key _Entryname has not yet been added to it, this call does nothing.

Unfreeze.description: Makes story-frozen (via Freeze) _Character no longer story-frozen.
Unfreeze.examples:
IF
ObjectTimerFinished((ITEM)_Item, "Timer_Delay")
AND
DB_AnimationWait(_Player, _Item)
THEN
Unfreeze(_Player);

TriggerRegisterForItems.description:
Ensures that whenever any item enters or leaves _Trigger, a respective ItemEnteredTrigger or ItemLeftTrigger event is thrown for this trigger/item combination, as well as a respective TemplateEnteredTrigger or TemplateLeftTrigger event.
Further InformationUnlike with character triggers, registering a trigger for items will not throw ItemEnteredTrigger/ItemTemplateEnteredTrigger events for all items already in that trigger.
Items in a character's inventory do not trigger this event when the character enters/exits the trigger.
TriggerRegisterForItems.seeAlso:
ItemEnteredTrigger
ItemLeftTrigger
TemplateEnteredTrigger
TemplateLeftTrigger
TriggerUnregisterForItems
TriggerRegisterForCharacter

RemoveStatusesWithType.description:
RemoveStatusesWithType will remove every status in any table of _StatusType from the _Target by the _Cause.
_Target can be a CHARACTER or ITEM.
_StatusType is the name of the StatusData table in the stats editor, without the prefix. Check the Further Information section to check the available names.
_Cause specifies the object, either a CHARACTER or ITEM, that the game should consider as the cause of this status removal. This is mainly useful for scripted skill applications where you want _Target to react as if _Cause removed this status.
You can pass NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 if there is no specific cause.Further InformationRight now, the current status data tables you can use for _StatusType parameter are:
BOOST
HEAL
FEAR
POLYMORPHED
KNOCKED_DOWN
INCAPACITATED
INVISIBLE
DEACTIVATED
EFFECT
SNEAKING
DOWNEDExecuting this call will remove any status applied to that character that is inside any table with that suffix, whatever the mod is storing the table.
You can get the type of a status through Osiris using GetStatusType.
RemoveStatusesWithType.seeAlso:
ApplyStatus
RemoveStatusesWithGroup
GetStatusType
HasActiveStatus
RemoveHarmfulStatuses
StatusAttempt
StatusAttemptFailed
StatusApplied
StatusRemoved

TriggerClearItemsOwner.description:
Clears current and original owner for all items inside of _AreaTrigger
Further InformationOriginal owner is the owner of the item before it got stolen by the player.

TransferItemsToUser.description:
Transfers all story items in _Character's inventory to another character controlled by the same user/player.
Further InformationThis is mainly used when dismissing companions.
TransferItemsToUser.examples:
PROC
PROC_GLO_PartyMembers_TransferStoryItems(_Origin,_, (INTEGER)_OnlyGoldAndPartyMembers)
AND
NOT DB_BlockStoryItemTransfer(_Origin)
AND
NOT DB_StoryItemsTransfered(1)
THEN
TransferItemsToUser(_Origin);

SendToCampChest.description: Sends an _Entity to the camp chest of the _Player

RemoveStatusesWithGroup.description:
This call removes all status with the same _StatusGroup from _Target.
_Target can be a CHARACTER or ITEM.
_StatusGroup is a string from StatusGroup column in any StatusData table in the Stats Editor.
_Cause specifies the object, either a CHARACTER or ITEM, that the game should consider as the cause of this status removal. This is mainly useful for scripted skill applications where you want _Target to react as if _Cause removed this status.
You can pass NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 if there is no specific cause.Further InformationThe overload without the _Cause will default that parameter to NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000.
You can use this call to remove all similar status. For example, if you want to drop Invisibility from a character, you should use this call using SG_Invisibility instead of trying to call every single invisibility status in the game one by one.
To remove individual statuses, use RemoveStatus instead.
RemoveStatusesWithGroup.seeAlso:
ApplyStatus
RemoveStatus
RemoveStatusesWithType
HasActiveStatus
RemoveHarmfulStatuses
StatusAttempt
StatusAttemptFailed
StatusApplied
StatusRemoved

SetInNarrativeCombat.description:
Adds an entity to a narrative combat.
Further InformationIt is best to track characters that are in the narrative combat with a DB.
SetInNarrativeCombat.examples:
PROC
PROC_EXA_Example_JoinNarrativeCombat((CHARACTER)_Character)
AND
DB_EXA_Example_NarrativeCombatID(_ID)
AND
IsInNarrativeCombat(_Character, _ID, 0)
AND
NOT DB_EXA_Example_NarrativeCombatCharacter(_ID, _Character)
THEN
SetInNarrativeCombat(_Character, _ID, 1);
DB_EXA_Example_NarrativeCombatCharacter(_ID, _Character);
 
PROC
PROC_EXA_Example_LeaveNarrativeCombat((CHARACTER)_Character)
AND
DB_EXA_Example_NarrativeCombatCharacter(_ID, _Character)
AND
DB_EXA_Example_NarrativeCombatID(_ID)
AND
IsInNarrativeCombat(_Character, _ID, 1)
THEN
SetInNarrativeCombat(_Character, _ID, 0);
NOT NOT DB_EXA_Example_NarrativeCombatCharacter(_ID, _Character);

TemplateAddTo.description: Adds _Count instances of the root template _ItemTemplate to _InventoryHolder. _InventoryHolder can either be a character or a container object. If _ShowNotification is 1 and _InventoryHolder is a player, an animation of the item's icon moving towards the player icon in the player list will be shown.
TemplateAddTo.examples:
PROC
PROC_StateSet_PermaDefeated(_Volo)
AND
NOT DB_GlobalFlag((FLAG)_Volo_Knows_Lobotomy)
THEN
TemplateAddTo((ITEMROOT)_VoloOperation_ErsatzEye, _Volo, 1);

RemoveTransforms.description: Removes all applied transforms from a character or item - either applied by call Transform or coming from a shapeshift status. Shapeshift statuses will be removed.

SetKnown.description:
Set the name of an _Item to the known or unknown variant depending on _Bool.
_Item: the item should have filled the Display Name (known) and Display Name Unknown (unknown state, default if the field is filled).
_Bool: 0 sets make the item use the Display Name Unknown name. 1 sets the item to use Display Name.Further InformationDisplay Name and Display Name Unknown are Item’s fields on the sidebar.

SendArenaCameraEvent.description: SendArenaCameraEvent starts an “arena camera“, also called a “combat event camera”. Arena cameras are special cinematics that don’t interrupt the combat like a normal dialogue, but shows something special happening within the combat, usually on a certain combat participant’s turn.
SendArenaCameraEvent.examples:
PROC
PROC_ArenaCamera_Start()
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
DB_InRegion(_Player, (TRIGGER)_AltarArea_DialogTrigger)
THEN
SendArenaCameraEvent(_Player, _Orin, "Ritual_ArenaCamEvent");

QuestMessageWithCounterShow.description: Not currently supported. Do not use.

PlayEffectAtPositionAndRotation.description: The same as PlayEffectAtPosition but with an added _Yangle parameter for Y axis rotation.

TimerCancel.description:
A call to make an existing Global _Timer stop and end. Will only work if a timer has already been launched.
Further InformationFor Object based timers, instead use call ObjectTimerCancel.
For Real Time timers, instead use call RealtimeObjectTimerCancel.
TimerCancel.examples:
IF
Died(_Player)
AND
TimerExists(_Timer)
THEN
TimerCancel((STRING)_Timer);

SetHasOsirisDialog.description:
Enable clicking on the character to start a dialog.
Further InformationIdentical to SetHasDialog.
By default, you can only click on characters to start a dialog if a dialog has been assigned using the editor sidebar’s Default Dialog property. Using this call, you can enable this interaction mode for any characters
If you assign a dialog using DB_Dialogs, this call is already used for all listed speakers of the dialog
The click itself results in a DialogStartRequested event, which is handled via the standard dialogue pipeline. If you wish to react to this event, override QRY_SelectCustomDialog or QRY_SelectDialogStartRequested_AfterGenerics. For now, you will have to look in story for how they are used.
Regardless of whether this property is true or false, you can always explicitly start a dialog using the character from story with QRY_StartDialog or related queries/calls.

IterateOriginTags.description: Call to iterate through a character’s tags and trigger an event for every tag with the defined string _Event. Will also trigger an event when it completes the iteration with the defined _FinishEvent.

SetMaxPartySizeOverride.description:
Overrides the default party size.
Further InformationIn BG 3 maximum party size is set to 4, but you can temporarily change it by using this call (for example, limit it to 2 members for a tutorial).
SetMaxPartySizeOverride.examples:
PROC
PROC_TUT_Helm_EndTutorial()
AND
DB_TUT_Start_InitialPartySizeAfterTUT(_Max)
THEN
SetMaxPartySizeOverride(_Max);
NOT DB_TUT_Start_InitialPartySizeAfterTUT(_Max);

Attack.description:
Causes _Character to attack _Target.
If _AlwaysHit is set to 1, then the attack will be guaranteed to hit. Set to 0 to have it not be guaranteed.
Attack.examples:
IF
EntityEvent((CHARACTER)_Character, "DruidAttack_KillGoblin")
AND
NOT DB_Dead(_CapturedGoblin)
AND
NOT DB_GlobalFlag(_CapturedGoblin_State_Released)
THEN
Attack(_Character, _CapturedGoblin, 1);

SetHitpointsPercentage.description:
Sets current hit points of _Entity to a _Percentage of its total maximum hit points. The result can’t exceed entity’s hit points maximum or be less than zero.
Further InformationThe accepted values for the _Percentage are between 0.0 and 100.0.
If it increases the hit points of the entity, it will be considered as healing. In that case The _HealingTypes can be specified as following:
“Guaranteed” (default value) - heals target of any type,
“Living” - will not heal undead or constructs,
“Construct” - will not heal undead or living,
“Undead” - will not heal constructs or living.
Setting _Percentage to zero may kill/destroy the target entity (if it can be killed/destroyed).

SetEntityEventDialog.description:
Like SetEntityEvent but with an additional parameter _Dialog.
Further InformationSee SetEntityEvent for more information.
Event is not sent to Osiris, and must be caught in Anubis/Constellations.
SetEntityEventDialog.examples:
PROC
PROC_WaitForEndOfCrimeAD((CHARACTER)_Criminal, (DIALOGRESOURCE)_Dialog)
AND
_Criminal != NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
AND
IsPartyMember(_Criminal, 1, 0)
THEN
SetEntityEventDialog(_Criminal, "WaitOnDialogEnd", _Dialog, 1);
SetEntityEventDialog.seeAlso:
EntityEvent
DualEntityEvent
SetEntityEvent
SetDualEntityEvent
SetEntityEventReal
SetDualEntityEventReal
SetDualEntityEventDialog
SetEntityEventGUID

SetTrapDiscovered.description:
Sets _Item as a discovered trap if _Bool is 1, and to undiscovered if not 0.
Only an armed, discovered trap can be disarmed.
SetTrapDiscovered.examples:
IF
ArmedTrapUsed(_Char,_TunnelChest)
AND
IsTrapDiscovered(_TunnelChest, 0)
THEN
SetTrapDiscovered(_TunnelChest, 1);
CreateExplosion(_TunnelChest,"Projectile_Fireball_Trap",-1);

SetWeaponUnsheathed.description:
Sets character’s weapon the sheathed or unsheathed.
Parameters:
_Character: The character who should sheathe or unsheathe their weapon
_Enabled: 1 for unsheathed and 0 for sheathed.
_Immediately: if 1, skips the unsheathe animation
SetWeaponUnsheathed.examples:
PROC
PROC_CRE_BattleStations()
AND
DB_CRE_BattleStations(_Gith, _)
THEN
SetWeaponUnsheathed(_Gith, 1, 0);

UserAddGold.description:
Divides the gold amount equally among all the characters of the User of  _Character.
Further InformationIf gold amount is negative it removes gold, and it tries to divide the removed quantity equally as well if it can.
Please note, this call doesn’t take into consideration whether player’s inventories are locked or not.
It is recommended you use AddGoldToMagicPockets instead, which takes locked inventories into account, and isn’t limited to one user.

AddedTo.description:
Thrown when an entity is added to another entity's inventory.
Parameters_Object: the entity that was added.
_InventoryHolder: the entity to whose inventory it was added.
_AddType: the type of addition to the inventory (Regular/Treasure/TradeTreasure). Options for _AddType are:
TradeTreasure - means it got added as part of a call to GenerateTreasure from story (based on the trade treasure specified in the side bar)
Treasure - means it got added as part of generating a character/container's regular inventory based on its treasure table in the side bar
Regular - is all other cases (scripted calls to add something to an inventory, picking something up, ...)Further InformationFor a specific item. If you want to check a broader template, use TemplateAddedTo.
AddedTo.examples:
IF
AddedTo(_OwlBear_Egg,(CHARACTER)_Player, _)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
NOT DB_PermaDefeated(_OwlBear)
AND
QRY_OnlyOnce("EggPickedUp")
THEN
SetFlag((FLAG)_OwlBear_EggPickedInCombat, NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, 0);
StartVoiceBark((VOICEBARKRESOURCE)_VB_EggPickedUp,_Player);

Activated.description: Event is triggered when _Object is activated.
Activated.examples:
IF
Activated((CHARACTER)_Guard)
AND
DB_CRIME_Guards_WaitForGuardActivation(_Guard,_InterrogationCrimeID)
AND
DB_CRIME_Guards_Called(_OriginalCrimeID,_InterrogationCrimeID,_Caller,_Guard,_Criminal,_InterrogationType,_Region,_X,_Y,_Z)
THEN
NOT DB_CRIME_Guards_WaitForGuardActivation(_Guard,_InterrogationCrimeID);
PROC_CRIME_Guards_CallFoundGuardDelayed(_Caller,_OriginalCrimeID,_Guard);

AllLoadedFlagsInPresetReceivedEvent.description: Event is triggered when all flags from the preset are loaded.

AnimationEvent.description:
Thrown whenever the animation system (Genome) throws the script event _EventName from the blueprint assigned to _Object.
Genome events need to be specifically marked for them to be sent to script.
Further InformationWhen a level or savegame is loaded, objects are set to their desired animation state, which in turn will send animation events. The boolean _WasFromLoad is used to intercept or ignore these animation events.
For gameplay logic you will generally only want to intercept animation events called during gameplay, when _WasFromLoad is 0.

ChangeAppearanceCompleted.description: This event arrives when a player uses the Magic Mirror to change character’s appearance and confirms the choices.

StartAttack.description:
Thrown when a game object, _Attacker begins an attack against another game object, _Defender.
_Defender: The character that was attacked.
_AttackOwner: The character that owns the _Attacker (e.g. in case of summons, or in case of gravity changes such as fall damage.
_Attacker: The object that attacked _Defender.
_StoryActionID: the story action ID of the attack.
StartAttack.examples:
IF
StartAttack(_, _, _NPC, _StoryActionID)
AND
_NPC != NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
AND
IsCharacter(_NPC, 1)
THEN
PROC_TryStartNPCAttackAD((CHARACTER)_NPC, _StoryActionID);

CharacterDisarmed.description: Sent when _Character is disarmed of an _Item that was equipped in the slot _SlotName.

AttachedToPartyGroup.description:
This event is raised when _Character player character is chained to another player character, either via a player action or through AttachToPartyGroup.
Further InformationWhen chaining a character to a different group, the event DetachedFromPartyGroup  event is not guaranteed to arrive before this event.
You can iterate over DB_Players and use InSamePartyGroup to check to which player(s) they have been chained.
AttachedToPartyGroup.examples:
IF
AttachedToPartyGroup(_Character)
AND
IsControlled(_Character, 0)
AND
DB_PartyMembers(_Player)
AND
DB_ShadowCurse_NotPartyProtected(_Player)
AND
InSamePartyGroup(_Player, _Character, 1)
THEN
DB_ShadowCurse_NotPartyProtected(_Character);

ChangeAppearanceCancelled.description: This event arrives when a player uses the Magic Mirror to change character’s appearance and back out of that menu without saving any changes.

CharacterLootedCharacter.description: Thrown when one character loots the other character.
CharacterLootedCharacter.examples:
IF
CharacterLootedCharacter(_Player, _FlamingFistCorpse)
AND
DB_GlobalFlag(_FlamingSpy_Knows_HiddenCorpse)
AND
DB_PartOfTheTeam(_Player)
THEN
PROC_HideMarker(_Player, "HAV_FlamingfistCorpse");

BackgroundGoalRewarded.description: Event that fires when a character successfully completes a background goal triggered via call AddBackgroundGoal. This means it didn’t fail for the reasons listed in: event BackgroundGoalFailed (e.g. goal was already completed).

CastSpell.description:
Entity that has started casting a spell.
Parameters:
_Caster: who or what is casting the spell. Items can also send these events.
_Spell: name of the spell including its spell category (Category_SpellName).
_SpellType: type of the spell.
_SpellElement: element of the spell.
_StoryActionID: story ID of the action of casting the spell. Can be useful to link casting a spell with applying a status.Further InformationList of spell types and elements can be found in UsingSpell.
CastSpell.examples:
IF
CastSpell((CHARACTER)_Player, "Raphael_RestoreSoul",_,_,_)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
QRY_OnlyOnce("SoulRestored")
THEN
PROC_TryStartAD((DIALOGRESOURCE)_AD_Raphael_Combat_SoulIsBack, _Monitor);
CastSpell.seeAlso:
UseSpell
AddSpell
RemoveSpell
UseSpellAtPosition
CastedSpell
CastSpellFailed
LearnedSpell
StartedPreviewingSpell
UsingSpell
UsingSpellAtPosition
UsingSpellInTrigger
UsingSpellOnTarget
CanLearnSpell
CanShowSpellForCharacter
GetSpellFromSet
HasSpell
IsSpellActive
SpellHasSpellFlag

BaseFactionChanged.description: Fired when the base faction of _Target changes from _OldFaction to _NewFaction (e.g. via call SetFaction).

AttemptedDisarm.description: Sent when a _Character finishes trying to disarming a _DisarmableItem with an _ItemUsedToDisarm, with 0 in the _Bool if they failed to disarm it and 1 if they succeeded.

AppearTeleportFailed.description:
Event that fires whenever the AppearAt call fails.
Parameters:
_Character - The character that tried to teleport
_AppearEvent - The original (STRING)_AppearEvent) present on the AppearAt call that failed.Further InformationAppearAt call does not work with corpses (use SetOnStage instead), so calling AppearAt on corpses does not fire this event, even if the call fails to make the corpse appear.
Most commonly, the cause of failure is lack of free area for the entity to be teleported to.
If the original AppearAt call was called with _PreventTeleportOnFailure(1) this event will not fire even if the call failed, since the teleport attempt is prevented.
AppearTeleportFailed.examples:
IF
AppearTeleportFailed(_Ogre, _)
AND
DB_FOR_SchoolOgre_SummonPlayer(_Ogre, _Player)
THEN
SetOnStage(_Ogre, 0);
NOT DB_FOR_SchoolOgre_SummonPlayer(_Ogre, _Player);
AppearTeleportFailed.seeAlso:
Teleported
ItemTeleported
TeleportedToCamp
TeleportedFromCamp
TeleportToFromCamp
TeleportToWaypoint
TeleportToFleeWaypoint

CharacterReservedUserIDChanged.description:
Thrown when a character gets assigned to a user, or when a character no longer is assigned to any user.
Further InformationA character can either be explicitly assigned to a user from story, or by the game engine when a new user connects to a game. In both cases, this event gets thrown.

CharacterMoveToAndTalkFailed.description:
This event is thrown when the _Character that is in the middle of performing this call CharacterMoveToAndTalk fails to move and start a dialogue with their _Target. The _MoveId holds the value of the movement that is to be cancelled and the _Reason is the specific reason that the dialogue and movement should fail.
(Examples of _Reason include: “Attacked”, “CharacterDied" and “TargetDied”)
Further InformationThrown when a CharacterMoveToAndTalk call fails as the _Character does not exist or is dead, if _Target does not exist or is dead/destroyed or is in a different level, if it takes longer than _TimeOut seconds seconds to reach _Target, or if pathfinding fails then a CharacterMoveToAndTalkFailed event will be thrown instead.

CharacterLoadedInPreset.description: Event is triggered when character _Character from a preset is loaded.

CharacterLeftParty.description:
Thrown when _Character has left the party.
Further InformationCan be triggered when a party summoned creature disappears, removing a character through RemovePartyFollower or RemoveAllPartyFollowers, or when a party follower dies or making a player into an NPC with MakeNPC.

ArmedTrapUsed.description:
Sent instead of UseFinished if a character attempts to Use an item that has is an armed trap.
Further InformationTypically, this event is used to trigger the actual behaviour of the trap.

DifficultyChanged.description:
Deprecated. Do not use.
Use RulesetModifierChangedString instead.

CharacterMoveFailedUseJump.description:
Throws this event when the _Character fails to move to a specific location fails to move to a location that could potentially be reached by jumping.
Further InformationThis event does not guarantee that the location the player clicks onto can be 100% reached by jumping as only simple checks are done to verify that it is a location that probably allows the player to jump to.

CombatEnded.description:
Sent when a combat ends, including for the "old" combat if it got merged with another one.
Parameters:
_CombatGuid: GUID of the combat that just ended.
CombatEnded.examples:
IF
CombatEnded(_CombatID)
AND
DB_SorcerousSundries_LorroakanCombat(_CombatID)
AND
DB_Defeated(_Lorroakan)
THEN
NOT DB_BlockedWaypointZone((TRIGGER)_RamaitzhsTower_RegionTrigger);
PROC_SorcerousSundries_RolanTryTeleportToRamazith((CHARACTER)_Prodigy);
CombatEnded.seeAlso:
EndCombat
CombatIsActive
IsInCombat
CombatStarted
EnterCombat
LeftCombat
FleeFromCombat

CharacterMoveToCancelled.description: Thrown when the _Character’s movement is cancelled. The specific movement can be tracked through the _MoveID.
CharacterMoveToCancelled.examples:
IF
CharacterMoveToCancelled(_Char,_ID)
AND
DB_CharacterMovement(_Char,_Event,_ID)
AND
IsInCombat(_Char,0)
THEN
PROC_CharacterMoveTo_Clear(_Char,_ID,"Cancelled");
PROC_CharacterMoveToCancelled(_Char,_Event);

EnteredChasm.description:
Triggers when an _Object entity gets pushed by _Cause entity into a _Chasm chasm seeder.
The position of a character before getting thrown into a chasm is returned through _fallbackPos parameters.
Further InformationBy default the object dies if fallen into a chasm, but the event helps to capture the moment before it happens to do something different, but a EnterChasmProcessed is required to suspend the default logic.

CharacterJoinedParty.description:
Thrown when _Character has joined the party.
Further InformationCan be triggered by casting a summon spell, adding a character through AddPartyFollower or making an NPC into a player with MakePlayer.

Deactivated.description: Event is triggered when object _Object is deactivated.
Deactivated.examples:
IF
Deactivated(_Hag)
AND
DB_GlobalFlag((FLAG)_Hag_State_ReadyForLair)
AND
NOT DB_GlobalFlag((FLAG)_Hag_State_HagInLair)
AND
NOT DB_GlobalFlag((FLAG)_Hag_State_HagDefeated)
THEN
SetEntityEvent(_Hag, "HagEscape_PastAtHatch");

Dying.description:
An event sent when a _Character is progressing to the state of “Dead”, but has not yet reached this. Useful to intercept something that needs to be established before the character dies, but has not yet reached that state.
Further InformationThis is not the same as being at Death’s Door - It is specifically when the character is for all intents and purposes a dead character, just in the process of becoming one.

BackgroundGoalFailed.description:
Event fires when a background goal was attempted to be completed/rewarded, but failed for one of these reasons:
The goal was already unlocked for the same background.
Attempted to unlock a goal for a character with the wrong/no background.
Goal doesn’t have an associated background defined.
Goal GUID is invalid.
Character is invalid.

CastedSpell.description:
Entity that has finished casting a spell and did not fail. Thrown after CastSpell.
Parameters:
_Caster: who or what casted the spell. Items can also send these events.
_Spell: name of the spell including its spell category (Category_SpellName).
