_SpellType: type of the spell.
_SpellElement: element of the spell.
_StoryActionID: story ID of the action of casting the spell. Can be useful to link casting a spell with applying a status.Further InformationList of spell types and elements can be found in UsingSpell.
CastedSpell.seeAlso:
UseSpell
AddSpell
RemoveSpell
UseSpellAtPosition
CastSpell
CastSpellFailed
LearnedSpell
StartedPreviewingSpell
UsingSpell
UsingSpellAtPosition
UsingSpellInTrigger
UsingSpellOnTarget
CanLearnSpell
CanShowSpellForCharacter
GetSpellFromSet
HasSpell
IsSpellActive
SpellHasSpellFlag

CanBeLootedCapabilityChanged.description:
Event that fires whenever an entity’s property “Lootable” is changed.
Parameters:
_LootingTarget - The entity which had the looting capability changed.
_canBeLooted - “1” if the target can be looted, “0” if not.Further InformationTrough script, you can use SetCharacterLootable call to change the “Lootable” property of a character. That call will trigger this event.
Note that even though _LootingTarget takes a GUIDSTRING type argument, the “Lootable” property exists only for CHARACTER type entities.
CanBeLootedCapabilityChanged.examples:
IF
CanBeLootedCapabilityChanged(_Character, 0)
AND
DB_OwnedCorpse((CHARACTER)_Character, _Character)
THEN
PROC_ClearCorpseOwner(_Character);

CharacterMadePlayer.description:
Thrown when _Character becomes a playable character.
Further InformationThis event will most commonly be the result of MakePlayer.
This event is also thrown for summons or party followers becoming playable characters.
If you want to react to a character joining the party, use CharacterJoinedParty instead.

AttackedBy.description:
Thrown when a game object, _Defender is attacked by another game object _Attacker.
_Defender: The character that was attacked.
_Attacker: The object that attacked _Defender.
_AttackerOwner: The character that owns the _Attacker (e.g. in case of summons, or in case of gravity changes such as fall damage.
_DamageType: Can be one of the following:
"Slashing"
"Piercing"
"Bludgeoning"
"Acid"
"Thunder"
"Necrotic"
"Fire"
"Lightning"
"Cold"
"Psychic"
"Poison"
"Radiant"
"Force"
"" (an empty string, e.g. when applying a status)
_DamageAmount: The amount of hitpoints lost due to the attack. Can be 0.
_DamageCause: Can be one of the following:
"Surface_Move": The _Defender moved into or through a harmful surface (poison/fire)
"Surface_Create": A harmful surface was created under the _Defender.  (WARNING!: this one currently never triggers, because when the surface gets created the engine does not yet know whether it will have a harmful effect or not.)
"Surface_Status": A surface applied a harmful status to the _Defender.
"Status_Enter": A harmful status was applied to the _Defender.
"Status_Tick": A harmful status ticked and applied its (negative) effects to the _Defender.
"Attack": A regular attack or harmful spell was cast on the _Defender.
"Offhand": The _Defender was attacked with an Offhand weapon
"Inventory_Item_Throw": the _Defender was thrown from the inventory of the character and received damage as a result
"World_Item_Throw": the _Defender was thrown while it was lying in the world and received damage as a result
"Passive": a harmful passive was applied to the _Defender.
"Aura": an aura caused a (different) harmful status to be applied to the _Defender.
"EnvironmentalInfluence": a harmful environmental influence was applied to the _Defender.
_StoryActionID: the story action ID of the attack.Further InformationAttacking consists of
Casting a spell that has any of the following SpellFlags: IsHarmful
Note: All regular attacks are handled as spells internally.
Applying a status that has any of the following StatusPropertyFlags: InitiateCombat
Applying a harmful status through a passive (?)
Applying a harmful status through an aura
Applying a harmful status through an environmental influence
_AttackerOwner can be the same as _Attacker; _AttackerOwner will be different in case of summoned characters, or in the case of items.
If the _Attacker is unknown, this event will not be thrown (i.e., _Attacker will never be NULL).

EnterCombatFailed.description:
Event that is thrown after a Character or an Item fails to enter combat following call EnterCombat.
Parameters:
_OpponentLeft: Object that tried to Join Combat.
_OpponentRight: Object that _OpponentLeft tried to enter combat against.Further InformationThe 2 arguments are the same 2 arguments from EnterCombat. So, a failed EnterCombat(_A, _B) will produce EnterCombatFailed(_A, _B).
Entering combat can fail because the objects are too far apart or not hostile to each other (nor to one of the other participants in the same combat, if one was already ongoing), because one of them is invisible, is offstage, cannot join combats, or is PermaDefeated.
Note that invisibility does get removed immediately after removing an invisibility status. Wait for the corresponding event StatusRemoved before making a EnterCombat call.
EnterCombatFailed.examples:
IF
EnterCombatFailed(_Switch, _Player)
AND
DB_SteelWatchFoundry_DeadMansSwitches(_, (ITEM)_Switch, _)
AND
DB_Players(_Players)
THEN
TeleportTo(_SteelWathcFoundry_FTBHelper, _Player, "TeleportedFTBHelper", 0, 0, 0, 0, 1);
ForceTurnBasedMode((CHARACTER)_Player, 1);
EnterCombatFailed.seeAlso:
CombatEnded
EndCombat
CombatIsActive
IsInCombat
CombatStarted
EnterCombat

Close.description: Sets the _Item defined to be closed.

Donated.description:
The event is triggered when the _FromObject donates something to _ToObject.
Further InformationRequires using a Trade dialog node with type “Donate”.
Donated.examples:
IF
Donated(_MovedItem, _Player, _Helper, _ItemValue)
AND
DB_Players((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
DB_Offerings((CHARACTER)_Helper, _, _)
THEN
PROC_EvaluateTradeDeal(_Player, _Helper, _ItemValue, 0);
SetFlag(_Event_GiveSomething, _Player);

GameModeStarted.description:
This is thrown when a new game is started.
Further InformationAt this point, no level or objects have been loaded yet, nor have any users been added to the game. Do not reference any objects nor perform any calls that refer to players or users at this point.

CameraReachedNode.description:
Sent every time the camera travelling along a _Spline reach a node.
Further Information_Spline is the UUID of the camera spline
_Character is the controlled character of the client whose camera is moving
_Event: In the editor, you can add a script event to a spline node. If one is filled in, it will be passed.
_Index is the number of the node along the spline
_Last is 1 if it is the last node (and the camera will thus stop) and 0 in every other case.

DeathTypeChanged.description:
A very specific type of event, called if a character already has a registered type of death (such as DEATHTYPE:Physical) and this has changed due to player actions (such as blowing up a body, making it DEATHTYPE:Explode)
Further InformationThis call relies on the _Entity already being dead, firstly, so will not fire on their death.
Secondly, the output of _DeathType will be the Entity’s original type of death, rather than what it has changed to.
DeathTypeChanged.examples:
IF
DeathTypeChanged((GUIDSTRING)_Entity, (DEATHTYPE)_DeathType)
AND
_NewDeathType == DEATHTYPE.Explode
THEN
SetFlag(_Flag)

DestroyedBy.description:
Thrown when item gets destroyed. DestroyingBy is sent shortly before that in the same frame.
_Item: The item that was destroyed.
_Destroyer: The character that destroyed the item. Can be NULL
_DestroyerOwner: The owner of the character that destroyed the item if it was a summon or party follower, otherwise _Destroyer
_StoryActionID: the story action ID of the operation that destroyed the item.

CrimeEnabled.description:
This event is thrown when a disturbance with name _Crime is enabled on _Character.
Further InformationDisturbances are enabled by default. Therefore, to receive this event, you must have disabled a disturbance beforehand. The event will be thrown when you re-enable it. As such, reacting to this event is usually an unnecessary extra step.

DetachedFromPartyGroup.description:
This event is raised when _Character player character is unchained from another player character, either via a player action or through DetachFromPartyGroup.
Further InformationWhen chaining a character to a different group, this event is not guaranteed to arrive before the AttachedToPartyGroup event.
DetachedFromPartyGroup.examples:
IF
DetachedFromPartyGroup(_Character)
AND
IsControlled(_Character, 0)
AND
NOT QRY_IsNotAloneInPartyGroup(_Character)
THEN
DB_ShadowCurse_NotPartyProtected(_Character);

DoorTemplateClosing.description:
Sent when item with Door use action is closed, shortly before it sends Closed event.
_ItemTemplate - root template of the door
_Item - the door
_Character - character that triggered the door to close

CreditsEnded.description: An event that fires when the credits have finished rolling.
CreditsEnded.examples:
IF
CreditsEnded()
AND
NOT DB_IsEditor(1)
AND
QRY_OnlyOnce("CreditsClosed")
THEN
DB_SceneStarted(1);

CharacterPickpocketSuccess.description:
Sent after a player successfully pickpockets an item from an NPC.
Further InformationThe _Item is the item reference before it potentially got merged into a stack in the player’s inventory.
Similarly, _ItemTemplate, _Amount and _GoldValue are passed along as parameters to be able to get the value of the stolen goods and to be able to return them in case the stolen item does get merged into a stack in the player’s inventory. The CrimeAddEvidence query accepts these extra parameters as well for this reason.
In general, don’t use this call directly, but rely on the crime system and react to the EmptyPocketNoticed crime. Otherwise you will probably both get a reaction from the crime system and from your scripting (unless you explicitly disable the EmptyPocketNoticed crime for your character using PROC_CharacterDisableCrime(_Char, "EmptyPocketNoticed"))
CharacterPickpocketSuccess.examples:
IF
CharacterPickpocketSuccess(_Thief, _Victim, _Item, _ItemTemplate, _Amount, _GoldValue)
AND
CharacterIsCrimeEnabled(_Victim, "EmptyPocketNoticed", 0)
THEN
PROC_MakeNPCHostile(_Thief,_Victim);

DisappearOutOfSightToCancelled.description:
An event that gets thrown when a disappear task is cancelled for any reason before it finishes.
If the _MovementID for the task is set to -1, the event will not be thrown.Disappear tasks are created by DisappearOutOfSightTo and are completed when the character reaches the target destination and is set off stage.
Parameters:
_Character: The character upon which the disappear task was called
_MovementID: An integer added to every disappear task.
Using the PROCs to create a disappear task always creates a valid _MovementID greater than -1
Leaving it undefined in DisappearOutOfSightTo defaults to setting it to -1Further InformationCommon reasons for a disappear task to get cancelled:
Character executing the task dies
Character executing the task is attacked
Character executing the task enters combat
Movement stopped by Osiris through the PurgeOsirisQueue callIn Osiris, the event is used to catch a cancelled disappear task such as from the systemic ways described above and resume it when the character is available again.

CombatPaused.description:
Event that fires whenever a combat is paused.
Parameters:
_CombatGuid: The combat that got paused.Further InformationMost usual the reason for combat getting paused is a dialog starting mid-combat, but can be done directly from script using call PauseCombat call. Use call ResumeCombat call to remove the pause.
A paused combat will not progress to the next round even if all objects in the initiative queue have ended their turn.
CombatPaused.examples:
IF
CombatPaused(_ID)
AND
DB_GLO_NarrativeCombat_ID("FinalCombat",_ID)
AND
NOT DB_Helm_FinalCombatPaused(1)
THEN
DB_Helm_FinalCombatPaused(1);
CombatPaused.seeAlso:
PauseCombat
ResumeCombat
CombatStarted
CombatEnded
IsInCombat
EnteredCombat
LeftCombat
CombatResumed

CastSpellFailed.description:
Entity that tried to cast a spell but failed. Thrown after CastSpell.
Parameters:
_Caster: who or what casted the spell. Items can also send these events.
_Spell: name of the spell including its spell category (Category_SpellName).
_SpellType: type of the spell.
_SpellElement: element of the spell.
_StoryActionID: story ID of the action of casting the spell. Can be useful to link casting a spell with applying a status.Further InformationList of spell types and elements can be found in UsingSpell.
CastSpellFailed.examples:
IF
CastSpellFailed((CHARACTER)_Gale_Tressym, "Target_MistyStep", _, _, _)
AND
DB_TressymFireballsPlayer(_Player)
THEN
PROC_Tara_FliesAway();
NOT DB_TressymFireballsPlayer(_Player);
CastSpellFailed.seeAlso:
UseSpell
AddSpell
RemoveSpell
UseSpellAtPosition
CastedSpell
CastSpell
LearnedSpell
StartedPreviewingSpell
UsingSpell
UsingSpellAtPosition
UsingSpellInTrigger
UsingSpellOnTarget
CanLearnSpell
CanShowSpellForCharacter
GetSpellFromSet
HasSpell
IsSpellActive
SpellHasSpellFlag

FleeFromCombat.description:
Event thrown when a Character flees a combat.
Parameters:
_Participant: Character that fled the combat.
_CombatGuid: Combat that was fled from.Further InformationSystemically leaving combat due to large distance is not considered fleeing from combat and will therefore not fire a FleeFromCombat event. The event is only fired when players manually flee a combat with the “Flee Combat” button of the UI.

CompanionSelectedForUser.description:
A player with _UserID has created their Daisy _Character in the character creator.
Further InformationDaisy is the internal name for the protector/dream character that is created at character creation.
CompanionSelectedForUser.examples:
IF
CompanionSelectedForUser(_Daisy,_UserID)
AND
DB_Avatars(_Avatar)
AND
GetReservedUserID(_Avatar,_UserID)
AND
NOT DB_GLO_DaisyDreams_Relationships(_Avatar, _)
AND
GetIdentity(_Daisy,0,_Gender)
THEN
DB_GLO_DaisyDreams_Relationships(_Avatar, _Daisy);
PROC_DaisyDesire_SetDaisyFor(_Avatar, _Daisy, _Gender);

CharacterSelectedForUser.description: A player with _UserID has created their _Character in the character creator.

DestroyingBy.description:
The event is thrown when item gets destroyed. In the same frame, but later in execution order, DestroyedBy is sent. A few frames afterwards, TemplateDestroyedBy is sent.
_Item: The item that is being destroyed.
_Destroyer: The character that is destroying the item. Can be NULL
_DestroyerOwner: The owner of the character that is destroying the item if it was a summon or party follower, otherwise _Destroyer
_StoryActionID: the story action ID of the operation that destroyed the item.
DestroyingBy.examples:
IF
DestroyingBy(_OwlBear_Egg,_,_,_)
AND
DB_CurrentLevel("WLD_Main_A")
AND
NOT DB_PermaDefeated(_OwlBear)
AND
QRY_OnlyOnce("FOR_OwlBear_Enrage")
THEN
SetFlag((FLAG)_Owlbear_State_EnragedBecauseCubOrEggDefeated);
SetTag(_OwlBear,(TAG)_AI_USESPELL);

CombatStarted.description: Thrown once a new combat with ID _CombatGuid has started and initialised.

CharacterOnCrimeSensibleActionNotificati.description:
This event fires when an NPC is selected to perform an Osiris reaction by the disturbance system.
Parameters:
_Character is the NPC performing the reaction.
_CrimeRegion is the crime region where the reaction is happening.
_CrimeID is the disturbance’s id.
_PriorityName is the name given to the Osiris reaction in stats.
_PrimaryDialog is the dialog specified for the action in stats.
_Criminal1..4 represents the criminals tallied for the crime.
_IsPrimary indicates whether the reaction is a primary reaction, The primary reaction is the one performed by the lead investigator. Everyone else elected as an investigator performs a secondary reaction.Further InformationThis event is one of the natural ones to hook into to create custom disturbance reactions.
If you create a new Osiris reaction in stats by giving it a custom name, you would need to use this call to make functioning script.
CharacterOnCrimeSensibleActionNotificati.examples:
IF
CharacterOnCrimeSensibleActionNotification(_NPC,_Region,_CrimeID,_PriorityName,_Dialog,_Criminal,_Criminal2,_Criminal3,_Criminal4,1)
AND
DB_DEN_GreetingReaction(_NPC,_)
AND
CrimeGetType(_CrimeID,"DEN_GreetingAD")
THEN
PROC_CharacterDisableCrime(_NPC, "DEN_GreetingAD");

DownedChanged.description:
DownedChanged gets thrown with _IsDowned 1 when _Character becomes downed.
DownedChanged gets thrown with _IsDowned 0 when _Character is no longer downed.
Further InformationUsed in low level systemic pipelines that allow scripting to check if a character becomes downed.

EnteredTrigger.description:
Thrown when a character enters a trigger that was registered for it.
Further InformationIf you register a trigger for a character while the character is currently inside the trigger, this event will also be thrown.
EnteredTrigger.examples:
IF
EnteredTrigger((CHARACTER)_Player, (TRIGGER)_DyingAttacker_ADTrigger)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
THEN
PROC_TryStartAD((DIALOGRESOURCE)_AD, _DyingAttacker);

DroppedBy.description:
Event that fires when a character _Mover drops _Object from their inventory into the world.
Further InformationCan be triggered by players manually dropping inventory objects, characters being disarmed, characters dropping items through Anubis or the Drop/DropTo calls being used.

Falling.description:
The _Entity has started falling. If someone else has caused this fall, they are passed as _Cause.
Further InformationWhen the fall ends, a Fell event is thrown.

HitProxy.description:
Event thrown when a _Proxy from a _Target receives an attack from _Attacker.
_Proxy: the physics object attached to the _Target.
_Target: the item or character that has the _Proxy that receives the attack.
_AttackerOwner: owner of the attacker. It can be the same character as _Attacker or can be different. For example, a summon will be displayed as an _Attacker, but the owner will be its summoner, but if a character attacks the proxy by themselve, it will be the same.
_Attacker: the direct attacker.
_StoryActionID: the ID of the action.
HitProxy.seeAlso:
GetHitProxyOwner
IsHitProxy
IsHitProxyOwner
AttachHitProxy
DetachHitProxy
DetachAllHitProxies
IterateHitProxies

CombatRoundStarted.description:
Event that is fired when a combat round starts for any specific combat.
Parameters:
_CombatGuid: Combat from which a new round has started.
_Round: Amount of rounds the combat has been going for, counting the one that fired this event.Further Information_Round starts counting from 1, not 0.
Be mindful with combat merges (event SwitchedCombat) if you need to keep track of rounds for script reasons.
CombatRoundStarted.examples:
IF
CombatRoundStarted(_CombatGuid, _Round)
AND
GetHostCharacter(_Player)
AND
IntegerToString(_Round, _RoundString)
AND
Concatenate("Round is: ", _RoundString, _FinalRes)
THEN
DebugText(_Player, _FinalRes);

CharacterSelectedAsBestUnavailableFallba.description:
This event fires in the event that an investigator currently busy with a lower priority disturbance is the only potential lead that can react to a higher priority disturbance.
Parameters:
_Character is the character selected as the fallback lead.
_CrimeRegion is the crime region in which the reaction takes place.
_UnavailableForCrimeID is the more important disturbance which has no lead.
_BusyCrimeID is the ID of the disturbance our fallback lead was involved in before the switch.Further InformationThis event is intended for generic scripting only, to support our disturbance system.
Usually characters that are selected in such a way end up starting combat with criminals.
CharacterSelectedAsBestUnavailableFallba.examples:
IF
CharacterSelectedAsBestUnavailableFallbackLead(_Char,_Region,_NewCrime,_OldCrime)
AND
CrimeHasProperty(_NewCrime, BackgroundReaction_db918cfb-a484-437c-93bb-8bc36fe98a9c, 0)
AND
IsSpeakerReserved(_Char,1)
AND
CrimeIsContinuous(_OldCrime,0)
THEN
PROC_ForceStopDialog(_Char);
PROC_CRIME_MakeTemporaryHostileToCriminalsOf(_Char, _OldCrime);
PROC_CRIME_MakeTemporaryHostileToCriminalsOf(_Char, _NewCrime);

CharacterStoleItem.description:
Thrown when _Character successfully stole _Item in the world.
Parameters:
(ITEM)_Item: the stolen item
(GUIDSTRING)_ItemRootTemplate: root template of the stolen item
(REAL)_X: X coordinate where the theft took place
(REAL)_Y: Y coordinate where the theft took place
(REAL)_Z: Z coordinate where the theft took place
(CHARACTER)_OldOwner: victim of the theft
(ITEM)_SrcContainer: container that used to contain the stolen item, NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000if the item was not in a container
(INTEGER)_Amount: how many of the items were stolen if they were a stack. Will always be 1 if item is not stackable.
(INTEGER)_GoldValue: total gold value that was stolen with _Amount taken into account.Further InformationStealing vs Pickpocketing
Pickpocketing an item throws different events:
CharacterPickpocketFailed on fail
CharacterPickpocketSuccess on success
CharacterStoleItem.examples:
IF
CharacterStoleItem(_Thief, _ZhentShipment_Chest, _, _X,_Y,_Z, _Victim, _, _, _)
AND
NOT DB_PartyMembers(_Victim)
AND
DB_PartyMembers(_Thief)
AND
NOT DB_GlobalFlag(_ZhentShipment_Quest_Intimidated)
AND
NOT DB_GlobalFlag(_ZhentShipment_State_ChestNotInPlace)
THEN
DB_ZhenShipment_ChestStolen(1);
PROC_GlobalSetFlagAndCache(_ZhentShipment_State_ChestNotInPlace);

EscortGroupLeaderChanged.description:
Triggered whenever there is a change in the leader of an escort group.
Parameters:
_OldLeader — The old leader of the group (can be NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 if there was no old leader)
_NewLeader — The new leader of the group (can be NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 if there is no new leader)
_Group — The name of the group leader that changed

CharacterTagEvent.description: Event that triggers for every iteration of IterateOriginTags with the defined string _Event.

ApprovalRatingChanged.description:
Thrown when an approval rating changed has been finished.
_RatingOwner: Typically the companion who offers the approval.
_RatingEntity: The character who is rated by the companion, typically the player.
_NewApproval: The new total approval rating of _RatingOwner
ApprovalRatingChanged.examples:
IF
ApprovalRatingChanged((CHARACTER)_DrowCommander, _Avatar, _Approval)
AND
_Approval > 39
AND
DB_ORI_Dating(_Avatar, (CHARACTER)_DrowCommander)
AND
GetFlag((FLAG)_State_Partnered, _Avatar, 0)
THEN
SetFlag((FLAG)_State_PartneredWithMinthara, _Avatar);

CharacterSelectedClimbOn.description:
Thrown when _Character right clicked an object and selected the Climb On action.
Further InformationThis event will be thrown before the climb, and will not show if this climb action will fail or not.

FlagLoadedInPresetEvent.description: The event is triggered when flag _Flag is loaded for object _Object when loading a preset.

CombatResumed.description:
Event that fires whenever a combat is resumed. For a combat to be resumed, it first needs to be paused.
Parameters:
_CombatGuid: The combat that was resumed.Further InformationCombats usually get paused due to dialogs starting mid-combat, but they can be manually paused using a call PauseCombat call. If you do so, make sure the combat gets eventually resumed, as this can easily create softlocks.
CombatResumed.examples:
IF
CombatResumed(_ID)
AND
DB_GLO_NarrativeCombat_ID("FinalCombat",_ID)
AND
DB_Helm_FinalCombatPaused(1)
THEN
NOT DB_Helm_FinalCombatPaused(1);
CombatResumed.seeAlso:
PauseCombat
ResumeCombat
CombatStarted
CombatEnded
IsInCombat
EnteredCombat
LeftCombat
CombatPaused

EnteredCombat.description: Thrown when _Object enters a combat with ID _CombatGuid.

Fell.description:
Thrown when an _Entity stops falling. If someone else has caused this fall, they are passed as _Cause.
Further InformationWhen it starts falling a Falling event is thrown.
After getting back up, a GotUp event is thrown.

CriticalHitBy.description:
Thrown when a character receives a critical hit from an attack by another character.
Further Information_AttackerOwner can be the same character as _Attacker. _AttackOwner will be different in the case of summoned characters.
CriticalHitBy.examples:
IF
CriticalHitBy(_Hag, _, _Player, _Id)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
THEN
PROC_TryStartAD(_AD, _Player);

LevelGameplayReady.description:
Thrown when a level has been loaded and is ready at both the server and client side, and the gameplay logic on the client side has also started running.
Further InformationSee  LevelGameplayStarted.

ArmorSetChanged.description: The event is triggered when the amor set of character _Character to _EArmorSet
ArmorSetChanged.examples:
IF
ArmorSetChanged((CHARACTER)_Player_Gale, _ArmourSet)
AND
NOT DB_Offstage(_GaleDouble)
THEN
PROC_SetArmourSet(_GaleDouble, _ArmourSet);

InventoryBoundChanged.description:
Thrown whenever an _Item changes whether it is bound to an inventory.
_IsBoundToInventory having a value of 0 means the _Item is no longer bound.
_IsBoundToInventory having a value of 1 means the _Item is now bound.
InventoryBoundChanged.examples:
IF
InventoryBoundChanged(_Item, 1)
AND
GetOwner(_Item, _Character)
THEN
PlayEffect(_Character, _ItemBoundVFX);

LevelUnloading.description:
Thrown when a level starts unloading.
Parameters:
_LevelName: The identifier name of the level that is being unloadedFurther InformationAt this point the level is still present on the server side, so all entities in the level are still available. Be careful with starting operations that may take a while to complete though, since they are about to be unloaded.

EnteredForceTurnBased.description: This event fires when an entity enters Turn based mode.
EnteredForceTurnBased.examples:
IF
EnteredForceTurnBased(_Player)
AND
DB_Players((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
NOT DB_GLO_Tutorials_IgnoreFTB((CHARACTER)_Player)
THEN
PROC_ShowUnifiedTutorialForPlayer((CHARACTER)_Player, (UNIFIEDTUTORIAL)_Turn_Based_Mode);

ApprovalRatingChangeAttempt.description:
Thrown when an approval rating change is attempted but has yet to complete.
_RatingOwner: Typically the companion who offers the approval.
_RatingEntity: The character who is rated by the companion, typically the player.
_AttemptedApprovalChange: The attempted delta approval change for _RatingOwner.
_ClampedApprovalChange: The old approval rating of _RatingOwner.
_NewApproval: The new total approval rating of _RatingOwnerFurther InformationSee ApprovalRatingChanged for when the approval change is completed.
ApprovalRatingChangeAttempt.examples:
IF
ApprovalRatingChangeAttempt(_Character, (CHARACTER)_DarkUrge, _, _, _)
AND
DB_ORI_DarkUrge((CHARACTER)_DarkUrge)
AND
NOT DB_ORI_DarkUrge_FavouriteExcluded(_Character)
AND
NOT DB_ORI_DarkUrge_FavouriteCharacter(_)
AND
DB_PartOfTheTeam(_Character)
THEN
DB_ORI_DarkUrge_FavouriteCharacter(_Character);

LongRestStartFailed.description: Event is triggered after the RequestLongRest and the long rest has failed to start.

Closed.description:
This event is thrown when the _Item defined is closed.
The _Item is usually a container or a door.
Closed.examples:
IF
Closed(_Door)
AND
DB_DoorsPreviousState(_Door, "open")
THEN
NOT DB_DoorsPreviousState(_Door, "open");

Died.description:
An event sent when a _Character has successfully progressed to the state of being Dead.
Further InformationBe aware that this is not dependent on a character being in DB_Dead as there are clauses to ensure they are added to this in the eventuality that this event fires.  This is a deeper rooted code understanding of the character being dead.

LevelTemplateLoaded.description:
Sent when a level template has been loaded.
Parameters:
_LevelTemplate: The level template that got loaded.Further InformationLevel templates are use for multiple purposes
Dividing a main level into smaller chunks that are faster/easier to work on. When loading a main level, such as WLD_Main_A, you will get this event for every sublevel/level template embedded in it
It is possible to swap out one such level template for another, as happens during the morning → night swap of camp.
Platforms are also level templates, and hence for them you will also receive this event.
LevelTemplateLoaded.examples:
IF
LevelTemplateLoaded(_CampCorner)
AND
DB_ActiveCamp(_Camp)
AND
DB_Camp_PersonalCornerLevelTemplate(_Camp, _Camper, _CampCorner)
THEN
PROC_CampSwap_UpdateAnubisCampPos(_Camp, _Camper);

Combined.description:
This event is fired when items are successfully combined together.
Eg:
Combining a Sussur Flower with the energy generator at the Arcane Tower in the Underdark.
Combining poison with the Booze Tub at the Goblin Camp.Further Information_Character represents the character who combined the items.
_NewItem represents the new item that is created after the combination. (the end product of the combination is defined in the Stats Editor under ItemCombos)
_Item_1 to _Item_5 represent the items that are used when the combination is performed.

LeveledUp.description:
Sent when a character levels up.
Further InformationThis event is thrown once per level the character gains. For Players, this is thrown when the character gains enough XP to advance to the next level, even if they don't spend their new points.
LeveledUp.examples:
IF
LeveledUp(_Avatar)
AND
DB_Avatars(_Avatar)
AND
GetLevel(_Avatar, _Level)
AND
_Level >= 5
THEN
SetFlag(_Flag, NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, 0);

MainPerformerStarted.description:
Event is triggered when a _Character plays an instrument.
Further Information_Event
Refers to the song spell that is being played.
eg: "Bard_Perform_MainTheme"
MainPerformerStarted.examples:
IF
MainPerformerStarted(_BardPlayer,_SpellInfix)
AND
DB_Players(_BardPlayer)
AND
DB_CRIME_MusicalPerformance(_ID,_BardPlayer,_DisturbanceName)
AND
DB_CRIME_MusicalPerformance_Status("PERFORM_POSITIVE",_DisturbanceName,"OnStatusApply")
AND
DB_CRIME_MusicalPerformance_Status("PERFORM_POSITIVE",_NPCJoningDisturbance,"OnMainPerformerStart")
AND
QRY_CRIME_MusicalPerformance_CorrectSpellInfix(_SpellInfix)
AND
DB_QRYRTN_CRIME_MusicalPerformance_CorrectedSpellInfix(_CorrectedSpellInfix)
AND
CrimeGetNewID(_CrimeID)
THEN
DB_CRIME_MusicalPerformanceSpell(_CrimeID,_CorrectedSpellInfix);
DB_CRIME_MusicalPerformance(_CrimeID,_BardPlayer,_NPCJoningDisturbance);
PROC_CharacterRegisterCrime(_BardPlayer,_NPCJoningDisturbance,_BardPlayer,NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000,_CrimeID);

FollowerCantUseItem.description: Thrown when _Character that is a summon tries to use a generic item (potions, scrolls, etc.), which it is not allowed to do.

LongRestFinished.description: Event triggered when finishing a long rest and waking up in the morning.

HappyWithDeal.description: Thrown when a player tries to perform a trade or barter.

PartyPresetLoaded.description: The event is triggered when a party preset _PartyPreset is fully loaded in level _LevelName.
PartyPresetLoaded.examples:
IF
PartyPresetLoaded(_,_)
AND
DB_Debug_PartyPreset_Level(_Level)
THEN
NOT DB_Debug_PartyPreset_Level(_Level);

ItemTeleported.description:
Event that is thrown whenever an ITEM type entity is teleported trough any means.
Further InformationNote that unlike event Teleported , the event does not inform about cause or spell used for the teleportation.
Items only fire this event if existing directly on the World. Items in the inventory of characters or items inside containers will not fire this event.(ITEM)_Target - Item that got teleported.
(REAL)_OldX, (REAL)_OldY, (REAL)_OldZ - Old position of the target.
(REAL)_NewX, (REAL)_NewY, (REAL)_NewZ - New position of the target.
ItemTeleported.examples:
IF
ItemTeleported((ITEM)_Object, _, _, _, _, _, _)
AND
QRY_IsExistingItem(_Object, 1)
AND
DB_TeleportToAndOffStage((GUIDSTRING)_Object)
THEN
PROC_SetOnStage(_Object, 0)
ItemTeleported.seeAlso:
Teleported
TeleportedToCamp
TeleportedFromCamp
TeleportToFromCamp
TeleportToWaypoint
TeleportToFleeWaypoint
AppearTeleportFailed

LeftTrigger.description:
Thrown when a character leaves a trigger that was registered for it.
Further InformationIf you unregister a trigger for a character while the character was inside the trigger and the trigger was registered for that character, this event will also be thrown.
If a character dies while it is in a trigger that is registered for it, this event will NOT be thrown.
LeftTrigger.examples:
IF
LeftTrigger(_Char, _SlowZone)
AND
IsTagged(_Char, ILLITHID, 1)
AND
DB_GLO_Brainquakes_SlowZone(_ID, _SlowZone, _VB)
THEN
RemoveStatus(_Char, "SLOW", NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000);

LevelLoaded.description:
Thrown when the level has been loaded and is ready at the server side, but not yet at the client side.
Parameters:
_LevelName: The identifier name of the level that has been loadedFurther InformationSee LevelGameplayStarted.

MissedBy.description:
Thrown when a character misses an attack from another character.
Further Information_AttackerOwner can be the same character as _Attacker.  _AttackOwner will be different in the case of summoned characters.
MissedBy.examples:
IF
MissedBy(_Hag, _ThrowOwner, _Thrower, _Id)
AND
DB_HagQuestGrenade_ThrowId(_Id)
THEN
PROC_QuestGrenadeThrownAtHag(_Id, _Thrower);

OnCrimeConfrontationDone.description:
Sent when an NPC finishes a confrontation.
Parameters:
_CrimeID: the disturbance/crime’s ID
_Investigator: the NPC that performed the confrontation action
_WasLead: 1 if _Investigator was the lead investigator, 0 if not.
_Criminal1 .. _Criminal4: the criminals that were confronted. This can be a subset of all criminals of the disturbance/crime. Some can be NULL.Further InformationA confrontation ends when script calls CrimeConfrontationDone from Osiris, or ConfrontationDone from Anubis. Starting a confrontation stops any timeouts for a disturbance/crime, and unless you end the confrontation explicitly, the NPC will stay stuck in the confrontation state forever (since we cannot predict how long the dialog or other reaction will take).
When a lead finishes the confrontation for a non-continuous disturbance/crime, it will resolve successfully.
OnCrimeConfrontationDone.examples:
IF
OnCrimeConfrontationDone(_CrimeID, _LeadInvestigator, 1, _Criminal1, _Criminal2, _Criminal3, _Criminal4)
AND
CrimeGetType(_CrimeID, _Type)
AND
QRY_CRIME_IsCrimeFamilyMember(_Type, "MoveForbiddenItem")
THEN
PROC_TryCreatePickUpEvidenceCrime(_CrimeID, "PickUpEvidence", _LeadInvestigator);

EndTheDayRequested.description: This is for internal use only. Part of a complex handshake between code and story for the camp and long rest pipeline.

OnCriminalMergedWithCrime.description:
Sent when a new criminal gets added to an existing disturbance/crime.
Parameters:
_CrimeID: the disturbance/crime’s ID
_Criminal: the added crminalFurther InformationThis can either happen because two disturbances/crimes merged, or because a new criminal was explicitly added.
OnCriminalMergedWithCrime.examples:
IF
OnCriminalMergedWithCrime(_CrimeID,_Criminal)
AND
DB_PlayerTrespassing(_,_CrimeID,_Trigger)
AND
NOT DB_PlayerTrespassing(_Criminal,_CrimeID,_Trigger)
THEN
DB_PlayerTrespassing(_Criminal,_CrimeID,_Trigger);

ObscuredStateChanged.description:
Thrown for _Object entity when its obscurity state is changed.
Further InformationObscured state represents D&D lighting rules which impact gameplay:
Heavily obscured = No visibility
Lightly obscured (half lit) = Half visibility
Clear (un-obscured) = Full visibility
ObscuredStateChanged.examples:
IF
ObscuredStateChanged(_Player, "Clear")
AND
DB_InCursedArea(_Player)
AND
NOT DB_InBlightedArea(_Player)
AND
DB_Players((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
HasActiveStatus(_Player, "SCL_AREA", 1)
AND
NOT DB_CantTalk_IgnoreCombat(_Player)
AND
QRY_OncePerUserAndNearbyPlayers_IsSet(_Player, "EntryPoint_VB")
AND
QRY_OncePerUserAndNearbyPlayers(_Player, "EntryPoint_RemovedCurseByLight")
THEN
PROC_TryStartAD((DIALOGRESOURCE)_PAD_CurseRemovedByLight, _Player);

OnCrimeMergedWith.description:
Sent when one disturbance/crime gets merged with another one.
Parameters:
_OldCrimeID: the crime into which the other crime got merged.
_NewCrimeID: the crime that got merged into the other oneFurther InformationAfterwards, _NewCrimeID no longer exists.
When merging, the criminals and evidence from _NewCrimeID are added to _OldCrimeID
Crime can only merge if their combined number of criminals (after removing duplicates) does not exceed four, and if their merge conditions are fulfilled.
Often used to update databases with information about a particular crime, so they refer to the newly merged crime.

EnteredLevel.description:
This event is sent for every character and item that enters the active level (LevelLoaded), or the level currently being loaded.
Parameters:
_Object: the character or item that entered the level
_ObjectRootTemplate: the root template of the character/item
_Level: the name of the level in which the entity got loadedFurther InformationThis event fires in all cases where a character/item enters a level, e.g. when
initially loading into a level, all items/characters in that level enter it
dynamically loading a level template for the first time (LoadLevelTemplate)
teleporting a global entity from another level to the current one (TeleportTo)
spawning a new character/item (e.g. CreateAt, or spawning a summon)This event does not fire for entities already in a level when loading a savegame, or when returning to a level that you already visited.

InventorySharingChanged.description:
Thrown whenever a _Character changes whether they allow sharing their inventory.
_SharingChanged having a value of 0 means the _Character no longer shares their inventory through the magic pockets system.
_SharingChanged having a value of 1 means the _Character now shares their inventory through the magic pockets system.
InventorySharingChanged.examples:
IF
InventorySharingChanged(_Character, 0)
AND
IsInInventoryOf(_QuestItem, _Character, 1)
THEN
Drop((ITEM)_QuestItem);

OnCrimeInvestigatorSwitchedState.description:
Thrown when an investigator of a disturbance switches to a new investigation/reaction state.
Parameters:
_CrimeID: the disturbance’s ID.
_Investigator: the NPC that is changing states.
_OldState: the state the NPC was in.
_ToState: the state the NPC has entered.Further InformationPossible values for the states are:
Idle: Not involved in any disturbance handling.
Investigation: Walking to a disturbance/crime scene.
LookingForSuspectsToInterrogate: Looking for player characters to interrogate.
LookingForSuspectsToConfront: Looking for player characters to react to (perform the primary reaction rather than start the interrogation dialogue).
LookingForCriminalsToConfront: Looking for one of the instigators/criminals of the disturbance to react to.
Confronting: Interrogating or reacting to a suspect or instigator/criminal.

HenchmanSelected.description: Thrown when entering character creation to prepare a _Hireling for _Player.

GameOption.description:
An event used to catch certain startup parameters and react to them in script.
_Key - the startup parameter you are detecting
_Value - the expected value of the parameter, this should be 0 (false) or 1 (true)

DialogStartRequested.description:
Thrown whenever _Player tries to start a conversation with _Target.
_Target: The object to which the conversation request is directed.
_Player: The player that initiated the conversationFurther InformationThis event can only be thrown if the target can accept dialog requests, i.e. if SetHasDialog is currently active.
DialogStartRequested.examples:
IF
DialogStartRequested(_, _Speaker)
AND
DB_DialogRequestCache_SpeakerList_Speakers(_DialogueResource, _ID, _Speaker, _SpeakerSlot)
AND
DialogSpeakerHasLines(_DialogueResource, _SpeakerSlot, 1)
AND
HasActiveStatusWithGroup(_Speaker, "SG_Invisible", 1)
THEN
RemoveStatusesWithGroup(_Speaker, "SG_Invisible", _Speaker);

LevelGameplayStarted.description:
Thrown when a level has been loaded and is ready at both the server and client side.
Parameters:
_LevelName: The identifier name of the level that has been loaded
_IsEditorMode: Indicates whether the game is run in the editor or notFurther InformationThe logic of the game engine is split into a server and a client side, even in single player mode. Some important things that run on the client side are visual effects and physics.
An example of something that is done only when the client side is ready, is playing the effect of the source collars on the characters in the tutorial and Fort Joy levels in DOS2 (since they're visual effects, and hence do not work if started when only the server side is ready)
See LevelLoaded for the event that is thrown when the level is ready on the server side (this happens before it is ready on the client side).
This event is also thrown when loading a savegame, so add additional logic for code that should be executed only the first time when this level is started (or trigger it from the INIT section of a story goal tied to this region instead).
When this event fires, the gamplay client logic is not yet running. Use LevelGameplayReady in that case. If you are not sure whether the gameplay logic needs to be running or not for the calls you use, it’s safest to use LevelGameplayReady.

MovedBy.description:
An event sent when an entity is moved by a character, returning what was moved and the character that moved them
Parameters:
_MovedEntity - The GUIDString of something that was moved
_Character - The character that moved the entity
MovedBy.examples:
IF
MovedBy((CHARACTER)_Skeleton, _Char)
AND
DB_UndeadGuardian(_Skeleton)
AND
DB_CanBeResurrected(_Skeleton)
THEN
PROC_ResurrectSkeletonsNoCinematic(_Char);

ReactionInterruptAdded.description: Event thrown when a _Character learns a spell that can be used as an interrupt reaction (e.g. Shield Blow).

ItemLeftTrigger.description: Thrown when an _Item is removed from a _Trigger that has been registered for item events.

GainedControl.description: An event that indicates that the player has gained control over the _Target character.
GainedControl.examples:
IF
GainedControl(_Scratch)
AND
DB_CourierDog_IsFetching(_Player, _Scratch, _ThrownObject)
THEN
PROC_CourierDog_ClearFetch(_Player, _Scratch, _ThrownObject, 0);

CharacterPickpocketFailed.description:
Sent when, in response to a RequestPickpocket event, StartPickpocket was called with 0 as _Success parameter.
Further InformationSee RequestPickpocket.
The default pipeline will register a PickPocketFailed crime when this event arrives, unless QRY_BlockPickpocket((CHARACTER)_Player, (CHARACTER)_NPC) succeeds (you don’t want a crime reaction if the pickpocketing was blocked altogether).
CharacterPickpocketFailed.examples:
// This is how this event is used by the default pipeline for pickpocketing NPCs
IF
CharacterPickpocketFailed(_Player,_Npc)
AND
QRY_BlockPickpocket(_Player, _Npc)
THEN
DB_PickpocketingBlocked(1);
//--- Check if player already stole items on that NPC
//--- Pass the item as evidence if it's the case.
IF
CharacterPickpocketFailed(_Player,_Npc)
AND
NOT DB_PickpocketingBlocked(1)
AND
IsPartyMember(_Npc, 1, 0)
THEN
PROC_CrimePickpocketFailedFlow(_Player,_Npc);

FailedToLoadItemInPreset.description:
Deprecated. Do not use.
Event is triggered when the game fails to load _NewItem to replace _OriginalItem for character _Character in level _Level.

CrimeProcessingStarted.description:
Sent in the frame after CharacterRegisterCrime, indicates whether someone immediately noticed it or not.
Parameters:
_CrimeID: the disturbance/crime’s ID
_ActedOnImmediately: 1 if an NPC noticed the disturbance immediately and is starting a confrontation, 0 if otherwise (could still have been heard and e.g. someone could have started investigating)Further InformationIf no one reacts immediately, no one heard it, it’s not a continuous disturbance, and it does not create a crime scene, then the disturbance will be removed by OnCrimeRemoved.
CrimeProcessingStarted.examples:
IF
CrimeProcessingStarted(_CrimeID, 0)
AND
CrimeGetType(_CrimeID, _Type)
AND
QRY_CRIME_IsCrimeFamilyMember(_Type, "MoveForbiddenItem")
AND
CrimeGetEvidence(_CrimeID, 1, _Evidence)
AND
GetOwner((ITEM)_Evidence, _Owner)
THEN
PROC_TryCreatePickUpEvidenceCrime(_CrimeID, "PickupEvidence_Unnoticed", _Owner);

Opened.description:
This event is thrown when the _Item defined is opened.
The Item is usually either a door or a container.

ForceDismissCompanion.description: The game has removed _Companion from the party to make room for a new player avatar that is joining the game.

RequestGatherAtCampSuccess.description: This is for internal use only. Part of a complex handshake between code and story for the camp and long rest pipeline.

OnStoryOverride.description: Event thrown when story overrides what _Target character was doing: teleport, movement, on/offstage.

ReposeAdded.description:
When _Character starts reposing, the ReposeAdded event is sent.
Reposing is an action where a character connects to a specific position, such as lying on a bed, sitting on a chair, or sitting on the ground and plays a default animation appropriate for the pose. The action aligns the character's position with an entity’s predefined socket if an entity is provided, or their current position if no object is specified.Parameters:
_Character: The character that started the repose
Only entities of type CHARACTER make sense for this call, even if it technically accepts all GUIDSTRING
_OnEntity: The entity that is being reposed on (eg. bed). Sends Null if not reposing on an entity
ReposeAdded.seeAlso:
ReposeRemoved
EndRepose
SitOnGround
LieOnGround
SitOnEntity
LieOnEntity
StandOnEntity
IsReposed
IsReposedSitting
IsReposedLying
IsReposedStanding
IsReposedConstrained
TemplateCanSitOn

EnteredSharedForceTurnBased.description:
This event fires when an entity enters Shared Turn based mode. Returns the entity (_Object) and the _ZoneID.
Further InformationThe returned _ZoneID can later be used in EnterSharedTurnBaseMode call to add additional player characters that were not available when the shared turn based mode was started.
EnteredSharedForceTurnBased.examples:
IF
EnteredSharedForceTurnBased(_Player, _Zone)
THEN
DB_CRE_SharedForceTurnBasedZone(_Zone);

IF
CombatEnded(_CombatID)
AND
DB_CRE_PlayersInCombatAtFTBStart(_CombatID, _Players)
AND
DB_CRE_SharedForceTurnBasedZone(_Zone)
THEN
EnterSharedTurnBaseMode(_Players, _Zone);
NOT DB_CRE_PlayersInCombatAtFTBStart(_CombatID, _Players);

Moved.description:
Thrown whenever an item has moved for one of the following reasons:
It was scattered, picked up, dropped, moved or teleported.

ObjectAvailableLevelChanged.description: Triggered when _Character gets a level up. Shows the level the character had and the level they have now.

DeathSaveStable.description: Thrown when the _Character is stabilized during their death saves. (Passes their death save three times)
DeathSaveStable.examples:
IF
DeathSaveStable(_StabilizedChar)
AND
NOT QRY_Tutorials_Stabilized_IsExcludedMoment()
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
QRY_IsInRange((GUIDSTRING)_Player,(GUIDSTRING)_StabilizedChar, 40.0)
THEN
PROC_ShowUnifiedTutorialForPlayer((CHARACTER)_Player, (UNIFIEDTUTORIAL)_Stabilized);

OnCrimeRemoved.description:
Sent when a non-continuous crime was registered, and then ended (e.g. timed out) before anyone reacted to it.
Parameters:
_CrimeID: the disturbance/crime’s ID
_Victim: the victim of the disturbance/crime
_Criminal1 .. _Criminal4: the criminals of the disturbance/crime. Some can be NULL.

LeftForceTurnBased.description: This event is fired when an Entity leaves turn based mode.

OnCrimeResolved.description:
Sent when a disturbance/crime has been resolved: either it timed out, or it has been handled/addressed successfully.
Parameters:
_CrimeID: the disturbance/crime’s ID
_Victim: the victim of the disturbance/crime
_Criminal1 .. _Criminal4: the criminals of the disturbance/crime. Some can be NULL.
OnCrimeResolved.examples:
IF
OnCrimeResolved(_CrimeID, _, _, _, _, _)
AND
DB_WeaponUnsheathedState(_Npc,_CrimeID,_)
THEN
PROC_CRIME_RestoreWeaponUnsheathed(_Npc, _CrimeID);

OnCrimeResetInterrogationForCriminal.description:
This event is thrown when interrogation for disturbance of _CrimeID is reset on a _Criminal, through the use of CrimeResetInterrogationForCriminals.
Further InformationThis event is not one we should work with, it’s there to handle some edge cases of our disturbance interrogation flow.
The common case for the reset is if the dialog fails to start or if the interrogation is cancelled.

InteractionCapabilityChanged.description:
Event InteractionCapabilityChanged gets thrown with _IsEnabled 1 when _Character becomes interactable.
Event InteractionCapabilityChanged gets thrown with _IsEnabled 0 when _Character becomes uninteractable.
Further InformationUsed in low level systemic pipelines that allow scripting to check if a character can act or not.

LeftLevel.description:
This event is sent for every character and item that leaves the active level (LevelLoaded), or the level currently being loaded.
Parameters:
_Object: the character or item that entered the leve
_Level: the name of the level in which the entity got loaded.Further InformationThis event fires in all cases where a character/item leaves a level or stops existing, e.g. when
A global entity gets teleported to a different level (TeleportTo)
a summon dies or expires
an item without a Template after Destruction in its Destroy Actions gets destroyedThis event does not fire when unloading a level (since EnteredLevel does not fire when returning to the level afterwards).
LeftLevel.examples:
IF
LeftLevel(_Character, "SCL_Main_A")
AND
DB_SCL_ShadowCurse_InSCL(_Character)
THEN
NOT DB_SCL_ShadowCurse_InSCL((CHARACTER)_Character);

LearnedSpell.description:
Sent when a character learns a new spell.
Parameters:
_Character: character learning the spell.
_Spell: name of the spell learned.
LearnedSpell.seeAlso:
UseSpell
AddSpell
RemoveSpell
UseSpellAtPosition
CastedSpell
CastSpell
CastSpellFailed
StartedPreviewingSpell
UsingSpell
UsingSpellAtPosition
UsingSpellInTrigger
UsingSpellOnTarget
CanLearnSpell
CanShowSpellForCharacter
GetSpellFromSet
HasSpell
IsSpellActive
SpellHasSpellFlag

RequestCanMove.description:
Arrives whenever the player tries to move an item (like a crate).
_RequestID is an integer identifier of the request. This number increases with every request.
Further InformationHas no relation to character movement.
This is primarily used by a script pipeline that allows certain actions to be blocked or intercepted for the purpose of custom reactions.

InteractionFallback.description:
This event triggers when the Story_Fallback use action is used on an item.
E.g. the player tries to go through a hole that only small or smaller creatures can fit through. The hole is an item that has multiple use actions. The first two are for small and tiny creatures to go through it (Teleport actions) the last use action is Story_Fallback, which triggers this event. We can catch this event and trigger an AD to inform the player why they failed to go through the hole.
The event returns what _Character failed using what _Item.
Further InformationThe Story_Fallback action can be assigned in the editor in the sidebar, under “Use Actions”

PingRequested.description:
Sent when script requested a ping from either script (Osiris or Anubis) or by a player.
Parameters:
_Character: the source of the ping request (script) or the player that pinged.

GoldChanged.description: Event that fires when the gold amount changes in the _InventoryHolder's inventory, and returns how much it changed by in _ChangeAmount.
GoldChanged.examples:
IF
GoldChanged((CHARACTER)_Player, _Value)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
_Value > 0
AND
DB_Backgrounds_Noble_GoldHoarded(_Id, _Threshold)
AND
GetGoldInMagicPockets(_Player, _Amount)
AND
_Amount >= _Threshold
THEN
PROC_Backgrounds_CompleteGoal(_Player, _Id);

EquipFailed.description:
Emitted when a player/character or script attempted to equip an item but failed.
Further InformationAn example of an item failing to equip could be if the equipment slot it is attempting to be equipped to is locked.
EquipFailed.seeAlso:
Equip
Unequip
Equipped
IsEquipable
Unequipped
UnequipFailed
TemplateEquipped
TemplateUnequipped

LongRestStarted.description: Event is triggered after the RequestLongRest and has succeeded.

Equipped.description: Emitted when an item is equipped.
Equipped.examples:
IF
Equipped(_MagicTorch, _Char)
AND
DB_Players(_Char)
AND
NOT DB_GlobalFlags(_State_PlayerEquippedMagicTorch)
THEN
SetFlag(_State_PlayerEquippedMagicTorch);
Equipped.seeAlso:
Equip
Unequip
IsEquipable
Unequipped
EquipFailed
UnequipFailed
TemplateEquipped
TemplateUnequipped

GotUp.description: Thrown when the _Target gets up from a knocked down state.

PlatformMovementFinished.description:
This event is thrown when the platform _Object specified in the parameter finishes any of its current movement.
The specific movement can be set by filling in the _EventId parameter.

HitpointsChanged.description:
Event is sent when HP value of character or item changes. _Percentage represents the hp value of the entity after the hp change that triggered the event takes effect.
Further InformationThis event is sent for entities only in case they are not currently shapeshifted (either via POLYMORPH_Status or Transform) with a shapeshift rule that has HP column filled in (you can check that in UUID editor): in that case, ShapeshiftedHitpointsChanged is sent instead.
HitpointsChanged.examples:
IF
HitpointsChanged((CHARACTER)_Hag,_)
AND
GetHitpoints(_Hag,_HP)
AND
_HP <= 0
AND
QRY_OnlyOnce("HagVomitsOutDeadVanra")
AND
NOT DB_GlobalFlag((FLAG)_Event_TeleportVanraToHag)
AND
NOT QRY_HagVomitsVanra_AddCharactersInTrigger((DIALOGRESOURCE)_HagVomitsOutDeadVanra)
THEN
PROC_HagKilled_SpawnDeadVanra();

ObjectTimerFinished.description:
An event called when a _Timer previously set on an _Object has successfully reached the end of the allotted time put on there originally. Used to catch the timers finishing and have calls played as a result of it.
Further InformationThis is specifically for timers centered around Objects. For Global timers, instead use TimerFinished.

PlatformMovementCanceled.description:
This event is thrown when the platform _Object specified in the parameter has any of its current movement stopped before it can finish.
The specific movement can be set by filling in the _EventId parameter.
PlatformMovementCanceled.examples:
IF
PlatformMovementCanceled(_Disc, _EventID)
AND
DB_SHA_Disc_Moving(_StartingPos)
THEN
DebugText(_Disc, _EventID);
NOT DB_SHA_Disc_Moving(_StartingPos);

KilledBy.description:
Thrown when a character is killed by another game object.
Further Information_AttackerOwner can be the same as _Attacker. _AttackOwner will be different in case of summoned characters, or in case of items.
If the attacker is unknown, this event will not be thrown (i.e. _Attacker will never be NULL).
KilledBy.examples:
IF
KilledBy(_Surgeon, _Killer, _, _)
AND
DB_PartyMembers(_Killer)
AND
NOT_AchievementBlocked(1)
THEN
PROC_Achievements_Unlock("BG3_Quest34");

HenchmanAborted.description: Thrown when hireling customization is cancelled by the _Player.

RequestEndTheDaySuccess.description: This is for internal use only. Part of a complex handshake between code and story for the camp and long rest pipeline.

ReactionInterruptUsed.description:
Event thrown when a _Character uses a reaction interrupt spell.
Further Information_IsAutoTriggered - Whether or not the interrupt reaction auto triggered, or was manually selected.

MessageBoxClosed.description: Thrown when a message box is closed.
MessageBoxClosed.examples:
IF
MessageBoxClosed(_, "GLO_CompanionLeftPermanently")
AND
DB_CAMP_Astarion_GurKidnapping_PlayPAD(_Avatar)
THEN
NOT DB_CAMP_Astarion_GurKidnapping_PlayPAD(_Avatar);
RealtimeObjectTimerLaunch(_Avatar, "CAMP_Astarion_GurKidnapping_PlayPAD", 2000);

PlatformDestroyed.description: This event is thrown when the platform _Object specified in the parameter is destroyed.

MessageBoxYesNoClosed.description:
Thrown when a message box has been closed with a yes or no response.
Further InformationYes = 1, No = 0.

Open.description: Sets the _Item defined to be opened.
Open.examples:
PROC
PROC_BlockUseOfItem(_Player, (ITEM)_GoblinCageDoor)
AND
IsLocked((ITEM)_GoblinCageDoor, 0)
THEN
Open(_GoblinCageDoor);
DB_CustomUseItemResponse(_Player, _GoblinCageDoor, 0);

OnStartCarrying.description:
Fires an event if the _CarriedObject is being carried by the _Carrier along with the position they get picked up from _pickupPosX, _pickupPosY, _pickupPosZ.
The _CarriedObjectTemplate and _StoryActionID is also returned as values.
Further InformationCurrently only used to check if a character is carrying an item or character to use as an improvised weapon that would be considered a crime.

EntityEvent.description:
Thrown whenever an entity (game object) receives an event. These events may come from SetEntityEvent, but many other APIs also throw events to objects and all of these can be caught using this event.
Further InformationIf a call requires a string for an event related to 1 entity, it is mostly likely an Entity Event being called.
E.g. TeleportTo or CharacterMoveTo.
EntityEvent.seeAlso:
DualEntityEvent
SetEntityEvent
SetDualEntityEvent
SetEntityEventReal
SetDualEntityEventReal
SetEntityEventDialog
SetDualEntityEventDialog
SetEntityEventGUID

RequestGatherAtCampFail.description: This is for internal use only. Part of a complex handshake between code and story for the camp and long rest pipeline.

OnThrown.description:
This event is fired when an item is thrown.
The event returns the _ThrownObject and its _ThrownObjectTemplate, the _Thrower, the _StoryActionID and the position of where the _ThrownObject landed, _throwPosX, _throwPosY, _throwPos.
OnThrown.fullDefinitions:
OnThrown((GUIDSTRING)_ThrownObject, (ROOT)_ThrownObjectTemplate, (GUIDSTRING)_Thrower, (INTEGER)_StoryActionID, (REAL)_throwPosX, (REAL)_throwPosY, (REAL)_throwPosZ)
ExampleIF
OnThrown(_ToyItem, _ExplosiveToy, _Player, _,_,_,_)
AND
NOT QRY_StartDialog_Fixed((DIALOGRESOURCE)_ExplosiveBearToy_AD, _ToyItem)
THEN
Die((ITEM)_ToyItem);

LeftCombat.description: Thrown when _Object leaves a combat with ID _CombatGuid.
LeftCombat.examples:
IF
LeftCombat((CHARACTER)_Player, _ID)
AND
DB_WildMagic_ADCombatCooldown(_Player, _Spell, _ID)
THEN
NOT DB_WildMagic_ADCombatCooldown(_Player, _Spell, _ID);

RelationChanged.description: The event is triggered when the relation between _SourceFaction and _TargetFaction changes to _NewRelation. _Permanent is 0 when it’s a temporary change, 1 when it’s a permanent change (Keep it short and concise.

MovieFinished.description: Triggered when the movie _MovieName is finished.

RespecCancelled.description:
An event thrown when you cancel out of respecced your character (Changed your character’s skills/abilities).
Parameters:
_Character the character whose Respec was cancelled.

PickupFailed.description:
Fires when a character _Character attempted to pick up item _Item and failed to do so (because the character was interrupted, item cannot be picked up, item was picked up or moved out of reach, …).
Further InformationThere is no equivalent “PickupSucceeded“ event, but there is the AddedTo event.

RollResult.description:
Thrown when a roll finishes or is cancelled. The roll can be triggered from script (e.g. RequestActiveRoll), or from certain points in code.
Parameters_EventName: The string passed to the roll call. Can be empty in case of a roll triggered from certain points in code.
_Roller: The character performing the roll.
_RollSubject: The object about which the roll is (e.g. an item in case of a hidden perception check).
_ResultType: The roll result:
0: _Roller failed their roll
1: _Roller succeeded their roll
2: This can only happen for active rolls. It means the roll was cancelled before it completed, because either
a new interactive dialogue started for the roller e.g. a roll was triggered from script and then an interactive dialogue was started by an NPC
the roller dies
the roller becomes incapacitated
the roller gets moved away more than 5 metres from their original position since the start of the roll
_IsActiveRoll: 1 if it was an active roll, 0 if it was a passive roll.
_Criticality: was it a Critical Success (or Failure)Further InformationDoes not trigger for rolls that are part of spells/stats.
Does trigger for disarm trap (_EventName == "disarm trap") and lockpicking (_EventName == "GAMEPLAY_LockPicking")
In case of rolls triggered from dialogues, the DialogRollResult triggers instead.

RemovedFrom.description:
Event thrown when an object is removed from a particular inventory.
Parameters:
_Object - The object that was removed
_InventoryHolder - the inventory the item was removed fromFurther InformationUsed for a specific item. If you want to check based on a template, use TemplateRemovedFrom.

RequestCanLoot.description:
Arrives whenever the player tries to loot a dead body.
_RequestID is an integer identifier of the request. This number increases with every request.
Looting is the default action performed on dead bodies. Actions such as pickpocketing do not trigger this event.
Further InformationThis is primarily used by a script pipeline that allows certain actions to be blocked or intercepted for the purpose of custom reactions.

LongRestCancelled.description: Event is triggered after RequestLongRest and the long rest is cancelled.

RequestCanLockpick.description:
Arrives whenever the player tries to start lockpicking.
_RequestID is an integer identifier of the request. This number increases with every request.
Further InformationThis is primarily used by a script pipeline that allows certain actions to be blocked or intercepted for the purpose of custom reactions.

ScreenFadeCleared.description: An event sent when a previously activated Screen Fade has been cleared and there is no longer a fade covering the screen.

RulesetModifierChangedBool.description:
When the value of a Ruleset Modifier changes, this event triggers.
Look for a ruleset in UUID Object Editor in RulesetModifiers and make sure you’re using the correct query according to the type of a ruleset.
Further InformationYou can also check for a value of a Ruleset Modifier at any point.
GetRulesetModifierBool
CheckRulesetModifierBool

ShortRestCapable.description:
An event thrown when a player _Character becomes capable of a short rest or incapable.
Further Information_Capable
1: Is capable of short resting.
0: Is incapable of short resting.

QueuePurged.description: Triggers after an Osiris queue is getting purged.
QueuePurged.examples:
IF
QueuePurged((CHARACTER)_Character)
AND
DB_DEN_WaitingAtGate(_Character)
AND
TriggerGetRandomPosition(_OutsideLeaveArea, _X, _Y, _Z)
THEN
NOT DB_DEN_WaitingAtGate(_Character);
PROC_CharacterMoveToPosition(_Character, _X, _Y, _Z, "Run", "DEN_NPC_OutsideDen");

RequestEndTheDayFail.description: This is for internal use only. Part of a complex handshake between code and story for the camp and long rest pipeline.

MoveCapabilityChanged.description:
Event MoveCapabilityChanged gets thrown with _IsEnabled 1 when _Character is no longer blocked from moving.
Event MoveCapabilityChanged gets thrown with _IsEnabled 0 when _Character is blocked from moving.
Further InformationUsed in low level systemic pipelines that allow scripting to check if a character can move or not.

RequestPickpocket.description:
Gets sent when a player tries to pickpocket an NPC or another player (sneak and click on an NPC).
Further InformationDo not use this event directly. You can, however, override QRY_BlockPickpocket((CHARACTER)_Player, (CHARACTER)_NPC) and let it succeed if you want to block pickpocketing for a particular character. The default pipeline has no other ways to override the default behaviour.
Script has to call call StartPickpocket  in response to this event with 0 or 1 as last parameter to indicate block/allow. This is done automatically by the default pipeline, with the value depending on whether QRY_BlockPickpocket succeeded or failed.
RequestPickpocket.examples:
// This is how this event is used by the default pipeline for pickpocketing NPCs
IF
RequestPickpocket(_Player, _Npc)
AND
QRY_BlockPickpocket(_Player, _Npc)
THEN
DB_PickpocketingBlocked(1);
PROC_TryStartAD(_AD_CannotPickpocket, _Player);

IF
RequestPickpocket(_Player, _Npc)
AND
DB_PickpocketingBlocked(1)
THEN
StartPickpocket(_Player, _Npc, 0);

IF
RequestPickpocket(_Player, _Npc)
AND
NOT DB_PickpocketingBlocked(1)
AND
IsPartyMember(_Npc, 1, 0)
THEN
PROC_GenTradeItems(_Player, _Npc);
StartPickpocket(_Player, _Npc, 1);

IF
RequestPickpocket(_Player, _Npc)
THEN
NOT DB_PickpocketingBlocked(1);

RequestTrade.description: Thrown when a player performs the action to start trading with an NPC from a dialogue.

ShareInitiative.description:
Fires when an entity has shared initiative.
This event was specifically created for triggering the tutorial message that explains shared initiative.
ShareInitiative.examples:
IF
ShareInitiative(_Player)
AND
DB_PartyMembers((CHARACTER)_Player)
THEN
PROC_GLO_Tutorials_TrackedTimerStart(_Player, "GLO_Tutorials_SharedInitiativeOverlapPrevention", 2000);

PROC
PROC_GLO_Tutorials_TrackedTimerFinished(_Player, "GLO_Tutorials_SharedInitiativeOverlapPrevention")
AND
IsAnyTutorialShowingFor((CHARACTER)_Player, 0)
THEN
PROC_ShowUnifiedTutorialForPlayer(_Player, (UNIFIEDTUTORIAL)_Shared_Initiative);

ShapeshiftedHitpointsChanged.description:
Thrown when a character or item's hit points change if they currently have a shapeshift form active that modifies HP (i.e., a shapeshift form that results in restoring the original form and original HP when the shapeshift form is reduced to 0 HP). This event replaces the regular HitpointsChanged event in such cases.
_Entity: The character or item whose hit points changed.
_Percentage: The character or item's remaining HP percentage after the change.Further InformationNo regular HitpointsChanged event is thrown when this event fires.
GetHitpoints, SetHitpoints and related APIs do always work on the current form, regardless of whether it's a shapeshift or not.
ShapeshiftedHitpointsChanged.seeAlso:
AttackedBy
HitpointsChanged
GetHitpoints
GetHitpointsPercentage
SetHitpoints
SetHitpointsPercentage
StatusApplied

ShortRested.description: Event is triggered on a _Character that has completed a short rest.
ShortRested.examples:
IF
ShortRested(_Player)
AND
DB_END_OptionsFollower_Follower(_Char, _CharacterFlag, _GlobalFlag, _Dialog)
AND
GetFlag(_CharacterFlag, _Player, 1)
AND
NOT DB_Defeated(_Char)
THEN
PROC_CharacterFullRestore(_Char);

Saw.description: The event is triggered when _TargetCharacter is spotted by _Character. _TargetWasSneaking is 0 if the target wasn’t sneaking and _TargetWasSneaking is 1 if the target was sneaking.

RulesetModifierChangedString.description:
When the value of a Ruleset Modifier changes, this event triggers.
Look for a ruleset in UUID Object Editor in RulesetModifiers and make sure you’re using the correct query according to the type of a ruleset.
Further InformationIn BG3 this is used to check the overall difficulty:
SCRIPTED_COMBAT_MECHANICS_cac2d8bd-c197-4a84-9df1-f86f54ad4521
EASY
MEDIUM
HARDYou can also check for a value of a Ruleset Modifier at any point.
GetRulesetModifierString
CheckRulesetModifierString
RulesetModifierChangedString.examples:
IF
RulesetModifierChangedString(SCRIPTED_COMBAT_MECHANICS_cac2d8bd-c197-4a84-9df1-f86f54ad4521,(STRING)_OldDifficulty, (STRING)_NewDifficulty)
AND
DB_InRegion((CHARACTER)_PartyMember, (TRIGGER)_PhilgravesMansion_LairCurseArea)
AND
NOT DB_Dead(_PartyMember)
AND
DB_LOW_PhilgravesMansion_DifficultyLairCurses(_OldStatus,_OldDifficulty)
AND
DB_LOW_PhilgravesMansion_DifficultyLairCurses(_NewStatus,_NewDifficulty)
AND
HasActiveStatus(_PartyMember,_OldStatus,1)
THEN
RemoveStatus(_PartyMember,_OldStatus);
ApplyStatus(_PartyMember, _NewStatus, -1.0, 0, NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000);

RestorePartyFinished.description: Is thrown when the RestoreParty call finishes.
RestorePartyFinished.examples:
IF
RestorePartyFinished()
AND
DB_RestorePartyRequest(1)
AND
DB_QueuedStatuses(_Char, _Status)
THEN
NOT DB_RestorePartyRequest(1);
ApplyStatus(_Char, _Status,-1.0,1,NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000);

RespecCompleted.description:
An event thrown when you successfully respecced your character (Changed your character’s skills/abilities).
Parameters:
_Character the character whose Respec was completed.

ShortRestProcessing.description: Thrown when a short rest has started but has not finished yet on the _Character that started the short rest.

ReadyCheckPassed.description: This even is triggered when a ready check with _Id that was requested passed.
ReadyCheckPassed.examples:
IF
ReadyCheckPassed("ReadyCheck_EbonLake")
AND
DB_UND_EbonLake_RaftUsed(_Dialog, _Player)
AND
QRY_StartDialog_Fixed(_Dialog, _Player)
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);

RulesetModifierChangedFloat.description:
When the value of a Ruleset Modifier changes, this event triggers.
Look for a ruleset in UUID Object Editor in RulesetModifiers and make sure you’re using the correct query according to the type of a ruleset.
Further InformationYou can also check for a value of a Ruleset Modifier at any point.
GetRulesetModifierFloat
CheckRulesetModifierFloat
RulesetModifierChangedFloat.examples:
IF
RulesetModifierChangedString(SCRIPTED_COMBAT_MECHANICS_cac2d8bd-c197-4a84-9df1-f86f54ad4521,(STRING)_OldDifficulty, (STRING)_NewDifficulty)
AND
DB_InRegion((CHARACTER)_PartyMember, (TRIGGER)_LairCurseArea)
AND
NOT DB_Dead(_PartyMember)
AND
DB_DifficultyLairCurses(_OldStatus,_OldDifficulty)
AND
DB_DifficultyLairCurses(_NewStatus,_NewDifficulty)
AND
HasActiveStatus(_PartyMember,_OldStatus,1)
THEN
RemoveStatus(_PartyMember,_OldStatus);
ApplyStatus(_PartyMember, _NewStatus, -1.0, 0, NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000);

OnShutdown.description: Event sent when the game is shut down. _IsEditorMode will be 1 if the game is run in the editor and 0 if not.
OnShutdown.examples:
IF
OnShutdownGameOption(_)
AND
DB_Misc_Tentacles(_Tentacle)
THEN
SetForceUpdate(_Tentacle, 0);

Resurrected.description: Use this to check if _Character has been resurrected.
Resurrected.examples:
IF
Resurrected(_Character)
AND
NOT DB_Players(_Character)
THEN
ClearFlag(_Flag, NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, 0);

RequestCanCombine.description: This event is called much like event Combined, except rather than being called on success, it is called when the player attempts to press the “Combine” button when trying to combine items together.
RequestCanCombine.fullDefinitions:
RequestCanCombine((CHARACTER)_Character, (ITEM)_Item_1, (ITEM)_Item_2, (ITEM)_Item_3, (ITEM)_Item_4, (ITEM)_Item_5, (INTEGER)_RequestID)
ExampleIF
RequestCanCombine(_Player,_OskarInteractableEasel,_WrongItem,_,_,_,_RequestID)
AND
DB_AtelierSpeakingPortraits((ITEM)_WrongItem,_,_OriginalPos)
AND
_WrongItem != _OskarPortrait
AND
CreateAtObject((CHARACTERROOT)_Nurse_NoXP,_WrongPortrait_EnemyLocation,0,1,"",1,_Enemy)
AND
GetPosition(_WrongPortrait_EnemyLocation,_X,_Y,_Z)
AND
FindValidPosition(_X,_Y,_Z,15.0, _Enemy, 1,_XF,_YF,_ZF)
THEN
TeleportToPosition(_Enemy,_XF,_YF,_ZF, "TeleportedWrongPortraitEnemy",0,0,0,0,1);
TeleportTo(_WrongItem,_OriginalPos,"TeleportedWrongPortrait",0,0,0,0,0);
DB_ConnectedEnemyPortrait(_WrongItem, _Enemy);

ScatteredAt.description: This event is sent when an item has been dropped in a random position around a coordinate point, usually as a result of the ScatterAt call. Shows the _Item scattered and its new _X, _Y, and _Z coordinates.

RandomCastProcessed.description:
There is a system where random spells get cast as a side effect of other actions (Wild Magic).
This system is activated through the TriggerRandomCast functor in the stats system.
When a random spell cast is attempted, the RandomCastProcessed event is thrown.
Further InformationParameters of the event:
_Caster caster of the spell
StoryActionID story action id of the random cast. The story action id will be the same for the effect that caused the RandomCastProcessed event, and the spell that is cast as a result of the random cast.
_SpellID name of the spell that is triggered as a result of the random cast
_RollResult roll result to see if a random cast should happen
_RandomCastDC DC that _RollResult needs to hit in order for a random cast to happen
To check if a random cast was successful, you check one of these conditions:
_RollResult >= _RandomCastDC
_SpellID != ""
RandomCastProcessed.examples:
IF
RandomCastProcessed((CHARACTER)_Player, _, _Spell, _, _)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
THEN
PROC_WildMagic_TryAD(_Player, _Spell);

ReposeRemoved.description:
When _Character stops reposing, the ReposeRemoved event is sent.
Reposing is an action where a character connects to a specific position, such as lying on a bed, sitting on a chair, or sitting on the ground and plays a default animation appropriate for the pose. The action aligns the character's position with an entity’s predefined socket if an entity is provided, or their current position if no object is specified.Parameters:
_Character : The character that ended the repose
Only entities of type CHARACTER make sense for this call, even if it technically accepts all GUIDSTRING
_OnEntity: The entity that was being reposed on (eg. bed). Sends Null if wasn’t reposing on an entity
ReposeRemoved.seeAlso:
ReposeAdded
EndRepose
SitOnGround
LieOnGround
SitOnEntity
LieOnEntity
StandOnEntity
IsReposed
IsReposedSitting
IsReposedLying
IsReposedStanding
IsReposedConstrained
TemplateCanSitOn

RulesetModifierChangedInt.description:
When the value of a Ruleset Modifier changes, this event triggers.
Look for a ruleset in UUID Object Editor in RulesetModifiers and make sure you’re using the correct query according to the type of a ruleset.
Further InformationYou can also check for a value of a Ruleset Modifier at any point.
GetRulesetModifierInt
CheckRulesetModifierBool
RulesetModifierChangedInt.examples:
IF
RulesetModifierChangedString(SCRIPTED_COMBAT_MECHANICS_cac2d8bd-c197-4a84-9df1-f86f54ad4521,(STRING)_OldDifficulty, (STRING)_NewDifficulty)
AND
DB_InRegion((CHARACTER)_PartyMember, (TRIGGER)_LairCurseArea)
AND
NOT DB_Dead(_PartyMember)
AND
DB_DifficultyLairCurses(_OldStatus,_OldDifficulty)
AND
DB_DifficultyLairCurses(_NewStatus,_NewDifficulty)
AND
HasActiveStatus(_PartyMember,_OldStatus,1)
THEN
RemoveStatus(_PartyMember,_OldStatus);
ApplyStatus(_PartyMember, _NewStatus, -1.0, 0, NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000);

SafeRomanceOptionChanged.description:
Thrown when a user changes their setting for Show Sexual and Violent Cinematics in the Accesibility tab in the Options menu.
Parameters:
_UserID: ID of the user in the current session.
_State: 1 with the intent to hide sexual scenes, and 0 to show them.Further InformationWhen a dialog starts, if the user had the option to have safe romance scenes the flag GLO_SafeRomance_Enabled_f46a2601-92d1-4b86-98b5-0dae4a290ff6 will be set on their character. If not it will be cleared.

ReactionInterruptActionNeeded.description: Event thrown when the spell cast system determines that _Object can use an interrupt, and is waiting for them to select one.

RequestCanPickup.description:
Arrives whenever the player tries to pick up an entity.
_RequestID is an integer identifier of the request. This number increases with every request.
Further InformationThis is primarily used by a script pipeline that allows certain actions to be blocked or intercepted for the purpose of custom reactions.

RequestCanUse.description:
Arrives whenever the player tries to use an object.
_RequestID is an integer identifier of the request. This number increases with every request.
Further InformationThis is primarily used by a script pipeline that allows certain actions to be blocked or intercepted for the purpose of custom reactions.

RequestCanDisarmTrap.description:
Callback to Story before Code processes a request to disarm a trap, when a _Character attempts to disarm an _Item.
Expects a RequestProcessed(_Character,_RequestID,_Result) reply with a matching _RequestID and _Character.
Further InformationGenerally already handled by the pipeline referenced in the example below.

StatusAttemptFailed.description:
Thrown when a _Status was being applied to a game object, but the status application failed.
_Object: The character on which the status is attempted.
_Status: The attempted status.
_Causee: The character or item originally responsible for attempting this status, if available; NULL otherwise.
_StoryActionID: the story action ID of the operation that caused the status to be applied. It will be the name that will appear on StatusAttempt for the same action.Further InformationReasons a status application can fail to apply are
The character is dead and the status is blocked for dead characters
The status was not force-applied, the status has IsInvulnerable in its StatusPropertyFlags, and the target is already invulnerable
The status was not force-applied, and the target is immune specifically to this status or its status group (as defined in its Character/Object stats).
If the status-specific apply checks, as specified in the OnApplyConditions in stats, fail, then this event will NOT be thrown. Instead, you will get a StatusApplied event immediately followed by a StatusRemoved event.
StatusAttemptFailed.examples:
IF
StatusAttemptFailed(_DarkUrge, "SLAYER_PLAYER", _, _)
AND
DB_ORI_DarkUrge((CHARACTER)_DarkUrge)
AND
HasAppliedStatus(_DarkUrge, "SLAYER_PLAYER_10", 0)
AND
HasAppliedStatus(_DarkUrge, "SLAYER_PLAYER", 0)
THEN
ClearFlag((FLAG)_State_SlayerForm, _DarkUrge, 0);
StatusAttemptFailed.seeAlso:
ApplyStatus
RemoveStatus
StatusApplied
StatusAttempt
StatusRemoved
HasActiveStatus

StartedPreviewingSpell.description:
Thrown when a character is preparing to cast a spell, but have not casted it yet. This is when selecting a spell and previewing it.
Parameters:
_Caster: who or what casted the spell. Items can also send these events.
_Spell: name of the spell including its spell category (Category_SpellName).
_IsMostPowerful: is the previewed spell the most powerful version of it.
_HasMultipleLevels: can it be casted with a higher level or not.Further InformationStartedPreviewingSpell() should only be used for logic that has to run only the moment the preview appears, since there is no event thrown if the use of the spell is cancelled.
