ReadyCheckFailed.description: This even is triggered when a ready check with _Id that was requested fails.

StartedFleeing.description:
Sent when a character starts fleeing
Parameters:
_Character: The one who flees.Further InformationThis call refers to fleeing as running to a certain distance, not the fleeing mechanic that allows the party to escape combat by teleporting away.

StoppedDisarmingTrap.description: Sent when a _Character stops disarming a _Trap (successfully or otherwise).

StatusAttempt.description: Thrown when _Status is applied to _Object, before determining if it will be blocked.
StatusAttempt.examples:
IF
StatusAttempt((CHARACTER)_Player, "SNEAKING", _, _)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
DB_GLO_ScryingEye(_Eye,(TRIGGER)_Region)
AND
DB_InRegion(_Player,_Region)
AND
DB_Sees(_Eye, _Player)
AND
QRY_GLO_ScryingEye_IsInArea(_Player, _Eye)
THEN
PROC_GLO_ScryingEye_StartFollow(_Eye, _Player, "Sneaking");

StoppedCombining.description: This event is fired when a character is stopped from performing a combine action.

StoppedLockpicking.description: Event is thrown when _Character and stops lockpicking _Item,
StoppedLockpicking.examples:
IF
StoppedLockpicking(_Player, _ZhentShipment_Chest)
AND
DB_PartyMembers(_Player)
AND
NOT QRY_PLA_ZhentShipment_SurvivorsBlockChestRD()
AND
IsOpened(_ZhentShipment_Chest, 0)
THEN
PROC_PLA_ZhentShipment_PlayCargoVB(_Player);

TagCleared.description: Event that triggers went the _Target has a specific _Tag cleared from it.
TagCleared.examples:
IF
TagCleared((CHARACTER)_Character, _BLOCK_RESURRECTION)
AND
DB_GLO_Playable(_Character)
THEN
DB_CanBeResurrected(_Character);

SwarmAIGroupLeft.description: This is part of the Swarm system. This event is triggered when a target leaves a swarm group with _Group string id.

StoppedSneaking.description: The event is triggered when _Character stops sneaking.

StatusApplied.description: Thrown when _Status has been applied to _Object.

TeleportedFromCamp.description:
Event that fires when a Character gets teleported from camp to a different location.
Parameters:
_Character - The character that got teleported.Further InformationNote that it fires both for PCs and NPCs.
The event needs a CampRegionTrigger in the Camp properly set, otherwise it won’t fire at all.
Note that it will fire for characters going offstage from Camp.
Note that it won’t fire if characters teleport to Camp from Camp itself (e.g. casting Dimension Door from inside camp). In those circumstances, TeleportToFromCamp event is fired instead.
TeleportedFromCamp.seeAlso:
Teleported
ItemTeleported
TeleportedToCamp
TeleportToFromCamp
TeleportToWaypoint
TeleportToFleeWaypoint
AppearTeleportFailed

SupplyTemplateSpent.description:
An event thrown when a supply item template has been used.
Parameters:
_TemplateID the template ID of the item.
_Amount the amount of items used

SwitchedCombat.description:
When two sets of characters in combat get so close together that the combats are merged, each object in one of those combats gets added to the other combat.
Parameters:
_Object: The object that was moved from one combat to the other.
_OldCombatGuid: GUID that identifies the old combat (the combat being merged into another one).
_NewCombatGuid: GUID that identifies the new combat (the combat into which the old one is getting merged).Further InformationOnce all objects from _OldCombatGuid have been merged into _NewCombatGuid, the first combat ends.
Note that this does not create a new combat, it just merges one into the other. Example:
Object _A and _B are in combat _C. Object _X and _Y are in combat _Z.
Combat merge happens. _C gets merged into _Z.
_A and _B will fire a SwitchedCombat event, the parameters of which will be SwitchedCombat(_A, _C, _Z) and SwitchedCombat(_B, _C, _Z) respectively.
After both _A and _B have finished switching combats, combat _C will end. Now, _A, _B, _X and _Y are all part of combat _Z.
SwitchedCombat.examples:
IF
SwitchedCombat(_Cultist,_OldCombatID,_NewCombatID)
AND
DB_Park_Cultists(_Cultist)
AND
DB_Park_CombatGUID(_OldCombatID)
THEN
DB_Park_CombatGUID(_NewCombatID);
NOT DB_Park_CombatGUID(_OldCombatID);
SwitchedCombat.seeAlso:
CombatEnded
EndCombat
CombatIsActive
IsInCombat
CombatStarted
EnterCombatFailed

TeleportToFromCamp.description:
Event that gets fired whenever a character teleports to/from Camp.
Parameters:
_Character - The character that got teleported.Further InformationNote that it fires both for PCs and NPCs.
The event needs a CampRegionTrigger in the Camp properly set, otherwise it won’t fire at all.
Use this event if you want to react to characters entering or exiting camp, but you don’t care which one it is. If you do care, use event TeleportedToCamp and TeleportedFromCamp instead.
This event does not replace those two (e.g. a character teleporting to Camp will fire both TeleportedToCamp and TeleportToFromCamp.
TeleportToFromCamp.examples:
IF
TeleportToFromCamp(_NonPlayer)
AND
NOT DB_Players(_NonPlayer)
THEN
PROC_TryStartAD((DIALOGRESOURCE)_GLO_AD_CannotUseNow_Muted, _NonPlayer);
TeleportToFromCamp.seeAlso:
Teleported
ItemTeleported
TeleportedToCamp
TeleportedFromCamp
TeleportToWaypoint
TeleportToFleeWaypoint
AppearTeleportFailed

StartedLockpicking.description: Thrown when the _Character starts an attempt to lockpick an _Item.

TemplateEnteredTrigger.description: Thrown when an _ItemTemplate is placed in a _Trigger that has been registered for item events.

SwarmAIGroupJoined.description: This is part of the Swarm system. This event is triggered when a target joins a swarm group with _Group string id.

TeleportToWaypoint.description:
Event that fires whenever a character requests fast travel to a Waypoint.
Parameters:
_Character - Character that gets teleported.
_Trigger - Destination trigger associated with the Waypoint.Further InformationIf the player fled a combat and got teleported to a waypoint for that reason, a event TeleportToFleeWaypoint is sent instead.
If the player teleports to camp waypoint, this event won’t fire. Instead, a event TeleportToFromCamp is sent instead.
TeleportToWaypoint.examples:
IF
TeleportToWaypoint(_Char, _Trigger)
AND
NOT QRY_GLO_BlockTeleportToWaypoint(_Char, _Trigger)
THEN
PROC_WaypointTeleportTo(_Char, _Trigger);
TeleportToWaypoint.seeAlso:
Teleported
ItemTeleported
TeleportedToCamp
TeleportedFromCamp
TeleportToFromCamp
TeleportToFleeWaypoint
AppearTeleportFailed

TemplateOpening.description: This event is thrown when the _Item with the specific _ItemTemplate defined is opened by a _Character.
TemplateOpening.examples:
IF
TemplateOpening(_, _Door, _)
AND
DB_TavernInvestigation_HalfElfDoors(_Door, _)
AND
QRY_OnlyOnce("Tavern_StartCollapse")
THEN
PROC_Tavern_StartCollapse();

TagSet.description: Event that triggers when a _Target has a specific _Tag set on it.

StartedDisarmingTrap.description: Sent when a _Character starts disarming a _Trap.

StartAttackPosition.description:
Thrown when a game object, _Attackerbegins an attack against another game object, _Defender and is much like StartAttack, however it also returns the position of the attack.
_X, _Y and _Z: The X, Y and Z values of the position of where the attack started,
_Defender: The character that was attacked.
_AttackOwner: The character that owns the _Attacker (e.g. in case of summons, or in case of gravity changes such as fall damage.
_Attacker: The object that attacked _Defender.
_StoryActionID: the story action ID of the attack.
StartAttackPosition.examples:
IF
StartAttackPosition(_, _, _, _, _NPC, _StoryActionID)
AND
_NPC != NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
AND
IsCharacter(_NPC, 1)
THEN
PROC_TryStartNPCAttackAD((CHARACTER)_NPC, _StoryActionID);

TagEvent.description: Event that triggers for every iteration of IterateTagsCategory with the defined string _Event.
TagEvent.examples:
IF
TagEvent(_Tag,"GLO_RaceTag")
AND
NOT DB_RaceTags(_Tag,_)
AND
NOT DB_RaceTags(_,_Tag)
AND
NOT DB_RaceTags_NoREALLY(_Tag)
AND
GUIDToString(_Tag,_TagStr)
AND
Concatenate("Tag in Race category is not in DB_RaceTags: ",_TagStr,_Str)
THEN
DebugBreak(_Str);
// This PROC of IterateTagsCategory will trigger the above event.
PROC
PROC_CheckRaceTags()
THEN
IterateTagsCategory(TAGCATEGORY.Race,"GLO_RaceTag","GLO_FillRaceTags_Finish");

TurnEnded.description: Thrown when _Object is in combat and its turn has ended.
TurnEnded.examples:
IF
TurnEnded((CHARACTER)_Character)
AND
DB_ADOnTurn((CHARACTER)_Character)
THEN
RealtimeObjectTimerCancel(_Character, "RapidMindMeld");
NOT DB_ADOnTurn((CHARACTER)_Character);

TemplateEquipped.description:
Emitted when an item is equipped.
Further InformationThe _ItemTemplate UUID is of the root template. So if you have S_EXA_BronzeSword_A and S_EXA_BronzeSword_B and they both inherit from the same root template WPN_BronzeSword_1, then when either of the swords are equipped they will both emit the same UUID from WPN_BronzeSword_1.
TemplateEquipped.seeAlso:
Equip
Unequip
Equipped
IsEquipable
Unequipped
EquipFailed
UnequipFailed
TemplateUnequipped

TurnStarted.description: Thrown when _Object is in combat and its turn has started.
TurnStarted.examples:
IF
TurnStarted(_MaskedServant)
AND
QRY_GLO_HagCanPossessMaskedVictims()
AND
DB_HAG_MaskedVictims((CHARACTER)_MaskedServant,_,_, _)
AND
HasActiveStatusWithGroup(_MaskedServant, "SG_Incapacitated", 0)
THEN
PROC_GLO_MaskedVictimPossessed(_MaskedServant);

TemplateUseStarted.description:
This event fires when the player starts using any instance of a given Root Template.
The Event returns the _Character using it, the _ItemTemplate of the item being used, and the specific instance of it as _Item.

TimelineScreenFadeStarted.description: Thrown in case the timeline of the dialog that’s ending has FadeOut property set to true and the fade is started.

TeleportToFleeWaypoint.description:
Event that gets thrown whenever a character is teleported to a Waypointed due to fleeing combat.
Parameters:
_Character - Character that gets teleported.
_Trigger - Destination trigger associated with the Waypoint.Further InformationWhen this event fires, no event TeleportToWaypoint  event is fired alongside it. This event replaces that event.
TeleportToFleeWaypoint.examples:
IF
TeleportToFleeWaypoint(_Player, _Test_WaypointTrigger)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
NOT DB_TEST_BlockedWaypoints(_Test_WaypointTrigger)
THEN
PROC_WaypointTeleportTo(_Player, _Test_WaypointTrigger);
TeleportToFleeWaypoint.seeAlso:
Teleported
ItemTeleported
TeleportedToCamp
TeleportedFromCamp
TeleportToFromCamp
TeleportToWaypoint
AppearTeleportFailed

UsingSpellOnTarget.description:
Event sent when a character uses a spell on a target.
Parameters:
_Caster: who or what casted the spell. Items can also send these events.
_Target: target of the spell.
_Spell: name of the spell including its spell category (Category_SpellName).
_SpellType: type of the spell.
_SpellElement: element of the spell.
_StoryActionID: story ID of the action of casting the spell. Can be useful to link casting a spell with applying a status.Further InformationList of spell types and elements can be found in UsingSpell.
UsingSpellOnTarget.examples:
IF
UsingSpellOnTarget((CHARACTER)_Hag,(CHARACTER)_Target,"Target_HAG_ViciousMockery",_,_,_)
AND
IsSummon(_Target,0)
AND
IsEnemy(_Hag,_Target,1)
THEN
PROC_TryStartAD((DIALOGRESOURCE)_HagLair_AD_ViciousMockery,_Hag,_Target);
UsingSpellOnTarget.seeAlso:

TradeEnds.description: Thrown after a player closes the trade UI.
TradeEnds.examples:
IF
TradeEnds((CHARACTER)_Player, (CHARACTER)_PickpocketTrader)
AND
DB_DialogNPCs(_Id, _PickpocketTrader, _)
AND
DB_DialogPlayers(_Id, _Player, 1)
AND
DB_DialogName(_Thieflings_Pickpocket_Dialog,_Id)
AND
QRY_DEN_Thieflings_CanPickpocket()
THEN
SetCanTrade(_PickpocketTrader, 0);
HappyWithDeal
RequestTrade
CanTrade
SetCanTrade

TemplateAddedTo.description:
Thrown when an entity that has a root template is added to another entity's inventory.
Parameters_ObjectTemplate: the root template of the entity that was added.
_Object: the entity that was added.
_InventoryHolder: the entity to whose inventory it was added.
_AddType: the type of addition to the inventory (Regular/Treasure/TradeTreasure). Options for _AddType are:
TradeTreasure - means it got added as part of a call to GenerateTreasure from story (based on the trade treasure specified in the side bar)
Treasure - means it got added as part of generating a character/container's regular inventory based on its treasure table in the side bar
Regular - is all other cases (scripted calls to add something to an inventory, picking something up, etc)Further InformationUsed if you want to look for a templated item. If you do not need to, you can use AddedTo.
TemplateAddedTo.examples:
IF
TemplateAddedTo(_Template, _, _Player, _)
AND
DB_BasementToyRoots(_Template)
AND
DB_Players((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
DB_InRegion(_Player, _Basement_SUB)
AND
QRY_OncePerUserAndNearbyPlayers(_Player, "PickedToyVoiceBark")
THEN
StartVoiceBark(_VB_PickedBasementToy, _Player);

TemplateUnequipped.description:
Emitted when an item is unequipped.
Further InformationThe _ItemTemplate UUID is of the root template. So if you have S_EXA_BronzeSword_A and S_EXA_BronzeSword_B and they both inherit from the same root template WPN_BronzeSword_1, then when either of the swords are unequipped they will both emit the same UUID from WPN_BronzeSword_1.
TemplateUnequipped.examples:
IF
TemplateUnequipped(_Template, _Char)
AND
DB_EXA_ProtectiveStaffTemplates(_Template)
AND
GetFlag(_State_IsProtected, _Char, 1)
THEN
ClearFlag(_State_IsProtected, _Char);
TemplateUnequipped.seeAlso:
Equip
Unequip
Equipped
IsEquipable
Unequipped
EquipFailed
UnequipFailed
TemplateEquipped

TutorialClosed.description:
This event is sent when a user closes a tutorial of type General (as defined in any UnifiedTutorials UUID table). These tutorials also known as popups with a confirm button.
Further InformationMainly used for chained tutorial messages, that is a tutorial message following another tutorial message. Note that due to the fact that we only get this on closing of General tutorials, it is only possible to create these types of tutorial chains/sequences:
General→General
General→SideNotif
In order to show the next tutorial message to the same user you need to get any of that user’s currently owned characters first.
TutorialClosed.examples:
IF
TutorialClosed(_UserId, _TutorialId)
AND
DB_GLO_Tutorials_TutorialChains_Optional_ShowingTo((INTEGER)_UserId,(UNIFIEDTUTORIAL)_TutorialId)
AND
DB_GLO_Tutorials_TutorialChains_Optional((UNIFIEDTUTORIAL)_TutorialId, _SubsequentTutorial)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
GetReservedUserID(_Player, _UserId)
AND
QRY_GLO_Tutorials_ShouldShowOptionalTutorial((CHARACTER)_Player,(UNIFIEDTUTORIAL)_SubsequentTutorial)
THEN
PROC_ShowUnifiedTutorialForPlayer(_Player, _SubsequentTutorial);

Unequipped.description: Emitted when an item is unequipped from a character.
Unequipped.examples:
IF
Unequipped(_MagicShield, _Char)
AND
GetFlag(_State_MagicShieldActive, _Char, 1)
THEN
ClearFlag(_State_MagicShieldActive, _Char);
Unequipped.seeAlso:
Equip
Unequip
Equipped
IsEquipable
EquipFailed
UnequipFailed
TemplateEquipped
TemplateUnequipped

TemplateUseFinished.description:
This Event fires when a character finishes using any instance of a given item root template.
E.g. When opening a door, it fires after the door is opened. When looting a container it fires after the loot window is closed. When reading a book, it fires after the readable window is closed.
The event returns what _Item was used, what _ItemTemplate it is an instance of, what _Character did the using, and _Success returns a 1 if the use was successful or a 0 if it was unsuccessful. E.g. using a door is not successful if the door is locked.

TemplatesCombined.description: The same as event Combined, but takes in templates instead of an (ITEM) it takes in an (ITEMROOT)parameter instead.

TemplateDestroyedBy.description:
The event is sent when item gets destroyed a few frames after the item throws DestroyingBy and DestroyedBy events.
_ItemTemplate: Root template of the destroyed item.
_Item: The item that was destroyed.
_Destroyer: The character that destroyed the item.
_DestroyerOwner: The owner of the character that destroyed the item if it was a summon or party follower, otherwise _Destroyer
_StoryActionID: the story action ID of the operation that destroyed the item.
TemplateDestroyedBy.examples:
IF 
TemplateDestroyedBy(_Platform_SpiderWeb, _Web, _Player, _,_)
AND
DB_InRegion(_Player, _Bottomless_Queen_WebArea)
AND
NOT DB_PermaDefeated((CHARACTER)_SpiderQueen)
THEN
PROC_RegisterInvestigationCrime_Matriarch(_Player);

UnlockedRecipe.description: Sent when a character unlocked a specific recipe.
UnlockedRecipe.examples:
IF
UnlockedRecipe(_Player, "ALCH_Potion_Antidote_Mugwort")
AND
QuestUpdateIsUnlocked(_Player, "GLO_Tadpole", "PoisonedByApprentice", 1)
THEN
QuestUpdate("GLO_Tadpole", "LearnedCureDaleland");

TemplateRemovedFrom.description:
Event thrown when an object that has a root template is removed from a particular inventory.
Parameters:
_ObjectTemplate: the root template of the entity that was added.
_Object - The object that was removed
_InventoryHolder - the inventory the item was removed fromFurther InformationUsed specifically for an item with a root template. If this isn’t important, you can use RemovedFrom.

TemporaryHostileRelationRemoved.description: Event is triggered after the temporary hostile relation set by SetRelationTemporaryHostile reverts back.
TemporaryHostileRelationRemoved.examples:
IF
TemporaryHostileRelationRemoved(_Enemy,_,_PlayerFaction)
AND
NOT DB_Players(_Enemy)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
IsCharacter(_Enemy,1)
AND
GetFaction(_Player,_PlayerFaction)
AND
GetAttitudeTowardsPlayer(_Enemy,_Player,_Attitude)
AND
_Attitude > -45
AND
NOT DB_DoNotChangeAttitudeAfterCombat((CHARACTER)_Enemy)
THEN
PROC_SetAttitude(_Enemy,_Player,-45);

UseStarted.description:
This Event fires when a character starts interacting with an item. This triggers before other effects of the interaction happen. E.g. When looting a container, the event fires before the loot window opens, and triggers even if locked.
The event returns what _Item was interacted with and what _Character did the interaction.

UserCampChestChanged.description: Is thrown when the camp _Chest is changed to a different _UserID.
UserCampChestChanged.examples:
IF
UserCampChestChanged(_UserId,_Chest)
AND
_Chest != NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
THEN
DB_Camp_UserCampChest(_UserId,_Chest);

UsingSpellAtPosition.description:
Event sent when a character uses a spell targeting a position in the world.
Parameters:
_Caster: who or what casted the spell. Items can also send these events.
_X, _Y, _Z: coordinates of the spell.
_Spell: name of the spell including its spell category (Category_SpellName).
_SpellType: type of the spell.
_SpellElement: element of the spell.
_StoryActionID: story ID of the action of casting the spell. Can be useful to link casting a spell with applying a status.Further InformationList of spell types and elements can be found in UsingSpell.
UsingSpellAtPosition.examples:
IF
UsingSpellAtPosition(_Player, _X, _Y, _Z, "Projectile_Jump", _, _, _StoryID)
AND
DB_CurrentLevel("WLD_Main_A")
AND
DB_PartyMembers((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
DB_GOB_FestivSleepers_JumpTrigger(_Trigger)
AND
DB_InRegion(_Player, _Trigger)
AND
PositionIsInTrigger(_X, _Y, _Z, _Trigger, 1)
THEN
DB_GOB_FestivSleepers_JumpNear(_Player, _StoryID, _X, _Y, _Z);
UsingSpellAtPosition.seeAlso:
UseSpell
AddSpell
RemoveSpell
UseSpellAtPosition

CastSpellFailed

StartedPreviewingSpell

MoviePlaylistFinished.description: Triggered when the movie playlist _MovieName is finished.

UseFinished.description:
This Event fires when a character finishes using an item.
E.g. When opening a door, it fires after the door is opened. When looting a container it fires after the loot window is closed. When reading a book, it fires after the readable window is closed.
The event returns what _Item was used and what _Character did the using, and _Sucess returns a 1 if the use was successful or a 0 if it was unsuccessful. E.g. using a door is not successful if the door is locked.
UseFinished.examples:
//Unsuccessful example
IF
UseFinished(_Player,(ITEM)_Tollhouse_Safe1,0)
AND
IsLocked(_Tollhouse_Safe1,1)
AND
DB_GlobalFlag((FLAG)_Tollhouse_LearnedSkeletonSafeCombo)
AND
QRY_OnlyOnce("Tollhouse_UnlockedSkeletonSafe")
THEN
PROC_TryStartAD(_Tollhouse_AD_SaveOpenedWithCombination,_Tollhouse_Safe1);
PROC_ItemUnlockAndOpen(_Tollhouse_Safe1);

//Successful example
IF
UseFinished(_Player, _WeaponsPuzzle_Note, 1)
AND
QRY_OncePerUserAndNearbyPlayers((CHARACTER)_Player,"Monastery_HiddenNoteReaction")
THEN
StartVoiceBark((VOICEBARKRESOURCE)_WeaponsPuzzle_VB_HiddenNoteReaction, _Player);

UserMakeWar.description:
Thrown when one user declares war on or make peace with another user via the UI, or in reaction to MakeWar or MakePeace.
Parameters_SourceUserID: The user that declared war/made peace, or the first parameter of MakeWar/MakePeace.
_TargetUserID: The user on whom war was declared or with whom peace was made, or the second parameter of MakeWar/MakePeace.
_War: 1 if war was declared, 0 if peace was made.Further InformationAt any point in the game, users can decide to start combat with the characters of another user. This is called "making war". The act of making war with another user does not mean that the party gets split. The users' characters remain in the same party, but will be hostile towards each other. On top of that the users that are at war will lock their inventories and will not share those with the party anymore.

UserEvent.description: Thrown for each user when iterating them.
UserEvent.examples:
IF
UserEvent(_UserID, "SafeRomance_Check")
AND
UserGetSafeRomanceOption(_UserID, _State)
THEN
PROC_SafeRomance_UpdateState(_UserID, _State);

VoiceBarkStarted.description: Thrown when a voicebark succeeds in finding characters and an AD for the voicebark called _Bark.

UserConnected.description:
Is thrown when a player with _UserID is connected to the game and starts character creation.
Further Information"Users" are the game term for human players in the game. Every client that connects to the game, including the one that started the host game, gets assigned a User ID.
User IDs are volatile: they can change at any time, in particular when loading a saved game, or when someone disconnects and reconnects. _UserProfileID contains a persistent User identifier.

UserDisconnected.description:
Is thrown when a player with _UserID disconnects from the ongoing game.
Further Information"Users" are the game term for human players in the game. Every client that connects to the game, including the one that started the host game, gets assigned a User ID.
User IDs are volatile: they can change at any time, in particular when loading a saved game, or when someone disconnects and reconnects. _UserProfileID contains a persistent User identifier.
UserDisconnected.examples:
IF
UserDisconnected(_UserID,_,_)
AND
DB_ResetTutorialsRequest(_UserID, _TutCompleted)
THEN
NOT DB_ResetTutorialsRequest(_UserID, _TutCompleted);

UsingSpellInTrigger.description:
Event sent when a character uses a spell on a target and the later is inside a trigger. UsingSpellInTrigger() is only thrown if the target is inside a global trigger.
Parameters:
_Caster: who or what casted the spell. Items can also send these events.
_Spell: name of the spell including its spell category (Category_SpellName).
_SpellType: type of the spell.
_SpellElement: element of the spell.
_Trigger: trigger in which the target is inside.
_StoryActionID: story ID of the action of casting the spell. Can be useful to link casting a spell with applying a status.Further InformationIf there are overlapping global triggers, you will only get an event for the first trigger encountered by code when looking for one (which one it is, is undefined).
If a spell has multiple targets, you can get multiple events for the same spell casting action.
A list with all the spell types and elements can be found in UsingSpell.
UsingSpellInTrigger.examples:
IF
UsingSpellInTrigger(_Lab_TrappedDevourer,"Projectile_Jump",_,_,(TRIGGER)_DevourerJumpStartTrigger, _ID)
AND
IsInTrigger(_Lab_TrappedDevourer, (TRIGGER)_DevourerJumpStartTrigger,1)
AND
DB_TUT_Lab_UsJumping(1)
THEN
DB_TUT_Lab_UsJumpID(_ID);

UserCharacterLongRested.description:
Thrown when the _Character of an user has long rested. _IsFullRest indicates with a bool whether that player had a full or half rest.
Further Information_IsFullRest
1 - Is a full rest.
0 - Is a half rest.

TeleportedToCamp.description:
Event that fires when a Character gets teleported to camp (usually because players used the “Go to Camp” option).
Parameters:
_Character - The character that got teleported to camp.Further InformationNote that it fires both for PCs and NPCs.
The event needs a CampRegionTrigger in the Camp properly set, otherwise it won’t fire at all.
TeleportedToCamp.seeAlso:
Teleported
ItemTeleported
TeleportedFromCamp
TeleportToFromCamp
TeleportToWaypoint
TeleportToFleeWaypoint
AppearTeleportFailed

VoiceBarkFailed.description: Thrown when a voicebark fails to find characters and an AD for the voicebark called _Bark.

UserAvatarCreated.description:
Thrown when a new player with _UserID created their avatar _Character and the guardian _Daisy in the character creator and connects to an existing game.
Further Information"Users" are the game term for human players in the game. Every client that connects to the game, including the one that started the host game, gets assigned a User ID.
User IDs are volatile: they can change at any time, in particular when loading a saved game, or when someone disconnects and reconnects. Never store this value in a database. Only use it immediately after requesting it, within the same frame. If you need a persistent identifier for a user (or rather, user profile), pass the result of this query to GetUserProfileID.

Unlocked.description: Emitted when an item is unlocked, either through script or gameplay.

TutorialBoxClosed.description:
Deprecated. Do not use.
Superseded by TutorialClosed in BG3 release version of the tutorial system.

DualEntityEvent.description: Like EntityEvent but with 2 entities. These events may come from SetDualEntityEvent, but also from other APIs and within code.
DualEntityEvent.examples:
IF
UseFinished(_Player,_Item, 0)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
NOT DB_CustomUseItemResponse(_Player,_Item,0)
AND
IsLocked(_Item, 1)
THEN
SetDualEntityEvent(_Player, _Item, "GLO_UsedLockedItem");
IF
DualEntityEvent(_Player, _Item, "GLO_UsedLockedItem")
THEN
...
DualEntityEvent.seeAlso:
EntityEvent
SetEntityEvent
SetDualEntityEvent
SetEntityEventReal
SetDualEntityEventReal
SetEntityEventDialog
SetDualEntityEventDialog
SetEntityEventGUID

ScreenFadeDone.description: An event sent when a previously activated Screen Fade has finished and successfully faded the screen to black. This is specifically thrown when the Screen has successfully faded to black.

PreMovedBy.description:
An event that happens just before an item is about to be moved, returning both the item about to be moved and the character attempting to move it.
Parameters:
_Item - The item being moved
_Character - The character doing the moving

DialogRollResult.description:
Thrown when a roll happens within a dialogue and finishes with a result.
Parameters are similar to RollResult.

ItemEnteredTrigger.description: Thrown when _Item is placed in the _Trigger that has been registered for item events.

Teleported.description:
This event is fired whenever character is teleported trough any means.
Parameters:
_Target - Character that got teleported.
_Cause - The Character that caused the teleport.
_OldX, _OldY, _OldZ - Old position of the target.
_NewX, _NewY, _NewZ - New position of the target.
_Spell - If a spell was used, the name of the spell in STRING format.Further InformationNote that the event does only fire for CHARACTER type entities.
Corpses of CHARACTER type entities also fire this event, but they must not be on a container / inventory.
If character got teleported trough script, the _Spell field will return "Invalid".
If no _Cause exists, it will return  NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
Teleported.seeAlso:
ItemTeleported
TeleportedToCamp
TeleportedFromCamp
TeleportToFromCamp
TeleportToWaypoint
TeleportToFleeWaypoint
AppearTeleportFailed

TutorialEvent.description:
Triggered by the engine on predefined set of various events defined in UUID table TutorialEvents.
Further InformationScript can’t otherwise observe such events as they are either too custom and thus don’t justify having a dedicated Osiris event (many UI events, RPG ruleset events, etc., example tutorial events: Movement_Depleted, UserAction_DisplayWorldTooltips) or too expensive (example tutorial event: UserAction_CameraMove) and thus should be disabled as soon as possible after being processed using DisableTutorialEvent.
All tutorial events are enabled by default.
Currently almost each tutorial event is disabled right after being caught for the first time.
Resetting tutorials in the game menu re-enables all tutorial events.
There’s a special script in place which currently:
prevents events from being triggered in Character Creation.
disables a sets of events which are designed to be irrelevant for later Acts of the game as soon as possible. If not disabled they would spam otherwise.

TemplateLeftTrigger.description: Thrown when an _Item is removed from a _Trigger that has been registered for item events.

UnequipFailed.description:
Emitted when a character/player or script fails to remove an item from an equipment slot.
Further InformationAn example of an item failing to unequip could be if the item it is attempting to remove is in a locked equipment slot.
UnequipFailed.seeAlso:
Equip
Unequip
Equipped
IsEquipable
Unequipped
EquipFailed
TemplateEquipped
TemplateUnequipped

UsingSpell.description:
The first event that gets thrown when a character or item starts casting a spell. At this point the spell casting can still fail.
Parameters:
_Caster: who or what casted the spell. Items can also send these events.
_Spell: name of the spell including its spell category (Category_SpellName).
_SpellType: type of the spell.
_SpellElement: element of the spell.
_StoryActionID: story ID of the action of casting the spell. Can be useful to link casting a spell with applying a status.Further InformationThe usual order of events when using and casting spells is:
Start of casting the spell: UsingSpell()
Optionally one or more of the following based on the spell target type(s), also immediately when starting the spell casting:
UsingSpellInTrigger
UsingSpellAtPosition
UsingSpellOnTarget
If casting the spell is successful,
One CastSpell event for every target as the spell casting animations get played.
CastedSpell when casting the spell finishes and all targets have been hit.
If casting the spell fails:
CastSpellFailedIf you wish to react to targets getting hit by spells, you can use AttackedBy and match the _StoryActionID from one of the spell casting events. Note, however, that these events only fire once the game has determined whether or not the (complete) attack has killed the target (i.e., in case of a multi-attack spell like Magic Missile, until all missiles have been processed), so they will be delayed.
List of spell types:
none
cone
multistrike
projectile
projectilestrike
rush
shout
storm
target
teleportation
wall
throwList of spell elements:
Abjuration
Conjuration
Divination
Enchantment
Evocation
Illusion
Necromancy
Transmutation
UsingSpell.examples:
IF
UsingSpell(_Player, "_Hagspawn_SummonHusband", _, _, _)
AND
DB_HAG_SummonHusbandWand(_SummonHusbandWand)
AND
NOT GetInventoryOwner(_SummonHusbandWand, _Player)
THEN
ToInventory(_SummonHusbandWand, _Player, -1, 1, 1);
UsingSpell.seeAlso:
UseSpell
AddSpell
RemoveSpell
UseSpellAtPosition
CastedSpell
CastSpell
CastSpellFailed
LearnedSpell
StartedPreviewingSpell
UsingSpellAtPosition
UsingSpellInTrigger
UsingSpellOnTarget
CanLearnSpell
CanShowSpellForCharacter
GetSpellFromSet
HasSpell
IsSpellActive
SpellHasSpellFlag

ShapeshiftChanged.description:
Thrown when _Character has gained or lost a shapeshift form.
Further Information_Race holds the new race that corresponds to the new shape
_Gender holds the new gender that corresponds to the new shape
_ShapeshiftStatus holds the status responsible for the shapeshift, or empty string (““) if the shapeshift is lost.

TextEvent.description: Event with name _Event thrown from ReCon with command oe _Event.
TextEvent.examples:
IF
TextEvent("PlayDebugText")
AND
GetHostCharacter(_Player)
THEN
DebugText(_Player, "Playing Debug Text");

CharacterGetCrimeRegion.description:
Returns the string of the crime region the _Character is currently in.
Further InformationCrime regions are areas defined by crime region triggers.

CanMove.description:
A query that checks whether the _Character reference is able to move, and not under any movement restricting status/effects.
Parameters:
_Character - The character to check if they have the ability to move
_Bool - Value of 0 means the character is unable to move, 1 means ableFurther InformationThis also checks whether the character is out of movement resources. If that isn’t a factor you want to consider, an alternative is IsMovementBlocked.

CharacterIgnoreActiveCrimes.description:
Tries to remove all unresolved crimes registered for the player character _Character.
Further InformationYou can first check with CharacterCanIgnoreActiveCrimes whether all crimes for the character can be removed.
Returns failure if some crime cannot be removed. This can happen if a player is currently being interrogated for a crime.
In case of failure, every crime that could be removed is still removed.
CharacterIgnoreActiveCrimes.examples:
PROC
PROC_HAV_Siege_TeleportCinematicActors()
AND
DB_HAV_Siege_CinematicPlayerPos(_Pos)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
DB_InRegion(_Player,_Siege_CinematicIncludeZone)
AND
CharacterIgnoreActiveCrimes(_Player, _)
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);

CanSitOn.description:
Returns whether _Item can be sat on.
Further InformationThis query returns 1 even if someone else is already occupying the item and there is no room for another character to "sit" on it, or if the item was marked as non-interactable.
Success/Failure: Default return value.
_Bool: 1 if the item has at least one of the following use actions: sit, lie, or constrain; 0 in other cases.

CanTrade.description:
Returns whether players can initiate trading with the NPC _Trader during dialogues.
Further InformationSuccess/Failure: Fails if _Trader does not exist.
_Bool: 1 or 0 depending on whether players currently can or cannot initiate trades with _Trader.
CanTrade.examples:
IF
DialogStarted((DIALOGRESOURCE)_DEN_Thieflings_Pickpocket, _)
AND
DB_GlobalFlag(_DEN_Hideout_State_ResolvedThieflings)
AND
CanTrade(_DEN_PickpocketTrader, 0)
THEN
SetCanTrade(_DEN_PickpocketTrader, 1);

CanJoinCombat.description:
Checks the entity property CanJoinCombat and returns 0 (false) or 1 (true).  This property dictates if the entity can be assigned a combat GUID.
Further InformationThis property can be seen in the side bar of the editor on both characters and items.
If an entity has CanJoinCombat set to false, it will take precedence over Can Fight. So a player attacking the entity will not trigger combat between the entities, even though Can Fight is true.
CanJoinCombat.examples:
IF
EnteredTrigger(_Character, S_EXA_CageTrigger_XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX)
AND
CanJoinCombat(_Character, 1)
THEN
SetCanJoinCombat(_Character, 0);

IF
LeftTrigger(_Character, S_EXA_CageTrigger_XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX)
AND
CanJoinCombat(_Character, 0)
THEN
SetCanJoinCombat(_Character, 1);

CharacterCanSpotCrimes.description:
Checks if _Character can spot crimes.
Returns Values:
Returns 0 if _Character can’t spot crimes or 1 if _Character can spot crimes.

CanSeeCached.description:
Same as CanSee but instead of casting a new raycast to determine whether _Source can see _Target, it uses a cached raycast which is more efficient, but can return stale results as it updates in a deferred (one-frame-delayed) way in response to events (entity moved, became offstage, etc).
Further InformationUse over CanSee when it doesn’t matter that the result may be a bit out-of-date and you want to improve performance.

CanSee.description: Returns whether _Source can see _Target. This call takes sneaking into account. Additionally, if _Source is an NPC or a summon, and _Target is also a character, this query registers a temporary event generator for that pair to throw event Saw  and event LostSightOf  events.
CanSee.examples:
IF
DB_Sees(_Player, _Brother)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
DB_HAG_HagAmbush_Brothers_DiedAfterLongRest(_Brother)
AND
CanSee(_Player, _Brother, 1)
AND
DB_GlobalFlag((FLAG)_Confrontation_PlayerSidedBrothers)
AND
QRY_OncePerUserAndNearbyPlayers(_Player, "FoundBrothersAfterLongRest")
THEN
StartVoiceBark(_VB_FoundBrothersAfterLongRest, _Player);

CombatGetActiveEntity.description: Return the the first active (not destroyed/dead) entity (Item or Character) in combat with ID _CombatGuid.
CombatGetActiveEntity.examples:
IF
DB_TakingIsobel_MainRoomCombatDialog(_Dialog)
AND
DB_TakingIsobel_MainRoomCombat(_CombatID)
AND
CombatGetActiveEntity(_CombatID, _Entity)
AND
NOT DB_PartyMembers((CHARACTER)_Entity)
THEN
SetEntityEventReal(_Entity, "GLO_CombatWait", 30.0, 1); //Needs to match duration of dialogue.
DB_TakingIsobel_MainRoomCombat_Paused(_Entity);

AnubisGetIsEnabled.description:
Checks if the character has a defined Anubis tree.
Parameters:
_Character: character whom to check
_AnubisEnabled: returns 1 if the character has an Anubis tree and 0 if they don’t.Further InformationTypically used to check if the character’s Anubis tree has crashed.
AnubisGetIsEnabled.examples:
IF
SavegameLoaded(_Major, _Minor, _Revision, _Build)
AND
QRY_VersionIsOlderThan(_Major, _Minor, _Revision, _Build, 4, 0, 8, 500)
AND
DB_JoinedParty(_PartyMember)
AND
AnubisGetIsEnabled(_PartyMember,0)
THEN
PROC_SavegamePatches_RestoreAnubisConfig(_PartyMember);

CombatGetGuidFor.description: Returns _CombatGUID of the combat in which _Object is currently participating. Fails, if _Object is not in combat.
CombatGetGuidFor.examples:
IF
EnteredCombat(_Player, _ID)
AND
DB_Players((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
CombatGetGuidFor(_BugBearVendor, _ID)
THEN
PROC_BugBearVendor_CombatAD(_ID);

CanFight.description:
Returns whether the _Entity’s CanFight property is set to True. Entities with the CanFight property set to True can get turns in a combat's initiative order.
Further InformationThe CanFight property should not be confused with the CanJoinCombat property. An object with CanJoinCombat and CanFight both set to False won't join combat or ever behave in a turn-based manner. For example, if such an object is set on fire by a character in a fight, their Burning status will tick in real-time. An object, in the same scenario, with CanJoinCombat set to True but CanFight set to False won't get a turn order but will have their Burning status tick at the end of a round. An object with CanJoinCombat and CanFight both set to True will enter the initiative order.
Behaviour for CanJoinCombat False but CanFight True is undefined and should not be used.
CanFight.examples:
QRY
QRY_LOW_CazadorsPalace_RitualRoom_IsSpawnThatCanFight((CHARACTER)_NPC)
AND
DB_LOW_CazadorsPalace_RitualRoom_Spawns(_NPC, _, _, _)
AND
CanFight(_NPC, 1)
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);

CheckRulesetModifierBool.description:
Checks if a chosen Ruleset Modifier set to a specific value.
Look for a ruleset in UUID Object Editor in RulesetModifiers and make sure you’re using the correct query according to the type of a ruleset.
Further InformationGetRulesetModifierBool can return the value of a chosen Ruleset.

CharacterRemoveTaggedItems.description: Deleted up to _Amount items from the inventory of the _Character that match the tag expression _Tags, and returns how many of them have been deleted as _AmountRemoved.
CharacterRemoveTaggedItems.examples:
PROC
PROC_MagicLock_ConsumeKey((CHARACTER)_Char, (ITEM)_Door)
AND
CharacterRemoveTaggedItems(_Char, "MAGICKEY", 1, _Number)
AND
_Number >= 1
THEN
Open(_Door);

ChangeApprovalRating.description:
Changes the approval value of the _RatingOwner (Typically a companion) towards the _RatedCharacter by _Delta.
Parameters
_RatingOwner: Typically the companion who offers the approval.
_RatedCharacter: The character who is rated by the companion, typically the player.
_Type: The scope of the change.
_Delta: The quantity of the approval change.
_Bool: Returns whether it succeeded or not.Further Information_Type:
Global: 0 - The change is always applied
Local: 1 - The change is applied within a certain radius
Private: 2 - The change is applied only if Companion and Avatar are in the same dialogue.
ChangeApprovalRating.examples:
IF
FlagSet(_Gale_State_TaraKiller, _Player, _)
AND
DB_Avatars((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
ChangeApprovalRating((CHARACTER)_Player_Gale, _Player, 1, -20, _)
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);

CharacterGetOwner.description: Returns the owner of _Character. Owned characters include party followers and summons.
CharacterGetOwner.examples:
PROC
PROC_MOO_MintharaFate_Checkpoint((CHARACTER)_Guard)
AND
CharacterGetOwner(_DrowCommander, _Player)
AND
DB_Sees(_Guard, _Player)
AND
NOT DB_MOO_MintharaFate_CheckpointPassed(1)
AND
QRY_StartDialog(_MintharaFate_Checkpoint, _EntranceGuard_001, _EntranceGuard_002, _DrowCommander, _Player)
THEN
DB_MOO_MintharaFate_CheckpointPassed(1);

CheckRulesetModifierFloat.description:
Checks if a chosen Ruleset Modifier set to a specific value.
Look for a ruleset in UUID Object Editor in RulesetModifiers and make sure you’re using the correct query according to the type of a ruleset.
Further InformationGetRulesetModifierFloat can return the value of a chosen Ruleset.
CheckRulesetModifierFloat.examples:
PROC
PROC_IronThrone_SetTotalTurns()
AND
DB_IronThrone_TotalTurnsDifficultySetting(_Difficulty, _TotalTurns)
AND
CheckRulesetModifierString(SCRIPTED_COMBAT_MECHANICS_cac2d8bd-c197-4a84-9df1-f86f54ad4521,_Difficulty,1)
THEN
DB_IronThrone_TotalTurns(_TotalTurns);

CanSpotSneakers.description:
Checks if _Character can spot sneaking character.
Returns Values:
Returns 0 if _Character can’t spot sneaking characters or 1 if _Character can spot sneaking characters.

CheckRulesetModifierString.description:
Checks if a chosen Ruleset Modifier set to a specific value.
Look for a ruleset in UUID Object Editor in RulesetModifiers and make sure you’re using the correct query according to the type of a ruleset.
Further InformationIn BG3 this is used to check the overall difficulty:
SCRIPTED_COMBAT_MECHANICS_cac2d8bd-c197-4a84-9df1-f86f54ad4521
EASY
MEDIUM
HARDGetRulesetModifierString can return the value of a chosen Ruleset.
CheckRulesetModifierString.examples:
PROC
PROC_SetTotalTurns()
AND
DB_TotalTurnsDifficultySetting(_Difficulty, _TotalTurns)
AND
CheckRulesetModifierString(SCRIPTED_COMBAT_MECHANICS_cac2d8bd-c197-4a84-9df1-f86f54ad4521,_Difficulty,1)
THEN
DB_TotalTurns(_TotalTurns);

CanBePickpocketed.description:
Returns the current value of the “Can Be Pickpocketed” property of an entity.
Further InformationThe query always fails if the entity does not exist.
Otherwise it returns 0 if the entity cannot be pickpocketed, and 1 if it can.
See also SetCanBePickpocketed

CrimeGetType.description:
This system query returns the _CrimeType of disturbance with _CrimeID.
Further InformationThe _CrimeType is essentially the name of the disturbance as found in the stats editor. Since the disturbance pipeline does not always explicitly refer to the name of the disturbance by opting to operate using the _CrimeID instead, this query is useful to check that we are working with the desired disturbance type.
Full DefinitionCrimeGetType([in](INTEGER)_CrimeID, [out](STRING)_CrimeType)
ExampleIF
CharacterOnCrimeSensibleActionNotification(_NPC, _Region, _CrimeID,_, _, _, _, _, _, 1)
AND
CrimeGetType(_CrimeID, _MyDisturbance)
AND
DB_OnlyReactOnceDisturbance(_MyDisturbance)
THEN
PROC_CharacterDisableCrime(_NPC, _MyDisturbance);

CharacterIsCrimeEnabled.description: This query will return a _Bool of value 1 if the disturbance with name _Crime is currently enabled on the _Character, and 0 otherwise.

CanLearnSpell.description:
Checks if a character can learn a spell or not from an item, usually for wizards finding scrolls.
Parameters:
_Character: character to check on if they can learn it or not.
_Item: item that has the spell.
_ValidateGoldCost: consider if the character has enough money or not. If set to 0 it will skip the validation.
_Bool: returns 1 if they can learn it or 0 if not.
CanLearnSpell.examples:
IF
AddedTo(_Item, _Player, _)
AND
DB_Players((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
QRY_IsExistingItem(_Item, 1)
AND
IsTagged(_Item, (TAG)_SCROLL, 1)
AND
CanLearnSpell(_Player, (ITEM)_Item, 0, 1)
THEN
PROC_ShowUnifiedTutorialForPlayer(_Player, (UNIFIEDTUTORIAL)_Learning_Spells);
CanLearnSpell.seeAlso:
UseSpell
AddSpell
RemoveSpell
UseSpellAtPosition
CastedSpell
CastSpell
CastSpellFailed
LearnedSpell
StartedPreviewingSpell
UsingSpell
UsingSpellAtPosition
UsingSpellInTrigger
UsingSpellOnTarget
CanShowSpellForCharacter
GetSpellFromSet
HasSpell
IsSpellActive
SpellHasSpellFlag

CanShowSpellForCharacter.description:
Check if the spell is visible for the character.
Parameters:
_Character: character to check on if it’s visible or not.
_SpellID: name of the spell, including the category to which it pertains (for example Target_FindFamiliar_Dog).
_Bool: returns 1 if it’s visible or 0 if not.Further InformationIt is good to check if the character has the spell with HasSpell before using this query.
CanShowSpellForCharacter.examples:
PROC
PROC_TryPlayerNightsongFirstTurn((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
DB_OnlyOnce("KethericIntro_Finished")
AND
DB_Is_InCombat(_Player,_CombatId)
AND
DB_Is_InCombat(_Nightsong,_CombatId)
AND
CanShowSpellForCharacter((CHARACTER)_Player, "Target_Help", 1)
THEN
PROC_ShowUnifiedTutorialForPlayer(_Player, (UNIFIEDTUTORIAL)_Helping_Downed_Characters__Nightsong);
CanShowSpellForCharacter.seeAlso:
UseSpell
AddSpell
RemoveSpell
UseSpellAtPosition
CastedSpell
CastSpell
CastSpellFailed
LearnedSpell
StartedPreviewingSpell
UsingSpell
UsingSpellAtPosition
UsingSpellInTrigger
UsingSpellOnTarget
CanLearnSpell
GetSpellFromSet
HasSpell
IsSpellActive
SpellHasSpellFlag

Concatenate.description: Concatenates _StringA and _StringB and returns string _Result as the result of concatenation.
Concatenate.examples:
IF
DB_OneShotPartyTrigger(_Trigger)
AND
DB_OneShotPlayerTrigger(_Trigger)
AND
GUIDToString(_Trigger, _TriggerName)
AND
Concatenate("Warning: Trigger ", _TriggerName, _NamedTrigger)
AND
Concatenate(_NamedTrigger, " is both a Oneshot Party and Oneshot Player trigger. Removing Oneshot Player entry.", _Message)
THEN
DebugBreak(_Message);

CreateAtObject.description:
Same as CreateAt but instead of requiring specific coordinates for the spawn location, it requires an object whose coordinates are taken instead.
Parameters:
_TemplateId: Template that _CreatedObject should be instanced from
Even if technically it accepts GUIDSTRING, only templates of type CHARACTERROOT should be used or it will fail and throw an assert
_Anchor: The object whose coordinates are taken for spawn location
_Temporary: If 1 and if the _CreatedObject is a CHARACTER, it will be a temporary one
_PlaySpawn: If 1 and if the _CreatedObject is a CHARACTER, it will play a spawn animation
_SpawnFinishEvent: If not an empty string, the event will be sent once _CreatedObject spawns. If it’s playing a spawn animation, it will be sent after the animation finishes.
_MatchOrientation: If 1, _CreatedObject will be also take the rotation of _Anchor
Returns _CreatedObject: The created object
CreateAtObject.examples:
PROC
PROC_Sussur_Wither((ITEM)_Item)
AND
IsInInventory(_Item,0)
AND
GetPosition(_Item,_X,_Y,_Z)
AND
CreateAtObject(_WitheredSussurPetals,_Item,0,0,"",0,_Petal1)
AND
CreateAtObject(_WitheredSussurPetals,_Item,0,0,"",0,_Petal2)
THEN
ScatterAt((ITEM)_Petal1,_X,_Y,_Z);
ScatterAt((ITEM)_Petal2,_X,_Y,_Z);

CanAllPartiesLongRest.description:
Checks whether all parties can long rest. Returns this value as a bool in _LongRestIsAllowed.
Further InformationReturn Values:
_LongRestIsAllowed
1: Long rest is allowed.
0: Long rest is not allowed.

CanCastSpellOnEnemyInSameCombat.description: Checks if _Character can cast _SpellID spell on target _Target.
CanCastSpellOnEnemyInSameCombat.examples:
QRY
QRY_GLO_Tutorials_CanCastAnySneakAttack((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
DB_GLO_Tutorials_SneakAttackSpells(_SneakAttackSpell)
AND
CanCastSpellOnEnemyInSameCombat((CHARACTER)_Player, _SneakAttackSpell, _ValidTarget) 
AND
_ValidTarget != NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);

CheckRulesetModifierInt.description:
Checks if a chosen Ruleset Modifier set to a specific value.
Look for a ruleset in UUID Object Editor in RulesetModifiers and make sure you’re using the correct query according to the type of a ruleset.
Further InformationGetRulesetModifierInt can return the value of a chosen Ruleset.
CheckRulesetModifierInt.examples:
PROC
PROC_IronThrone_SetTotalTurns()
AND
DB_IronThrone_TotalTurnsDifficultySetting(_Difficulty, _TotalTurns)
AND
CheckRulesetModifierString(SCRIPTED_COMBAT_MECHANICS_cac2d8bd-c197-4a84-9df1-f86f54ad4521,_Difficulty,1)
THEN
DB_IronThrone_TotalTurns(_TotalTurns);

CharacterCanIgnoreActiveCrimes.description:
Check whether all unresolved crimes registered for the player character _Character can be removed.
Further InformationThe only case in which this query can fail (other than by specifying invalid parameters), is when _Character is currently being confronted for one their crimes (= in a crime dialog).
This query does not actually ignore/stop any crimes. If this query succeeds, use CharacterIgnoreActiveCrimes to perform the operation.

CreateNarrativeCombat.description:
Creates a narrative combat ID.
Further InformationNarrative combat IDs are different from normal combat IDs in that they ignore ALL combat generics (ending combat, removing dead characters on death, entering combat, ending combat, etc.) and will not merge with normal combat.
This makes narrative combat fairly bug prone. It’s usually best to reserve narrative combat for extreme circumstances within very contained environments.
Script is responsible for ALL handling of narrative combat.
It is possible for this combat to exist without any combat entities, which makes tracking the combat very important.
CreateNarrativeCombat.examples:
PROC
PROC_EXA_Example_StartNarrativeCombat()
AND
CreateNarrativeCombat(_ID)
THEN
DB_EXA_Example_NarrativeCombatID(_ID);

CrimeGetTension.description:
This system query returns the _Tension of disturbance with _CrimeID.
Further Information_Tension is a numeric value that is applied to an area to modify reactions and triggers alarm. It represents how likely the NPCs are to let you get away with a crime. Different crimes will raise the tension, and you can get the current tension level by using the subject query.
You could use the subject query to make some custom modification to your region if the tension goes above a certain level. For example, guards in our systems are designed to upgrade their equipment when the tension passes a certain point.
Full DefinitionCrimeGetTension([in](INTEGER)_CrimeID, [out](INTEGER)_Tension)

CharacterRegisterCrimeWithPosition.description:
This query is what registers a new disturbance for a given character at a given position. Disturbances are specific events and circumstances in the game that can be user-defined using the stats editor. NPCs will react to disturbances using what we call generic behaviours.
_Player is the source of the disturbance, aka instigator or criminal. It cannot be NULL.
_CrimeType the type of disturbance as defined in the stats editor.
_Evidence can reference a character, item, trigger, that is important to this disturbance. E.g. for a Steal crime it's the stolen item, and when trespassing it's the trespass trigger. It can be NULL.
_Victim can reference the character that should be preferred for reacting to this disturbance under certain conditions. It is also important for victim-only disturbances, in which case either the victim reacts or no-one does.
_CrimeID is an integer by which the crime is tracked internally.
_X, _Y, _Z is the position of the disturbance.Further InformationThe query will fail if the character is not available(ie. not in the level) or if the disturbance does not exist. The query will also fail if ShutdownCrimeSystem was called, but this isn’t something that would occur during a typical play session.
Full Definitionquery CharacterRegisterCrimeWithPosition([in](CHARACTER)_Player, [in](STRING)_CrimeType, [in](GUIDSTRING)_Evidence, [in](CHARACTER)_Victim, [in](REAL)_X, [in](REAL)_Y, [in](REAL)_Z, [in](INTEGER)_CrimeID, [out](INTEGER)_Success)

CharacterRegisterCrime.description:
This query is what registers a new disturbance for a given character. Disturbances are specific events and circumstances in the game that can be user-defined using the stats editor. NPCs will react to disturbances using what we call generic behaviours.
_Player is the source of the disturbance, aka instigator or criminal. It cannot be NULL. Unless the position is explicitly specified through CharacterRegisterCrimeWithPosition , this is the location of the disturbance.
_CrimeType the type of disturbance as defined in the stats editor.
_Evidence can reference a character, item, trigger, that is important to this disturbance. E.g. for a Steal crime it's the stolen item, and when trespassing it's the trespass trigger. It can be NULL.
_Victim can reference the character that should be preferred for reacting to this disturbance under certain conditions. It is also important for victim-only disturbances, in which case either the victim reacts or no-one does.
_CrimeID is an integer by which the crime is tracked internally.Further InformationThe query will fail if the character is not available(ie. not in the level) or if the disturbance does not exist. The query will also fail if ShutdownCrimeSystem was called, but this isn’t something that would occur during a typical play session.
Full Definitionquery CharacterRegisterCrime([in](CHARACTER)_Player, [in](STRING)_CrimeType, [in](GUIDSTRING)_Evidence, [in](CHARACTER)_Victim, [in](INTEGER)_CrimeID, [out](INTEGER)_Success)

CrimeGetLeadInvestigator.description:
Query used to get the lead investigator of a disturbance with _CrimeID.
Further InformationThe lead investigator is the primary NPC that should react to the witnessed disturbance. This NPC is selected by code based on their tags and the data definitions.

DrawSurfaceOnPath.description:
Draws a continuous line of width _Radius of surface type _SurfaceType behind the _FollowObject as it moves, with lifetime _Lifetime. The _OwnerObject parameter can be used to set the owner of this surface.
Further InformationThis query currently does not work correctly if _Radius is >= 2.0
The _Lifetime only starts ticking after StopDrawSurfaceOnPath has been called for the _SurfaceActionHandle. It can be -1.0 to make the surface permanent.
DrawSurfaceOnPath.examples:
IF
EntityEvent(_ThayanOrb, "FOR_ThayanCellar_OrbEmerged")
AND
DrawSurfaceOnPath(_ThayanOrb, _FOR_ThayanOrb, "SurfaceFire", 0.5, 30.0, _Handle)
THEN
DB_FOR_ThayanCellar_SurfaceHandle(_Handle);
ApplyStatus(_ThayanOrb, "FOR_THAYANCELLAR_TRAPFUEL", 60.0, 1, NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000);

CrimeGetCriminal.description:
Return the Nth criminal of a crime.
Parameters:
_CrimeID: the disturbance/crime’s ID
_CriminalIndex: the index of the criminal to get, 1-based
_Criminal: the Nth criminal of that disturbance/crimeFurther InformationIf _CriminalIndex is <= 0 or > the number of criminals, the query fails. It will never return NULL.

ConcatenateInteger.description: Adds _Number to the end of _String and returns result in _Result.

CrimeInvestigatorConfrontSuspect.description:
This query tells code that _Interrogator spotted a _Suspect while in investigation state for disturbance _CrimeID. The query returns _StartInterrogation as 1 for interrogate or 0 for confront, depending on the investigation kind.
The query will fail if:
The _CrimeID is invalid.
The _Investigator is not investigating.
The disturbance is not in an investigation state.
The _Investigator cannot confront the suspect.

CrimeHasEvidence.description:
Returns whether the specified character/item/trigger is part of the evidence of a disturbance/crime.
Parameters:
_CrimeID: the disturbance/crime’s ID
_Evidence: the thing of which to check whether it’s part of the evidence
_HasEvidence: 1 if _Evidence is part of the disturbance/crime’s evidence, 0 if notFurther InformationSee CrimeTransferEvidenceTo.
CrimeHasEvidence.seeAlso:
CharacterRegisterCrime
CrimeAddEvidence
CrimeAddItemEvidence
CrimeGetEvidence
CrimeGetNumberOfEvidence
CrimeMergeEvidenceFrom

CalculatePassiveSkill.description:
This query calculates a passive value of a target’s skill that doesn’t require a dice roll.
Further InformationPer D&D, a passive value is 10+Skill Modifier.
If you’re looking for a background roll that doesn’t open an active roll window, look at RequestPassiveRoll instead.
CalculatePassiveSkill.examples:
PROC
PROC_Tavern_CheckDrowArmourIdentification()
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
CalculatePassiveSkill(_Player, "History", _Skill)
AND
_Skill >= 15
THEN
SetFlag(_Knows_DrowArmour, NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, 0);

CombatGetInvolvedPlayersCount.description: Returns a number of players _NumPlayers participating in combat with combat ID _CombatGuid.
CombatGetInvolvedPlayersCount.examples:
IF
EnteredCombat(_Zombie, _ID)
AND
GetTemplate(_Zombie, (CHARACTERROOT)_Nurse_NoXP)
AND
CombatGetInvolvedPlayersCount(_ID,_CountPlayers)
AND
_CountPlayers > 0
AND
CombatGetInvolvedPlayer(_ID,1,_Player)
AND
NOT DB_PortraitZombieCombat(_ID, _Player)
THEN
PROC_TryStartAD(_PAD_ReactionPortraitZombies, _Player);
DB_PortraitZombieCombat(_ID, _Player);

CombatGetInvolvedPartyMember.description:
Checks all party members participating in combat with ID _CombatGuid and returns _PartyMember who is at index _PartyMemberIndex.
Further Information_PartyMemberIndex starts from 1.
CombatGetInvolvedPartyMember.examples:
PROC
PROC_SniperSetup((TRIGGER)_Ambush)
AND
DB_AmbushGroupTarget(_Ambush, _Ambusher)
AND
GetCombatGroupID(_Ambusher, _ID)
AND
CombatGetGuidFor(_Ambusher, _GUID)
AND
CombatGetInvolvedPartyMember(_GUID, 1, _Player)
THEN
RemoveStatus(_Cultist_Sniper, "AMBUSHING");
RemoveStatus(_Cultist_Sniper, "INVISIBLE");
SetCombatGroupAndEnterCombat(_Cultist_Sniper, _ID, _Ambusher);

CrimeInvestigatorEnterInterrogationState.description:
This query is used if CrimeInvestigatorConfrontSuspect succeeds and the _Investigator will interrogate the suspect. When the query succeeds, interrogation will begin.
The query will fail if:
The _CrimeID is invalid.
The _Investigator is not investigating.
If the disturbance’s ignoreCantTalk property is false and the _Investigator cannot talk.Further InformationIf the query fails, the _Investigator will enter the confrontation state instead.

CrimeStopIgnoringCriminal.description:
This query makes investigators consider _Criminal for a given _CrimeID.
Further InformationThis assumes that you’ve used CrimeIgnoreCriminal to previously make investigators ignore the _Criminal in question.
Full DefinitionCrimeStopIgnoringCriminal([in](INTEGER)_CrimeID, [in](CHARACTER)_Criminal, [out](INTEGER)_Bool)
ExampleIF
EntityEvent(_Player, "CrimeStopIgnoringCharacter")
AND
DB_CrimeIDToApplyIgnoreFor(_CrimeID)
AND
CrimeStopIgnoringCrime(_CrimeID, (CHARACTER)_Player, 1)
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);

DataGetBackgroundTagAt.description: Return the name of a Tag from the list of “Background Tags” at _Index position - tags that show a character’s Background.
DataGetBackgroundTagAt.examples:
PROC
PROC_GLO_DataGetOriginTags((CHARACTER)_Origin)
AND
DataGetBackgroundTagCount(_Origin,_Count)
AND
QRY_DoNTimes(_Count)
AND
DB_QRY_RTN_DoNTimes(_Index)
AND
DataGetBackgroundTagAt(_Origin,_Index,_BackgroundTag)
THEN
SetTag(_Origin,_BackgroundTag);

DataGetGoldValue.description:
Query that returns gold value of something from the GoldValues table in the UUID Object editor, such as bribes or price adjustments. This table provides gold values that scale with level.
_Type - Pass in the Name_UUID of the value you are looking for, such as BribesNonSevereCrimeMin_d6a0f75a-a24f-4a8a-ac36-fd5f68ce074f
_Level - Pass in the level you want the value scaled to
_Value - Returns the gold value from the table
DataGetGoldValue.examples:
PROC
PROC_CrimeBribes_GetBribeBounds((INTEGER)_HighBase,(INTEGER)_CriminalLevel)
AND
DB_CrimeBribes_GoldTables(_HighBase,_MinTable,_MaxTable)
AND
DataGetGoldValue(_MinTable,_CriminalLevel,_MinGold)
AND
DataGetGoldValue(_MaxTable,_CriminalLevel,_MaxGold)
AND
IntegerSubtract(_MaxGold,_MinGold,_RandBase)
AND
IntegerSum(_RandBase,1,_Rand)
THEN
DB_CrimeBribes_GetBribeBounds(_MinGold,_Rand);

CrimeAddItemEvidence.description:
This query adds the _Evidence item to the list of evidence of disturbance _CrimeID. The query can also accept variables of items such as _EvidenceTemplate, _OriginalOwner, _Amount and _TotalGoldValue.
The difference between this and CrimeAddEvidence is that this version of the query is designed for items specifically. Items have value, can be stacked, have owners, etc. This is information that is relevant to the disturbance system in the event we want to use those variables.
Further InformationEvidence is usually registered alongside the disturbance. However, more can be added later using this database
Evidence is a dynamic collection of items, characters and triggers that can be associated with an individual disturbance. It can be used to check afterwards what the disturbance was about, or to interact with the evidence.
Besides of being added directly with this query, it can be added by the disturbance system when disturbances get merged. It can also be:
transferred, e.g. from a thief to the rightful owner
dropped, e.g. from a thief when no one is nearby that can take possession
valued, e.g. to calculate the fine
counted and enumerated
checked for the presence of a particular entity
searched in the inventory of a set of characters
CrimeAddItemEvidence.fullDefinitions:
CrimeAddItemEvidence([in](INTEGER)_CrimeID, [in](ITEM)_Evidence, [in](GUIDSTRING)_EvidenceTemplate, [in](CHARACTER)_OriginalOwner, [in](INTEGER)_Amount, [in](INTEGER)_TotalGoldValue, [out](INTEGER)_Bool)
ExamplePROC
PROC_CharacterRegisterCrime_Success((CHARACTER)_Char, "MyDisturbance", _, _, _, _CrimeID)
AND
DB_ExtraEvidenceForCharacterl(_CrimeID, _Evidence, _EvidenceTemplate, _OriginalOwner, _Amount, _Value)
AND
CrimeAddItemEvidence(_CrimeID, _Evidence, _EvidenceTemplate, _OriginalOwner, _Amount, _Value, 1)
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);

CombatIsActive.description:
Checks if the combat GUID is still active.
Further InformationThis is a way to check the validity of a combat GUID that is entity agnostic.
CombatIsActive.examples:
IF
CombatStarted(_CombatGUID)
THEN
DB_Example_CombatGUIDs(_CombatGUID);

IF
LeftTrigger(_Player, _Trigger)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
DB_Example_Trigger(_Trigger)
AND
DB_Example_CombatGUIDs(_CombatGUID)
AND
CombatIsActive(_CombatGUID, 1)
THEN
EndCombat(_CombatGUID);
NOT DB_Example_CombatGUIDs(_CombatGUID);

CrimeHasProperty.description:
Returns whether the disturbance/crime definition of a particular disturbance has a certain property.
Parameters:
_CrimeID: the disturbance/crime ID to check
_DisturbanceProperty: the property to check
_HasProperty:  1 if the disturbance’s definition has the property, 0 if not
CrimeHasProperty.examples:
IF
CharacterSelectedAsBestUnavailableFallbackLead(_Char,_Region,_NewCrime,_OldCrime)
AND
CrimeHasProperty(_NewCrime, _BackgroundReaction, 0)
AND
IsSpeakerReserved(_Char,1)
AND
CrimeIsContinuous(_OldCrime,0)
THEN
PROC_ForceStopDialog(_Char);
PROC_CRIME_MakeTemporaryHostileToCriminalsOf(_Char, _OldCrime);
PROC_CRIME_MakeTemporaryHostileToCriminalsOf(_Char, _NewCrime);

ConcatenateReal.description: Adds _Number to the end _String and returns the result in _Result.

ConcatenateNewLine.description: Add a new line to _StringA, then adds _StringB and returns the result in _Result.

CreateOutOfSightAtDirectionFromObject.description:
Same as CreateOutOfSightAtDirection but instead of using specific coordinates, takes the position of _AnchorObject as a starting point together with the position of _DirectionFrom objects to calculate an angle to search for valid position in.
Parameters:
_TemplateId: Template that _CreatedObject should be instanced from
Even if technically it accepts GUIDSTRING, only templates of type CHARACTERROOT should be used or it will fail and throw an assert
_AnchorObject: The initial object that is used together with _DirectionFrom object to determine where the out of sight position is going to be looked for. It starts at _AnchorObject and looks in the angle towards _DirecitonFrom
_DirectionFrom: See above
_Temporary: If 1 and if the _CreatedObject is a CHARACTER, it will be a temporary one
_PlaySpawn: If 1 and if the _CreatedObject is a CHARACTER, it will play a spawn animation
_SpawnFinishEvent: If not an empty string, the event will be sent once _CreatedObject spawns. If it’s playing a spawn animation, it will be sent after the animation finishes.
Returns _CreatedObject: The created object
CreateOutOfSightAtDirectionFromObject.examples:
QRY
QRY_CRIME_Guards_SpawnGuard((STRING)_Region,(STRING)_Pool,(CHARACTER)_Criminal,(CHARACTERROOT)_Template,(STRING)_SpawnEvent)
AND
_Template != NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
AND
DB_CRIME_Guards_RegionSpawnPool(_Region,_Pool,_HomeBaseLocation,_LostAndFoundChest,_Max,_InUse)
AND
_InUse < _Max
AND
CreateOutOfSightAtDirectionFromObject(_Template,_Criminal,_HomeBaseLocation,1,0,_SpawnEvent,_Guard)
AND
IntegerSum(_InUse,1,_NewInUse)
THEN
DB_QRYRTN_CRIME_Guards_SpawnGuard(_Guard);
NOT DB_CRIME_Guards_RegionSpawnPool(_Region,_Pool,_HomeBaseLocation,_LostAndFoundChest,_Max,_InUse);
DB_CRIME_Guards_RegionSpawnPool(_Region,_Pool,_HomeBaseLocation,_LostAndFoundChest,_Max,_NewInUse);

CharacterTemplatesInTrigger.description:
Returns the amount of characters in the _Trigger whose root template matches _ItemTemplate.
Return Values:
_Count: The amount of characters found.Further InformationThe _Count can be 0 - then no one was found.

GetActionResourceValuePersonal.description:
A query used to check the amount of action resources you have available from a selection.
Parameters:
_Player The character to check
_ResourceName the string of the Resource name: Such as “SorceryPoint” or “WarlockSpellSlot”
_ResourceLevel the level of the resource the character has (EG: For Warlock spell slots, a 5 indicates a 5th level)
_Amount the amount of resources you have (EG 2.0 might indicate 2 spell slots)

CrimeTypeGetTension.description:
This system query returns the _Tension of the main disturbance _CrimeType as defined in the stats editor.
Further InformationNot to be confused with CrimeGetTension which returns the tension of a specific disturbance.
_Tension is a numeric value that is applied to an area to modify reactions and triggers alarm. It represents how likely the NPCs are to let you get away with a crime. Different crimes will raise the tension, and you can get the current tension level by using the subject query.
You could use the subject query to make some custom modification to your region if the tension goes above a certain level. For example, guards in our systems are designed to upgrade their equipment when the tension passes a certain point.
Full DefinitionCrimeTypeGetTension([in](STRING)_CrimeType, [out](INTEGER)_Tension)

GetCombatGroupID.description:
Returns the combat group ID string of the given entity.
Further InformationThere are two ways to create a combat group, through Osiris or through the editor. This will change how the combat ID is represented in Osiris.
When the combat group is made in the combat group editor, querying the ID in Osiris will return the GUID as a string.
GetCombatGroupID(_Character, "XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX")Otherwise, if the ID is created and set in Osiris, it will return as the string written in the script.
GetCombatGroupID(_Character, "EXA_Example_CombatGroup")This is important to keep in mind when querying the ID, as trying to query the combat group ID with it’s name when it was made in the editor will return false.
If the entity does not have a combat group ID, it will return as an empty string "".
GetCombatGroupID.examples:
IF
EntityEvent(_Character, "EXA_Example_CombatEvent")
AND
GetCombatGroupID(_Character, "EXA_Example_CombatGroup")
THEN
SetCombatGroupID(_Character, "");

IF
CombatStarted(_CombatGUID)
AND
DB_EXA_Example_CombatID(_CombatGUID)
AND
DB_Exa_CombatGroupCharacters(_Character)
AND
GetCombatGroupID(_Character, "XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX")
THEN
PROC_SetRelationToPlayers(_Character, 0);

CrimeFindEvidence.description:
This query is the one that we use to have an NPC _Searcher go through a _Criminal’s inventory to try to find the evidence related to the disturbance of _CrimeID. It has output parameters that can adjust the level of guilt:
_SomeEvidenceFoundForCurrentCrime
_FoundAllEvidenceForCurrentCrime
_GuiltyFound

CrimeTransferLeadershipTo.description:
Makes another NPC the lead investigator of a disturbance/crime.
Parameters:
_OldLead: the current lead investigator of the disturbance/crime
_CrimeID: the disturbance/crime’s ID of which _OldLead is the current lead investigator
_NewLead: the new lead investigator
_Bool: 1 if the transfer was successful, 0 if notFurther InformationThis query will fail if
_OldLead is not the current lead investigator of the disturbance/crime, or
if _NewLead
is already handling another disturbance/crime (unless he’s just investigating/looking for suspects)
is not available for reacting to the disturbance/crime

CombatGetInvolvedPlayer.description:
Checks all players participating in combat with ID _CombatGuid and returns _Player who is at index _PlayerIndex.
Further Information_PlayerIndex starts with 1
CombatGetInvolvedPlayer.examples:
IF
EnteredCombat(_Guard, _CombatID)
AND
DB_GuildhallEntrance_Guards(_Guard)
AND
CombatGetInvolvedPlayer(_CombatID, 1, _Player)
AND
QRY_OnlyOnce("GuildhallEntrance_RemainHostile")
AND
NOT DB_GlobalFlag((FLAG)_Event_HostileGuards)
THEN
PROC_GlobalSetFlagAndCache((FLAG)_Event_HostileGuards);

GetDistanceToPosition.description: Returns the distance between the specified _Object and the position _X, _Y, _Z.

CrimeAddCriminal.description:
This query adds the _Criminal character to the list of criminals of disturbance _CrimeID.
Further InformationBy default, registering a disturbance is only done on a single character. Rather than use this call to add other characters as criminals directly, they would have to perform the disturbance action to be registered as criminals. This query is used when disturbances merge, in order to be able to add the criminals of the old disturbance to the new one.
Otherwise, you would use this call if character need to be added after the original disturbance, for reasons you decide on.
Full DefinitionCrimeAddCriminal([in](INTEGER)_CrimeID, [in](GUIDSTRING)_Criminal, [out](INTEGER)_Bool)
ExamplePROC
PROC_CharacterRegisterCrime_Success((CHARACTER)_Char, "MyDisturbance", _, _, _, _CrimeID)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
_Char != _Player
AND
CrimeAddCriminal(_CrimeID, _Player, 1)
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);

CrimeGetNumberOfEvidence.description:
Returns the number of evidence objects associated with a particular disturbance/crime.
Parameters:
_CrimeID: the disturbance/crime’s ID
_NumEvidence: the number of evidence objects associated with itFurther InformationSee CrimeTransferEvidenceTo.
CrimeGetNumberOfEvidence.examples:
QRY
QRY_CRIME_GetFirstPickupableEvidenceIndex((INTEGER)_CrimeID)
AND
CrimeGetNumberOfEvidence(_CrimeID, _NumEvidence)
AND
QRY_DoNTimes(_NumEvidence)
AND
DB_QRY_RTN_DoNTimes(_Index)
AND
NOT DB_QRY_RTN_CRIME_GetFirstPickupableEvidenceIndex(_)
AND
CrimeGetEvidence(_CrimeID, _Index, _Evidence)
AND
QRY_CRIME_IsValidEvidence(_Evidence, 1)
THEN
DB_QRY_RTN_CRIME_GetFirstPickupableEvidenceIndex(_Index);
CrimeGetNumberOfEvidence.seeAlso:
CharacterRegisterCrime
CrimeAddEvidence
CrimeAddItemEvidence
CrimeGetEvidence
CrimeHasEvidence
CrimeMergeEvidenceFrom

GetAbility.description: Returns the value _Value of an attribute _Attribute of character _Character.
GetAbility.examples:
PROC_CrimeSetupWarning((STRING)_CrimeRegion,(INTEGER)_CrimeID,(CHARACTER)_Warner,(CHARACTER)_Criminal1,(CHARACTER)_Criminal2,(CHARACTER)_Criminal3,(CHARACTER)_Criminal4,(DIALOGRESOURCE)_WarningDialog)
AND
DB_Players(_Criminal1)
AND
GetAttitudeTowardsPlayer(_Warner,_Criminal1,_Attitude)
AND
GetAbility(_Criminal1,"Charisma",_CharismaValue)
AND
IntegerSum(_Attitude,_CharismaValue,_Total)
AND
// With Charisma 10 and 0 attitude, allows for 1 assault, 1 item destroy, 2 vandalise, 2 pickpocket,
// 4 trespassing, .. before a non-guard NPC victim will get angry (which in turn results in calling
// guards to arrest)
_Total < -10
THEN
SetFlag((FLAG)_Crime_IsAngry, _Warner, 0);

DialogIsCrimeDialog.description: Checks if if the dialog instance is a crime dialog.

CreateAt.description:
Creates an instance of the root template defined by _TemplateId (in <root template name>_<root template UUID> format) at (_X, _Y, _Z). Currently, only characters and items are supported.
If the created object is a character, then
if _Temporary is 1, the character will be a temporary one. A temporary character's corpse is not saved, which is useful to avoid very large save file sizes if it is used to spawn large numbers of characters.
if _PlaySpawn is 1, the character's default spawn animation will be played.For other object types the above two parameters are ignored.
If _SpawnFinishEvent is not an empty string, this event will be sent once the object has spawned. This works for all object types, and independently of whether or not the spawn animation gets played (but if one is played, it will only be sent once the animation finishes).
Return Values:
Success/Failure: Fails if _TemplateID is not a character or item template or does not exist.
In case of characters, also fails if no valid AI-grid position can be found within 5 metres of the specified position.
_CreatedObject: The created object.

CrimeGetVictim.description:
Return the victim of a crime.
Parameters:
_CrimeID: the ID of the crime/disturbance
_CrimeVictim: the victim of the crime. Can be NULLFurther InformationThe victim of the crime is the one that was specified by CharacterRegisterCrime or CharacterRegisterCrimeWithPosition.
CrimeGetVictim.examples:
IF
CrimeProcessingStarted((INTEGER)_CrimeID, 0)
AND
NOT DB_GlobalFlag(_ConflictedFlind_State_Gone)
AND
CrimeGetVictim(_CrimeID, _Zhent)
AND
DB_ZhentShipment_Agents(_Zhent)
THEN
PROC_CRIME_StopForAllCriminals(_CrimeID);

CrimeAreaGetTensionModifier.description: Return the tension level of a crime area trigger. See CrimeAreaSetTensionModifier for more information about crime area tension.

GetCanPickUp.description:
Checks whether an item can be picked up and added to an inventory.
Further InformationThis can be controlled by both the item sidebar property “Can Be Picked Up“ or the Osiris call SetCanPickUp.
GetCanPickUp.examples:
PROC
PROC_BlockUseOfItem((CHARACTER)_Char,(ITEM)_Item,(INTEGER)_RequestID)
AND
GetCanPickUp(_Item,1)
AND
IsPublicDomain(_Item,0)
THEN
PROC_CRIME_BuyFromTrader_Check(_Char,_Item,_RequestID);

CrimeGetDetectionRange.description:
This system query returns the _DetectionRange of disturbance with _CrimeID.
Further InformationDetection range is the range at which the crime can be noticed by an investigator.
The subject query doesn’t really have much use, but it exists!
Full DefinitionCrimeGetDetectionRange([in](INTEGER)_CrimeID, [out](REAL)_Range)

CrimeTypeHasProperty.description:
Returns whether the disturbance/crime definition of a particular disturbance type has a certain property.
Parameters:
_DisturbanceType: the name of the disturbance/crime type to check, as defined in stats
_DisturbanceProperty: the property to check
_HasProperty: 1 if the disturbance’s definition has the property, 0 if not
CrimeTypeHasProperty.examples:
QRY
QRY_CRIME_BlockRegisterCrime((CHARACTER)_Char,(STRING)_CrimeType,(INTEGER)_StoryActionID,(GUIDSTRING)_Evidence,(CHARACTER)_Victim,(INTEGER)_CrimeID)
AND
CrimeTypeHasProperty(_CrimeType,(DISTURBANCEPROPERTY)_BlockIfPlayerInCombat,1)
AND
DB_PartyMembers(_Char)
AND
DB_Is_InCombat(_Char,_)
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);

CrimeGetEvidence.description:
Return the Nth piece of evidence of a disturbance/crime.
Parameters:
_Crime: the disturbance/crime ID
_Index: the index of the evidence you want to obtain, 1-based. If larger than the number of evidence entities, the query fails.
_Evidence: the returned evidenceFurther InformationSee CrimeTransferEvidenceTo.
CrimeGetEvidence.examples:
IF
CrimeProcessingStarted(_CrimeID, 0)
AND
CrimeGetType(_CrimeID, _Type)
AND
QRY_CRIME_IsCrimeFamilyMember(_Type, "MoveForbiddenItem")
AND
CrimeGetEvidence(_CrimeID, 1, _Evidence)
AND
GetOwner((ITEM)_Evidence, _Owner)
THEN
PROC_TryCreatePickUpEvidenceCrime(_CrimeID, "PickupEvidence_Unnoticed", _Owner);
CrimeGetEvidence.seeAlso:
CharacterRegisterCrime
CrimeAddEvidence
CrimeAddItemEvidence
CrimeGetNumberOfEvidence
CrimeHasEvidence
CrimeMergeEvidenceFrom
CrimeTransferEvidenceTo
OnCrimeMergedWith

CrimeAddEvidence.description:
This query adds the _Evidence object to the list of evidence of disturbance _CrimeID.
Further InformationEvidence is usually registered alongside the disturbance.
Evidence is a dynamic collection of items, characters and triggers that can be associated with an individual disturbance. It can be used to check afterwards what the disturbance was about, or to interact with the evidence.
Besides of being added directly with this query, it can be added by the disturbance system when disturbances get merged. It can also be:
transferred, e.g. from a thief to the rightful owner
dropped, e.g. from a thief when no one is nearby that can take possession
valued, e.g. to calculate the fine
counted and enumerated
checked for the presence of a particular entity
searched in the inventory of a set of charactersFull DefinitionCrimeAddEvidence([in](INTEGER)_CrimeID, [in](GUIDSTRING)_Evidence, [out](INTEGER)_Bool)
ExamplePROC
PROC_CharacterRegisterCrime_Success((CHARACTER)_Char, "MyDisturbance", _, _, _, _CrimeID)
AND
DB_ExtraEvidenceForCharacter(_Char, _Evidence)
AND
CrimeAddCriminal(_CrimeID, _Evidence, 1)
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);

Exists.description:
Returns whether or not _Object is a reference to a character, item, or trigger that exists in the current context.
Further InformationThis call is only valid if a level has already been loaded.
An object exists if it is global, or local in the current level. This includes local objects from persistent level template instances.
A character keeps existing after it dies, unless it was a temporary character and a savegame got reloaded after it died.
Static items (aka scenery) never exist, only dynamic items. A dynamic item stops existing when it gets destroyed if its "Template After Destruction" is not set in the "Destroy Parameters" of its "Destroy Actions" in the sidebar. If it does have a "Template After Destruction", it keeps existing after destruction.
There are no extra conditions for triggers.
This call currently always returns false for splines, level templates, and other game objects that can be otherwise referenced from Osiris.Return Values
Success/Failure: Default return value.
_Exists: 1 if _Object is a character, item, or trigger that exists in the current context, and 0 otherwise.
Exists.examples:
IF
AddedTo(_Item, _Owner, _)
