AND
DB_HasItemEvent(_Item, _Event)
AND
Exists(_Item, 1)
THEN
SetOnStage(_Item, 1);

GetActiveModStartupLevel.description: Returns the string identification of a startup level for the current active mod.
GetActiveModStartupLevel.examples:
IF
CharacterCreationFinished()
AND
GetActiveModStartupLevel(_FirstLevel)
AND
DB_CharacterCreationTransitionInfo(_FirstLevel,(STRING)_Movie)
AND
DB_Players(_Char)
THEN
PROC_MovePartyToStart(_Char,_FirstLevel,_Movie);

FactionGetParentFaction.description:
Returns the _ParentNumber parent faction of _Target, as defined in the faction editor. _ParentNumber is one-based.
Success/Failure: Silently fails if there is no _ParentNumber parent faction of _Faction.
_ParentFaction: the _ParentNumber parent faction of _Faction.

GetHostCharacter.description:
Returns _Character with the current main avatar (player character that was made with the character creator) of the host of the game.
Parameters:
_Character: character of the host.Further InformationWhile powerful, it is recommended to only use this query for debug options, since you usually want to react to things to the character who’s performing the action or aroun the area the events are happening. Only use for extreme edge cases or things in which you really need to pass a character, specially since it will not work well in multiplayer scenarios (player B interacts with fountain, player A who is across the map gets cursed because of it).
GetHostCharacter.examples:
PROC
PROC_GoblinToast_RegisterPoisoning()
AND
GetHostCharacter(_Player)
THEN
QuestUpdate(_Player, "HIDDEN_WLD_Boosters", "GoblinToast_Poisoned");

GetHitProxyOwner.seeAlso:
HitProxy
IsHitProxy
IsHitProxyOwner
AttachHitProxy
DetachHitProxy
DetachAllHitProxies
IterateHitProxies

GetClosestAliveUserPlayer.description:
Returns the closest alive player to _Target entity and _Distance between them for specific _UserID passed.
Further InformationPlayers are party members within DB_Players, other party members like summons and followers are ignored by the query.
GetClosestAliveUserPlayer.examples:
PROC
PROC_SwitchToLivingCharacter()
AND
DB_KilledWithLance(_Target)
AND
IsControlled(_Target, 1)
AND
GetReservedUserID(_Target, _UserID)
AND
GetClosestAliveUserPlayer(_Target, _UserID, _NewCharacter, _)
THEN
MakePlayerActive(_NewCharacter);

DataGetGodTagCount.description: Get the total number of “God Tags” - tags that show gods that the character worships.
DataGetGodTagCount.examples:
PROC
PROC_GLO_DataGetOriginTags((CHARACTER)_Origin)
AND
DataGetGodTagCount(_Origin,_Count)
AND
QRY_DoNTimes(_Count)
AND
DB_QRY_RTN_DoNTimes(_Index)
AND
DataGetGodTagAt(_Origin,_Index,_GodTag)
THEN
SetTag(_Origin,_GodTag);

CrimeGetCriminalsCount.description:
Obtain the number of criminals that a disturbance/crime has.
Parameters:
_CrimeID: the disturbance/crime’s ID
_CriminalsCount: the number of criminals the count hasFurther InformationUse in combination with CrimeGetCriminal.
CrimeGetCriminalsCount.examples:
PROC
PROC_CRIME_MakeTemporaryHostileToCriminalsOf((CHARACTER)_Char, (INTEGER)_CrimeID)
AND
CrimeGetCriminalsCount(_CrimeID, _CriminalsCount)
AND
QRY_DoNTimes(_CriminalsCount)
AND
DB_QRY_RTN_DoNTimes(_Index)
AND
CrimeGetCriminal(_CrimeID, _Index, _Criminal)
THEN
PROC_CRIME_SetRelationTemporaryHostile(_CrimeID, -1, _Char, _Criminal);

GetBaseFaction.description:
Returns the base faction of _Target, which must be a character or item.
The base faction is
the faction as set by the Osiris SetFaction or Anubis SetFaction call, or if that never happened
the faction assigned in the editorReturn Values:
Success/Failure: Default return value.
_Faction: the base faction of _Target.

CreateOutOfSightAtDirection.description:
Same as CreateAt but it tries to spawn the _CreatedObject at a valid position in the direction specified by _DirectionAngle with a starting point determined by the _X, _Y, _Z coordinates. Unlike AppearOutOfSightToPosition, if no valid position is found, the query will always fail and no _CreatedObject will be created.
Parameters:
_TemplateId: Template that _CreatedObject should be instanced from
Even if technically it accepts GUIDSTRING, only templates of type CHARACTERROOT should be used or it will fail and throw an assert
_X, _Y, _Z: The initial coordinates that are used together with _DirectionAngle to determine where the out of sight position is going to be looked for. It starts from the coordinates and searches in the angle specified.
_DirectionAngle: See above
_Temporary: If 1 and if the _CreatedObject is a CHARACTER, it will be a temporary one
_PlaySpawn: If 1 and if the _CreatedObject is a CHARACTER, it will play a spawn animation
_SpawnFinishEvent: If not an empty string, the event will be sent once _CreatedObject spawns. If it’s playing a spawn animation, it will be sent after the animation finishes.
Returns _CreatedObject: The created object

GetClosestPlayerToPosition.description:
Returns the closest player to the passed coordinates and _Distance between the player and the specified position.
Further InformationPlayers are party members within DB_Players, other party members like summons and followers are ignored by the query.

CrimeIsContinuous.description:
Returns whether a disturbance/crime is continuous.
Parameters:
_CrimeID: the disturbance/crime’s ID
_Bool: 1 if the disturbance/crime is continue, 0 otherwise.
CrimeIsContinuous.examples:
IF
CharacterSelectedAsBestUnavailableFallbackLead(_Char,_Region,_NewCrime,_OldCrime)
AND
CrimeHasProperty(_NewCrime, Stealth_96ae44a0-7bee-4468-b539-19de16b78025, 0)
AND
CrimeHasProperty(_NewCrime, _BackgroundReaction, 0)
AND
IsSpeakerReserved(_Char,1)
AND
CrimeIsContinuous(_OldCrime,0)
THEN
PROC_ForceStopDialog(_Char);
PROC_CRIME_MakeTemporaryHostileToCriminalsOf(_Char, _OldCrime);
PROC_CRIME_MakeTemporaryHostileToCriminalsOf(_Char, _NewCrime);

CrimeGetCriminals.description:
Obtain all the criminals of a particular disturbance/crime.
Parameters:
_CrimeID: the disturbance/crime’s ID
_Criminal1 .. _Criminal4: the criminals of the disturbance/crime; except for _Criminal1, they can be NULLFurther InformationCriminals can be players or NPC (but if they can be NPCs, ensure the reaction never results in starting an interactive dialog with the criminal).

GetClosestAlivePlayer.description:
Returns the closest _Player to _Target that is alive and returns the _Distance.
Further InformationBe careful if you check for extra conditions on the returned player: if any of those conditions fail, no other players will be considered.

GetEquippedItem.description:
Returns in _ItemGUID the item equipped by _Character in slot _Slotname, if any.
Further InformationSuccess/Failure: Fails if _Character does not exist, or if no item is equipped in _Slotname.
The valid values for _Slotname are:
Helmet
Amulet
Breast
Belt
Gloves
Leggings
Boots
Weapon
Shield
Ring
Ring2
GetEquippedItem.examples:
IF
FlagSet((FLAG)_Laezel_State_NoOffhandWeaponBehaviourActive, _, _)
AND
GetEquippedItem((CHARACTER)_Player_Laezel, "Melee Offhand Weapon", _OffhandItem)
THEN
DB_CAMP_Laezel_TemporaryUnequippedOffhand((ITEM)_OffhandItem);
Unequip((CHARACTER)_Player_Laezel, _OffhandItem);

GetByTagInInventory.description:
Returns the first found _Entity with a given _Tags in the _InventoryHolder’s inventory.
Return Values:
_Entity:
The GUIDSTRING of the first found object that matches the _Tags.Further Information_Tags is expecting a string tag expression and may include multiple Tags to check for. For example, the string “APPLE && PEAR” will return the first item it finds that has either the tag APPLE or PEAR.
_InventoryHolder can be either a character or an item.

GetMaxStackAmount.description: Returns the maximum item stack value of _Item as an integer in _MaxAmount.

FactionIsTagged.description:
Returns whether all/any (LQUANT.All / LQUANT.ANY) entities with _Faction and all of its child factions available in the current level (*) are tagged with _Tag.
locals in the current level or globals in any level including the current oneReturn Values:
Success/Failure: Default return value.
_Bool: 0 if the condition is not satisfied, 1 otherwise

FindValidPosition.description:
Finds a valid position for an object on the AI-grid near a source position using a flood fill up to a specified radius.
If _Object is non-null and an item or a character, the returned position will have enough space to fit its AI-bounds, and the object itself will be ignored while looking for a new valid position (if you look for a new position for a giant object one metre from its current position, the object would "collide" with itself when placed at that position it were not ignored).
FindValidPosition.examples:
PROC
PROC_TUT_LowerDeck_TeleportCharToImpRoom((CHARACTER)_Char)
AND
GetPosition(_ImpRoom_CombatPlayerPosition, _X, _Y, _Z)
AND
FindValidPosition(_X, _Y, _Z, 10.0, _Char, 1, _Xf, _Yf, _Zf)
THEN
DB_TUT_LowerDeck_Teleported(1);
TeleportToPosition(_Char, _Xf, _Yf, _Zf, "TeleportedToImpRoom", 0, 1, 0, 0, 1);

GetAppliedSplatterLevel.description:
Check how much splatters of a specific type are currently displayed for a certain entity.
Parameters:
_Entity: Entity you are checking.
_SplatterType: Type of splatter you are interested in checking.
_SplatterLevel: How much splatter of the specified _SplatterType is on your _Entity.Further InformationSupported SPLATTERTYPE types are: Blood, Bruise, Dirt and Sweat.
_SplatterLevel gives you an approximation on how much “splatter” is on the entity using the AMOUNT type. Supported AMOUNT types are (in ascending order): NONE = 0, BARELY = 1, SOME = 2, HALF = 3, LOTS = 4, MOST = 5, ALL = 6.
GetAppliedSplatterLevel.examples:
PROC
PROC_DialogFlagSetup((DIALOGRESOURCE)_Circus_Checkpoint, _,_,_, _Player)
AND
GetAppliedSplatterLevel(_Player, SPLATTERTYPE.Blood, _SplatterLevel)
AND
DB_WYR_Circus_SplattersLevels(_SplatterLevel)
THEN
DB_WYR_Circus_PlayerSplatterFlags((FLAG)_Circus_Checkpoint_State_Bloodied, (CHARACTER)_Player);

GetItemByTemplateInPartyInventory.description:
Returns the first _Item matching the given _ItemTemplate in the inventory of the specified _Character or any other inventories of party members of that _Character.
Return Values:
_Item: returns the name and GUID of the item.
GetItemByTemplateInPartyInventory.examples:
IF
EnteredTrigger(_Character, _ItemCheckTrigger)
AND
GetItemByTemplateInPartyInventory((ITEMROOT)_ItemTemplate, (CHARACTER)_Character, (ITEM)_Item)
THEN
//Do whatever you plan to do with the _Item

GetHandlingCrimeID.description: Returns the _CrimeID of a disturbance the character is currently investigating.

CombatGetInvolvedPartyMembersCount.description: Returns a number of party members _NumPartyMembers participating in combat with combat ID _CombatGuid.
CombatGetInvolvedPartyMembersCount.examples:
IF
LeftCombat(_Player,_ID)
AND
DB_PossessionCombatID(_ID)
AND
CombatGetInvolvedPartyMembersCount(_ID,0)
AND
NOT DB_Dead(_DoubtingArtist)
AND
QRY_CheckPoltergeistsAlive()
AND
NOT DB_OskarWakingUp(_,_)
THEN
Die(_DoubtingArtist, DEATHTYPE.Necrotic, NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, 0, 0);
SetFlag((FLAG)_State_FledPossession);
QuestUpdate("GLO_DoubtingArtist", "ArtistIsDead_AbandonedInPossession");
NOT DB_PossessionCombatID(_ID);

GetDefaultState.description:
Returns the Default State value set to the character.
Further InformationDefault State can be found in the editor in the Sidebar when a character is selected, and you can find all the values there. As you can see this value describes whether a charcter starts their existence alive or in various forms of death. In the case of the value Idle this query actually returns an empty string.
GetDefaultState.examples:
IF
Died(_Corpse)
AND
NOT DB_CorpseCleanup_Ignore(_Corpse)
AND
NOT DB_CanBeResurrected(_Corpse)
AND
NOT DB_GLO_CharacterCorpseDialog(_Corpse,_)
AND
GetDefaultState(_Corpse, "")
THEN
DB_CorpseCleanup_ReadyToBeRemoved(_Corpse);

DialogRemoveActorFromDialog.description: Removes an _Actor (character or item) from a dialog while dialog is active.

GetCanInteract.description: This query checks if the _Item can be interacted with. The _Bool  returns a 1 if yes, and a 0 if no. Non-interactable items cannot be clicked on or interacted with by players (area-of-effect spells etc won't affect them either), and generally behave like scenery.
GetCanInteract.examples:
IF
EnteredTrigger(_Player, (TRIGGER)_TriggerEntranceDoor)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
GetCanInteract((ITEM)_StreetDoor, 0)
AND
GetFlag((FLAG)_PlayerHasLetter, _Player, 1)
THEN
SetCanInteract(_StreetDoor, 1);

DataGetGodTagAt.description: Return the name of a Tag from the list of “God Tags” at _Index position - tags that show gods that the character worships.
DataGetGodTagAt.examples:
PROC
PROC_GLO_DataGetOriginTags((CHARACTER)_Origin)
AND
DataGetGodTagCount(_Origin,_Count)
AND
QRY_DoNTimes(_Count)
AND
DB_QRY_RTN_DoNTimes(_Index)
AND
DataGetGodTagAt(_Origin,_Index,_GodTag)
THEN
SetTag(_Origin,_GodTag);

CrimeIgnoreCriminal.description:
This query will make a _Criminal of _CrimeID be ignored by the crime’s investigators.
Further InformationThis is rarely used, but you could make use of this if you wanted to track reactivity on a player by player basis. You can stop the ignore by using the opposite query query CrimeStopIgnoringCriminal .
Full DefinitionCrimeIgnoreCriminal([in](INTEGER)_CrimeID, [in](CHARACTER)_Criminal, [out](INTEGER)_Bool)
ExampleIF
EntityEvent(_Player, "CrimeIgnoreCharacter")
AND
DB_CrimeIDToApplyIgnoreFor(_CrimeID)
AND
CrimeIgnoreCrime(_CrimeID, (CHARACTER)_Player, 1)
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);

GetBaseWeaponDamageType.description:
Returns the primary damage type done by the weapon.
Parameters:
_Item: The weapon being checked
_DamageType: The weapon’s damage type.
GetBaseWeaponDamageType.examples:
PROC
PROC_MOO_Jailbreak_CheckValidTool((ITEM)_Item)
AND
IsWeapon(_Item, 1)
AND
GetBaseWeaponDamageType(_Item, "Bludgeoning")
THEN
DB_MOO_Jailbreak_ToolOnGround(_Item);

CrimeGetNewID.description:
This system query generates a new _CrimeID to be used when registering a disturbance.
_CrimeID is an unique integer and every new call of CrimeGetNewID will use the next available integer.
Full DefinitionCrimeGetNewID([out](INTEGER)_CrimeID)
ExampleIF
EnteredTrigger((CHARACTER)_Player, _ForbiddenTrigger)
AND
DB_ForbiddenTriggers(_ForbiddenTrigger)
AND
CrimeGetNewID(_CrimeID)
THEN
DB_ForbiddenPlayer(_CrimeID, _Player);
PROC_CharacterRegisterCrime(_Player, "MyForbiddenCrime", _ForbiddenTrigger, NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, _CrimeID);

GetRulesetModifierBool.description:
Gets the current value of a chosen Ruleset Modifier.
Look for a ruleset in UUID Object Editor in RulesetModifiers and make sure you’re using the correct query according to the type of a ruleset.
Further InformationCheckRulesetModifierBool can check if a certain value is set for a chosen Ruleset Modifier.
GetRulesetModifierBool.examples:
PROC
PROC_BloodOfLathander_StartQuestTimer((GUIDSTRING)_Player)
AND
GetRulesetModifierString(SCRIPTED_COMBAT_MECHANICS_cac2d8bd-c197-4a84-9df1-f86f54ad4521,"EASY")
THEN
ObjectQuestTimerLaunch(_Player, "Lance_Explosion_QuestTimer", "BloodOfLathander_TurnTimer", 42000, 0);

IF
LeftTrigger((CHARACTER)_PartyMember, (TRIGGER)_PhilgravesMansion_LairCurseArea)
AND
GetRulesetModifierString(SCRIPTED_COMBAT_MECHANICS_cac2d8bd-c197-4a84-9df1-f86f54ad4521,(STRING)_Difficulty)
AND
DB_DifficultyLairCurses(_Status,_Difficulty)
AND
HasActiveStatus(_PartyMember,_Status,1)
THEN
RemoveStatus(_PartyMember,_Status);

GetAiHint.description: Returns the _AiHint tag of the AiHintArea Triggers that the _Target is currently set to.

DataGetReallyTagAt.description:
Return the name of a Tag from the list of “Really Tags” at _Index position - tags that show who this character is really, ignoring any disguises or shapeshifting.
Further InformationThese tags usually show the race of a character or if they are the Origin character.
DataGetReallyTagAt.examples:
PROC
PROC_GLO_DataGetOriginTags((CHARACTER)_Origin)
AND
DataGetReallyTagCount(_Origin,_Count)
AND
QRY_DoNTimes(_Count)
AND
DB_QRY_RTN_DoNTimes(_Index)
AND
DataGetReallyTagAt(_Origin,_Index,_ReallyTag)
THEN
DB_OriginReallyTags(_Origin,_ReallyTag);
SetTag(_Origin,_ReallyTag);

GetPerformGroupJoinRadius.description:
Returns the real value for characters to be able to join the performance crowd of a bard music performance.
Further Information_PerformGroupJoinRadiusexpresses the range in real.
GetPerformGroupJoinRadius.examples:
IF
StatusApplied((CHARACTER)_Character, "PERFORM_POSITIVE_THEPOWER", _, _)
AND
DB_SCE_TieflingFollowUp_DoingPerformance((CHARACTER)_DEN_Bard)
AND
_Character != _DEN_Bard
AND
GetPerformGroupJoinRadius(_Radius)
AND
QRY_IsInRange(_Character,_DEN_Bard,_Radius)
THEN
DB_SCE_TieflingFollowUp_DoingPerformance((CHARACTER)_Character);

CrimeIsTensionOverWarningTreshold.description:
This query will return a _Bool of value 1 if _Character is in a high tension crime area, and 0 if the tension is not high in the crime area.
Further InformationThe query will fail if the _Character is not in a crime area.

GetDisplayName.description:
Returns a translated string key _TranslatedStringKey for _Target.
Further InformationAfterwards _TranslatedKeyString could be used with:
ResolveTranslatedString
DialogSetVariableTranslatedString
GetDisplayName.examples:
PROC
PROC_Jergal_SetDialogVariables((DIALOGRESOURCE)_Dialog)
AND
DB_Jergal_DeadPlayers(_Player, _Variable, _, _)
AND
GetDisplayName(_Player, _TranslatedStringKey)
THEN
DialogSetVariableTranslatedString(_Dialog, _Variable, _TranslatedStringKey);

GetGender.description:
Returns the current gender of the character.
Parameters:
_Character = the character you’re checking
_AllowShapeshiftOverride = Values 0 or 1 (representing bool values false an true). When set to 0 returns the gender of true shape. When set to 1 returns the gender value of the current shape. Example: Gale is “Male“ but if he shapeshifts into a female elf his current shape is “Female“ while his true shape is still “Male“
_Gender = the return value in string form.
GetGender.examples:
PROC
PROC_Apprentice_TadpoleSceneSetUp((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
GetGender(_Player,0,_Gender)
AND
DB_Apprentice_TadpoledGender(_Gender, _Flag)
THEN
SetFlag(_Flag, _Apprentice);

GetClosestPlayer.description:
Returns the closest player to _Target entity and _Distance between them.
Further InformationPlayers are party members within DB_Players, other party members like summons and followers are ignored by the query.
Will return dead characters as well. For checking only alive characters use GetClosestAlivePlayer.
GetClosestPlayer.examples:
PROC
PROC_ItemTransformedPuttingAD()
AND
DB_InitCompleted(1)
AND
GetClosestPlayer(_ChestOfMundane, _Player, _Distance)
AND
_Distance < 5.0
AND
QRY_OncePerUserAndNearbyPlayers(_Player, "ItemTransformed")
THEN
PROC_TryStartAD(_PAD_ItemTransformed, _Player);

DataGetReallyTagCount.description:
Get the total number of “Really Tags” - tags that show who this character is really, ignoring any disguises or shapeshifting.
Further InformationThese tags usually show the race of a character or if they are the Origin character.
DataGetReallyTagCount.examples:
PROC
PROC_GLO_DataGetOriginTags((CHARACTER)_Origin)
AND
DataGetReallyTagCount(_Origin,_Count)
AND
QRY_DoNTimes(_Count)
AND
DB_QRY_RTN_DoNTimes(_Index)
AND
DataGetReallyTagAt(_Origin,_Index,_ReallyTag)
THEN
DB_OriginReallyTags(_Origin,_ReallyTag);
SetTag(_Origin,_ReallyTag);

GetEquippedWeapon.description:
Returns in _ItemGUID the weapon wielded by _Character in their right hand.
Further InformationSuccess/Failure: Fails if _Character does not exist, or if the character is not wielding a weapon in their right hand.
This is equivalent to GetEquippedItem  for the Weapon slot.

GetLevel.examples:
PROC
PROC_GLO_GithChokepoint_CheckLevel((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
DB_CurrentLevel(_CurrentLevel)
AND
DB_PLA_GithChokepoint_LevelRequirement(_CurrentLevel, _LevelRequired)
AND
GetLevel(_Player, _Level)
AND
_Level >= _LevelRequired
AND
SysCount("DB_Players", 1, _PlayerCount)
AND
_PlayerCount >= 3
THEN
SetFlag((FLAG)_GithChokepoint_State_MeetsLevel, 0);

FlagCleared.description: Thrown when _Flag is cleared from _Speaker in a _DialogInstance.
FlagCleared.examples:
IF
FlagCleared(_MintharaFate_Event_EnteredForbiddenArea, _, _)
AND
DB_MOO_MintharaFate_WaitingPos(_Trigger, _WaitingPos)
THEN
NOT DB_MOO_MintharaFate_WaitingPos(_Trigger, _WaitingPos);
PROC_CharacterMoveTo(_GOB_DrowCommander, _WaitingPos, "Walk", "");

GetRegion.description:
Returns the level in which the object currently is.
Parameters:
_Object: The object being checked
_Region: The level of the object
GetRegion.examples:
QRY
QRY_EntityIsCurrentlyUnreachable((GUIDSTRING)_Entity)
AND
Exists(_Entity,1)
AND
GetRegion(_Entity,_Level)
AND
DB_LevelUnreachable(_Level)
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);

CrimeGetEvidenceValue.description:
Returns the combined value of the evidence for a particular disturbance/crime.
Parameters:
_CrimeID: the ID of the disturbance/crime
_GoldValue: the calculated valueFurther InformationThis calculated value is usually less that what you see in the trade interface because it does not include any attitude modifiers.

GetItemByTagInInventory.description:
Returns the first _Item matching the given _Tags in the inventory of the specified _InventoryHolder.
Return Values:
_Item: returns the name and GUID of the item.Further Information_InventoryHolder can be a character or an item.
_Tags is expecting a string tag expression and may include multiple Tags to check for. For example, the string “APPLE && PEAR” will return the first item it finds that has either the tag APPLE or PEAR.
GetItemByTagInInventory.examples:
//An example using a Player walking in to a trigger
IF
EnteredTrigger(_Player, _ItemTagCheckTrigger)
AND
GetItemByTagInInventory(_Tag, _Player, _Item)
THEN
//Do whatever you need to with the _Item

GetParentPlatform.description:
Returns the platform instance or entity _Platform that belongs to the _Item that acts as the platform representative.
Further InformationA platform representative is an item that, when you destroy it, destroys the entire platform. It can be set in the sidebar for an item.

GetIdentity.description:
Returns the current identity value of the character.
Parameters:
_Character = the character you’re checking
_AllowShapeshiftOverride = Whether to get the value from true shape or current shape. Check GetGender for more info
_Identity = the return value in string form. Values can be found in UUID Table Enumerations>Identity
GetIdentity.examples:
IF
CompanionSelectedForUser(_Daisy,_UserID)
AND
DB_Avatars(_Avatar)
AND
DB_GLO_DaisyDreams_Relationships(_Avatar, _OldDaisy)
AND
DB_GLO_DaisyDreams_Daisies(_OldDaisy)
AND
GetReservedUserID(_Avatar,_UserID)
AND
GetIdentity(_Daisy,0,_Gender)
THEN
DB_GLO_DaisyDreams_Relationships(_Avatar, _Daisy);
NOT DB_GLO_DaisyDreams_Relationships(_Avatar, _OldDaisy);
PROC_DaisyDesire_SetDaisyFor(_Avatar, _Daisy, _Gender);

GetFlagDescription.description:
Given a _Flag returns its description in _FlagDescription as a string.
Parameters:
_Flag: flag GUID from which we want to get its name.
_FlagDescription: returned description of the flag.
GetFlagDescription.examples:
PROC
PROC_GetFlagInfo((FLAG)_Flag)
AND
GetFlagName(_Flag, _FlagName)
AND
GetFlagDescription(_Flag, _FlagDescription)
AND
Concatenate(_FlagName, ": ", _Substring)
AND
Concatenate(_Substring, _FlagDescription, _Message)
THEN
DebugBreak(_Message);

GetArmourSet.description:
Return the current armor set for character _Character.
Return Values:
_EArmorSet - the current armor set of character _Character. Possible values: Normal, Vanity (camp).
GetArmourSet.examples:
//If player starts a dialog in camp during night mode and they manually swapped to armour, switch to vanity for the duration of the dialogue)
PROC
PROC_StartDialog_ChangeClothing((DIALOGRESOURCE)_Dialog,(INTEGER)_DialogInstance)
AND
DB_Camp_NightMode(1)
AND
DialogIsAutomated(_Dialog, 0)
AND
DB_DialogRequestCache_SpeakerList_Players(_Dialog,_DialogInstance,_Player,_)
AND
DB_PlayerInCamp((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
NOT DB_Camp_TempVanityArmour(_Player,_)
AND
NOT QRY_CAMP_VanityArmour_BlockChangeForCharacter(_Player, _Dialog)
AND
QRY_GetArmourSet(_Player)
AND
DB_QRYRTN_GetArmourSet(ARMOURSET.Normal)
THEN
PROC_SetArmourSet(_Player,ARMOURSET.Vanity);
DB_Camp_TempVanityArmour(_Player,_DialogInstance);

GetHitpoints.description: Returns the amount of _HP the _Entity has left.

GetEquippedShield.description:
Returns in _ItemGUID the shield wielded by _Character in their left hand.
Further InformationSuccess/Failure: Fails if _Character does not exist, or if the character has no item in their left hand.
This is similar to GetEquippedItem for the Shield slot, except only for shield items.

GetGold.description: Query that returns the _Amount of gold possessed by _InventoryHolder. The holder can be anything with an inventory, including an item.
GetGold.examples:
IF
FlagSet((FLAG)_Event_GiveAllGold, _Player, _)
AND
GetGold(_Merchant, _GoldAmount)
AND
_GoldAmount > 0
AND
IntegerProduct(-1, _GoldAmount, _AntiGoldAmount)
THEN
AddGold(_Merchant, _AntiGoldAmount);
AddGold(_Player, _GoldAmount);

GetFirstInventoryOwnerCharacter.description:
Returns the _FirstInventoryCharacterHolder of a specific _Object. Containers and corpses have their own inventories even when carried; if the _Object is in one of these, it will look up the chain for the character who is carrying the container the _Object is in.
Return Values:
_FirstInventoryCharacterHolder:
The GUIDSTRING of the character that has the _Object. Fails if no character can be found.Further Information_Object can be either a character or an item.
GetFirstInventoryOwnerCharacter.examples:
IF
FlagSet((FLAG)DoSomethingToItemXOwner_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, _, _)
AND
GetFirstInventoryCharacterOwner(ItemX_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, _FirstInventoryCharacterHolder)
THEN
//Do whatever you want to the character that is the _FirstInventoryCharacterHolder

GetHitpointsPercentage.description: Returns the amount of _HP the _Entity has left relative to its current maximum hit points as a _Percentage.
GetHitpointsPercentage.examples:
IF
AttackedBy((ITEM)_HalsinPortal,_,_,_,_,_,_)
AND
HasActiveStatus ((ITEM)_HalsinPortal, "DAMAGEDPORTAL_FIRST", 0)
AND
GetHitpointsPercentage((ITEM)_HalsinPortal, _Percentage)
AND
_Percentage < 70.0
AND
NOT DB_Defeated(_HalsinPortal)
THEN
ApplyStatus((ITEM)_HalsinPortal,"HAV_HALSINLAKESIDE_DAMAGEDPORTAL_FIRST",-1.0,1,(ITEM)_HalsinPortal);

GetObscuredStateAtPosition.description:
Returns the obscured state at the passed coordinates.
Further InformationObscured state represents D&D lighting rules which impact gameplay:
Heavily obscured = No visibility
Lightly obscured (half lit) = Half visibility
Clear (un-obscured) = Full visibility

GetMaxPartySize.description:
Returns the maximum party size.
Return Values:
_MaxPartySize: maximum party size.
GetMaxPartySize.examples:
PROC
PROC_CheckPartyFull()
AND
SysCount("DB_Players",1,_Int1)
AND
DB_PlayerSlotReserved(_Int2)
AND
IntegerSum(_Int1,_Int2,_IntTotal)
AND
GetMaxPartySize(_Max)
AND
_IntTotal >= _Max
THEN
SetFlag((FLAG)_MaxPlayerCountReached);

GetInventoryOwner.description: Returns the inventory/container that holds _Object in _TopLevelInventoryHolder.

GetInnerDistanceToPosition.description:
Returns the inner distance between the specified _Object and the position _X, _Y, _Z.
This is the distance with the bounds of the object subtracted.

GetActiveArchetype.description:
Returns currently active Archetype of the character.
Further InformationCurrently active Archetype of the character can be different from its base one in case it was changed with RequestSetBaseArchetype
Archetypes are used to influence AI scoring for which actions characters choose in combat
You can see examples of Archetypes under \Gustav\Data\Public\Gustav\AI\Archetypes

GetFlag.description: Returns whether _Flag is currently set on _Object (a character or an item).

GetInterruptCount.description:
Returns the amount of _Interrupts available to the _Character.
Further InformationAn example of an interrupt is the Reaction system for characters (eg: Counterspell).

GetDisableUse.description: This query checks if an item’s use was disabled by SetDisableUse.  This only checks if the use actions are blocked, and the item may otherwise be interactable (attackable, movable, throwable, etc).
GetDisableUse.examples:
PROC
PROC_ReactivateSwitches()
AND
DB_Switches(_Switch)
AND
GetDisableUse(_Switch, 1)
THEN
SetDisableUse(_Switch, 0);

GetDeathType.description: Returns the type of death that a character had as a string. This uses a _String rather than the newer Deathtype (Such as DEATHTYPE:Physical)
GetDeathType.examples:
IF
Died(_Character)
AND
GetDeathType((CHARACTER)_Character, (STRING)_DeathType)
THEN
SetFlag(_Flag);

GetDefaultEquipmentSet.description:
Returns the default equipment set of the target character.
Further InformationThe query returns the stats name of the set as a string. So the set will need to be defined in stat the stats editor first.

GetMaxHitpoints.description:
Returns the current maximum number of hit points for _Entity.
Return Values:
_MaxHP: The maximum number of hit points of an entity.Further InformationFails if _Entity is NULL or invalid.

GetItemByTemplateInUserInventory.description:
Returns the first _Item matching the given _ItemTemplate in the inventory of the specified _Character or any other inventories controlled by that User.
Return Values:
_Item: returns the name and GUID of the item.Further InformationThis call is not often used, consider GetItemByTemplateInPartyInventory instead.
GetItemByTemplateInUserInventory.examples:
IF
EnteredTrigger(_Character, _ItemCheckTrigger)
AND
GetItemByTemplateInUserInventory((ITEMROOT)_ItemTemplate, (CHARACTER)_Character, (ITEM)_Item)
THEN
//Do whatever you plan to do with the _Item

GetRotation.description:
Returns the rotation of the _Target as rotations around the X, Y and Z axes (Y = vertical axis). It works on any kind of game object.
Further InformationIf _Target is an item and the item is inside the inventory of a character or a container, this call returns the rotation of the character/outermost container.
The results of this call correspond to the Xr, Yr and Zr values you can see in the editor when selecting an object (as long as it hasn't rotated since the game started).
All returned angles are in the interval [-180, 360].
The rotation angle increases when rotating in the clockwise direction.
GetRotation.examples:
PROC
PROC_CRE_StatuePuzzle_EvaluateRotations()
AND
GetRotation(_LathanderStatue_Sunrise, _, _SunriseY, _)
AND
GetRotation(_LathanderStatue_Sunset, _, _SunsetY, _)
AND
DB_CRE_LathanderStatues(_LathanderStatue_Sunrise, _SunriseMinY, _SunriseMaxY)
AND
DB_CRE_LathanderStatues(_LathanderStatue_Sunset, _SunsetMinY, _SunsetMaxY)
AND
_SunriseY>=_SunriseMinY
AND
_SunriseY<=_SunriseMaxY
AND
_SunsetY>=_SunsetMinY
AND
_SunsetY<=_SunsetMaxY
THEN
PROC_CRE_StatuePuzzle_OpenSecretDoor();

GetFaction.description:
Returns the current faction of _Target, which must be a character or item.
The current faction is
the faction as overridden by a stats boost, or if none
the base factionReturn Values:
Success/Failure: Default return value.
_Faction: the base faction of _Target.
GetFaction.examples:
PROC
PROC_State_Changed(_ShadowHeart, "Recruitment", "DEN")
AND
GetFaction(_ShadowHeart, _Faction)
THEN
PROC_SetRelationMutual(_Faction, (FACTION)_Nautiloid_DiningIntDev, 50);

GetItemByTemplateInInventory.description:
Returns the first _Item matching the given _ItemTemplate in the inventory of the specified _InventoryHolder.
Return Values:
_Item: returns the name and GUID of the item.Further Information_InventoryHolder can be a character or an item.
GetItemByTemplateInInventory.examples:
IF
EnteredTrigger(_Player, _GreaseBottleTrigger)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
DB_GOB_ChickenChase_GreaseBottles(_GreaseBottleTrigger, _Goblin)
AND
GetItemByTemplateInInventory(_GRN_GreaseBottle, _Goblin, _GreaseBottle)
THEN
UseSpell(_Goblin, "Throw_Throw", _GreaseBottleTrigger, _GreaseBottle);

GetItemByTemplateTagInInventory.description:
Returns the first _Item matching the given _ItemTemplate and _Tags in the inventory of the specified _InventoryHolder.
Not that while the input is called _Tags, it is in fact expecting a single Tag as a string.Return Values:
_Item: returns the name and GUID of the item.Further Information_InventoryHolder can be a character or an item.
GetItemByTemplateTagInInventory.examples:
//An example using a Player walking in to a trigger
IF
EnteredTrigger(_Player, _ItemTagCheckTrigger)
AND
GetItemByTemplateTagInInventory(_ItemTemplate, _Tag, _Player, _Item)
THEN
//Do whatever you need to with the _Item

FlagSet.description: Thrown when _Flag is set on _Speaker in the _DialogInstance.

GetHasOsirisDialog.description:
Get the previously set value by a call to SetHasOsirisDialog.
Further InformationSee SetHasOsirisDialog.
This query is generally used to temporary save, change and then later restore the value of that property.

GetRace.description:
Returns the current race of the character.
Parameters:
_Character = the character you’re checking
_CanPolymorphOverride = Whether to get the value from true shape or current shape. Check GetGender for more info (despite different of the parameter)
_Race = the return value in string form. Values can be found in UUID Table RacesFurther InformationYou may wish to actually check racial TAGs instead.
GetRace.examples:
IF
DB_ReportKiller_HostileToTarget(_Player, _Victim, _CombatID)
AND
DB_WitnessKiller(_Witness, _Victim)
AND
DB_Sees(_Witness, _Player)
AND
GetRace(_Player, 1, _Race)
AND
GetGender(_Player, 1, _Gender)
THEN
DB_WitnessKiller_SawAsShape(_Witness, _Player, _Race, _Gender, _Victim, _CombatID);

GetItemByTagInPartyInventory.description:
Returns the first _Item matching the given _Tags in any inventory in the party of the specified _Character.
Not that while the input is called _Tags, it is in fact expecting a single Tag as a string.Return Values:
_Item: returns the name and GUID of the item.
GetItemByTagInPartyInventory.examples:
//An example using a Player walking in to a trigger
IF
EnteredTrigger(_Player, _ItemTagCheckTrigger)
AND
GetItemByTagInPartyInventory(_Tag, _Player, _Item)
THEN
//Do whatever you need to with the _Item

GetCurrentCharacter.description:
Returns the one character the provided user is currently controlling.
Example: Player party consists of Wyll (avatar), Karlach (companion) and a summoned Skeleton. The player decided to directly control Karlach. The query is now returning the Karlach entity.
_User parameter refers to UserID which can be obtained via GetReservedUserID.

GetRelation.description:
Returns _Relation between _SourceFaction and _TargetFaction.
Return Values:
_Relation - interger value that can be:
0: _SourceFaction & _TargetFaction are Enemies
50: _SourceFaction & _TargetFaction are Neutral or Persistent Neutral
100: _SourceFaction & _TargetFaction are Allied
GetRelation.examples:
PROC
PROC_UND_DuergarCamp_CheckAndRemoveHostiles((CHARACTER)_Character)
AND
NOT DB_Is_InCombat(_Character,_)
AND
NOT DB_CantMove(_Character) // takes in account interactive dialogs, petrification, statuses etc
AND
GetFaction(_Character,_Faction)
AND
GetRelation(_Faction,_Hero,0)
THEN
PROC_DisappearOutOfSight(_Character,"Run",1,"UND_DuergarCamp_HostileGone");

GetOwner.description:
Returns the owner of the _Item as _Character, which is the last character to have the item in their inventory. This can be overridden through SetOwner.
Further InformationAn item's owner is cleared when that owner dies.
GetOwner.examples:
IF
StatusRemoved(_KerriPortrait, "POSSESSEDPORTRAIT", _, _)
AND
GetOwner((ITEM)_TorchOfRevocation, _Player)
THEN
PROC_OskarsBeloved_FreeKerri(_Player);

GetBaseArchetype.description:
Returns the base archetype of a character set in its sidebar.
Further InformationArchetypes are used to influence AI scoring for which actions characters choose in combat
You can see examples of Archetypes under \Gustav\Data\Public\Gustav\AI\Archetypes
GetBaseArchetype.examples:
IF
DB_AttackOnDen_WaveParticipants(_Raider,1)
AND
NOT DB_AttackOnDen_Spiders(_Raider)
AND
GetBaseArchetype(_Raider,_Archetype)
AND
_Archetype == "goblin_mage"
THEN
SetAiHint(_Raider,_RAMPARTS);

GetObscuredState.description:
Returns current obscured state of the _Object entity.
Further InformationObscured state represents D&D lighting rules which impact gameplay:
Heavily obscured = No visibility
Lightly obscured (half lit) = Half visibility
Clear (un-obscured) = Full visibility
GetObscuredState.examples:
IF
EntityEvent((CHARACTER)_Character, "CheckObscuredState")
AND
DB_ShadowCurse_TrackLight(_Character)
AND
GetObscuredState(_Character, _String)
AND
_String != "Clear"
THEN
DB_ShadowCurse_EnvironmentalConditions(_Character);

GetAnubisConfig.description:
Returns the Anubis config currently set on the object.
Parameters:
_Object - object on which the Anubis config is checked
_AnubisConfig - the config
GetAnubisConfig.examples:
PROC
PROC_AddAnubisConfigDB((CHARACTER)_Char)
AND
GetAnubisConfig(_Char, _AnubisConfig)
THEN
DB_AnubisConfigs(_Char, _AnubisConfig);

GetBodyType.description:
Returns the current identity value of the character.
Parameters:
_Character = the character you’re checking
_AllowShapeshiftOverride = Whether to get the value from true shape or current shape. Check GetGender for more info
_Identity = the return value in string form. Values can be found in UUID Table Enumerations>Identity
GetBodyType.examples:
IF
CompanionSelectedForUser(_Daisy,_UserID)
AND
DB_Avatars(_Avatar)
AND
DB_GLO_DaisyDreams_Relationships(_Avatar, _OldDaisy)
AND
DB_GLO_DaisyDreams_Daisies(_OldDaisy)
AND
GetReservedUserID(_Avatar,_UserID)
AND
GetIdentity(_Daisy,0,_Gender)
THEN
DB_GLO_DaisyDreams_Relationships(_Avatar, _Daisy);
NOT DB_GLO_DaisyDreams_Relationships(_Avatar, _OldDaisy);
PROC_DaisyDesire_SetDaisyFor(_Avatar, _Daisy, _Gender);

GetPortalIsOpen.description:
Returns whether the room portal associated with _Item is open.
Further InformationFails if _Item is invalid or not a room portal.
_Bool: 1 if the room portal associated with _Item is open, 0 otherwise.

GetPlatformUnderObject.description:
Returns the platform instance or entity _Platform that a character or item _Object is standing on.
Further InformationHelps check what items should move with a specific platform if a platform is set to move.

GetGoldInMagicPockets.description: A query that returns how much gold a _Character has access to in magic pockets, including the camp chest(s). Will exclude locked inventories.
GetGoldInMagicPockets.examples:
IF
GoldChanged((CHARACTER)_Player, _Value)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
_Value > 0
AND
DB_GLO_Backgrounds_Noble_GoldHoarded(_Id, _Threshold)
AND
GetGoldInMagicPockets(_Player, _Amount)
AND
_Amount >= _Threshold
THEN
PROC_GLO_Backgrounds_CompleteGoal(_Player, _Id);

GetSafeRomanceOption.description:
Returns if the user controlling the _Character has the option Show Sexual and Violent Cinematics enabled or not.
Parameters:
_Character: character for who we want to know if the option is enabled.
_State: returns 1 if enabled, and 0 if disabled.Further InformationSee SafeRomanceOptionChanged for more information.

GetIndividualRelation.description:
Returns individual _Relation between _Entity and _Faction (after it was changed via SetIndividualRelation).
Return Values:
_Relation - interger value that can be:
0: _SourceFaction & _TargetFaction are Enemies
50: _SourceFaction & _TargetFaction are Neutral or Persistent Neutral
100: _SourceFaction & _TargetFaction are Allied

GetInnerDistanceTo.description:
Returns the inner distance between Object1, and Object2.
This is the distance with the bounds of the objects subtracted.

GetRulesetModifierInt.description:
Gets the current value of a chosen Ruleset Modifier.
Look for a ruleset in UUID Object Editor in RulesetModifiers and make sure you’re using the correct query according to the type of a ruleset.
Further InformationCheckRulesetModifierInt can check if a certain value is set for a chosen Ruleset Modifier.
GetRulesetModifierInt.examples:
IF
LeftTrigger((CHARACTER)_PartyMember, (TRIGGER)_LairCurseArea)
AND
GetRulesetModifierString(SCRIPTED_COMBAT_MECHANICS_cac2d8bd-c197-4a84-9df1-f86f54ad4521,(STRING)_Difficulty)
AND
DB_DifficultyLairCurses(_Status,_Difficulty)
AND
HasActiveStatus(_PartyMember,_Status,1)
THEN
RemoveStatus(_PartyMember,_Status);

PROC
PROC_BloodOfLathander_StartQuestTimer((GUIDSTRING)_Player)
AND
NOT GetRulesetModifierString(SCRIPTED_COMBAT_MECHANICS_cac2d8bd-c197-4a84-9df1-f86f54ad4521,"EASY")
THEN
ObjectQuestTimerLaunch(_Player, "Lance_Explosion_QuestTimer", "BloodOfLathander_TurnTimer", 24000, 0);

GetPosition.description:
Returns the position of the _Target, with _Y being the vertical coordinate. It works on any kind of game object.
Further InformationIf _Target is an item and the item is inside the inventory of a character or a container, this call returns the position of the character/outermost container.

CrimeIsSuspended.description:
Returns whether a disturbance is suspended.
Parameters:
_CrimeID: the disturbance’s ID
_Bool: 1 if the disturbance is suspended, 0 otherwise.Further InformationSuspending is a deliberate process done to a disturbance of a given _CrimeID with CrimeSuspend.

GetReservedUserID.description:
Returns the ID of the user that controls _Character in _User.
Further InformationSuccess/Failure: Fails if _Character does not exist or is NULL.
_User: The user ID of the player to which _Character is assigned. If it is not assigned to anyone, the special value -65536 is returned.
"Users" are the game term for human players in the game. Every client that connects to the game, including the one that started the host game, gets assigned a User ID.
User IDs are volatile: they can change at any time, in particular when loading a saved game, or when someone disconnects and reconnects. Never store this value in a database. Only use it immediately after requesting it, within the same frame. If you need a persistent identifier for a user (or rather, user profile), pass the result of this query to GetUserProfileID.
GetReservedUserID.examples:
QRY
QRY_GLO_Brainquakes_PlayEmperorAD((DIALOGRESOURCE)_EmperorAD,(STRING)_UserProfileID)
AND
DB_Avatars(_Avatar)
AND
GetReservedUserID(_Avatar,_UserID)
AND
GetUserProfileID(_UserID,_UserProfileID)
THEN
PROC_EmperorAD(_Avatar,_EmperorAD);

GetItemByTagInUserInventory.description:
Returns the first _Item matching the given _Tags in any inventory of the User who controls the specified _Character.
Return Values:
_Item: returns the name and GUID of the item.Further Information_Tags is expecting a string tag expression and may include multiple Tags to check for. For example, the string “APPLE && PEAR” will return the first item it finds that has either the tag APPLE or PEAR.
Not commonly used, consider GetItemByTagInPartyInventory instead.
GetItemByTagInUserInventory.examples:
//An example using a Player walking in to a trigger
IF
EnteredTrigger(_Player, _ItemTagCheckTrigger)
AND
GetItemByTagInUserInventory(_Tag, _Player, _Item)
THEN
//Do whatever you need to with the _Item

GetApprovalRating.description:
Queries the approval rating between _RatingOwner and the _RatedCharacter in _Rating.
Parameters:
_RatingOwner: Typically the companion who offers the approval.
_RatedCharacter: The character who is rated by the companion, typically the player.
_Rating: The queried approval rating between the two characters.
GetApprovalRating.examples:
IF
DB_InCamp((CHARACTER)_DrowCommander)
AND
DB_Avatars(_Avatar)
AND
GetApprovalRating((CHARACTER)_DrowCommander, _Avatar, _CurrentApproval)
AND
_CurrentApproval > 30
THEN
PROC_CampRelationshipDialog((CHARACTER)_DrowCommander, (DIALOGRESOURCE)_Minthara_InParty, (FLAG)_Minthara_IPRD_RomanceBegins, 0, 31);
PROC_Try_CampRelationshipDialog((CHARACTER)_DrowCommander, 1);

DataGetBackgroundTagCount.description: Get the total number of “Background Tags” - tags that show a character’s Background.
DataGetBackgroundTagCount.examples:
PROC
PROC_GLO_DataGetOriginTags((CHARACTER)_Origin)
AND
DataGetBackgroundTagCount(_Origin,_Count)
AND
QRY_DoNTimes(_Count)
AND
DB_QRY_RTN_DoNTimes(_Index)
AND
DataGetBackgroundTagAt(_Origin,_Index,_BackgroundTag)
THEN
SetTag(_Origin,_BackgroundTag);

GetSpellFromSet.description:
Returns the spell of a specific spell set if any. If no spell can be found the query will fail.
Parameters:
_SpellSetID: ID of the spell set.
_SpellIndex: index containing the spell in the spell set.
_SpellID: returns the spell name in the _SpellIndex of the _SpellSetID.Further InformationSpell sets can be found and created in the stats editor under "ModName"/SpellData/SpellSet.
GetSpellFromSet.seeAlso:
UseSpell
AddSpell
RemoveSpell
UseSpellAtPosition
CastedSpell
CastSpell
CastSpellFailed
LearnedSpell
StartedPreviewingSpell
UsingSpell
UsingSpellAtPosition
UsingSpellInTrigger
UsingSpellOnTarget
CanLearnSpell
CanShowSpellForCharacter
HasSpell
IsSpellActive
SpellHasSpellFlag

GetDistanceTo.examples:
IF
EnteredTrigger(_Player, _BloodBank_AstarionReactionTrigger)
AND
IsInPartyWith(_Player_Astarion, _Player, 1)
AND
GetDistanceTo(_Player_Astarion, _Player, _Dist)
AND
_Dist < 8.0
AND
QRY_OnlyOnce("BloodBankApproached")
THEN
PROC_TryStartAD((DIALOGRESOURCE)_ApproachedBloodBankInventoryList_PAD, _Player_Astarion);
PROC_TriggerUnregisterForPlayers(_BloodBank_AstarionReactionTrigger);

GetAttitudeTowardsPlayer.description:
Returns the amount of _Attitude non-player character _Character has towards _Player
Further InformationAttitude affect item prices when trading with an NPC.
GetAttitudeTowardsPlayer.examples:
PROC
PROC_HAG_SetDoublesAttitudeToPlayers((CHARACTER)_HagDouble)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
GetAttitudeTowardsPlayer(_Hag, _Player, _Attitude)
THEN
PROC_SetAttitude(_HagDouble, _Player, _Attitude);

GetRelationRaw.description:
Returns _Relation between _SourceFaction and _TargetFaction.
Return Values:
_Relation - interger value that can be:
0: _SourceFaction & _TargetFaction are Enemies
50: _SourceFaction & _TargetFaction are Neutral
51: _SourceFaction & _TargetFaction are Persistent Neutral
100: _SourceFaction & _TargetFaction are AlliedFurther InformationThe only difference between this call and GetRelation is that this call returns 50 for Neutral and 51 for Persistent Neutral factions and GetRelation returns 50 for both types.
GetRelationRaw.examples:
IF
EntityEvent((CHARACTER)_Witness,_GuardIterator)
AND
DB_CRIME_GuardKillerCombatWitness(_Killer,_Guard,_GuardFaction,_Region,_Pool,_GuardIterator)
AND
_Witness != NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
AND
NOT DB_Defeated(_Witness)
AND
GetFaction(_Witness,_WitnessFaction)
AND
GetRelationRaw(_WitnessFaction,_GuardFaction,_Relation)
AND
// Neutral or better
_Relation >= 50
AND
// But not persistently neutral
_Relation != 51
AND
IsPartyMember(_Witness,1,0)
AND
IsSummon(_Witness,0)
AND
QRY_CRIME_Guards_ValidGuardKillerWitnessTag(_Witness)
AND
NOT QRY_CRIME_Guards_GuardKillerExcludeWitness(_Witness)
AND
CanSee(_Witness,_Guard,1)
THEN
PROC_CRIME_GuardKillerSpotted_MaybeAD(_Witness,_Killer,_Guard);
PROC_CRIME_GuardKiller_Noticed(_Witness,_Killer,_Guard,_Region,_Pool);

GetGlobalPriceModifier.description:
Checks the current global adjustment of all prices.
Parameters:
_Modifier: The current modifier

GetDirectInventoryOwner.description:
Returns the _DirectInventoryOwner of instances of a specific _Object.
Return Values:
_DirectInventoryOwner:
The GUIDSTRING of the owner.Further Information_Object and _DirectInventoryOwner can be either a character or an item.
GetDirectInventoryOwner.examples:
IF
FlagSet((FLAG)DoSomethingToItemXOwner_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, _, _)
AND
GetDirectInventoryOwner(ItemX_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, _InventoryOwner)
AND
IsCharacter(_InventoryOwner, 1)
THEN
//Do whatever you want to the character that is the _InventoryOwner 
//Will not do anything if the _InventoryOwner is an Item

ConcatenateGUID.description: Adds _GUIDstring to the end of _String and returns result in _Result.

GetOriginalOwner.description:
Returns the original owner of the _Item as _Character. This can be changed through SetOriginalOwner.
Further InformationAn item is considered stolen if its owner does not match its original owner.
If an item is not yet stolen (i.e. its owner and original owner are identical) and that original owner dies, the owner & original owner are both cleared.
GetOriginalOwner.examples:
IF
AddedTo(_Item, _BackPack, _)
AND
DB_CompanionLeavingBackPack((CHARACTER)_Companion, (CHARACTER)_Player, _BackPack)
AND
// Only items have an (original) owner
QRY_IsExistingItem(_Item, 1)
AND
GetOriginalOwner((ITEM)_Item, _Companion)
THEN
SetOriginalOwner(_Item, _Player);

GetRulesetModifierString.description:
Gets the current value of a chosen Ruleset Modifier.
Look for a ruleset in UUID Object Editor in RulesetModifiers and make sure you’re using the correct query according to the type of a ruleset.
Further InformationIn BG3 this is used to check the overall difficulty:
SCRIPTED_COMBAT_MECHANICS_cac2d8bd-c197-4a84-9df1-f86f54ad4521
EASY
MEDIUM
HARDCheckRulesetModifierString can check if a certain value is set for a chosen Ruleset Modifier.
GetRulesetModifierString.examples:
PROC
PROC_BloodOfLathander_StartQuestTimer((GUIDSTRING)_Player)
AND
GetRulesetModifierString(SCRIPTED_COMBAT_MECHANICS_cac2d8bd-c197-4a84-9df1-f86f54ad4521,"EASY")
THEN
ObjectQuestTimerLaunch(_Player, "Explosion_QuestTimer", "BloodOfLathander_TurnTimer", 42000, 0);

IF
LeftTrigger((CHARACTER)_PartyMember, (TRIGGER)_PhilgravesMansion_LairCurseArea)
AND
GetRulesetModifierString(SCRIPTED_COMBAT_MECHANICS_cac2d8bd-c197-4a84-9df1-f86f54ad4521,(STRING)_Difficulty)
AND
DB_DifficultyLairCurses(_Status,_Difficulty)
AND
HasActiveStatus(_PartyMember,_Status,1)
THEN
RemoveStatus(_PartyMember,_Status);

GetRulesetModifierFloat.description:
Gets the current value of a chosen Ruleset Modifier.
Look for a ruleset in UUID Object Editor in RulesetModifiers and make sure you’re using the correct query according to the type of a ruleset.
Further InformationCheckRulesetModifierFloat can check if a certain value is set for a chosen Ruleset Modifier.
GetRulesetModifierFloat.examples:
PROC
PROC_BloodOfLathander_StartQuestTimer((GUIDSTRING)_Player)
AND
GetRulesetModifierString(SCRIPTED_COMBAT_MECHANICS_cac2d8bd-c197-4a84-9df1-f86f54ad4521,"EASY")
THEN
ObjectQuestTimerLaunch(_Player, "Lance_Explosion_QuestTimer", "BloodOfLathander_TurnTimer", 42000, 0);

IF
LeftTrigger((CHARACTER)_PartyMember, (TRIGGER)_LairCurseArea)
AND
GetRulesetModifierString(SCRIPTED_COMBAT_MECHANICS_cac2d8bd-c197-4a84-9df1-f86f54ad4521,(STRING)_Difficulty)
AND
DB_DifficultyLairCurses(_Status,_Difficulty)
AND
HasActiveStatus(_PartyMember,_Status,1)
THEN
RemoveStatus(_PartyMember,_Status);

GetAngleXToDirection.description:
Returns the angle around the Y-axis between two points in degrees
Further InformationTwo points _DirFromX, _DirFromZ and _DirToX, _DirToZ form a line.
East (represented by the red arrow) is always 0 degrees.
The angle (beta (β) in the diagram) goes up with anticlockwise rotation.
The angle returned is between -180 and 180.

GetFlagName.description:
Given a _Flag returns its name in _FlagName as a string.
Parameters:
_Flag: flag GUID from which we want to get its name.
_FlagName: returned name of the flag.
GetFlagName.examples:
PROC
PROC_GetFlagInfo((FLAG)_Flag)
AND
GetFlagName(_Flag, _FlagName)
AND
GetFlagDescription(_Flag, _FlagDescription)
AND
Concatenate(_FlagName, ": ", _Substring)
AND
Concatenate(_Substring, _FlagDescription, _Message)
THEN
DebugBreak(_Message);

GetStackAmount.description:
Returns the _ExactItemAmount stacked in _Item.
Parameters:
_Item: The item stack that is being queried.
_ExactItemAmount: Returns the amount of items currently stacked into the item.
_TotalAmount: Returns the total amount that can be stacked into the item.
GetStackAmount.examples:
IF
ItemEnteredTrigger(_Item, _LakeEncounter_ItemTrigger, _)
AND
Exists(_Item,1)
AND
GetOwner(_Item, _Owner)
AND
DB_Players(_Owner)
AND
GetStackAmount(_Item, _Amount, _)
THEN
DB_EbonLake_StoredItems((ITEM)_Item, (INTEGER)_Amount, (CHARACTER)_Owner);

GetStatusType.description:
Returns the _StatysType from _StatusID.
_StatusID: the name of the Status in any StatusData table.
_StatusType is the name of the StatusData table in the stats editor, without the prefix. Check the Further Information section to check the available names.Further InformationRight now, the current status data tables you can use for _StatusType parameter are:
BOOST
HEAL
FEAR
POLYMORPHED
KNOCKED_DOWN
INCAPACITATED
INVISIBLE
DEACTIVATED
EFFECT
SNEAKING
DOWNEDExecuting this call will remove any status applied to that character that is inside any table with that suffix, whatever the mod is storing the table.
GetStatusType.seeAlso:
ApplyStatus
RemoveStatusesWithType
HasActiveStatus
HasActiveStatusOfType
RemoveHarmfulStatuses
StatusAttempt
StatusAttemptFailed
StatusApplied
StatusRemoved

GetStatString.description:
Returns the name of the Stat assigned to supplied entity.
Further InformationReturns empty string if the object has no stat set to it. Thus the query is suitable as a rule filter by supplying specific stats string value(s).
Stats value of an item can set and found in the Siderbar when the item is selected in the Glasses editor.

GetTemplate.examples:
IF
MovedFromTo((ITEM)_GortashGloves, _Gortash, (ITEM)_CampChest, _)
AND
DB_Camp_UserCampChest(_, _CampChest)
AND
GetTemplate(_GortashGloves, _MAG_Gortash_Gloves)
AND
QRY_GetClosestAvailableCharacterTo(_Gortash, 0)
AND
DB_ClosestAvailableCharacterTo_PreferAvatar(_Char, _Gortash, _, _)
THEN
ToInventory(_GortashGloves, _Char);

GetTeleportTarget.description:
This query will return the guidstring of the teleport target of any given Item, if any exists.
Further InformationNote that the signature of the call is misleading: this query will return the Guidstring of the target, even if said target is not an item (for example, a trigger).
You can find this information on the editor, on the “Use Actions” parameter. There, if an item has a “Teleport” use action, you will find the target of it under TeleportTarget->Target->Target.
Note that this query must be called on the item that has the Teleport action, not the Source of said action.
If the teleport target was overridden using SetTeleportTargetOverride, it will return that one.
GetTeleportTarget.examples:
IF
TextEvent("docuhelper")
AND
GetTeleportTarget(_TestSituation_PortalItem, _Res)
AND
GUIDToString(_Res, _String)
THEN
DebugLog(_String);

GetSurfaceNameByTypeIndex.description: Returns the name of the surface that has the index _SurfaceIndex in the internal numbered list of surface types as _SurfaceName.

GetSwarmGroup.description:
Returns the string id of a swarm group that _Target is assigned to.
Further InformationQuery fails if there’s no swarm group.

GetStatusTurns.description: Returns the remaining lifetime of a status with _StatusID on _Source, which can either be a character or an item.
GetStatusTurns.examples:
PROC
PROC_JaheirasHouse_MakeStatusPermanent((STRING)_Status, (GUIDSTRING)_Orb)
AND
GetStatusTurns(_Orb, _Status, _Turns)
AND
_Turns != -1.0
THEN
RemoveStatus(_Orb, _Status);
ApplyStatus(_Orb, _Status, -1.0, 1);

GetTextEventParamString.description: Event with name _Event thrown from ReCon with command oe _Event.
GetTextEventParamString.examples:
IF
TextEvent("PlayDebugText")
AND
GetHostCharacter(_Player)
THEN
DebugText(_Player, "Playing Debug Text");

GetSurfaceCloudAt.description: Returns the _Surface in the cloud layer at the _Target's location.

GetSurfaceSize.description:
Returns the size of the surface type on layer _SurfaceLayer in which _Target is standing. Valid values for _SurfaceLayer are
0 = Ground layer
1 = Cloud layer

GetSurfaceGroundOwnerAt.description:
Returns the _OwnerCharacter of the surface in the ground layer at the _Target location.
Further InformationSuccess/Failure: Fails if the _Target is outside the AIgrid.
_OwnerCharacter: The owner of the ground-layer surface at the _Target's position, if the surface has an owner who is a character. Null otherwise.
_OwnerItem: The owner of the ground-layer surface at the _Target's position, if the surface has an owner who is an item. Null otherwise.

GetTextEventParamInteger.description:
Returns the parameter at the specified _Number position in ReCon Text Event as an Integer.
oe TextEvent Parameter1 Parameter2
Further InformationIf the parameter at the _Number position is not a number, the query returns the Integer 0.
GetTextEventParamInteger.examples:
IF
TextEvent("COL_TeleportPlayer")
AND
GetTextEventParamInteger(1, _Num)
THEN
PROC_TeleportPartiesToColony(_Num);

HasAppliedStatusWithGroup.description:
Returns whether _Target has any status part of _StatusGroup. _Target may or may not yet actually be under its influence, and the status application may still fail.
_Target can be a CHARACTER or ITEM.
_StatusGroup is a string from StatusGroup column in any StatusData table in the Stats Editor.
_Bool: 0 if _Target has no applied status of _StatusGroup, 1 if it has.Further InformationIn virtually all cases, use HasActiveStatusWithGroup instead. The main purpose of this query is for crime reactions, and is only needed due to frame delays between code and Osiris.
HasAppliedStatusWithGroup.examples:
IF
StatusRemoved(_Object, _Status, _, _)
AND
DB_GLO_Mad(_Object)
AND
IsStatusFromGroup(_Status, "SG_Mad", 1)
AND
HasAppliedStatusWithGroup(_Object, "SG_Mad", 0)
THEN
NOT DB_GLO_Mad(_Object);
PROC_GLO_Mad_RestoreIndivRelation(_Object);
HasAppliedStatusWithGroup.seeAlso:
ApplyStatus
RemoveStatus
HasAppliedStatus
HasActiveStatus
StatusAttempt
StatusAttemptFailed
StatusApplied
StatusRemoved

GetTadpolePowersCount.description: Returns the number of Tadpole Powers _Powers the specified _Character has.

GetSurfaceCloudOwnerAt.description:
Returns the _OwnerCharacter of the surface in the cloud layer at the _Target location.
Further InformationSuccess/Failure: Fails if the _Target is outside the AIgrid.
_OwnerCharacter: The owner of the cloud-layer surface at the _Target's position, if the surface has an owner who is a character. Null otherwise.
_OwnerItem: The owner of the cloud-layer surface at the _Target's position, if the surface has an owner who is an item. Null otherwise.

GetUserName.description:
Returns _UserName of a User with ID _UserId.
Return Values:
Success/Failure: Fails if the server component of the game has not started yet, or if no player with _UserID is connected.
_UserName: Name of the User.Further Information"Users" are the game term for human players in the game.
Name of a User is the name of their in-game profile (not the name of their avatar!).
For any persistent identification it’s better to use Profile ID that can be accessed through GetUserProfileID.
GetUserName.examples:
...
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
GetReservedUserID(_Player, _UserID)
AND
GetUserName(_UserID, _UserName)
THEN
DebugBreak(_UserName);

GetSurfaceTypeIndex.description:
Returns the index value _Index of a surface type with string identifier _Surface.
Further InformationSurface _Surface correlates with index:
"SurfaceNone" or an unknown surface will return 0.
"SurfaceWater"
"SurfaceWaterElectrified"
"SurfaceWaterFrozen"
"SurfaceBlood"
"SurfaceBloodElectrified"
"SurfaceBloodFrozen"
"SurfacePoison"
"SurfaceOil"
"SurfaceLava"
"SurfaceGrease"
"SurfaceWyvernPoison"
"SurfaceWeb"
"SurfaceDeepWater"
"SurfaceVines"
"SurfaceFire"
"SurfaceAcid"
"SurfaceTrialFire"
"SurfaceBlackPowder"
"SurfaceShadowCursedVines"
"SurfaceAlienOil"
"SurfaceMud"
"SurfaceAlcohol"
"SurfaceInvisibleWeb"
"SurfaceBloodSilver"
"SurfaceChasm"
"SurfaceHellfire"
"SurfaceCausticBrine"
"SurfaceBloodExploding"
"SurfaceAsh"
"SurfaceSpikeGrowth"
"SurfaceHolyFire"
"SurfaceBlackTentacles"
"SurfaceOvergrowth"
"SurfacePurpleWormPoison"
"SurfaceSerpentVenom"
"SurfaceInvisibleGithAcid"
"SurfaceBladeBarrier"
"SurfaceSewer"
"SurfaceWaterCloud"
"SurfaceWaterCloudElectrified"
"SurfacePoisonCloud"
"SurfaceExplosionCloud"
"SurfaceShockwaveCloud"
"SurfaceCloudkillCloud"
"SurfaceMaliceCloud"
"SurfaceBloodCloud"
"SurfaceStinkingCloud"
"SurfaceDarknessCloud"
"SurfaceFogCloud"
"SurfaceGithPheromoneGasCloud"
"SurfaceSporeWhiteCloud"
"SurfaceSporeGreenCloud"
"SurfaceSporeBlackCloud"
"SurfaceDrowPoisonCloud"
"SurfaceIceCloud"
"SurfacePotionHealingCloud"
"SurfacePotionHealingGreaterCloud"
"SurfacePotionHealingSuperiorCloud"
"SurfacePotionHealingSupremeCloud"
"SurfacePotionInvisibilityCloud"
"SurfacePotionSpeedCloud"
"SurfacePotionVitalityCloud"
"SurfacePotionAntitoxinCloud"
"SurfacePotionResistanceAcidCloud"
"SurfacePotionResistanceColdCloud"
"SurfacePotionResistanceFireCloud"
"SurfacePotionResistanceForceCloud"
"SurfacePotionResistanceLightningCloud"
"SurfacePotionResistancePoisonCloud"
"SurfaceSporePinkCloud"
"SurfaceBlackPowderDetonationCloud"
"SurfaceVoidCloud"
"SurfaceCrawlerMucusCloud"
"SurfaceCloudkill6Cloud"

GetVarUUID.description:
Returns the _UUID of the variable _VarName of _Source, if it is a UUID (GUID).
Further InformationVariables set in Anubis.

GetVarFloat3.description: Returns the value of the variable _VarName of _Source, if it is a Float3, i.e. a set of coordinates.
GetVarFloat3.examples:
IF
OnCrimeInvestigatorSwitchedState(_CrimeID,_Investigator,_InvestigationState,"Idle")
AND
DB_CRIME_InvestigationStates(_InvestigationState)
AND
QRY_CRIME_NoImmediateInvestigationAbort(_CrimeID)
AND
GetVarFloat3(_Investigator,"CrimePos",_X,_Y,_Z)
AND
ConcatenateInteger("CRIME_InvestigationExtension_",_CrimeID,_TimerName)
AND
Random(5000,_RandVal)
AND
IntegerSum(10000,_RandVal,_Delay)
AND
CrimeGetNewID(_NewCrimeID)
THEN
PROC_CharacterRegisterCrimeWithPosition(_Investigator,"DummyInvestigation",NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000,_X,_Y,_Z,_Investigator,_NewCrimeID);
ObjectTimerLaunch(_Investigator,_TimerName,_Delay);
DB_CRIME_InvestigatorExtension(_Investigator,_TimerName,_NewCrimeID);

GetUserCount.description:
Returns a number of current Users in the game.
Return Values:
Success/Failure: Fails if the server component of the game has not started yet.
_UserCount: Number of current Users.
GetUserCount.examples:
PROC
PROC_FlagReactionAfterDialog(_Player, _State_EndEpilogue)
AND
GetUserCount(_Count)
AND
_Count > 1
THEN
ClearFlag((FLAG)_State_EndEpilogue, _Player, 0);
ReadyCheckGlobal("ReadyCheck_FinalToast", "ReadyMessage_FinalToast", 1, (CHARACTER)_Player);

GetWeaponScoreForCharacter.description:
Checks how good the AI estimates the weapon to be for the given character.
Paramaters:
_Weapon: The weapon being checked.
_Character: The character for whom the weapon is checked.
_Score: The score of the weapon.
GetWeaponScoreForCharacter.examples:
// Find best weapon for this NPC
PROC
PROC_LootDistribution_SelectBestWeaponForNPC((STRING)_Pool,(CHARACTER)_NPC)
AND
DB_LootDistribution_ActiveContainer(_Pool,_StashContainer)
AND
DB_LootDistribution_InPoolStash(_Pool,_StashContainer,_Item)
AND
IsWeapon(_Item,1)
AND
GetWeaponScoreForCharacter(_Item,_NPC,_NewScore)
AND
DB_LootDistribution_BestWeapon(_CurrentWeapon,_PoolOrEmpty,_CurrentScore)
AND
_NewScore > _CurrentScore
THEN
NOT DB_LootDistribution_BestWeapon(_CurrentWeapon,_PoolOrEmpty,_CurrentScore);
DB_LootDistribution_BestWeapon(_Item,_Pool,_NewScore);

GetUseRemotely.description: Checks if _Item has the UseRemotely flag set. See SetUseRemotely for more information.

GetVarObject.description: Returns the _UUID of the variable _VarName of _Source, if it is a character, item, or trigger.

GetTextEventParamUUID.description:
Returns the parameter at the specified _Number position in ReCon Text Event as a UUID (GUID).
oe TextEvent Parameter1 Parameter2
Further InformationIf the parameter at the _Number position is not a valid UUID, the query fails.
GetTextEventParamUUID.examples:
IF
TextEvent("TestAlarmSound")
AND
GetTextEventParamUUID(1, _AlarmUUID)
THEN
PlaySound(_AlarmUUID, "Vault_AlarmSound_Start");

HasAppliedStatusOfType.description:
Returns whether _Target has any status in a StatusType table. _Target may or may not yet actually be under its influence, and the status application may still fail.
_Target can be a CHARACTER or ITEM.
_StatusType is the name of the StatusData table in the stats editor, without the prefix. Check the Further Information section to check the available names.
_Bool: 0 if _Target has no applied status of _StatusType, 1 if it has.Further InformationIn general, the recommendation is not to HasAppliedStatus variants and, instead, use HasActiveStatus queries, like HasActiveStatus or HasActiveStatusWithGroup.
Right now, the current status data tables you can use for _StatusType parameter are:
BOOST
HEAL
FEAR
POLYMORPHED
KNOCKED_DOWN
INCAPACITATED
INVISIBLE
DEACTIVATED
EFFECT
SNEAKING
DOWNEDExecuting this call will remove any status applied to that character that is inside any table with that suffix, whatever the mod is storing the table.
You can get the type of a status through Osiris using GetStatusType.
HasAppliedStatusOfType.examples:
IF
StatusRemoved(_Object, _PolymorphStatus,_, _)
AND
DB_Is_Polymorphed(_Object)
AND
GetStatusType(_PolymorphStatus,"POLYMORPHED")
AND
HasAppliedStatusOfType(_Object,"POLYMORPHED",0)
THEN
NOT DB_Is_Polymorphed(_Object);
HasAppliedStatusOfType.seeAlso:
ApplyStatus
GetStatusType
HasAppliedStatus
HasAppliedStatusWithGroup
HasActiveStatus
RemoveHarmfulStatuses
StatusAttempt
StatusAttemptFailed
StatusApplied
StatusRemoved

HasActiveStatus.description: Returns whether _Target is currently under the influence of _Status.
HasActiveStatus.examples:
PROC
PROC_FlagReactionAfterDialog(_Player, _Event_GiveBuff)
AND
HasActiveStatus(_Player, "SHA_ALTAR_BUFF", 0)
THEN
ApplyStatus(_Player, "SHA_ALTAR_BUFF", -1.0, 1);

GetVarFloat.description: Returns the _VarValue of the variable _VarName of _Source, if it is a floating-point number.

GetVarInteger.description: Returns the _VarValue of the variable _VarName of _Source, if it is an integer.
GetVarInteger.examples:
IF
DualEntityEvent(_Player, _FarmableItem, "GEN_FarmWithNature_CheckPlayer")
AND
NOT DB_NatureSkillCheck((CHARACTER)_Player, (ITEM)_FarmableItem, _)
AND
NOT DB_NatureSkillCheck_AwaitingResult(_Player, _FarmableItem)
AND
GetVarInteger(_FarmableItem, "DC", _DC)
AND
QRY_TemporaryDCsubstitute(_DC)
AND
DB_QRYRTN_TemporaryDCsubstitute(_DCsub)
THEN
DB_NatureSkillCheck_AwaitingResult(_Player, _FarmableItem);
PROC_TrySkillCheck((CHARACTER)_Player, _FarmableItem, "Nature", _DCsub, "GLO_FarmableItemCheck");

GetTextEventParamReal.description:
Returns the parameter at the specified _Number position in ReCon Text Event as a Real.
oe TextEvent Parameter1 Parameter2
Further InformationIf the parameter at the _Number position is not a number, the query returns the Real 0.0.

GetStatusCurrentLifetime.description:
Return the _DurationLeft in seconds of the _StatusID in the selected _Source entity (ITEM or CHARACTER).
_Target can be a CHARACTER or ITEM.
_Status is a string from any StatusData table in the Stats editor.
_DurationLeft is the duration in seconds. If _StatusIDis not active, it will return 0.
GetStatusCurrentLifetime.examples:
PROC
PROC_GLO_Hardcore_SyncStatus((GUIDSTRING)_Target, (GUIDSTRING)_Origin, (STRING)_Status)
AND
HasAppliedStatus(_Origin, _Status, 1)
AND
GetStatusCurrentLifetime(_Origin, _Status, _Duration)
THEN
ApplyStatus(_Target, _Status, _Duration, 1, _Target);

GetWorldTransformFromLocal.description: Transforms local coordinates + rotation pair from _Object's local coordinate space to world coordinate space.
GetWorldTransformFromLocal.examples:
PROC
PROC_Combat_DragonHitProxy_UpdatePositions()
AND
DB_Combat_Dragon_HitProxies(_Proxy, _Identifier, _ProxyOwner, _Xoffset, _Yoffset, _Zoffset)
AND
GetWorldTransformFromLocal(_ProxyOwner, _Xoffset,_Yoffset,_Zoffset, 0.0 , 0.0, 0.0, _DestX, _DestY, _DestZ, _, _, _)
THEN
TeleportToPosition(_Proxy,_DestX, _DestY, _DestZ,"",0);

GUIDToString.description:
Will convert a given GUIDSTRING object to a string containing its full TechnicalName_GUID.
e.g. (GUIDSTRING)S_Player_ShadowHeart_3ed74f06-3c60-42dc-83f6-f034cb47c679 to "S_Player_ShadowHeart_3ed74f06-3c60-42dc-83f6-f034cb47c679"
Further InformationThe given GUIDSTRING object does not necessarily have to exist or be valid. It basically returns whatever you put in, technical name included, as a string for use in Osiris, as long as story allows it to compile.
See also: GetUUID (returns only the GUID after validating it)
GUIDToString.examples:
PROC
PROC_StateSet_Defeated((GUIDSTRING)_Entity)
AND
GUIDToString(_Entity,_EntityString)
AND
Concatenate(_EntityString," was defeated.",_DebugText)
THEN
DebugText(_Entity,_DebugText);

GetUserProfileID.description:
Returns a persistent UUID string that identifies the user with id _UserID.
Further InformationSuccess/Failure: Fails if the server component of the game has not started yet, or if no player with _UserID is connected.
_UserProfileID: A UUID in string format that identifies the profile used by this user. This UUID is persistent across game sessions. The UUID for a profile is randomly generated when the profile is first created.
GetUserProfileID.examples:
QRY
QRY_GLO_Brainquakes_PlayEmperorAD((DIALOGRESOURCE)_EmperorAD,(STRING)_UserProfileID)
AND
DB_Avatars(_Avatar)
AND
GetReservedUserID(_Avatar,_UserID)
AND
GetUserProfileID(_UserID,_UserProfileID)
THEN
PROC_EmperorAD(_Avatar,_EmperorAD);

HasOnUse.description: This query checks if the _Item has the given _UseAction, and _Bool returns 1 if yes or a 0 if no.
HasOnUse.examples:
IF
AddedTo(_Item, _Player)
AND
DB_InitialTutorialsShown(1)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
HasOnUse(_Item,"skillbook",1)
THEN
PROC_ShowUnifiedTutorialForPlayer(_Player,(UNIFIEDTUTORIAL)_Skillbook);

GetVarFixedString.description: Returns the _VarValue of the variable _VarName of _Source, if it is a Fixedstring.

IntegerDivide.description: Returns the _Quotient of division of _A by _B.
IntegerDivide.examples:
IF
ObjectTimerFinished((CHARACTER)_Goblin, "GoblinToast_DamageAfterToast")
AND
GetHitpoints(_Goblin, _HP)
AND
_HP > 1
AND
IntegerDivide(_HP, 2, _Quotient)
THEN
PROC_SetHitpoints_BlockSelfHealing(_Goblin, _Quotient);

HasRecipeUnlocked.description:
Returns whether the party to which character _Character belongs has the recipe with ID _RecipeID unlocked.
Further InformationSuccess/Failure: Fails if _character does not exist, or if it's not in a party.
HasRecipeUnlocked.examples:
IF
QuestUpdateUnlocked(_Player, "GLO_Tadpole", "PoisonedByApprentice")
AND
HasRecipeUnlocked(_Player, "ALCH_Potion_Antidote_Mugwort", 1)
THEN
QuestUpdate("GLO_Tadpole", "LearnedCureDaleland");

HasAppliedStatus.description:
Returns whether _Status has been applied to _Target. _Target may or may not yet actually be under its influence.
Further InformationHasActiveStatus should be used in most cases rather than this one since it will return the status that is active.

HasTadpolePower.description: Checks if the specified _Character has the specified Tadpole Power _Power.

HasAppearanceVisualTag.description:
Returns bool value (0 or 1) whether the character has a visual Tag or not.
Further InformationVisual tags are generally tags associated with visual elements chosen in Character Creation. They can be found in the Tags column in UUID table CharacterCreationAppearanceVisuals. As such they are only associated with avatars.

IntegerModulo.description:
Returns the remainder after division of _Num by _Mod as _Return.
Further InformationAttempts to calculate the remainder after division by 0 causes undefined behavior and is not caught.

HasSpell.description:
Check if a character has a spell or not.
Parameters:
_Character: character to check.
_Spell: name of the spell, including the category to which it pertains (for example Target_FindFamiliar_Dog).
_Bool: returns 1 if they have it or 0 if not.
HasSpell.examples:
IF
TagCleared((CHARACTER)_Char, (TAG)_FORBIDDENKNOWLEDGE)
AND
HasSpell(_Char, "Target_CursedTome_WakeTheDead", 1)
THEN
RemoveSpell(_Char, "Target_CursedTome_WakeTheDead", 1);
HasSpell.seeAlso:
UseSpell
AddSpell
RemoveSpell
UseSpellAtPosition
CastedSpell
CastSpell
CastSpellFailed
LearnedSpell
StartedPreviewingSpell
UsingSpell
UsingSpellAtPosition
UsingSpellInTrigger
UsingSpellOnTarget
CanLearnSpell
CanShowSpellForCharacter
GetSpellFromSet
IsSpellActive
SpellHasSpellFlag

IntegerToReal.description: Converts an integer type number to a real number e.g. 4 to 4.00
IntegerToReal.examples:
IF
DB_IntegerAmount((INTEGER)_IntAmount)
AND
IntegerToReal(_IntAmount, _RealAmount)
THEN
DB_RealAmount((REAL)_RealAmount);

IsAnyTutorialShowingFor.description:
Checks if any tutorial is currently being shown to a user who owns this character.
Further InformationImportant note: There’s some delay between calling ShowTutorial and this actually returning true so you can’t use this immediately after calling ShowTutorial. Instead, add a tiny delay timer and then run the check.
Usually used to temporarily suppress showing another tutorial in order to not overwhelm the player.
You can’t ask if any particular tutorial is being shown.

HasPassive.description: Returns _BoolHasPassive (0 for false, 1 for true) if the desired _Entity has the specific passive represented by the _PassiveID.
HasPassive.examples:
PROC
PROC_GLO_Tutorials_EvaluateSeeingInTheDarkTutorial((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
HasPassive((GUIDSTRING)_Player,"Darkvision",0)
AND
HasPassive(_Player,"SuperiorDarkvision",0)
AND
HasActiveStatus(_Player, "LIGHT", 0)
AND
CanShowSpellForCharacter((CHARACTER)_Player, "Target_Light", 0)
AND
NOT QRY_GLO_Tutorials_HasTorchEquipped(_Player)
AND
NOT DB_Is_InCombat(_Player, _)
THEN
PROC_ShowUnifiedTutorialForPlayer((CHARACTER)_Player, (UNIFIEDTUTORIAL)_Seeing_in_the_Dark);

IntegerToString.description: Converts an integer to a string.
IntegerToString.examples:
INIT
DB_DummyNumber(8);
KB
IF
DB_DummyNumber((INTEGER)_Number)
AND
IntegerToString(_Number,_NumberString)
AND
Concatenate("DummyNumber_Identifier_",_NumberString)
THEN
DB_DummyNumberIdentifier((STRING)_NumberString); //will be "DummyCounter_Identifier_8"

IsContainer.description: Returns whether _Item is in a container. An item is considered a container if it has an open/close use action.
IsContainer.examples:
IF
AddedTo(_DangerousBook_Tome, _Container, _)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
_Container != _Player
AND
GetFlag((FLAG)_DangerousBook_State_BookOwner, _Player, 1)
AND
IsCharacter(_Container, 0)
AND
IsContainer((ITEM)_Container, 1)
THEN
DB_FOR_DangerousBook_MoveTome(_Player);

HasLineOfSight.description:
Checks if there is a line of sight between _Source and _Target, whether an unobstructed 3D-line can be drawn between the two objects.
Further InformationThis query does not take into account sneaking, sight ranges, statuses, or anything else.
The objects can be any kind of object (character, item, trigger, spline, etc)
HasLineOfSight.examples:
QRY
QRY_HAG_Lair_HasNearbyThreat()
AND
DB_InRegion(_Player, _HagLair_NearbyBox)
AND
HasLineOfSight(_Hag, _Player, 1)
AND
IsInvisible(_Player, 0)
THEN
LookAtEntity((CHARACTER)_Hag, _Player); 
PROC_SpotPlayers_Spotted((CHARACTER)_Hag, "HAG_Hag_Lair", _Player);

IntegerMax.description: Returns the greater of two integers _A and _B as _Maximum.
IntegerMax.examples:
PROC
PROC_COL_Vault_TryConnectionAD((ITEM)_Source)
AND
QRY_COL_Vault_CanSpeak()
AND
QRY_IncreaseCounter("ConnectionsSinceLastAD")
AND
DB_GlobalCounter("ConnectionsSinceLastAD", _Count)
AND
_Count >= 2
AND
IntegerSubtract(6, _Count, _Diff)
AND
IntegerMax(_Diff, 1, _MinCount)
AND
IntegerProduct(_MinCount, _MinCount, _Squared)
AND
Random(_Squared, 0)
THEN
PROC_COL_Vault_ConnectionAD(_Source);

IntegerProduct.examples:
IF
GoldChanged(_Helper, _Amount)
AND
DB_LOW_StormshoreTabernacle_SingleGods(_, (CHARACTER)_Helper,_, _Chest, _)
AND
_Amount > 1
AND
IntegerProduct(-1, _Amount, _MinusAmount)
AND
IntegerSubtract(_Amount,1,_Amount2)
THEN
AddGold(_Chest, _Amount2);
AddGold(_Helper, _MinusAmount);

IntegerMin.description: Returns the smaller of two integers _A and _B as _Minimum.
IntegerMin.examples:
PROC
PROC_GLO_HagCounterswap_CheckNewDoublesToSpawn((INTEGER)_NewDoublesAmount)
AND
SysCount("DB_HAG_HagLair_Doubles",1,_ExistingDoublesAmount)
AND
DB_GLO_MaximumHagDoublesAmount(_MaximumHagDoublesAmount)
AND
IntegerSubtract(_MaximumHagDoublesAmount,_ExistingDoublesAmount,_FreeSlots)
AND
IntegerMin(_FreeSlots,_NewDoublesAmount,_AvailableHags)
AND
_AvailableHags > 0
THEN
DB_GLO_HagCounterswap_NewDoublesToSpawn(_AvailableHags);

HasSkill.description:
Checks if character _Character has skill _Skill.
Return Values:
_Value: 0 if the character doesn’t have the skill and 1 if the character has the skill.

IntegerSum.description: Returns the sum of integers _A and _B as _Sum.
IntegerSum.examples:
PROC
PROC_State_Changed((CHARACTER)_Harper, "DriderHarpers", "MovingToAmbush")
AND
Random(1000, _RandTimeDelay)
AND
IntegerSum(2000, _RandTimeDelay, _Delay)
THEN
PROC_RemoveDialog(_Harper);

HasTaggedItem.description: This query is used to check if an (GUIDSTRING)_Entity has the specified string of (STRING) _Tags. It returns an integer (INTEGER)_Bool. 0 is false, 1 is true.

HasRangedWeaponEquipped.description:
Check if a ranged weapon type is equipped.
Further InformationYou may check for “Mainhand", “Offhand", or “Any" if you want to check for either the Mainhand or Offhand.
HasRangedWeaponEquipped.examples:
QRY
QRY_HasAnyWeaponEquipped((CHARACTER)_Char)
AND
HasMeleeWeaponEquipped(_Char,"Any",_Melee)
AND
HasRangedWeaponEquipped(_Char,"Any",_Ranged)
AND
DB_LogicOr(_Melee,_Ranged,1)
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);

IsGameStateRunning.description:
Query used to check is if the game is in the "Running' state. Running is the state where a level is completely loaded and the player is in the game.
_Isrunning - returns 1 if game is in “Running” state, 0 if not.
IsGameStateRunning.examples:
PROC
PROC_LoopEffect_Queued()
AND
NOT DB_GLO_Effects_SavegameLoading(1)
AND
IsGameStateRunning(0)
THEN
DB_GLO_Effects_SavegameLoading(1);

GetUUID.description:
Will convert a given GUIDSTRING object to a string containing its full TechnicalName_GUID.
e.g. (GUIDSTRING)S_Player_ShadowHeart_3ed74f06-3c60-42dc-83f6-f034cb47c679 to "3ed74f06-3c60-42dc-83f6-f034cb47c679"
Further InformationThis will also validate the given GUIDSTRING before converting to string. This means the entity has to (still) exist for this query to work, otherwise it will assert.
See also: GUIDToString (returns technical name + GUID as string)
GetUUID.examples:
PROC
PROC_RunGlobalTimerForEntity((GUIDSTRING)_Entity)
AND
GetUUID(_Entity,_UUIDString)
AND
Concatenate("GLO_TimerForEntity_",_Entity,_TimerName)
THEN
TimerLaunch(_TimerName,2000);

GetSurfaceTurns.description: Returns the number of remaining turns of the surface at _Target's AI-grid position in the layer _SurfaceLayer.

IsHitProxyOwner.description:
Check if _OwnerObject has any HitProxy object attached.
_IsProxyOwner: 0 for false, 1 for true.
IsHitProxyOwner.seeAlso:
HitProxy
GetHitProxyOwner
IsHitProxy
AttachHitProxy
DetachHitProxy
DetachAllHitProxies
IterateHitProxies

IsBoss.description:
Checks if character _Target is a marked as Boss.
Return Values:
0 - if _Target is not marked as Boss.
1 - if _Target is marked as Boss.
IsBoss.examples:
PROC
PROC_CheckUnlockOneRoundBossKillGoal((CHARACTER)_Player, (CHARACTER)_Boss)
AND
IsTagged(_Player, (TAG)_SOLDIER, 1)
AND
IsBoss(_Boss, 1)
AND
QRY_OnlyOnce("BossKilledInOneRound")
THEN
PROC_GLO_Backgrounds_CompleteGoal(_Player, "Act2BossKilled");

IsGameLevel.description: Returns whether the current level is a non-character-creation, non-lobby level.

IsFalling.examples:
IF
DestroyedBy((ITEM)_My_Table,_,_,_)
AND
IsFalling((ITEM)_My_Cup,0)
THEN
SetGravity((ITEM)_My_Cup, GRAVITYTYPE.Enabled);

IsCharacter.description: Returns whether or not_Object  is a character.
IsCharacter.examples:
IF
EnteredTrigger(_Char, (TRIGGER)_SilenceTrigger)
AND
IsCharacter(_Char, 1)
AND
