NOT DB_PermaDefeated(_SilentLibrary_Librarian_Item)
THEN
ApplyStatus(_Char, "LIBRARIANSILENCE_STATUS", -1.0, 1, _SilentLibrary_Librarian_Item);

IsDead.examples:
PROC
PROC_SHA_AFamiliarFace_InitScene()
AND
IsDead(_Monitor, 1)
THEN
Resurrect(_Monitor);

InventoryGetGoldValue.description: Query that returns the total _Value of all the items and gold in the _InventoryHolder’s inventory. The holder can be anything with an inventory, including an item.

IsDropInCCActive.description: Returns the bool value of whether a Character Creation, triggered by any player during drop-in multiplayer sessions, is active.

IsClosing.description: Returns whether _Door is closing. This query only works for doors.
IsClosing.examples:
QRY
QRY_DEN_Gate_IsOpeningOrClosing()
AND
DB_DEN_GateInUse(_Gate)
AND
IsClosing(_Gate, 1)
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);

IsActive.description:
Checks if an _Object is active.
Return Values:
_Active:
0 - _Object is not active
1 - _Object is active.
IsActive.examples:
IF
EnteredTrigger(_Player,_Fortress_SUB)
AND
DB_Players((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
IsActive(_WYR_South_Civilian,0)
THEN
PROC_WYR_South_RefugeesRemove();

IsControlled.description: Returns whether _Character is currently controlled by a player.

GetVarString.description: Returns the _VarValue of the variable _VarName of _Source, if it is a String.
GetVarString.examples:
PROC
PROC_RollResult("GLO_FarmableItemCheck", _Player, (ITEM)_FarmableItem, 1)
AND
GetVarString(_FarmableItem, "RewardId", _RewardId)
THEN
DB_NatureSkillCheck(_Player, _FarmableItem, 1);
PROC_UND_FarmMushroom(_Player, _FarmableItem, _RewardId);

IsHitProxy.description:
Check if _ProxyObject is a Hit Proxy entity or not.
_IsProxy: 0 for false, 1 for true.
IsHitProxy.seeAlso:
HitProxy
GetHitProxyOwner
IsHitProxyOwner
AttachHitProxy
DetachHitProxy
DetachAllHitProxies
IterateHitProxies

IsConsumable.description: Checks if the specified _Item is a consumable.

IsFloating.description:
Check if an entity is floating.
Parameters:
_Object - The object you are checking.
_Bool - The query return. “1” if it is floating, “0” if not.Further Information“Floating” is one of the flags that can be set on Stats on any given entity.
You can check it trough the Debug Window function, on the “[OBJ] Properies”
Following changes apply to “Floating” entities:
Unaffected by GROUND type surfaces (affected by CLOUD)
Does not trigger preassure plate type traps
No fall damage
No footsteps

IsImmuneToStatus.description:
Query to check if _Target is immune to the specified _Status, applied by _Cause.
_Target can be a CHARACTER or ITEM.
_Status is a string from any StatusData table in the Stats editor.
_Cause specifies the object, either a CHARACTER or ITEM, that the game should consider as the cause of this status.
_Bool: 0 for false, 1, for true.
IsImmuneToStatus.seeAlso:
IsImmuneToFallDamage
IsImmuneToStatusGroup
ApplyStatus
HasActiveStatus
StatusApplied
StatusRemoved

IsEquipable.description: Checks if the item passed to it is an equipable item.
IsEquipable.examples:
PROC
PROC_EXA_EquipGear((CHARACTER)_Char)
AND
DB_EXA_CollectionOfGear((ITEM)_Item)
AND
IsEquipable(_Item, 1)
THEN
Equip(_Char, _Item);
IsEquipable.seeAlso:
Equip
Unequip
Equipped
Unequipped
EquipFailed
UnequipFailed
TemplateEquipped
TemplateUnequipped

IsDestroyed.description:
Returns if the _Item is destroyed.
Return Values:
_Destroyed: 1 if the _Item is destroyed, 0 otherwise.Further InformationFails if _Item is NULL or invalid.

IsGlobal.description:
Checks if the _Object is marked as Global.
Return Values:
0 if _Object is not Global
1 if _Object is Global
IsGlobal.examples:
QRY
QRY_IsExistingGlobal((GUIDSTRING)_Object,(INTEGER)_IsGlobal)
AND
Exists(_Object,1)
AND
IsGlobal(_Object, _IsGlobal)
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);

IsInInventory.description: Returns whether _Item is in an inventory. This includes both character and container inventories.

IsInDangerousSurfaceFor.description:
Checks if target position with coordinates _X, _Y, _Z and within a _Radius is in dangerous surface for_Character.
Setting _AlsoIfCanStopMovement to 1 makes the query include surfaces that don't damage, but stop movement (slick, sticky, etc.).Return Values:
_IsDangerous: 1 if the position is considered dangerous for _Character is , 0 otherwise.Further InformationThe query uses path influences to determine which surfaces are dangerous for _Character. For characters this is specified in stats.
Setting _Radius as -1.0 will make the query check the exact position.
IsInDangerousSurfaceFor.examples:
PROC
PROC_GlobalFlagReactionAfterDialog(_Event_CaptainDeactivatesBarrier)
AND
GetPosition(_CaptainBackDoorPos, _X, _Y, _Z)
AND
IsInDangerousSurfaceFor(_X, _Y, _Z, _CrecheCaptain, -1.0, 0, 0)
THEN
CharacterMoveTo((CHARACTER)_CrecheCaptain, _CaptainBackDoorPos, "Walk", "CaptainWalkToBarrier");

IsInTrigger.description:
Returns whether _Object is inside _Trigger. It does not require that _Trigger has been registered for _Object. _Object can be a character, item, or another object such as a trigger or spline.
In the non-character/item case, it will check whether the center/"position" (as shown in the editor) of this other object is in the trigger (it will not check whether an entire box trigger is inside the boundaries in another box trigger).
If _Object is an item and it is currently inside a container or a character's inventory, the query will check whether the outermost container/character is inside _Trigger.
IsInTrigger.examples:
IF
DB_InRegion(_DrowCommander, _DrowEscapeArea)
AND
NOT DB_Is_InCombat(_DrowCommander, _)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
NOT DB_Is_InCombat(_Player, _)
AND
CharacterGetOwner(_DrowCommander, _Player)
AND
IsInTrigger(_DrowCommander, _Haven_SUB, 0)
THEN
PROC_MOO_MintharaFate_StartEscapeDialog(_Player);

IgnoreGenerics.description:
Returns whether an item’s “Ignore Generics” property is set in the editor sidebar.
Parameters:
_Item: the item whose property to check
_Bool: 1 if the checkbox is ticked, 0 if not.Further Information“Generics” is another term that we use(d) for disturbances/crimes.
By itself, that checkbox does not do anything. It only influences behaviour because of what script does based on the result of this query.
IgnoreGenerics.examples:
PROC
PROC_CrimeVandaliseCheckOwner((ITEM)_Item,(CHARACTER)_Player,(CHARACTER)_Summon,(INTEGER)_StoryAction)
AND
IgnoreGenerics(_Item,0)
AND
GetOwner(_Item,_Owner)
AND
_Owner != NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
AND
IsPartyMember(_Owner,1,0)
THEN
PROC_CrimeVandaliseCheckVandal(_Item,_Owner,_Player,_Summon,"",_StoryAction);

IsLadder.description: Returns whether given _Item is a ladder in _Bool.
IsLadder.examples:
PROC
PROC_ProcessUseOfItem((CHARACTER)_Char,(ITEM)_Item,(INTEGER)_RequestID)
AND
NOT DB_CustomUseItemResponse(_Char,_Item,_)
AND
NOT DB_HandledRequest(_Char,_Item,_RequestID)
AND
CanSitOn(_Item,0)
AND
IsLadder(_Item,0)
AND
IsPublicDomain(_Item,0)
AND
IgnoreGenerics(_Item,0)
THEN
DB_HandledRequest(_Char,_Item,_RequestID);
PROC_CrimeTryRegisteringUseForbiddenItem(_Char,_Item,_RequestID,"Item");

IsInCombat.description: Returns true or false if the entity passed into the query is in combat. The entity can be a character or item.
IsInCombat.examples:
IF
EnteredTrigger(_Player, S_EXA_ExampleTrigger_XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
IsInCombat(_Player, 0)
THEN
SetFlag(EXA_Example_State_EnteredTriggerOutOfCombat_XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX, _Player);

IsEnemy.description: Returns whether _Character is an enemy of _OtherCharacter.
IsEnemy.examples:
IF
AttackedBy(_Rat, _Player, _, _, _, _, _)
AND
NOT DB_GlobalFlag((FLAG)_LastJusticiar_State_Hostile)
AND
DB_SHA_LastJusticiar_UltimatumRats((CHARACTER)_Rat, _)
AND
IsEnemy((CHARACTER)_Rat, (CHARACTER)_Player, 0)
THEN
PROC_GlobalSetFlagAndCache((FLAG)_LastJusticiar_State_Hostile);

IsInvisible.description:
Checks if the _Object is invisible or not.
Further Information_Bool will return 1 if the _Object is invisible and 0 if not.
This call will check if the _Object is invisible either through script or status effects such as spells, scrolls or potions used in game.
IsInvisible.examples:
QRY
QRY_HAG_Lair_HasNearbyThreat()
AND
DB_InRegion(_Player, _HagLair_NearbyBox)
AND
HasLineOfSight(_Hag, _Player, 1)
AND
IsInvisible(_Player, 0)
THEN
LookAtEntity((CHARACTER)_Hag, _Player); 
PROC_SpotPlayers_Spotted((CHARACTER)_Hag, "HAG_Hag_Lair", _Player);

IsInMagicPockets.description:
Checks if the _Object is in the inventory of the _Source or in any party inventory accessible to them, including camp chests.
The return value _Bool is 1 if it is in any of these inventories and 0 if it is not.
IsInMagicPockets.seeAlso:
SetMagicPocketsFlag
MagicPocketsDrop
MagicPocketsDropByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTag
MagicPocketsMoveTo
MagicPocketsMoveToByTemplate
MagicPocketsMoveToByTag
ClearMagicPocketsFlag
TaggedItemsGetCountInMagicPockets
TemplateGetCountInMagicPockets
StatStringGetCountInMagicPockets

IsInPartyWith.description:
Sets _Bool to 1 if _Character is in the same party as _Target, or 0 if they are not.
Further InformationTechnically the engine supports multiple parties, but BG3 has been designed to only have 1 party exist at the same time. Because of this, the query expects a _Character and a _Target.

IsAlly.description: Returns whether _Character is an ally of _OtherCharacter.
IsAlly.examples:
IF
BaseFactionChanged(_FlamingSpy, _, _FlamingSpy_Hostile)
AND
DB_Is_InCombat((CHARACTER)_FlamingSpy, _ID)
AND
DB_Is_InCombat(_Character, _ID)
AND
NOT DB_PartyMembers((CHARACTER)_Character)
AND
IsAlly(_Character, _FlamingSpy, 0)
THEN
LeaveCombat(_Character);

IsCharacterCreationLevel.description: Checks if the provided level is the Character Creation type of level. Such level is loaded after pressing the New Game button.
IsCharacterCreationLevel.examples:
IF
LevelLoaded(_Level)
AND
IsCharacterCreationLevel(_Level, 1)
THEN
StartCharacterCreation();

IsInForceTurnBasedMode.description: Checks if a given _PlayerCharacter is in Turn Based mode, _IsInFTB returns 1 if yes, and 0 if no.
IsInForceTurnBasedMode.examples:
IF
EntityEvent(_, "UND_Bulette_TriggerSpawn")
AND
DB_UND_Bulette_IsActive(_ActiveEncounter)
AND
DB_UND_Bulette_CombatArenas(_ActiveEncounter, _ArenaTrigger)
AND
DB_InRegion(_Player, _ArenaTrigger)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
GetDistanceTo(_Player, _Bulette_BurrowHelper, _Dist)
AND
_Dist < 15.0
AND
IsInCombat(_Player, 0)
AND
IsInForceTurnBasedMode(_Player, 0)
THEN
ForceTurnBasedMode(_Player, 1);

IsOpened.description:
Returns whether _Item is opened.
Further InformationThis query works both for doors and for items with an open/close use action. For items that cannot open/close, the query can either fail or return 0.
IsOpened.examples:
IF
FlagSet(_Armoury_Event_DoorOpens, _, _)
AND
IsDestroyed(_ArmouryDoor, 0)
AND
IsOpened(_ArmouryDoor, 0)
THEN
PROC_ItemUnlockAndOpen(_ArmouryDoor);

IsMoving.description:
Checks if an _Item is currently being moved.
_Bool will return 1 if it’s locked, 0 if unlocked.

IsJunk.description:
Checks if the _Item is marked as Junk.
Return Values:
0 - _Item is not marked as Junk
1 - _Item is marked as Junk
IsJunk.examples:
PROC
PROC_CrimeVandaliseCheckOwner((ITEM)_Item,(CHARACTER)_Player,(CHARACTER)_Summon,(INTEGER)_StoryAction)
AND
IgnoreGenerics(_Item,0)
AND
GetOwner(_Item,_Owner)
AND
_Owner == NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
AND
IsJunk(_Item,0)
THEN
PROC_CrimeVandaliseCheckVandal(_Item,NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000,_Player,_Summon,"NoOwner",_StoryAction);

IsLocked.description: Checks if an item is locked by returning an integer. 1 if it’s locked, 0 if unlocked.
IsLocked.examples:
PROC
PROC_TryLockDoor()
AND
IsLocked(_EXA_Door, 0) //Only continues if it returns 0
THEN
Lock(_EXA_Door, "EXA_Door_Key");

IsInteractionDisabled.description:
If the _Character is interaction disabled, the returned _Bool value will be 1, and 0 otherwise.
Further InformationInteractivity is often disabled by things like status effects that have the DisableInteractions property.

IsPartyFollower.description: Returns whether _Character is a party follower.
IsPartyFollower.examples:
IF 
FleeFromCombat(_, _ID)
AND
DB_GlobalFlag(_State_LeadersAreDead)
AND
DB_Is_InCombat(_Halsin, _ID)
AND
IsPartyFollower(_Halsin, 1)
THEN
DB_GLO_Halsin_ReadyForDen(1);

IsItem.description: Returns whether or not _Object is an item.
IsItem.examples:
IF
EntityEvent(_Item, "OmeluumInventory")
AND
IsItem(_Item, 1)
THEN
ToInventory(_Item, (ITEM)_OmeluumConfiscatedItemsBag, 1, 0, 1);

IsDroppedOnDeath.description: A query to check if a specific entity is set to be dropped on death.
IsDroppedOnDeath.examples:
IF
AddedTo(_Entity, _NPC, _)
AND
IsDroppedOnDeath(_Entity, _CurrentBool)
THEN
SetIsDroppedOnDeath(_Entity, _NewBool)

IsImmuneToFallDamage.description:
Checks if _Object is immune to fall damage.
_Target can be a CHARACTER or ITEM.
_Bool: 0 for false, 1, for true.
IsImmuneToFallDamage.examples:
IF
ObjectTimerFinished((CHARACTER)_Character, "UnderdarkEntrance_EnteredTrigger")
AND
DB_FOR_Bottomless_UnderdarkEntrance_Entered(_Character)
AND
IsInTrigger(_Character, _UnderdarkEntrance, 1)
AND
IsImmuneToFallDamage(_Character, 0)
AND
HasActiveStatus(_Character, "FLY", 0)
THEN
PROC_FOR_Bottomless_UnderdarkEntrance_DoDamage((CHARACTER)_Character);

IsOpening.description: Returns whether _Door is opening. This query only works for doors.
IsOpening.examples:
PROC
PROC_DEN_DruidAttack_LockPrison()
AND
IsOpening(_PrisonMainDoor,1)
THEN
Close(_PrisonMainDoor);

IsPlayer.description:
Returns whether _Character is player-controlled.
Further InformationPlayer-controlled characters include:
Avatars: the character chosen during character creation
Recruited companions: NPCs turned into player-controlled characters
Summons by other player-controlled characters
Party followers of player-controlled characters
Player-controlled characters explicitly do not include followers set using Follow.
IsPlayer.examples:
IF
EnteredTrigger(_Char, _Trigger)
AND
DB_DEN_InDenTriggers(_Trigger)
AND
IsPlayer(_Char, 1)
AND
NOT DB_DEN_PlayerInDen(_Char)
THEN
DB_DEN_PlayerInDen(_Char);
SetFlag((FLAG)_State_PlayerInDen, _Char);

IsMovable.description:
Checks if an _Item is moveable.
_Bool will return 1 if it’s locked, 0 if unlocked.
IsMovable.examples:
IF
DB_HuntCoward_WardRobeState(0)
AND
DB_EscapingZhentarim_UnderHiddenEntrance(_Item)
AND
Exists(_Item,1)
AND
IsMovable(_Item, 1)
AND
GetPosition(_Cellar_TriggerDown, _X, _Y, _Z)
THEN
ScatterAt(_Item, _X, _Y, _Z);

IsNeutral.description: Returns whether _Character and _OtherCharacter are neutral to each other.

IsReposedStanding.description: Same as IsReposed but also checks for whether _Character is standing on an entity.
IsReposedStanding.seeAlso:
ReposeAdded
ReposeRemoved
EndRepose
SitOnGround
LieOnGround
SitOnEntity
LieOnEntity
StandOnEntity
IsReposed
IsReposedSitting
IsReposedLying
IsReposedConstrained
TemplateCanSitOn

IsPartyMember.description: Returns whether _Character is currently in a party.
IsPartyMember.examples:
IF
UseStarted(_Player, _TavernInvestigation_Obstruction)
AND
IsInTrigger(_Player, _TavernInvestigation_Obstruction_SimpleClear, 0)
AND
DB_CantTalk(_Player)
AND
GetFlag(_State_TriedOnce, (GUIDSTRING)_Player, 0)
AND
IsPartyMember((CHARACTER)_Player, 1, 1)
THEN
RequestActiveRoll(_Player, _TavernInvestigation_Obstruction, "Athletics", (DIFFICULTYCLASS)_DC_Legacy_15, "TavernInvestigation_RollSuccess");

IsRangedWeapon.description:
Returns whether the object is a weapon.
Parameters:
_Item: the object checked
_IncludeThrown: if 1, thrown weapons are included
_Bool: returns 1 if it is a ranged (or thrown, see above) weapon, or 0 if it is not.
IsRangedWeapon.examples:
IF
Equipped(_Item, _Player)
AND
DB_Players((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
IsRangedWeapon(_Item, 1, 1)
AND
DB_GLO_Tutorials_TrackedTimers((GUIDSTRING)_Player, "Tutorials_TemporaryEquipmentTutSuppression", "Finished")
THEN
PROC_ShowUnifiedTutorialForPlayer((CHARACTER)_Player, (UNIFIEDTUTORIAL)_Ranged_Attacks);

IsInDangerOfAttackOfOpportunity.description: Returns 1 if _Victim is within a “attack of opportunity” distance from any enemy, returns 0 if the character is safe.
IsInDangerOfAttackOfOpportunity.examples:
IF
TurnStarted(_Player)
AND
DB_PartyMembers((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
IsControlled(_Player, 1)
AND
IsInDangerOfAttackOfOpportunity(_Player, 1)
AND
QRY_GLO_Tutorials_IsAfterHelmFightStart()
THEN
PROC_ShowUnifiedTutorialForPlayer((CHARACTER)_Player, (UNIFIEDTUTORIAL)_Disengage);

IsPublicDomain.description:
Check if _Item ownership is public domain or not.
_Bool: 0 for false, 1 for true.Further InformationA public domain item is one that can be picked up without triggering a crime (yellow outline).
IsPublicDomain.examples:
PROC
PROC_BlockUseOfItem((CHARACTER)_Char,(ITEM)_Item,(INTEGER)_RequestID)
AND
GetCanPickUp(_Item,1)
AND
IsPublicDomain(_Item,0)
THEN
PROC_CRIME_BuyFromTrader_Check(_Char,_Item,_RequestID);

IsReposed.description:
Checks if _Character is currently reposed.
Reposing is an action where a character connects to a specific position, such as lying on a bed, sitting on a chair, or sitting on the ground and plays a default animation appropriate for the pose. The action aligns the character's position with an entity’s predefined socket if an entity is provided, or their current position if no object is specified.Parameters:
_Character : The character that is being queried for reposing
Only entities of type CHARACTER make sense for this call, even if it technically accepts all GUIDSTRING
_Bool: Returned by the query - 1 for true, 0 for false
IsReposed.examples:
PROC
PROC_Circus_StartCombat()
AND
IsReposed(_Circus_DressingRoomSleeper, 1)
THEN
EndRepose(_Circus_DressingRoomSleeper);
IsReposed.seeAlso:
ReposeAdded
ReposeRemoved
EndRepose
SitOnGround
LieOnGround
SitOnEntity
LieOnEntity
StandOnEntity
IsReposedSitting
IsReposedLying
IsReposedStanding
IsReposedConstrained
TemplateCanSitOn

IsTradable.description:
Returns whether or not if an object or _Entity can be traded with an NPC.
If the object or _Entity can be traded the _Bool returns 1, and if otherwise, _Bool returns 0.
Further InformationMore information on the different tradable states an object or _Entity can be set to can be found here SetIsTradable.

IsOnlyPlayerInParty.description:
Sets _Bool to 1 if _Character is the only player in their party, or 0 if they are not.Further InformationPlayer is any avatar or companion, meaning _Character will still be considered the only player even if they have party followers or summons.
Technically the engine supports multiple parties, but BG3 has been designed to only have 1 party exist at the same time. Because of this, the query will function the same as IsOnlyPlayer

IsSpeakerReserved.description: Returns whether _Speaker is already in an interactive dialog.
IsSpeakerReserved.examples:
IF
EnteredCombat(_Goblin, _)
AND
DB_FOR_GnomeGoblins((CHARACTER)_Goblin, _, _, _)
AND
DB_FOR_GnomeGoblins(_OtherGoblin, _, _, _)
AND
IsSpeakerReserved(_OtherGoblin, 1)
THEN
PROC_ForceStopDialog(_OtherGoblin);

IsStoryItem.description: Returns whether _Item is a story item.
IsStoryItem.examples:
IF
EntityEvent(_Item, "InventoryCheck")
AND
IsItem(_Item, 1)
AND
IsStoryItem((ITEM)_Item, 1)
AND
DB_ORI_DarkUrge((CHARACTER)_DarkUrge)
THEN
ToInventory(_Item, _DarkUrge, -1, 1, 1);

IsImmuneToStatusGroup.description:
Query to check if _Target is immune to the specified _StatusGroup, applied by _Cause.
_Target can be a CHARACTER or ITEM.
_StatusGroup is a status group set in any StatusData table.
_Cause specifies the object, either a CHARACTER or ITEM, that the game should consider as the cause of this status. This is mainly useful for scripted skill applications where you want _Target to react as if _Cause removed this status.
_Bool: 0 for false, 1 for true.
IsImmuneToStatusGroup.examples:
PROC
PROC_GithChokepoint_DoFrightenedSavingThrow((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
_Player != _Player_Laezel
AND
IsTagged(_Player, (TAG)_REALLY_GITHYANKI, _IsGithyanki)
AND
DB_Players(_AnyPlayerSource)
AND
IsImmuneToStatusGroup(_Player, "SG_Frightened", _AnyPlayerSource, 0)
AND
QRY_OnlyOnce("GithChokepoint_FrightenedCheck")
THEN
RequestPassiveRoll(_Player, _DragonShadow, "SavingThrow", "Wisdom", (DIFFICULTYCLASS)_DC_Legacy_15, _IsGithyanki, "GithChokepoint_FrightenedSV");
IsImmuneToStatusGroup.seeAlso:
IsImmuneToFallDamage
IsImmuneToStatus
ApplyStatus
HasActiveStatus
StatusApplied
StatusRemoved

IsReposedConstrained.description: Same as IsReposed but also checks for whether _Character is constrained on another entity.
IsReposedConstrained.seeAlso:
ReposeAdded
ReposeRemoved
EndRepose
SitOnGround
LieOnGround
SitOnEntity
LieOnEntity
StandOnEntity
IsReposed
IsReposedSitting
IsReposedLying
IsReposedStanding
TemplateCanSitOn

IsSpellActive.description:
Check if a character has a spell prepared or not from their spellbook.
Parameters:
_Character: character to check.
_SpellID: name of the spell including its spell category (Category_SpellName).
_Bool: returns 1 if the spell is prepared or 0 if not.
IsSpellActive.seeAlso:
UseSpell
AddSpell

ItemTagIsInInventory.description: Query that returns the total number _Count in the inventory of _InventoryHolder  that has a _Tags tag.
ItemTagIsInInventory.examples:
QRY
QRY_UND_DuergarCamp_PlayerGroupHasExplosives()
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
ItemTagIsInInventory("EXPLOSIVES",_Player,_Count)
AND
_Count >= 1
THEN
QuestUpdate("UND_CaveIn","LearnedExplosiveHasSomeAlready");

IsWeaponUnsheathed.description:
Checks whether the character’s weapon is currently unsheathed.
Parameters:
_Character: The character being checked.
_Bool: Returns 1 if unsheathed, 0 otherwise.
IsWeaponUnsheathed.examples:
PROC
PROC_CRIME_StoreWeaponUnsheathed((CHARACTER)_Npc, (INTEGER)_CrimeID)
AND
NOT DB_WeaponUnsheathedState(_Npc, _CrimeID, _)
AND
IsWeaponUnsheathed(_Npc, _FightMode)
THEN
DB_WeaponUnsheathedState(_Npc, _CrimeID, _FightMode);

IsTagged.description: Returns whether _Target is is tagged with _Tag.
IsTagged.examples:
QRY
QRY_LOW_DarkUrgeInParty()
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
IsTagged(_PLayer, (TAG)_DARK_URGE, 1)
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);

IsTrap.description: Returns whether the _Item is a trap, as determined by the sidebar trap property, returning 1 for the _Bool if yes and 0 if not.
IsTrap.examples:
//REGION Summoning a trap
IF
EnteredLevel(_Item,_,_Level)
AND
DB_CurrentLevel(_Level)
AND
QRY_IsExistingSummon(_Item,1)
AND
IsItem(_Item,1)
AND
IsTrap((ITEM)_Item,1)
THEN
ObjectTimerCancel(_Item,"CRIME_TrapSummoned");
DB_CRIME_SummonedTrap(_Item,0);
ObjectTimerLaunch(_Item,"CRIME_TrapSummoned",700);

PlayLoopEffectAtPosition.description: Plays _FxName on _Object at a specific position and outputs _FxHandle.

RealDivide.description: Returns the _Quotient of division of _A by _B.
RealDivide.examples:
PROC
PROC_LOW_SharGrotto_MoveAltar((INTEGER)_Value)
AND
IntegerToReal(_Value, _RealValue)
AND
RealDivide(_RealValue, 1000.0, _Percentage)
AND
RealProduct(0.300, _Percentage, _MoveHeight)
AND
DB_LOW_SharGrotto_LossAltarPos(_Altar, _X, _Y, _Z)
AND
RealSubtract(_Y, _MoveHeight, _NewY)
THEN
ItemMoveToPosition(_Altar, _X, _NewY, _Z, 1.0, 1.0, "MoveAltar", 0);

ItemIsInUserInventory.description:
Returns whether a specific _Item is in any inventory owned by the user who controls the passed _Character. Can also optionally move that _Item if it is found to the _Character’s inventory if _MoveAndReport is set to 1.
Return Values:
Success/Failure: Default return value.
_Bool: 0 if the condition is not satisfied, 1 otherwise

IsInInventoryOf.description:
Checks if a specific _Object is in a specific _Inventory.
Return Values:
_Bool:
0: The _Object is not in the _Inventory.
1: The _Object is within the _Inventory.Further Information_Object and _Inventory can be a character or an item.

IsFreshCorpse.description:
Returns if _Object death type is compatible with Speak with Dead and similar post-death dialogs.
Return Values:
_Bool: returns 1 if _Object death type is compatible with post-death dialogs and 0 if it does.Further InformationHas the same result as the FreshCorpse condition in stats.
By itself death type doesn’t block any post-death dialogs from starting, it’s a safety measure that can be used to avoid broken cinematics when needed.
The death types that considered incompatible:
Acid
Chasm
Electrocution
Explode
Incinerate
Radiant
Necrotic
Disintegrate

IsTrapArmed.description: Returns 1 in _Bool if _Item is an armed trap, and 0 otherwise.
IsTrapArmed.examples:
PROC
PROC_RollResult("GLO_HiddenItemCheck", _Player, _HiddenItem, 1)
AND
IsControlled(_Player, 1)
AND
IsTrapArmed((ITEM)_HiddenItem, 1)
AND
TemplateIsInInventory((ITEMROOT)_TOOL_GEN_Disam_Toolkit, _Player, _Count)
THEN
PROC_GLO_Tutorials_EvaluateTrapsTutorial((CHARACTER)_Player, _Count);

IsStatusFromGroup.description:
Check if the _Status belongs to _StatusGroup.
_Status is a string from any StatusData table in the Stats editor.
_StatusGroup is a string from StatusGroup column in any StatusData table in the Stats Editor.
_Bool: 1 for true (the status belongs to the status group) and 0 to false.
IsStatusFromGroup.examples:
IF
StatusRemoved(_Object, _Status, _, _)
AND
DB_GLO_Mad(_Object)
AND
IsStatusFromGroup(_Status, "SG_Mad", 1)
AND
HasAppliedStatusWithGroup(_Object, "SG_Mad", 0)
THEN
NOT DB_GLO_Mad(_Object);
PROC_GLO_Mad_RestoreIndivRelation(_Object);
IsStatusFromGroup.seeAlso:
ApplyStatus
RemoveStatus
RemoveStatusesWithGroup
HasActiveStatus
HasActiveStatusWithGroup
HasAppliedStatusWithGroup
StatusApplied
StatusRemoved

IsProficientWith.description: Returns an integer of 0 or 1 if the character is proficient with an item.

IsTorch.description:
Checks if _Item is a torch.
Return Values:
_Bool: 0 if _Item is not a torch, 1 if _Item is a torch.
IsTorch.examples:
QRY
QRY_GLO_Tutorials_HasTorchEquipped((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
DB_EquippedItemSlots(_Slot)
AND
GetEquippedItem(_Player,_Slot,_Torch)
AND
IsTorch(_Torch,1)
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);

IsWeapon.description:
Returns if the object is a weapon.
Parameters:
_Item: The item checked
_Bool: returns 1 if the item is a weapon and 0 if it is not.
IsWeapon.examples:
PROC
PROC_MOO_Jailbreak_CheckValidTool((ITEM)_Item)
AND
IsWeapon(_Item, 1)
AND
GetBaseWeaponDamageType(_Item, "Bludgeoning")
THEN
DB_MOO_Jailbreak_ToolOnGround(_Item);

RealMin.description: Returns the smaller of two real values _A and _B as _Minimum.

IsReposedOnEntity.description:
Same as IsReposed but also checks for whether _Character is reposed on a specific entity.
Parameters:
_Character : The character that is being queried for reposing
Only entities of type CHARACTER make sense for this call, even if it technically accepts all GUIDSTRING
_OnEntity: Entity that is checked for whether _Character is reposing on
_Bool: Returned by the query - 1 for true, 0 for false
IsReposedOnEntity.examples:
PROC
PROC_TransformChamber_KillSlaves((ITEM)_TransformChamber_Lever)
AND
DB_TransformChamber_Stations(_Victim, _Station, _)
AND
IsReposedOnEntity(_Victim, _Station, 1)
AND
NOT DB_PermaDefeated(_Victim)
AND
HasActiveStatus(_Victim, "SAPPED", 1)
THEN
PROC_StopLoopEffect(_Victim, "BrainDrain");
Die(_Victim, DEATHTYPE.Psychic, NULL_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, 1, 0);
IsReposedOnEntity.seeAlso:
ReposeAdded
ReposeRemoved
EndRepose
IsReposed
IsReposedSitting
IsReposedLying
IsReposedConstrained
IsReposedStanding

IsTrapDiscovered.description: Returns 1 for _Bool if the _Item is a discovered trap, and 0 otherwise.
IsTrapDiscovered.examples:
IF
ArmedTrapUsed(_Char,_TunnelChest)
AND
IsTrapDiscovered(_TunnelChest, 0)
THEN
SetTrapDiscovered(_TunnelChest, 1);
CreateExplosion(_TunnelChest,"Projectile_Fireball_Trap",-1);

IsReposedLying.description: Same as IsReposed but also checks for whether _Character is lying.
IsReposedLying.seeAlso:
ReposeAdded
ReposeRemoved
EndRepose
SitOnGround
LieOnGround
SitOnEntity
LieOnEntity
StandOnEntity
IsReposed
IsReposedSitting
IsReposedStanding
IsReposedConstrained
TemplateCanSitOn

IsOnStage.description:
Checks if _Object is on stage.
Return Values:
_Bool:
0: _Object is not on stage (off stage).
1: _Object is on stage.

PartyGetGold.description:
Query that returns the full gold amount of the whole party of _Character.
Further InformationPlease note, this call doesn’t take into consideration whether player’s inventories are locked or not, so it will report on money stored in a locked inventory.
It is recommended you use query GetGoldInMagicPockets instead, which will exclude locked inventories. This is important if you are checking available gold for paying someone for example.
PartyGetGold.examples:
PROC
PROC_CheckPartyWealth((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
PartyGetGold(_Player,_Gold)
AND
_Gold > 1000
THEN
DB_State_PartyWealthyEnough(1);

PositionIsInTrigger.description:
Checks if a point with coordinates _X, _Y and _Z is within _Trigger.
Specific Osiris references, if used, should be in code formatting.
Return Values:
_Bool:
0: The point is not within _Trigger
1: The point is within _Trigger

ObjectTimerExists.description: A query to check if there is an existing _Timer attached to an _Object that has been launched and exists. Timers cease to exist when they have been Cancelled or Finished.
ObjectTimerExists.examples:
IF
Died(_Object)
AND
ObjectTimerExists(_Object, _Timer, 1)
THEN
ObjectTimerCancel(_Object, (STRING)_Timer);

IsReposedSitting.description: Same as IsReposed but also checks for whether _Character is sitting.
IsReposedSitting.seeAlso:
ReposeAdded
ReposeRemoved
EndRepose
SitOnGround
LieOnGround
SitOnEntity
LieOnEntity
StandOnEntity
IsReposed
IsReposedLying
IsReposedStanding
IsReposedConstrained
TemplateCanSitOn

RealSubtract.description: Returns the difference between _A and _B as _Result, as _A - _B
RealSubtract.examples:
IF
FlagSet((FLAG)_Event_HardOption, _TorturedAdventurer, _)
AND
GetHitpointsPercentage(_TorturedAdventurer, _Percent)
AND
RealSubtract(_Percent, 25.0, _NewPercent)
THEN
PROC_SetHitpointsPercentage_BlockSelfHealing(_TorturedAdventurer, _NewPercent);

Random.description: Returns a random integer value between 0 and (_Modulo - 1). For a negative _Modulo, the result will be between _Modulo and 0.
Random.examples:
IF
EntityEvent((CHARACTER)_Gur, "GurTeleported")
AND
Random(1500, _Random)
THEN
RealtimeObjectTimerLaunch(_Gur, "AppearDelay", _Random);

RealToString.description:
Converts a real number to a string.
Further InformationIt will convert up to 6 decimal digits and will then round to the closest number for the sixth digit, if there are more decimals after it. So 3.14151684  will become “3.141517”.
RealToString.examples:
IF
TextEvent("test")
AND
GetHostCharacter(_Player)
AND
RealToString(3.1415168465745232546458587678754, _String)
THEN
DebugText(_Player, _String); //This will show up as 3.141517

RealProduct.examples:
QRY
QRY_END_MorphicPool_GetTimeDifference((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
GetDistanceTo(_Player, _RapidMindMeldDistancePoint, _PlayerDistance)
AND
DB_END_MorphicPool_RapidMindMeldMaxDistance(_MaxDistance)
AND
RealDivide(_PlayerDistance, _MaxDistance, _NormalizedDistance)
AND
DB_END_MorphicPool_ActiveRapidMindMeldStartTime(_MaxTime)
AND
IntegerToReal(_MaxTime, _RealMaxTime)
AND
RealProduct(_NormalizedDistance, _RealMaxTime, _RealAdjustedTime)
AND
RealToInteger(_RealAdjustedTime, _AdjustedTime)
THEN
PROC_END_MorphicPool_SetAdjustedTime(_AdjustedTime);

IsSummon.description:
Returns whether _PotentialSummon is a summon.
Further InformationSummons can be player-controlled or not.
Summons can also be distinguished by the fact that hey have the SUMMON tag.
If you wish to know whether a summon is player-controlled, also check IsPlayer.
IsSummon.examples:
PROC
PROC_END_BrainBattle_CheckRemoveFollower((GUIDSTRING)_Character)
AND
IsCharacter(_Character, 1)
AND
IsSummon((CHARACTER)_Character, 1)
THEN
SetOnStage(_Character, 0);

StartGameplayTimeline.description:
Deprecated. Do not use.
Use StartWorldTimeline instead.

PartyGetActionResourceValue.description:
A query used to check the amount of resources a player has of a particular skillset (Such as Warlock Spell Slots)
Parameters:
_Player The character to check
_ResourceName the string of the Resource name: Such as “SorceryPoint” or “WarlockSpellSlot”
_Amount The amount of resources you have
PartyGetActionResourceValue.examples:
QRY
QRY_HasTadpoleJar((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
PartyGetActionResourceValue(_Player, "TadpolePowerPoint", _Amount)
AND
_Amount >= 1.0
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);

ResolveTranslatedString.description: Takes _TranslatedStringKey and return a localized text _TranslatedString for it.
ResolveTranslatedString.examples:
PROC
PROC_GEN_SaveTitle()
AND
ObjectGetTitle(_Orin, _TranslatedStringKey)
AND
ResolveTranslatedString(_TranslatedStringKey, _TranslatedString)
THEN
DB_GEN_SavedTitle(_TranslatedStringKey, _TranslatedString);

IsInNarrativeCombat.description: Confirms if the entity is in a narrative combat.
IsInNarrativeCombat.examples:
PROC
PROC_EXA_Example_JoinNarrativeCombat((CHARACTER)_Character)
AND
DB_EXA_Example_NarrativeCombatID(_ID)
AND
IsInNarrativeCombat(_Character, _ID, 0)
AND
NOT DB_EXA_Example_NarrativeCombatCharacter(_ID, _Character)
THEN
SetInNarrativeCombat(_Character, _ID, 1);
DB_EXA_Example_NarrativeCombatCharacter(_ID, _Character);

ObjectGetTitle.description:
Returns a translated string key _TranslatedStringKey for _Target’s title.
Further InformationAfterwards _TranslatedStringKey could be used with ResolveTranslatedString.
ObjectGetTitle.examples:
PROC
PROC_Orin_SaveTitle()
AND
ObjectGetTitle(_Orin, _TranslatedStringKey)
AND
ResolveTranslatedString(_TranslatedStringKey, _TranslatedString)
THEN
DB_Orin_SavedTitle(_TranslatedStringKey, _TranslatedString);

StartWorldTimeline.description:
Starts a world timeline _Dialog dialogue within _Trigger.
Return Values:
_Success: returns 1 if the dialogue successfully started, 0 otherwise.
_DialogID: returns a dialogue instance ID, if the timeline dialogue started successfully.Further InformationFails if _Dialog or _Trigger are NULL or invalid.
StartWorldTimeline.examples:
IF
LevelGameplayStarted("BGO_Main_A", _)
AND
NOT DB_GLO_ActiveWorldTimelines(_Boats, "BGO_Main_A", _)
AND
StartWorldTimeline(_Boats, TimelineSceneTrigger_Boats, _, _ID)
THEN
DB_GLO_ActiveWorldTimelines(_Boats, "BGO_Main_A", _ID);

TemplateIsInInventory.description:
Returns the _Count of items of the given _ItemTemplate in the inventory of the specified _InventoryHolder.
Return Values:
_Count: the number of instances of the given template in the inventory, can be 0.Further Information_InventoryHolder can be a character or an item.
TemplateIsInInventory.examples:
IF
FlagSet((FLAG)CheckForItemX_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000), _InventoryHolder, _)
AND
TemplateIsInInventory(ItemXTemplate_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, _InventoryHolder, _Count)
AND
_Count >= 1
THEN
//Do whatever you want with the item

TimerExists.description: A query to check if there is an existing _Timer that has been launched and exists. Timers cease to exist when they have been Cancelled or Finished.
TimerExists.examples:
IF
Died(_Object)
AND
TimerExists(_Timer, 1)
THEN
TimerCancel((STRING)_Timer);

RealSqrRoot.description: Returns the (real) square root of a real number.
RealSqrRoot.examples:
IF
DB_RealAmount((REAL)_Amount)
AND
RealSqrRoot(_Amount,_SqrAmount)
THEN
DB_RealSqrAmount((REAL)_SqrAmount);

IsStuck.description:
Returns whether _Item is in a stuck state. Stuck condition is caused by certain statuses: PETRIFIED, FROZEN, etc.
Further Information_IsStuck: 0 if _Item is not stuck, 1 otherwise.
Success/Failure: Default return value.
IsStuck.examples:
PROC
PROC_BlockUseOfItem(_Player, _EscapingZhentarim_HiddenEntrance)
AND
IsStuck(_EscapingZhentarim_HiddenEntrance, 0)
AND
IsLocked(_EscapingZhentarim_HiddenEntrance, 0)
AND
NOT DB_PLA_HuntCoward_WardRobeState(1)
THEN
DB_PLA_HuntCoward_Wardrobe_PlannedState(1);
DB_CustomUseItemResponse(_Player, _EscapingZhentarim_HiddenEntrance, 0);

RealToInteger.description:
Truncates/rounds down a real number to an integer.
Further InformationBe warned: if you are dynamically altering numbers in Osiris and converting between reals and integers, due to float imprecision a small margin might build up and you might end up with a round number one lower than expected. The Osiris log also incorrectly rounds some numbers so it may not be immediately obvious why this is happening.
RealToInteger.examples:
IF
DB_RealAmount((REAL)_RealAmount)
AND
RealToInteger(_RealAmount, _IntAmount)
THEN
DB_IntegerAmount((INTEGER)_IntAmount);

PlayLoopBeamEffect.description:
Similar to PlayBeamEffect in which it attempts to play a beam effect _FxName from the _SourceBone of _Object, to the _TargetBone of _Target, but with it being looped.
The query returns true if the VFX was played successfully.
Further InformationIf no bone is specified, the default bone is used.
The query outputs the _FxHandle of the specified _FxName. This can be used to call StopLoopEffect.

StatStringIsInInventoryOf.description:
Returns the _Amount of instances of a specific _StatString is in a specific _InventoryHolder’s inventory.
Return Values:
_Amount:
The number of objects in the inventory that match the _StatStringFurther Information_InventoryHolder can be a character or an item.
Consider looking by RootTemplate instead of Stats by using TemplateIsInInventory instead.
StatStringIsInInventoryOf.examples:
IF
FlagSet((FLAG)CheckForItemX_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000), _InventoryHolder, _)
AND
StatStringIsInInventory("UNI_ItemX", _InventoryHolder, _Amount)
AND
_Amount >= 1
THEN
//Do whatever you want if the item is present

TemplateCanSitOn.description:
Despite its name, this query checks if _ItemTemplate is a template that has sockets for a character to either sit, lie or constrain on it.
Notably it does not check for the Standing type of repose, which was added later and this query was not updated to support itParameters:
_ItemTemplate: Template that is queried
_Bool: Outputted result - 1 for true, 0 for false
TemplateCanSitOn.examples:
IF
ItemEnteredTrigger(_Item, _ExampleTrigger, _)
AND
GetTemplate(_Item, _ItemTemplate)
AND
TemplateCanSitOn((ITEMROOT)_ItemTemplate, 1)
THEN
Use(_ExampleCharacter, _Item, 1, 1, "Example_RestedOnObject");
TemplateCanSitOn.seeAlso:
ReposeAdded
ReposeRemoved
EndRepose
SitOnGround
LieOnGround
SitOnEntity
LieOnEntity
StandOnEntity
IsReposed
IsReposedSitting
IsReposedLying
IsReposedStanding
IsReposedConstrained

TaggedItemsGetCountInMagicPockets.description: Returns the number of items that fit the tag expression _Tags in the inventory of _Source or any party inventories accessible to them (including camp chests) as _Amount.
TaggedItemsGetCountInMagicPockets.examples:
IF
UseStarted(_Player,_DirtMound)
AND
DB_ShovelArea(_DirtMound,_)
AND
NOT DB_Shovelling_Mound(_,_,_DirtMound,_)
AND
TaggedItemsGetCountInMagicPockets("_SHOVEL",_Player,_Count)
AND
_Count > 0
AND
IsTagged(_Player,(TAG)_WILDSHAPE,0)
THEN
UseSpell(_Player,"Target_Dig",_DirtMound);
TaggedItemsGetCountInMagicPockets.seeAlso:
SetMagicPocketsFlag
MagicPocketsDrop
MagicPocketsDropByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTag
MagicPocketsMoveTo
MagicPocketsMoveToByTemplate
MagicPocketsMoveToByTag
ClearMagicPocketsFlag
IsInMagicPockets
TemplateGetCountInMagicPockets
StatStringGetCountInMagicPockets

TemplateGetDisplayString.description: Returns a display name _StringHandle of an item root template _ItemTemplate.
TemplateGetDisplayString.examples:
IF
DialogStarted((DIALOGRESOURCE)_Courier_Dog,_)
AND
QRY_OnlyOnce_Reset("Courier_Dog_SetFetchObject")
AND
DB_Courier_Dog_FetchObject(_ItemTemplate)
AND
QRY_OnlyOnce("Courier_Dog_SetFetchObject")
AND
TemplateGetDisplayString(_ItemTemplate,_StringHandle)
THEN
DialogSetVariableTranslatedString((DIALOGRESOURCE)_Courier_Dog,"Courier_Dog_FetchedItemDescription",_StringHandle);

TemplateIsInPartyInventory.description:
Returns the _CountMoved of items of the given _ItemTemplate in the party’s inventory.  These items can all be optionally moved to to the provided _Character if _MoveAndReport is set to 1.
You need to provide a _Character even if you do not want to move the items.
Return Values:
_CountMoved: the number of instances of the given template in the inventory, can be 0.Further Information_InventoryHolder can be a character or an item.
Note this query is very rarely used, TemplateGetCountInMagicPockets is preferred.
TemplateIsInPartyInventory.examples:
//Items will not be moved because _MoveAndReport is 0
IF
FlagSet((FLAG)CheckForItemX_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000), _Character, _)
AND
TemplateIsInInventory(ItemXTemplate_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, _Character, _CountMoved, 0)
AND
_Count >= 1
THEN
//Do whatever you want with the item

IsSubstring.description:
Checks whether _StringA contains _StringB as a substring.
e.g. _StringB “fox“ is a substring of  _StringA “the quick brown fox”.
IsSubstring.examples:
PROC
PROC_UnequipVanitySlots((CHARACTER)_Character)
AND
DB_EquippedItemSlots(_Slot)
AND
IsSubstring(_Slot, "Vanity", 1)
AND
GetEquippedItem(_Character, _Slot, _Item)
THEN
Unequip(_Character, _Item);

ItemIsInPartyInventory.description:
Returns whether a specific _Item is in the party’s inventory. Can also optionally move that _Item if it is found to a specific _Character’s inventory if _MoveAndReport is set to 1.
Return Values:
Success/Failure: Default return value.
_Bool: 0 if the condition is not satisfied, 1 otherwise

TriggerGetRandomPosition.description:
Returns random position inside of _Trigger.
Further InformationWorks only with Box and Sphere triggers.
The query backend ensures that the random position will be on the ai gird if possible.
TriggerGetRandomPosition.examples:
PROC
PROC_PetrifiedDrow_RevealSpectator((CHARACTER)_Player, (CHARACTER)_Creature)
AND
DB_PetrifiedDrow_PetrifiedCreatures(_Creature, _, _, _, _, _AppearArea, _, _)
AND
TriggerGetRandomPosition(_AppearArea, _X, _Y, _Z)
AND
QRY_OnlyOnce("PetrifiedDrow_Reveal")
THEN
TeleportToPosition(_PetrifiedDrow_Spectator, _X, _Y, _Z, "PetrifiedDrow_SpectatorTeleported", 0, 0, 0);

SatisfiesTagExpression.description:
Checks if the _Target satisfies the specified _TagExpression.
Further Information_TagExpression - A tag expression is a string that represents a logic expression involving tags, and (&), or (|), and not (!) operators.
e.g. SatisfiesTagExpression(_Target, "TagA & TagB", _Bool)
Returns true if _Target has both TagA and TagB
SatisfiesTagExpression.examples:
IF
AddedTo(_Corpse,_Player,_)
AND
DB_Players((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
DB_QuestIsOpened("WYR_Windmill")
AND
QRY_QuestUpdateIsUnlockedForAny("WYR_Windmill","FindBody")
AND
NOT QRY_QuestUpdateIsUnlockedForAny("WYR_Windmill","ReturnBody")
AND
SatisfiesTagExpression(_Corpse,"DEFEATED & HUMANOID & !PLAYABLE",1)
THEN
QuestUpdate("WYR_Windmill", "ReturnBody");

UserRemoveTaggedItems.description: Finds up to _Amount items that match tag expression _Tags in the inventory of the _Character and other characters controlled by the same user, returns the number found as _AmountRemoved and deletes those items.
UserRemoveTaggedItems.examples:
PROC
PROC_MagicLock_ConsumeKey((CHARACTER)_Char, (ITEM)_Door)
AND
UserRemoveTaggedItems(_Char, "MAGICKEY", 1, _Number)
AND
_Number >= 1
THEN
Open(_Door);

WentOnStage.description:
Use this to check if _Object has been put on stage or not.
_IsOnStageNow:
0: _Object is not on stage
1: _Object is on stage.
WentOnStage.examples:
IF
WentOnStage((CHARACTER)_CaravanMember, 0)
AND
DB_SCL_Drider_CaravanGroup(_CaravanMember, _)
AND
GetFlag(_Event_RunsAwayFromCaravan, _CaravanMember, 1)
THEN
PROC_SCL_Drider_RemoveFromCaravanGroup(_CaravanMember);

TemplateIsInUserInventory.description:
Returns the _CountMoved of items of the given _ItemTemplate in the User who controls a specific _Character.  These items can all be optionally moved to to the provided _Character if _MoveAndReport is set to 1.
You need to provide a _Character even if you do not want to move the items.
Return Values:
_CountMoved: the number of instances of the given template in the inventory, can be 0.Further Information_InventoryHolder can be a character or an item.
Note this query is very rarely used, TemplateGetCountInMagicPockets is preferred.
TemplateIsInUserInventory.examples:
//Items will not be moved because _MoveAndReport is 0
IF
FlagSet((FLAG)CheckForItemX_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000), _Character, _)
AND
TemplateIsInUserInventory(ItemXTemplate_00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, _Character, _CountMoved, 0)
AND
_Count >= 1
THEN
//Do whatever you want with the item

RemoveApprovalRating.description:
Removes the approval value shard between the _RatingOwner and the _RatedCharacter.
Parameters:
_RatingOwner: Typically the companion who offers the approval.
_RatedCharacter: The character who is rated by the companion, typically the player.
_Bool: Returns whether it succeeded or not.

Substring.description:
Returns a substring _Result of the specified string _String starting at index _Start with length _Count.
Further InformationThe first character counts as index 1, NOT 0.
Using -1 as _Count will NOT work and return an empty string.
Substring.examples:
INIT
DB_TechnicalName("S_UND_UnfortunateGnome");
KB
IF
DB_TechnicalName((STRING)_TechnicalName)
AND
Substring(_TechnicalName,3,3,_RegionPrefix)
THEN
DB_RegionPrefix((STRING)_RegionPrefix); //will be "UND"

UserGetSafeRomanceOption.description:
Returns if the _UserID has the option Show Sexual and Violent Cinematics enabled or not.
Parameters:
_UserID: user for who we want to know if the option is enabled.
_State: returns 1 if enabled, and 0 if disabled.Further InformationSee SafeRomanceOptionChanged for more information.

GetSurfaceGroundAt.description: Returns the _Surface in the ground layer at the _Target's location.
GetSurfaceGroundAt.examples:
IF
TurnStarted(_Volo)
AND
HasActiveStatus(_Volo,"TIED_TO_CHAIR",1)
AND
GetSurfaceGroundAt(_VoloFate_ExecutionCart,"SurfaceFire")
THEN
PROC_TryStartAD((DIALOGRESOURCE)_VoloFate_AD_CryHelpEachTurn,_Volo);

InSamePartyGroup.description:
Returns _Bool as 1 if _Character1 and _Character2 are chained player characters, or _Bool 0 if they are not.Further InformationPlayers can be chained together with  call AttachToPartyGroup
Players can be unchained with call DetachFromPartyGroup
InSamePartyGroup.examples:
PROC
PROC_UND_EbonLake_Raft_ComposeCrew((CHARACTER)_Character)
AND
DB_Players(_Character2)
AND
_Character != _Character2
AND
IsInPartyWith(_Character2, _Character, 1)
AND
InSamePartyGroup(_Character2, _Character, 1)
THEN
DB_UND_EbonLake_Raft_Crew(_Character2);

IsEquipmentWithProficiency.description:
Check if an item is for an assigned equipment proficiency.
Further InformationExample Proficiencies:
"Maces"
"Longswords"
"Warhammers"
"Battleaxes"
"Clubs"
"Daggers"
"Staffs"
IsEquipmentWithProficiency.examples:
IF
ItemEnteredTrigger(_Weapon, _ItemTrigger, _)
AND
DB_TrackedWeaponTypesAndFlags(_WeaponType, _Flag)
AND
IsEquipmentWithProficiency(_Weapon, _WeaponType, 1)
THEN
SetFlag(_Flag);

IsImmortal.description: Checks if character _Character is immortal. Return 0 if the character is not immortal and 1 if the character is immortal.
IsImmortal.examples:
IF
LeftLevel(_GithKnight, "WLD_Main_A")
AND
IsImmortal(_GithKnight, 1)
THEN
SetImmortal(_GithKnight, 0);
PROC_SetInvulnerable(_GithKnight, 0);

ItemGetSupplyValue.description:
A query that returns the amount of supplies a particular item is worth for Long Rest purposes.
Parameters:
_Item the item to be checked.
_Value the supply value of the item
ItemGetSupplyValue.examples:
PROC
PROC_OnItemDonated((CHARACTER)_Player, (ITEM)_Item)
AND
NOT DB_GLO_Background_Acolyte_Donator(_Player)
AND
NOT DB_GLO_Backgrounds_Blocked("Acolyte_LongRestDonation")
AND
ItemGetSupplyValue(_Item, _Value)
AND
_Value > 0
THEN
DB_GLO_Background_Acolyte_Donator(_Player)

IsClosed.description:
Returns whether _Item is closed.
Further InformationThis query works both for doors and for items with an open/close use action. For items that cannot open/close, the query can either fail or return 0.
IsClosed.examples:
IF
UseFinished(_Player, (ITEM)_LossDoor, _)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
DB_GlobalFlag((FLAG)_State_ViconiaDefeated)
AND
IsClosed((ITEM)_LossDoor, 1)
AND
QRY_OnlyOnce("LossDoorInteract")
THEN
PROC_TryStartAD((DIALOGRESOURCE)_SharGrotto_PAD_LossDoor, _Player);

IsNarrativeCombat.description:
Confirms if the GUID passed into it is a narrative combat.
Further InformationNormal combat and narrative combat are mostly the same in the engine, with the main exception being that narrative combat GUIDs are checked when generic events take place.
As there should always be detailed tracking narrative combats, it should be very edge-case to require a query to confirm the combat id is classified as a narrative comabt.

HasActiveStatusWithGroup.description:
Returns _Bool whether _Target is currently under the influence of a status part of _StatusGroup.
_Target is a CHARACTER or an ITEM.
_StatusGroup is a string from StatusGroup column in any StatusData table in the Stats Editor.
_Bool: 1 for true (hast the status group) and 0 to false.
HasActiveStatusWithGroup.examples:
IF
TurnStarted(_GoblinRaider_Captain)
AND
NOT DB_CantTalk_IgnoreCombat(_GoblinRaider_Captain) 
AND
QRY_OnlyOnce("DEN_GoblinRaider_Captain_AD")
AND
HasActiveStatusWithGroup(_GoblinRaider_Captain, "SG_Unconscious", 0)
THEN
PROC_TryStartAD((DIALOGRESOURCE)_RaidingParty_AD_GoblinCaptain,_GoblinRaider_Captain);

IsOnlyPlayer.description: Sets _Bool to 1 if _Character is the only player, or 0 if they are not.Further InformationPlayer is any avatar or companion, meaning _Character will still be considered the only player even if they have party followers or summons.

ItemGetGoldValue.description: Query that returns the value of _Item from data.
ItemGetGoldValue.examples:
PROC
PROC_OnItemDonated((CHARACTER)_Player, (ITEM)_Item)
AND
NOT DB_GLO_Backgrounds_Blocked("Soldier_DonationsGiven")
AND
ItemGetGoldValue(_Item, _Value)
AND
_Value > 0
THEN
PROC_GLO_Backgrounds_CompleteGoal(_Player, "Soldier_DonationsGiven");

IsForceUpdate.description: Checks if _Target is being force update (which is set up via SetForceUpdate). Returns 0 if it is not, 1 if it is.
IsForceUpdate.examples:
IF
DB_InRegion(_Player, (TRIGGER)_MonkAmulet_LakeArea)
AND
DB_PartyMembers(_Player)
AND
IsForceUpdate(_LavaElemental, 0)
THEN
SetForceUpdate(_LavaElemental, 1);

TemplateGetCountInMagicPockets.description: Returns the number of items of the root template _Root in the inventory of _Source or any party inventories accessible to them (including camp chests) as _Amount.
TemplateGetCountInMagicPockets.examples:
QRY
QRY_GLO_Tutorials_UserHasLockpick((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
TemplateGetCountInMagicPockets(_ThievesTools_A_Closed, _Player, _Count)
AND
_Count > 0
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);
QRY
QRY_GLO_Tutorials_UserHasLockpick((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
TemplateGetCountInMagicPockets(_ThievesTools_A_Open, _Player, _Count)
AND
_Count > 0
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);
TemplateGetCountInMagicPockets.seeAlso:
SetMagicPocketsFlag
MagicPocketsDrop
MagicPocketsDropByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTag
MagicPocketsMoveTo
MagicPocketsMoveToByTemplate
MagicPocketsMoveToByTag
ClearMagicPocketsFlag
IsInMagicPockets
TaggedItemsGetCountInMagicPockets
StatStringGetCountInMagicPockets

IsMovementBlocked.description:
A query to use to see if a character is blocked from being able to move.
Parameters:
_Character - The character to check if they have the ability to move
_Bool - Value of 0 means the character does not have their movement blocked, 1 means they doFurther InformationUnique from CanMove in that it ignores checking movement resources, only focusing on if something prevents movement.
IsMovementBlocked.examples:
IF
FlagSet((FLAG)_Event_MarcusToBalcony, _, _)
AND
IsMovementBlocked((CHARACTER)_FlamingSpy, 0)
THEN
CharacterMoveTo((CHARACTER)_FlamingSpy, _MarcusBalconyPoint, "Stroll", "");

PlayLoopEffectAtPositionAndRotation.description:
Similar to PlayEffectAtPositionAndRotation in which it attempts to play an effect _FxName scaled to _Scale, at the position _X, _Y, _Z, with rotation _XAngle,  _YAngle, _ZAngle.
The query returns true if the VFX was played successfully.
Further InformationThe query outputs the _FxHandle of the specified _FxName. This can be used to call StopLoopEffect.

IsInventoryEmpty.description:
Returns whether or not the _InventoryHolder’s inventory is empty.
Return Values:
_Bool:
0: inventory is not empty; i.e. has objects in it.
1: inventory is empty.
IsInventoryEmpty.examples:
IF
UseFinished(_Player, _Chest, 1)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
IsInventoryEmpty(_Chest, 1)
THEN
//Do something with the empty chest

WasTutorialCompletedForUser.description:
Checks whether the player has already finished the tutorial level.
Further InformationCheck the truthiness of the record in player’s profile created via call CompleteTutorialForUser.
WasTutorialCompletedForUser.examples:
//automatic reset of tutorials if you've finished the Tutorial level
PROC
PROC_TUT_CharacterCreation_ShowTutorialForUser((INTEGER)_UserID)
AND
QRY_TUT_CharacterCreation_GetUserDummy(_UserID)
AND
DB_QRYRTN_TUT_CharacterCreation_GetUserDummy(_CCDummy)
AND
WasTutorialCompletedForUser(_UserID, _TutCompleted)
THEN
DB_ResetTutorialsRequest(_UserID, _TutCompleted); //cache and wait for character creation level to be loaded
IF
DB_CurrentLevel(_Level)
AND
IsCharacterCreationLevel(_Level,1) //are we in character creation now?
AND
DB_ResetTutorialsRequest(_UserID, _TutCompleted)
THEN
NOT DB_ResetTutorialsRequest(_UserID, _TutCompleted);
ResetTutorialsRequest(_UserID, _TutCompleted);

OpenCharacterLootUI.description:
Opens an inventory view of the _Target character for the _Looter character (a player).
Parameters:
_Looter - Character doing the looting.
_Target - Character being looted.
_Bool - “0” if _Target is not lootable at all, “1” if it is.Further InformationThis is the same inventory view you get when looting dead bodies.
_Target does not need to be dead.
Being lootable depends on two things:
The lootable flag needs to be set on the character, either via the side bar in the editor or via SetCharacterLootable call. Note that the query will only check this condition and fail if it's not met, and not the next one.
The character needs to be in a lootable state. All dead characters are by default in a lootable state, but others can also be made lootable by applying the Lootable boost, normally through a status, KnockOut or Surrendered characters.
Note that there are always some frame delays before a status takes effect in the game (wait for the CanBeLootedCapabilityChanged event to arrive).
When this query returns 1, it results in a RequestCanLoot event. If the character was not in a lootable state, then regardless of what Osiris replies, the looting will fail.
OpenCharacterLootUI.examples:
PROC
PROC_FlagReactionAfterDialog(_NPC, (FLAG)_NonLethal_Loot, _, _DialogID)
AND
DB_DialogPlayers(_DialogID, _Player, _)
AND
OpenCharacterLootUI((CHARACTER)_Player, (CHARACTER)_NPC, 1)
THEN
ClearFlag((FLAG)_NonLethal_Loot, _NPC);

RealMax.description: Returns the greater of two real values _A and _B as _Maximum.
RealMax.examples:
QRY
QRY_END_MorphicPool_GetTimeDifference((CHARACTER)_Player)
AND
GetDistanceTo(_Player, _RapidMindMeldDistancePoint, _PlayerDistance)
AND
DB_END_MorphicPool_RapidMindMeldMaxDistance(_MaxDistance)
AND
RealDivide(_PlayerDistance, _MaxDistance, _NormalizedDistance)
AND
DB_END_MorphicPool_ActiveRapidMindMeldStartTime(_MaxTime)
AND
IntegerToReal(_MaxTime, _RealMaxTime)
AND
RealProduct(_NormalizedDistance, _RealMaxTime, _RealAdjustedTime)
AND
RealToInteger(_RealAdjustedTime, _AdjustedTime)
THEN
PROC_END_MorphicPool_SetAdjustedTime(_AdjustedTime);

SpellHasSpellFlag.description:
Check if a spell has a flag set or not.
Parameters:
_SpellID: name of the spell including its spell category (Category_SpellName).
_SpellFlag: name of the spell flag to check.
_HasFlag: returns 1 if the flag is set for that spell or 0 if not.Further InformationSpell flags can be found in the SpellFlags field of a spell’s definition in the stats editor.
SpellHasSpellFlag.examples:
IF
UsingSpellOnTarget(_Player,_DyingSmuggler,_Spell,_,_,_)
AND
DB_DyingAttacker((CHARACTER)_DyingSmuggler)
AND
NOT DB_DyingDialogStarted(_)
AND
SpellHasSpellFlag(_Spell,"IsHarmful",0)
AND
QRY_SelectAndStartDialog(_DyingSmuggler,_Player)
THEN
DB_DyingDialogStarted(1);
SpellHasSpellFlag.seeAlso:
UseSpell
AddSpell
RemoveSpell
UseSpellAtPosition
CastedSpell
CastSpell
CastSpellFailed
LearnedSpell
StartedPreviewingSpell
UsingSpell
UsingSpellAtPosition
UsingSpellInTrigger
UsingSpellOnTarget
CanLearnSpell
CanShowSpellForCharacter
GetSpellFromSet
HasSpell
IsSpellActive

StatStringGetCountInMagicPockets.description: Returns the number of items that have the _StatName stats in the inventory of _Source or any party inventories accessible to them (including camp chests) as _Amount.
StatStringGetCountInMagicPockets.seeAlso:
SetMagicPocketsFlag
MagicPocketsDrop
MagicPocketsDropByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTemplate
MagicPocketsDestroyLocalItemsByTag
MagicPocketsMoveTo
MagicPocketsMoveToByTemplate
MagicPocketsMoveToByTag
ClearMagicPocketsFlag
IsInMagicPockets
TaggedItemsGetCountInMagicPockets
TemplateGetCountInMagicPockets

IsInvisibleByScript.description:
Works much like IsInvisible, but only checks if an _Object has been set to invisible forcibly through script. This does not account for spells, scrolls and potions.
Further Information_Bool will return 1 if the _Object is invisible through script and 0 if not.
An object set to become invisible with the SetVisible call will return 1.

StayInAiHints.description:
Returns if the _Target is set to stay within AiHints or if they are not.
The _Bool will return 1 if true and 0 if false.

IsPoisoned.description:
Returns whether given _Item is poisoned.
Further InformationChecks an ITEM_IS_POISONED internal flag on an item.
IsPoisoned.examples:
IF
UseStarted(_Player,_PoisonedItem)
AND
DB_Players(_Player)
AND
IsPoisoned(_PoisonedItem,1)
THEN
PROC_PlayerUsedPoisonedItem(_Player);

UserGetGold.description: Query that returns the full gold amount of all characters controlled by the User of _Character.

RealSum.description: Returns the sum of real values _A and _B as _Sum.
RealSum.examples:
QRY
QRY_UND_KuoToaGod_UpdateStatusDuration((REAL)_Duration)
AND
HasActiveStatus(_KuoToaGod, "BLOODLUST_REDCAP", 1)
AND
GetStatusTurns(_KuoToaGod, "BLOODLUST_REDCAP", _Turns)
AND
IntegerProduct(_Turns, 6, _TurnInSeconds)
AND
IntegerToReal(_TurnInSeconds, _TurnReal)
AND
RealSum(_TurnReal, _Duration, _NewTotalDuaration)
THEN
ApplyStatus(_KuoToaGod, "BLOODLUST_REDCAP", _NewTotalDuaration, 1);

IntegerSubtract.description: Returns the difference between _A and _B as _Result, as _A - _B.
IntegerSubtract.examples:
PROC
PROC_GLO_DefeatCounter_Notify("IRN_Prisoners", _PrisonerCount, _DefeatedPrisoners, _PermaDefeatedPrisoners)
AND
IntegerSum(_DefeatedPrisoners, _PermaDefeatedPrisoners, _TotalDefeatedPrisoners)
AND
_TotalDefeatedPrisoners!=0
AND
IntegerSubtract(_PrisonerCount, _TotalDefeatedPrisoners, _RemainingPrisoners)
AND
_RemainingPrisoners == 1
AND
NOT DB_GlobalFlag(_State_AllButOne)
THEN
PROC_GlobalSetFlagAndCache((FLAG)_State_AllButOne);

HasMeleeWeaponEquipped.description:
Returns true if the Melee type is equipped.
Further InformationYou may check for “Mainhand", “Offhand", or “Any" if you want to check for either the Mainhand or Offhand.
HasMeleeWeaponEquipped.examples:
QRY
QRY_HasAnyWeaponEquipped((CHARACTER)_Char)
AND
HasMeleeWeaponEquipped(_Char,"Any",_Melee)
AND
HasRangedWeaponEquipped(_Char,"Any",_Ranged)
AND
DB_LogicOr(_Melee,_Ranged,1)
THEN
DB_NOOP(1);

GetEnterCombatRange.description: Returns _EnterCombatRange which is a range in which entities can enter combat.
GetEnterCombatRange.examples:
IF
EnteredCombat(_Object,_Combat)
AND
DB_PartyMembers((CHARACTER)_Object)
AND
GetEnterCombatRange(_EnterCombatRange)
AND
DB_PartyMembers(_PartyMember)
AND
_PartyMember != _Object
AND
IsAlly(_PartyMember,_Object,1)
AND
GetDistanceTo(_PartyMember,_Object,_Distance)
AND
_Distance <= _EnterCombatRange
AND
NOT QRY_IsInAllowJoinCombatDialog(_PartyMember)
THEN
PROC_TEMP_RequestStopDialog(_PartyMember);
